itinerary: <p>Transfer from train or bus station to Titilaka hotel.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Train tickets to Machu Picchu in Expedition service for guide for full day tour. From / to Ollantaytambo station. </p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up from your hotel and transferred to the local market in Surquillo, which has one of the greatest variety of products in the city. Explore the enormous diversity of fruits and vegetables available here and try them! Then, you will be transferred to the Chinatown of Lima, a traditional and striking space where you can find the best of the Chinese colony in Lima such as restaurants, Chinese products, and more. From here, you will leave for Cordano, a famous and traditional bar located next to the Government Palace. Here you can choose between different Peruvian typical appetisers. Then go on a sightseeing tour through the Main Square of Lima, where you can appreciate the buildings around it like the City Town Hall, the Cathedral, the Government Palace and the Archbishopric. After this exciting visit, you will be transferred back to your hotel.</div><p><br/></p><p></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the afternoon you will be picked up from your hotel to take a tour of the seafront of the districts of San Isidro, Miraflores and Barranco, one of the most attractive areas of the city. Enjoy the breathtaking view of the Pacific Ocean from the top of the cliffs and pass by the famous &#34;Love Park&#34;. Then, you will be transferred to the Pisco Museum for a demonstrative experience. Here, you will learn about the history, the varieties and the tradition around this national drink. The activity of approximately one hour includes the preparation of a cocktail by an experienced bartender and the tasting of ´tapas´, to accompany the drink. The perfect activity to complement and close the day after visiting the city. Finally, you will be transferred back to your hotel.</div><p><br/></p><p>*Panoramic tour subject to transit availability. </p><p><br/></p>
<p>Train tickets to Machu Picchu in Vistadome service for guide for full day tour. From / to  Ollantaytambo station. <br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Begin  your  tour riding through the charming streets hidden in the heart of Miraflores,  before  heading to the impressive pre-inca ruins located in the middle  of  the  city,  of  the  archeological center of Huaca Pucllana that operated between the V and VIII centuries A.C. Continue to the Olivar of San Isidro, that stands out among the parks of the hemisphere and symbolizes the virreynal  and  republican  tradition  of  this district, highlighted by its magnificent  olives  dating  from virreynal times.  Return to Miraflores and stop  on  the  way  to replenish your energies enjoying a typical snack in a traditional location.  Finish  the  course  riding by the sea and passing by cliffs, parks, and going  by modern shopping malls, sculptures, a bridge and an old lighthouse.</p>
<p>Welcome briefing.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you will be picked up from your hotel in Lima and transferred south to Paracas for 3 hours and 30 minutes. Upon arrival, you will start the 4x4 adventure route to the impressive Paracas National Reserve, home to hundreds of marine species, migratory birds and more. Along the route, you will stop at Bahía de Independencia from where you can observe the amazing landscape. You will continue along the well-known Barlovento beach and through the dunes of the Paracas coast, route of the famous Dakar Rally. Later, you will arrive at Gallinazo Beach where individual tents will be installed. Enjoy dinner based on the catch of the day and end today´s experience with a bonfire in front of the sea and an impressive fireworks show.</div><p><br/></p><p>#2</p><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Have breakfast at the camp and take advantage of the free morning to enter the sea, practice sandboarding, walk or take pictures. Return to the camp for lunch based on the catch of the day and then begin your return to Paracas in the 4x4 vehicle. From Paracas you will be transferred back to Lima, where you will arrive during the night.</div><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">The employees of the lodge will pick you up from the airport and will transfer you to the fluvial port to take a short trip (40 minutes) to the Corto Maltés Lodge. After getting installed in your bungalow, and resting for a while, you will hike for three hours to see a great variety of medicinal plants, tropical trees and insects. You will go to the scenic viewpoint before dusk to watch the toucans, and you will take a nighttime tour after the sunset to look for caimans. *Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored. </p><p style="text-align: justify;">#2 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will get up early to take a short hike until a nearby collpa. Collpas are clay deposits that the parrots and macaws visit in large quantities to eat this substance, as it helps them to clean their stomachs. After this display, you will take a tour to Monkey Island, a unique place with a large population of spider monkeys, black monkeys, capuchin monkeys, squirrel monkeys, and tamarins. You will then sail to Lake Sandoval, a meander abandoned by the river and, due to its particular ecosystem of calm waters, it is home to an exuberant fauna, which includes, among others, river otters, or shanshos, exotic birds with a prehistoric appearance. After enjoying lunch in the open air, you will return to the lodge, and in the evening you will take a hike to get acquainted with night animals, which only get out of their lairs after the sunset. </p><p style="text-align: justify;">#3 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">After having breakfast, a motor boat will transfer you to Puerto Maldonado and then to the airport.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you will be picked up from Paracas to start the 4x4 adventure route to the impressive Paracas National Reserve, home to hundreds of marine species, migratory birds and more. Along the route, you will stop at Bahía de Independencia from where you can observe the amazing landscape. You will continue along the well-known Barlovento beach and through the dunes of the Paracas coast, route of the famous Dakar Rally. Later, you will arrive at Gallinazo Beach, where the fresh lunch, based on the catch of the day, will be served. Then you will have time to rest on the beach, enter the sea, practice sandboarding, walk or take pictures. Finally, you will begin the return to Paracas.</div><p><br/></p>
<p>Welcome briefing Cusco.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: left;"><span style="text-align: justify;">The employees of the lodge will receive you upon your arrival at the airport and will transfer you to the fluvial port of the Infierno native community, located at 45 minutes from Puerto Maldonado. From there you will sail for two and a half hours to Refugio Amazonas. During this trip, you will enjoy lunch while you are watching a great variety of local birds, such as black skimmers, crowned egrets and flycatchers. Upon your arrival at the lodge, you will receive a brief introduction about the local wildlife and, after resting, you will take a short hike to a 35-meter high watch tower built with scaffoldings. You will watch the sunset amidst toucans, parrots and macaws, and will return to the lodge to have dinner and rest. After having breakfast, you will hike for approximately one hour to visit Lake Sachavacayoc, a flooded area that houses particular Amazonian species due to its calm waters. During a canoe trip, you will find turtles, caimans, giant otters and riparian birds, among them, the ´shansho´, which has a prehistoric appearance. Next, you will return to the lodge to have lunch, take a short rest, and hike on the Ethnobotanical Path, where you will learn about the daily use of the natural resources of the jungle as medicines, construction and food. After having breakfast, you will take a short hike to a nearby collpa. Collpas are clay deposits that parrots and macaws visit in large quantities to eat this substance, as it helps them clean their stomachs. In this same place, it is possible to watch blue-headed parrots, and Andean cocks-of-the-rocks, a beautiful endemic species. After watching hundreds of birds fluttering around you, you will walk on a path that enters the <span id="selectionBoundary_1558132175277_7517874940562344" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>chestnut forest, as this local nut has become an important income for native communities. After learning how they are harvested and transported, you will return to the lodge to have lunch and then you will visit a parcel of native crops. In the evening, you will have dinner and go to rest. After having breakfast, a motor boat will transfer you to Puerto Maldonado and then to the airport.</span></div><p><br/></p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p>

Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours time. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu pueblo' and is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes are the train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by the UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Overnight in Aguas Calientes. Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions of the citadel. Visit the Intipunku, which in Castilian means 'Sun Gate' and is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. It was kind of a checkpoint control and the sun rises every day through this door if viewed from the most important buildings of the citadel. You may climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means 'Young Mountain' as opposed to Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is the 'Temple of the Moon', a complex of underground constructions likely to have been used by the elite. Finally you will be able to know the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and halfway has a gap of several meters above which were two large wooden tables. Thus the Incas could isolate the city by simply withdrawing them and thus remain protected. In the afternoon head to the train station of Aguas Calientes and from there take the train back.

Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu pueblo' and is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes there is a train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Overnight in Aguas Calientes. Breakfast at the hotel. After a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge.Return to Aguas Calientes to enjoy lunch. You will return to the Aguas Calientes train station in the afternoon to board your train back to the Ollantaytambo station.

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Upon your arrival at Puerto Maldonado Airport, you will head to the pier just 15 minutes away. Experience the charm of the forest sailing 45 minutes along the Madre de Dios River to the hotel. After a brief presentation, enjoy a nice lunch with local ingredients. Take a nap, drink a refreshingtea and get ready for the afternoon excursion. After meeting with your guide, choose from the many excursions available. You can enter the junglethrough a guided walk along the trail system in the natural area of  the hostel, where you will discover the Amazon Basin. This tour provides anexcellent introduction to the rainforest environment, passing by impressive trees such as the Shihuahuaco and the strangely beautiful strangler fig, offering excellent opportunities to observe birds and exotic insects. At nightfall, do the River by Night Tour: a guided boat ride by the afternoon sailing through the Madre de Dios River. Witness the transformation from a diurnal to a nocturnal world: birds and other daytime animals give way tospecies specially adapted to the night. While enjoying the nature around you, watch for the appearance of nightjars, owls,capybaras and up to three different species of alligators. Then you can enjoy dinner served in the main dining room. After dinner, you can choose to go to your cabin, or joina nice talk about the experience of life in the Amazon while listening to the intriguing sounds of the jungle at night.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Breakfast starts very early for those who want to avoid the heat and enjoy nature to the fullest. During morning, you can make an excursion to Lake Sandoval in the Tambopata National Reserve. Sail on a wooden canoe acrossthe lake, home to many species that are endangered such as the giant river otter, red howler monkeys, red-bellied macaws, anacondas and side-neckturtles. Then you will return to the lodge for lunch. After lunch, you can visit the Inkaterra Canopy and the Anaconda Walk. When you reach the first tower, 29 meters above the forest floor, begins your encounter with the species that inhabit the treetops. You will literally walk through the rainforest for more than a quarter mile in a suspended bridge networklinking eight observation platforms, where you will see colorful toucans, woodpeckers, trogons, monkeys, sloths, among other species. If you wish, you can extend your tour to appreciate the sunset in one of the towers. Once you return to the hotel, enjoy a presentation about nature in the Eco Center andthe Happy Hour. Then dinner will be served. </p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Enjoy a walk through the Gamitana Creek, located 25 minutes by boat from the lodge. Explore the jungle for an hour and a half along the winding Gamitana Creek, home to piranhas, alligators, turtles and birds, until reaching the Sanipanga stop. Sail along the river appreciating the abundant flora and fauna of the rainforest. Return to the Gamitana model farm for a brief guided tour, where you will learn more about an Amazon model farm. Finally, return to the lodge to enjoy your lunch. After lunch, you will make a trip to the Hacienda Concepcion, with the possibility to view more than 200 different plant species, and learn about current uses and benefits of local flora. Then you will sail 30 minutes on a canoe looking for turtles, nightjars and exotic herons. Next, perform the Jungle Night Tour though the trails of Inkaterra Ecological Reserve, on 2 hours-excursions. Step into the forest and experience the mystery of the animal activity that awakens in the jungle at sunset. You will hear the night sounds of the forest and discover species that only emerge in the dark. Finally, you will return to the lodge. </p><p style="text-align: justify;">#4</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After having breakfast, you will check out from the lodge and sail through the Madre de Dios River to Puerto Maldonado City, where you will visit Inkaterras Butterfly House, the only one in Peru, where you can see a large amount of endemic butterflies. Peru has the greatest variety of butterfliesin the world with over 3800 species and many of them can be seen at the Butterfly House. Finally, you will be transferred to the airport.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will be transferred to the bus station from where you will head south of Lima, in direction to Paracas, in Pisco. Upon your arrival, you will be transferred to the airfield to board a light aircraft to fly over the Nasca Lines (Subject to weather conditions). Intriguing and huge figures like the monkey, spider and condor can be seen from the sky. You will go back to your hotel at the end of the day. </p><p>#2 <br/></p><p style="text-align: justify;">After having breakfast, you will go to ´El Chaco´ Port, to take a tour to the Ballestas Islands, part of the Paracas National Reserve. This area is an extraordinary place due to its multiple attractions. It is a natural reserve with beautiful geological formations, impressive fauna and vestiges of a pre-Inca culture that dominated the desert to install a successful society in adverse conditions. The Ballestas Islands are home to a large population of sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species found in the archipelago, an ideal place for their reproduction and coexistence. You will be transferred to Lima at the end of the tour.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="color: #17365d;"></span></p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#1</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Arrival to the city of Iquitos. A representative waits for your arrival at the airport. From here, the journey starts towards Nauta, a small riverside town on the banks of the Marañón River. Once there, you are welcomed at the private port. When boarding, enjoy a fantastic panoramic view from the observation area. Participate in an orientation talk and get to know the entertainment centre that offers movies, as well as an extensive library of books about flora and fauna of the area. The ship also has a fully equipped bar where you can try any regional or international combination of liquors and aromas. Dinner is served during the afternoon.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#2</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Wake up early to appreciate one of the most beautiful areas of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve: Fundo Casual. The walk through this area will take you to the depths of the Terra Firme jungle (un-flooded forest) where you can appreciate the local fauna. Return to the boat for lunch on board. After this, visit Nauta Caño where you have the opportunity to sail in kayak. Also, go fishing for the famous piranha. Return to the boat where dinner is served.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#3</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, take a walk through the canopy of the Amazon Park, a unique experience in the jungle! At the height of more than 25 meters and a length of 500 meters, the &#34;Canopy Walkway&#34; offers a view from the top of the trees, the best observation point to admire the jungle´s wildlife and vegetation. After lunch on the boat, cross through the Yanayacu and Pucate rivers, passing through different streams and lagoons. This is one of the best preserved and protected areas of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve. If you want to interact directly with the waters of the Amazon, go out in one of the kayaks or paddle boards; and you will even have the opportunity to swim with the curious pink dolphins. Return to the boat and dinner on board.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#4</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, visit a local community to meet the people who have inhabited the Amazon for generations. During the visit, the villagers share their traditions and customs with visitors. Upon returning, lunch is served and, later, go on a boat exploration of the Yarapa River, a tributary of the Ucayali River and a nature reserve protected by a local community. During the afternoon, enjoy the farewell dinner.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#5</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">During the morning disembark in the port of Nauta and head towards the airport. On the way, visit the Floating Mammals Rescue and Rehabilitation Centre to learn about manatees and other rescued species, as well as plans for the species conservation. Finally, arrive at the airport. You are accompanied by a representative who assists you on the way to your departure from the city.</p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="color: #17365d;"></span></p>

Today you will arrive at Iquitos, a bustling river city, and the largest city in the Peruvian Rainforest, with 600,000 people living in its four districts. During the 20th century, the city attracted many Europeans immigrants who contributed to a period of great wealth, commercial, and social development. You will be transferred by coach to the Itaya River for embarkation. After a short orientation and welcome reception, enjoy a traditional welcome dinner on board. This morning you have the chance to search for the elusive, freshwater, pink river dolphin at the Tahuayo River. This area is a favorite feeding spot for these medium-sized dolphins with little or no dorsal. You may also have the opportunity to enjoy a refreshing swim in this pristine area. Then enter another tributary of the Amazon River that afternoon, the Yarapa River, teeming with tropical flowers, where you will explore its shores and small creeks in search of rare and unusual Amazonian wildlife, including species of monkeys, birds, and iguanas not seen elsewhere. This morning you awake at the birthplace of the Amazon River: the convergence of the Ucayali & Marañon Rivers. After exploring the area, visit the San Francisco village, where you will be greeted by happy smiles from the villagers. After taking time to stroll around the village, visiting the local people´s homes and observing their daily lives, you have an opportunity to visit the local school (when in session), and ask the teacher any questions you may have. The visit ends with a time to shop the village arts and crafts market, where all items are made by the local women and children. This tributary of the Amazon River has small clusters of ribereños people (those who live symbiotically with the rivers), making their living through small-scale agricultural and charcoal commerce. Visit a local shamanic healing clinic and listen to a native shaman speak about the mythical world of hallucinogenic and medicinal plants in the Amazon basin. Upon disembarkation in Iquitos, enjoy a panoramic city tour of the farthest inland port that serves ocean vessels in the world. Pass the historic area of the city, where many of the wonderful houses of the 20th century rubber barons, including the famous Casa de Fierro (the Iron House). After disembarkation, visit the Manatee Rescue Center, a fantastic project committed to saving orphaned manatees and reintroducing them into the wild. Learn about their importance to the world´s ecosystem, and why they so endangered in this part of the Amazon. Maybe you will have the opportunity to swim with these gentle giants. Following this visit, you will be transferred to the airport for your return flight.

Your tour begins with a train ride to Machu Picchu. In about an hour and a half reach the town of Aguas Calientes, also known as 'Machu Picchu'. This is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. Finally you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. You may also optionally visit the Intipunku, the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnight in Aguas Calientes. Head to the train station of Aguas Calientes and from there take the train back to Ollanta station.

<p>#1 </p><p>After an early breakfast, we pick you up from your hotel and leave the city of Cusco. On the route we will take a short break to visit Tarawasi Archaeological Site near the town of Limatambo (1.5 hrs. approx. from Cusco). After leaving Limatambo, we pass through the mountain village of Mollepata, where we stop for a short coffee break before ascending a winding mountain road to a place called Challacancha (12,690 ft.). Here we will meet our mule driver and emergency horse and begin our trek to Soraypampa (3,900m/ 14,730ft.). In this place we will lunch, and after a short rest continue to climb toward Humantay Lake (3,869 m). Then go back to Soraypampa, which will be the first campsite, and from where we will enjoy a magnificent view of the Salkantay Mountain (20,571ft.) the second most sacred peak in Inca mythology. </p><p>#2 </p><p>This is the main day! After an early breakfast, we hike up to the highest point on the trek which is the Salkantay Pass at 4,638m (15,213ft.). There we will stop to enjoy the amazing views of snowcapped peaks of the Vilcabamba Mountain Range in every direction, and the glaciated south face of Salkantay towering above us, where Andean condors can be seen. From the pass we descend towards Wayracmachay to lunch and then continue our descent along the left bank of the Salkantay River. Observe the landscape become increasingly green, changing from highland to jungle or cloud forest. Feel the warm air rising from the jungle, accompanied by colorful butterflies and striking orchids, while arriving to the second camp at Andenes (9,481ft.), a beautiful spot surrounded with vegetation. </p><p>#3 </p><p>Today we will hike along the Santa Teresa River Valley, through more populated rural areas. We pass through banana, granadilla, avocado and coffee plantations. A hot picnic lunch is served along the way. After lunch, we hike 2 hours approximately before a private vehicle meets us for a short drive to the beginning of the ?Llactapata Inca Trail? (30 minutes approx.). From the head of the newly restored Inca Trail we easily make our way to the last campsite at Lucmabamba. In the evening we will have a coffee taste demonstration with our host, a local family. Dinner will be served and then spend the night in the camp. </p><p>#4 </p><p>After breakfast, we will head uphill for 2-3 hours towards Llactapata Pass (8,974ft.) and descend to the archaeological complex of the same name. Besides, we will enjoy a distant but spectacular view of Machu Picchu Sanctuary from the southeast, a view few travelers get the chance to admire. Lunch is served in a scenic viewpoint, looking out to Machu Picchu. After that, begin the final descent to the Aobamba River through lush bamboo forests, orchards and coffee plantations. Finally we will get to the Hydroelectric Power Station, to board the train to Aguas Calientes and spend the night. There you can stay in the hotel of your choice. </p><p>#5 </p><p>After an early breakfast, we make our way to the bus station for the ride up to Machu Picchu. The guided tour of the sanctuary lasts two hours and at the end, you shall have free time to explore the place at your leisure, or simply relax and meditate. We will return by bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch and board the train to Ollantaytambo. From this point, a private vehicle will drive us back to Cusco and you will be transferred to your hotel. You arrival to Cusco will be approximately at 7 or 8 pm.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/>-----------------------------<br/><!--StartFragment-->Level: Moderate<br/>Maximum altitude: 4650 m a.s.l.<br/>Walking distance: 44 km - 27,34 miles<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p>Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a train trip from the Ollanta train station to the town of Aguas Calientes. There you will find the train station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb early to it. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.</p><p><br/>#2</p><p>Breakfast at the hotel. After a short trip you will arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resting place for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, meaning &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. At the end of the tour you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch. In the afternoon you will transfer to the train station of Aguas Calientes to take your train back to Ollanta station.<br/></p>

<p>You will be picked up from your hotel and transferred to the Piuray lagoon, an hour away from the city, to live an unforgettable experience on the water. Upon arrival, you will be welcomed with hot drinks, water and snacks. Take advantage of the first minutes here to contemplate the surroundings and take photos. Before entering the lake, participate in a brief safety and paddling talk after which you will receive the implements according to the chosen activity (paddle or kayak). Enter the lagoon and paddle for an hour and an hour and a half through the calm waters. When leaving the lake, you can take a walk around and appreciate the landscape. After this, enjoy the Andean buffet based on native potatoes, sweet potatoes, chicken, vegetables and other local ingredients, are cooked in the clay oven, giving them a unique touch. After lunch, you will be transferred to your next destination.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a perfect lunch picnic next to the Huaypo lagoon in the Sacred Valley of the Incas. Marvel at the view and the meal, made with natural ingredients of the area.<br/></p>

<p>This activity focused on children offers an exceptional approach to art from the perfect space: the Art Museum of Lima. Here, the children will begin the experience with a guided tour of the exhibition room where they will learn about some of the exhibited art pieces that will serve as inspiration for the next stage of the tour: the art workshop. Here they will design and create their own works of art for about an hour. A great alternative for entertainment and a creative experience.</p><p><br/></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Participate in a workshop of one of the most important traditions in the Andean world: ceramics. This experience is carried out in the Sacred Valley with the help of an expert, who starts by explaining about the different techniques of elaboration with this material such as lamination and pottery wheel, and about the history of this activity in the area. Make your own design with clay during this exciting experience.<br/></p>
<p>Dinner at Tawa restaurant.<br/><br/></p>
<p>Lunch at Tawa restaurant. <br/><br/></p>

<p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment-->Travel to the port of Puno where a boat will be waiting to take you to the beautiful Uros Islands. The Uros are an indigenous people of Peru that live on a series of man-made islands, created entirely from the buoyant totora reeds that grow abundantly in the shallows of the lake. The islands are composed of layers of woven totora, which are constantly replenished from the top as they rot from the bottom. The Uros people also use reeds to construct their homes. After visiting this fascinating island, sail to Taquile, a small slice of heaven that once belonged to the Inca Empire. After a brief introduction, the local community will give you an insight into their customs and traditions with a cultural demonstration. Lunch is enjoyed on the island before the return trip to Puno.</p>
<p>Dinner at Makoto Sushi Bar &amp; Restaurant.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Lunch at Makoto Sushi Bar &amp; Restaurant.</p><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Visit Pisac, one of the most attractive towns of the impressive Sacred Valley, in a fun bike tour. You will be picked up from your hotel and transferred to the valley, where the equipment will be handed, and a brief safety talk will be held. Start the journey in Corao until reaching the viewpoint of Taray, from where you can appreciate the extent of the valley. Continue to Pisac, known for its picturesque local market, textile works, crafts and its archaeological site where temples, a residential settlement, and more are located. Finally, a vehicle will be waiting near the bridge of Pisac to load the equipment and transport you back to your hotel in Cusco.</div><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Take one of the most spectacular hiking excursions through the Andes mountains, towards the Humantay lagoon (4180 masl). Enter the impressive landscape, the rich culture and the vast history of this area of the world. Start the tour leaving Cusco at 5:00 am in a vehicle to Soraypampa (3900 masl) for approximately 3 hours. In this section appreciate the varied fauna, such as the Andean condor or swallows and observe the imposing Salkantay and Humantay snow peaks. From Soraypampa begin the ascent of approximately two hours towards the Humantay lagoon. After exploring and photographing the place for around one hour, start the descent walk to Soraypampa (1 hour and 15 minutes). At arrival in Soraypampa, enjoy a picnic lunch. Finally, start the return to Cusco, where you will arrive at around 5:30 in the afternoon.</div><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Participate in interactive chocolate and pisco workshops. Start the tour at the ChocoMuseo where the workshop &#34;from bean to bar&#34; will be held. Here you will learn about the whole process from the harvesting of cacao to its final consumption. You will also taste various chocolates. Then, continue to the Pisco Museum, where you will participate in a guided tasting of 4 different types of pisco. Also, a traditional Peruvian dish will be prepared: ceviche or tiradito. <br/></p>
<p>Departure from Machu Picchu at 12:46 am on the Vistadome train (602), to Ollantaytambo station with arrival time at 14:19 pm.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up from the port of Puno and taken to the floating islands of Uros. The Uros are an ancient culture that inhabits a series of artificial islands built on the basis of reeds growing in the lake itself. <span style="text-align: justify;">These are constructed by weaving reed in the areas where it grows thicker and thus forming a natural layer. On it they build their houses and cook outdoors in order to prevent fires. After a journey of one and a half hours you reach Amantaní, a circular island 3.4 kilometres in diameter on average. The people have maintained their identity and highland customs and have attracted the attention of the tourist industry through living experiences. At Amantaní you will be welcomed by the inhabitants and put up in one of their homes that has been prepared to receive visitors.</span></p><p></p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast with the locals you will set off by boat from Amantaní to the island of Taquile. In contrast to the other islands in the lake, where the people speak Aimara, here they speak Quechua. It was part of the Inca <span style="text-align: justify;">Empire and vestiges of that culture can still be seen in their buildings. However the Spanish also came here, forbade them from wearing their traditional costumes and forced them to use peasant dress. When you arrive </span><span style="text-align: justify;">you will be given a short description of the island and a tour round it before lunch. After lunch you will go to the harbour for the trip back to Puno.</span></p><p><span style="text-align: justify;"></span></p><p></p><p></p>
<p>half day in Machu Picchu with local portuguese speaking guide.</p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will leave the city via Yura, a district characterised by impressive geographical features such as gorges, mountain chains and canyons. You will enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, a vast plain where you can see vicuñas; native and domesticable Andean camelid considered a national symbol. Then you will pass through Vizcachani, the Pampas de Toccra and you will skirt the crater of the Chucura volcano to reach Patapampa (4800 masl). Have lunch in the mythical town of Chivay and during the afternoon rest in the hotel of your choice, or you can optionally visit the thermal baths of La Calera. Dinner at the hotel. </p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, head to the Condor Cross, from where you can appreciate the greatness of the Colca Valley (whose canyon is the second deepest in the world with more than 4 kilometres on its deepest side) and then you will daydream: see the huge and majestic condor fly just a few meters away. On the way back to Chivay you will visit the villages of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma, Yanque and the viewpoints of Antahuilque and Choquetico. Upon arrival at Chivay, lunch will be served. Afternoon at leisure. </p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, you will visit Coporaque, a picturesque village that preserves a chapel built in 1569, and the Colinas de Chininia, incredible pre-Inca constructions in vertical natural walls. From there you will go to <span style="text-align: justify;">the Mirador de Ocolle, the Colcas of Chininia - former storage tanks for agricultural products - and make a short walk to the archaeological remains of Uyo-Uyo. You will return to Chivay in the afternoon for lunch. Afternoon at leisure.</span></p><p></p><p>#4</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Depart from Chivay to the city of Puno taking the detour to Patahuasi, with a brief stop in Lagunillas lake. You will continue through a magical journey of highland plains, stone forests and celestial lagoons populated by white herons that dry their wings with the icy wind above 4,000 meters high. After approximately six hours of travel you will arrive in Puno.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">You will start the tour of Sacsayhuamán, one of the emblematic buildings of the Incas (located a few minutes from the town centre and declared Cultural Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO) erected by the Inca Pachacutec in the fifteenth century. It is an immense fortress built with large stones whose transfer and carving is still a mystery. You will return to the city and continue the tour through the neighbourhood of San Blas, originally inhabited by the Inca nobility. Today this neighbourhood is a conglomerate of beautiful cobbled streets, and popular among artisans. You will continue towards the Cathedral. This is the most impressive monument of the Main Square. Its construction began in 1560 and was completed in 1654. For its constructions, thousands of stones were extracted and transported from the Sacsayhuaman fortress. Then, visit the beautiful Church of La Merced, a masterpiece of modern baroque style. Marvel at its beautiful interior courtyard, carved wooden ceilings and imposing altars. You will finish your tour visiting Coricancha, also known as ´The Temple of the Sun´ (QuriKancha; golden temple). Upon the arrival of the Spanish, it became the basis for the construction of the Santo Domingo Convent.</div><p><br/></p>

<p>Explore two of the greatest attractions of the Sacred Valley on an exciting bicycle tour. You will be picked up from your hotel and transferred to the valley, where the equipment will be handed and a brief safety talk will be <span style="text-align: justify;">held. Start the journey at the archaeological site of Moray. Like many Inca sites, its exact function remains a mystery, although its design in the form of concentric circular terraces implies that each level had a different microclimate. From there, head towards Maras, where the salt lagoons create a dazzling scenery. In times of the Incas, the salt deposits were an important economic centre and of great value for the imperial economy. Continue to the Vilcanota River, cross a bridge and reach the transportation service, which will be waiting to transfer you back to your hotel.</span></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">Let your imagination soar in two magnificent workshops offered by Art Trail, an artistic and cultural development project where Cusco traditions are rescued and given new life in artistic sessions. In the first activity, Cusco Colonial Glazed Ceramics, learn how to make a horse or bull of Pucara, a traditional Cusco figure, and apply fine finishes with a layer of enamel, giving it a glazed effect. In the second, the Colonial Gold Leaf application, enjoy painting with the characteristic colours of the mountain range and then finish decorating your work with gold leaf, a traditional technique that Art Trail has recovered and applied to contemporary crafts. Both workshops are taught by renowned Cusco artists. By the end of the sessions, you’ll have two personalised works that will be delivered to your hotel the following day. After the workshops, return to your hotel.<br/></p><p></p>

<p>#1</p><p>Today you will tour the Peruvian highlands on a journey that will take you through beautiful landscapes in Arequipa, Puno and Cusco. You will leave from the station of Arequipa on direction to Puno. Enjoy a cocktail and then dine on board.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Witness the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and continue the journey. Breakfast will be served on board and after this you will visit the Islands of Uros, a set of floating islands made of totora, plants that grow in the lake. Then visit Taquile Island, from where you will have a wonderful view of the lake. Lunch will be held at the Collata Beach, which is part of the island and you will enjoy a demonstration of typical dances and craftsmanship of the inhabitants of the island. Subsequently, you will be transferred to the station of Lake Titicaca and enjoy tea and will continue the road to Cusco. After a cocktail, dinner will be served on board. During the night you will arrive at Marangoni station.</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast on board, continue your journey starting from the Marangoni station towards Cusco. On the way you will stop in Raqchi, the archaeological site also known as the Temple of Viracocha, where Inca storages and parts of the main building can be seen. You will arrive at the station of Cusco by noon.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Visitors will embark on one of the most amazing routes in the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful ¨Andean Explorer¨. The train has two dining cars, lounge wagon and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the Sierra. It also has elegant suites and cabins where passengers can rest comfortably. The journey begins leaving Cusco towards Puno. Passengers will enjoy a delicious lunch and then make the first stop in Raqchi; admirable archaeological site with remains of Inca temples and buildings. The journey will continue and after teatime, be amazed by a breathtaking sunset at La Raya, the limit between Cusco and Puno, surrounded by towering mountains. Dinner will be served in the train and you will reach Lake Titicaca station at night.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will enjoy the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and breakfast will be served on board. During the day you can optionally choose to visit the Uros Islands on a typical boat, visit the Taquile Island, lunch in a private area on the beach or a show of traditional dances and crafts demonstration. <br/></p>

During the day you can optionally choose to visit the Uros Islands on a typical boat, visit the Taquile Island, lunch in a private area on the beach or a show of traditional dances and crafts demonstration. <br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Visitors will embark on one of the most amazing routes in the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful Andean Explorer. The train has two dining cars, lounge wagon and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the Sierra. It also has elegant suites and cabins where passengers can rest comfortably. The journey begins leaving Puno towards Cusco. Enjoy a delicious lunch made of fresh, local ingredients, designed by the chefs of Belmond Hotel Monasterio. Then visit La Raya, the limit between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by towering mountains. Dinner will be served in the train and arrival to the Cusco region is set at night.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">During the second and last day of the joruney, you will enjoy breakfast on board and arrive to Cusco.<br/></p>

The train has two dining cars, lounge wagon and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the Sierra. Dinner will be served in the train and arrival to the Cusco region is set at night.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">During the second and last day of the joruney, you will enjoy breakfast on board and arrive to Cusco.</p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will travel the Peruvian highlands on a journey that will take you through wonderful landscapes in Cusco, Puno and Arequipa. During the morning, the train will leave Cusco in direction of Puno and will you have lunch on board. The first stop will be at the archaeological site of Raqchi, also known as Temple of Viracocha where Inca storages and parts of the main building can be seen. In the afternoon you will be served tea and enjoy the sunset in La Raya, border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by imposing mountains. Then you will have a cocktail and dinner on board. You will arrive at the station of Lake Titicaca around 10p.m.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Witness the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and continue your journey. Breakfast will be served on board and after this you will visit the Islands of Uros, a set of floating islands made of totora, plants that grow in the lake. Then visit Taquile Island, from where you will have a wonderful view of the lake. Lunch will be served at Collata Beach, which is part of the island and you will enjoy a demonstration of typical dances and craftsmanship of the inhabitants of the island. Afterwards, you will be transferred to the station of Lake Titicaca. You will be served a tea and will continue the trip heading to Arequipa. After a cocktail, dinner will be served on board.</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will have breakfast in the Saracocha Lagoon in the Puno region and later you will visit the Sumbay caves in Arequipa, where cave paintings from the Paleolithic period representing human and animal figures can be seen on the walls. Disembark in km. 99 to visit the Colca Canyon, more than 4 kilometers deep in its deepest part. After this visit you will have lunch on board and take a panoramic tour that will take you through the main buildings of the &#34;White City&#34;. Finally, you will be transferred to Arequipa´s airport.<br/></p>

In the afternoon you will be served tea and enjoy the sunset in La Raya, border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by imposing mountains. Then you will have a cocktail and dinner on board. You will arrive at the station of Lake Titicaca around 10p.m.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Witness the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and continue your journey. Disembark in km. 99 to visit the Colca Canyon, more than 4 kilometers deep in its deepest part. After this visit you will have lunch on board and take a panoramic tour that will take you through the main buildings of the &#34;White City&#34;. Finally, you will be transferred to Arequipa´s airport.<br/></p>

During the morning, the train will leave Cusco in direction of Puno and will you have lunch on board. The first stop will be at the archaeological site of Raqchi, also known as Temple of Viracocha where Inca storages and parts of the main building can be seen. In the afternoon you will be served tea and enjoy the sunset in La Raya, border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by imposing mountains. Then you will have a cocktail and dinner on board. You will arrive at the station of Lake Titicaca around 10p.m.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Witness the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and continue your journey. Breakfast will be served on board and after this you will visit the Islands of Uros, a set of floating islands made of totora, plants that grow in the lake.
