Lunch will be served at Collata Beach, which is part of the island and you will enjoy a demonstration of typical dances and craftsmanship of the inhabitants of the island. Afterwards, you will be transferred to the station of Lake Titicaca. You will be served a tea and will continue the trip heading to Arequipa. After a cocktail, dinner will be <span style="text-align: justify;">served on board.</span></p><p></p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will have breakfast in the Saracocha Lagoon in the Puno region and later you will visit the Sumbay caves in Arequipa, where cave paintings from the Paleolithic period representing human and animal figures can be seen on the walls. Disembark in km. 99 to visit the Colca Canyon, more than 4 kilometers deep in its deepest part. After this visit you will have lunch on board and take a panoramic tour that will take you through the main buildings of the &#34;White City&#34;. Finally, you will be transferred to Arequipa´s airport.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Explore Lima´s old town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, packed with fascinating historic buildings. The route begins at the traditional Pueblo Libre district, where you visit the Larco Museum, located in a beautifully <span style="text-align: justify;">restored viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid from the seventh century. The museum boasts a complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver objects, as well as pieces of erotic art. Then visit the Casa Aliaga, a colonial mansion granted by chief conquistador Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains, Jeronimo de Aliaga. This is the only house from that time, still belonging to the same family. Continue with a scenic tour through the colonial downtown. See the Main Square, where you can appreciate the grandiose architecture of the Spanish Empire. Admire Lima´s Cathedral, built during the 16th century. Finish the trip returning to your hotel. </span></p><p></p>
<p>A transportation service with a guide will pick you up from your hotel in Cusco, to transfer to your hotel in Urubamba.<br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel at the established time and will transfer you to the Ollantaytambo train station.<br/></p>
<p>Private site guide in Portuguese available in Machu Picchu.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">In the morning, a transportation service, accompanied by a guide in English and Spanish, will pick you up from your hotel in Cusco or Sacred Valley to transfer to the enchanting Lake Piuray which is 3435 meters above sea level in the district of Chinchero. Once there, you will find the variety of flora and fauna that surrounds it, as crops of barley, wheat, potato, lupine, eucalyptus trees, as well as sheep and cattle. On the way you will stop at the highest viewpoint where you can appreciate the spectacular scenery that showcases the entire valley and 5 mountain peaks: Ausangate (6,372 masl), Salkantay (6,271 masl), Veronica (5,850 masl), Sahuasiray (5,777 masl) and Chicon (5,600 masl). Arriving at the lake, you will receive first of all, an indispensable introductory talk for you to learn the techniques needed to develop the activity in the best way, and the last step before entering the water, will perform a warm-up to prevent possible cramps. Once in the water, you will enjoy a very special experience where you can relax, swim and sunbathe. After the activity, you will enjoy the facilities and visit the natural beauty of the place. Finally, enjoy a box lunch before returning to Cusco.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will enjoy an excellent day doing stand up paddle in Cusco. You will be transferred to the SUP premises at the banks of Lake Piuray located in the beautiful Sacred Valley. The activity begins with a general briefing before you put your wetsuit on, and the  receive another more specific briefing about the technique, how to paddle, how to stand, turn around, etc. Before entering the water, you will warm up to avoid cramping. Depending on the level of the group you will will paddle between 1 to 1.5 hours, where you will learn to use easily the paddle board. During the activity you willalso relax, swim and sunbathe and then return to base. Once ashore, go to bath. change clothe, relax and enjoy the facilities, while appreciating the magnificent views of the place. Finally, you will be taken back to Cusco.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You tour will start in downtown Lima, where you will make a scenic tour of the built-up area, a World Heritage listed by UNESCO. You will visit the historical San Martin Square and then you will arrive at the Main Square to see the Presidential Palace and the Lima Cathedral, impressive constructions that are still being used. You will then visit the Aliaga House, an ancient viceregal construction delivered by Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jeronimo de Aliaga) when he founded the city. It may be the only house that still belongs to the same family until today. Next, you will visit the Santo Domingo Church, built along forty years and completed at the end of the 16th century. It is also known as the Minor Basilica and Maximum Convent of our Lady of the Rosary. The church has several altars and viceregal sculptures, and exhibits different times and aesthetic expressions. From here you will go to the San Marcos Manor House, where you will be picked up by your transportation service and transferred to your hotel.<br/></p>
<p>A mobility will transfer you from the aerodrome to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you will be picked up from your hotel in a 4x4 vehicle and transferred to Paracas in a journey of approximately 3 hours with moments of off-road adventures over the dunes of the Peruvian coast. Once in Paracas, enjoy the different attractions and activites in the area. During the afternoon you will be transferred back to Lima in the 4x4 vehicle.<br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Early morning departure from Cusco to drive to the locality of Pachar, located 8 kms. from Ollantaytambo, where you will find the Via Ferrata, a climbing route  300 meters high.  Here you will have the opportunity to climb a mountain with a safety system permanently installed, for which you do not require any previous experience. While climbing,  enjoy the mystical landscapes of the Sacred Valley of the Incas. Upon completing the ascent, <span style="text-align: justify;">enjoy an energyzing snack while you admire the spectacular scenery and recover your energies.  Then descend 100 mt of rappel,  with an extra &#34;shoot&#34; of adrenaline that will make your experience an unforgettable adventure. At the end of the excursion, transfer back to Cusco and  your hotel. </span></p><p></p>

<p>#1</p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">You will depart from Cusco towards Lares early in the morning, from where the trek to Huacahuasi begins. Once in Lares, you will visit the thermal baths. In this place, enjoy the warm waters to which healing properties are attributed. After lunch, start the hike to the Huacahuasi camp, which you will reach after approximately two and a half hours of walk. Upon arrival, have some coffee, and after some free time, enjoy.</span></p><p>#2</p><div><span style="text-align: justify;"><br/></span></div><div><span style="text-align: justify;">Resume the trek after breakfast. Today´s final destination is the camp located in the district of Patacancha (3560 masl). During the tour, you will pass through the highest point of the hike: Ipsay (4400 masl). From here, you will be able to marvel at the impressive landscape where the imposing snow-capped Veronica (5750 masl) stands out. Lunch en route. Continue the walk, now descending, until arriving at Patacancha. Upon arrival, have some coffee, and after some free time, enjoy dinner.</span><br/><br/></div><div>#3</div><div><span style="text-align: justify;"><br/></span></div><div><span style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, resume the walk and head to the archaeological site of Pumamarca where you can appreciate its impressive Inca terraces, canals and buildings. Lunch on the way. Then continue towards the town of Ollantaytambo, located in the Sacred Valley, protected by mountains. Here you can also appreciate the impressive archaeological site, which served as a resting place for the Inca forces. Then, head to the local station, where you will board the train that will take you to Aguas Calientes, the previous stop to the Inca citadel of Machu Picchu. Accommodation at the selected hotel.</span><br/><br/>#4<br/><br/><div style="text-align: justify;">Early in the morning, take a 25-minute bus ride to Machu Picchu, the lost city of the Incas. It is believed that the archaeological site was built around 1450 by order of Inca Pachacútec as his holiday residence. However, a century later, the city was abandoned after the Spanish conquest, saving itself from the destruction that reached other Inca settlements. During the guided tour, you will learn about the origins of this impressive citadel, exploring its most outstanding monuments as the main square, the royal quarters, the temple of the three windows, the circular towers, the sacred clock and the cemeteries. You will have free time to visit the citadel on your own and then return to Aguas Calientes for lunch. Finally, during the afternoon, board the train back to Ollantaytambo, from where you will be transferred to your hotel in the city of Cusco.</div><p></p></div>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will depart from Cusco towards Lares early in the morning, from where the trek to Huacahuasi begins. Once in Lares, you will visit the thermal baths. In this place, enjoy the warm waters to which healing properties are attributed. After lunch, start the hike to the Huacahuasi camp, which you will reach after approximately two and a half hours of walk. Upon arrival, have some coffee, and after some free time, enjoy.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Resume the trek after breakfast. Today´s final destination is the camp located in the district of Patacancha (3560 masl). During the tour, you will pass through the highest point of the hike: Ipsay (4400 masl). From here, you will be able to marvel at the impressive landscape where the imposing snow-capped Veronica (5750 masl) stands out. Lunch en route. Continue the walk, now descending, until arriving at Patacancha. Upon arrival, have some coffee, and after some free time, enjoy dinner.</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, resume the walk and head to the archaeological site of Pumamarca where you can appreciate its impressive Inca terraces, canals and buildings. Lunch on the way. Then continue towards the town of Ollantaytambo, located in the Sacred Valley, protected by mountains. Here you can also appreciate the impressive archaeological site, which served as a resting place for the Inca forces. Then, head to the local station, where you will board the train that will take you to Aguas Calientes, the previous stop to the Inca citadel of Machu Picchu. Accommodation at the selected hotel.</p><p>#4</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Early in the morning, take a 25-minute bus ride to Machu Picchu, the 'lost city of the Incas'. It is believed that the archaeological site was built around 1450 by order of Inca Pachacútec as his holiday residence. However, a century later, the city was abandoned after the Spanish conquest, saving itself from the destruction that reached other Inca settlements. During the guided tour, you will learn about the origins of this impressive citadel, exploring its most outstanding monuments as the main square, the royal quarters, the temple of the three windows, the circular towers, the sacred clock and the cemeteries. You will have free time to visit the citadel on your own and then return to Aguas Calientes for lunch. Finally, during the afternoon, board the train back to Ollantaytambo, from where you will be transferred to your hotel in the city of Cusco.<br/></p>
<p>Shared transfer from Paracas to Lima in Movil Shuttle.<br/></p>
<p>Shared transfer from Lima to Paracas in Movil Shuttle.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will be transferred from your hotel to visit the varied and vibrant San Pedro market in Cusco, accompanied by an experienced chef, to choose local ingredients, where you will learn to select local vegetables and herbs for your delight. With the best and freshest ingredients, you will start your cooking lesson to prepare delicious Peruvian recipes, from traditional to molecular cuisine. Enjoy your lunch overlooking the hotel eucalyptus forest or the Main Square of Cusco.<br/></p>
<p>Transfer from Chachapoyas airport to the selected hotel in Chachapoyas centre.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p>Your tour will start at Pisac. The citadel is a town built in the form of partridge known for two reasons: the craft market, established between colonial buildings, and for hosting one of the most impressive complexes of<br/>terraces in the valley. The market has dozens of stalls displaying and selling products crafted by the residents of the area. Then have lunch at the agreed restaurant  and be transferred to your hotel in the Sacred Valley<br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">to enjoy the evening and overnight. </div><p></p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will drive to the village of Ollantaytambo, a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited like in the past. Pachacutec (1438 - 1572) conquered the town, destroyed it and built a new one absorbing it as part of his empire. The fortress of Ollantaytambo is a major archaeological site that although it was called a &#34;fortress&#34; it was a &#34;tambo&#34; town for  rest and lodging of long distance  travellers. It is without doubt one of the most monumental works of the empire. Then have lunch at the agreed restaurant and be transferred to your hotel. Afternoon at leisure.</p><p>#3</p><p>You will be transferred to the Ollanta train station to travel to Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that  served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, <span style="text-align: justify;">which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by the UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world.</span></p><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour visiting the Coricancha, also known as &#34;The Temple of the Sun&#34; (Quri Kancha 'Golden Temple'), which was  built by the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572) and at the arrival of the Spaniards became the basis for the construction of the  Santo Domingo Convent. Here, during the Inca empire reverence was surrendered to the sun (&#34;Inti&#34;), and there was a time when it was covered with gold. Then visit the Cathedral, the most imposing monument in the Plaza de Armas. Its construction began in 1560 and ended only in 1654. Almost a century of construction. For this purpose hundreds of stones were extracted and transported from Sacsayhuaman fortress under the command of the Ecclesiastical Council. You will visit the local market and then continue your tour to the fortress of Sacsayhuaman, one of the most emblematic buildings of the Incas (located just minutes from town center) built by the Inca Pachacutec in the fifteenth century. This is a huge fortress built with large stones whose transportation remains still a mystery. Each June 24th, during the winter solstice, the feast of Inti Raymi is recreated here, where the sun is worshipped. Continue to Quenqo, located 4 kilometers from the city of Cusco. Quenqo is an archaeological complex of mainly religious use where it is believed agricultural rituals were performed. Finally get to know Puka Pucara, located 7 kilometers from the city of Cusco. Puka Pucara, which means &#34;Red Fortress&#34;, was an architectural complex of alleged military use with multiple spaces, plazas, baths, aqueducts, walls and towers, and it is believed the entourage of the Inca used it while he rested in Tambomachay. Return to the hotel<br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">You will be transferred from Arequipa to Puno or Juliaca by bus, approximately 7 hours.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and will take you to the airport. A representative will assist you during the journey.

Upon  arrival  in Puno, transfer from the airport to your hotel, visiting en route the city of Lampa, also known as the pink city or city of marvels. Lampa is a city that preserves its colonial structure,  being its highlights the  Temple  of  Santiago  Apostol,  where a replica of the Piedad of Miguel Angel  Buonaroti  is  exhibited;  its  colonial  church which houses several paintings  of  the Cusco school and whose altars and pulpits are sculped  in wood;  and  its  Colonial  Bridge built out of sillar, which has three archs from where the picturesque extinct volcano of Coachico can be appreciated
<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel in Sacred Valley and transfer you to the Cusco airport.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Enjoy a delicious Lunch at <span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">UNU Tiobamba restaurant.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/></span><br/><br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Participate in a sweet experience in the chocolate workshop in Paste 101. This workshop begins with the presentation of the chef and an explanation about fine cacao, its collection and production in Peru. Then you will taste different varieties of chocolate and continue with the preparation of your own dessert: chocolates, creams and biscuits. Finally, the dish will be served and tasted. </div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy the Virtuoso benefits in Cusco by receiving a welcome kit which includes: a passport holder, an 8gb data chip to stay connected and decorative Andean pompoms.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, you are picked up from your hotel to start today’s route at the San Isidro market, a modern market with a great variety of products. Fruits are a speciality and we recommend you make a stop at stall 30 of Mr Pedro Ortiz, where you will have the opportunity to taste exotic Peruvian fruits such as cherimoya, lucuma or pitahaya. Next, visit a restaurant located on the seafront, where you will learn how to prepare a pisco sour and ceviche, Peru’s signature cocktail and dish. After the delicious lunch, transfer back to the hotel and enjoy your last night in Lima.</div>

<div style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and transfer you to the &#34;El Catador&#34; artisanal winery, where you may watch the production process of wine and pisco (Peruvian brandy), which has remained unchanged since 1856. Next, you will go to the Vista Alegre winery, an industrial winery that has the capacity to produce 5 million litres of Pisco per year. Here, appreciate the manufacturing process. After touring the winery, we invite you to taste some varieties of pisco and sweet wines. The tour continues to the Tacama winery, where you will learn about its industrial techniques for the preparation of a great variety of red and white wines and piscos. Its seedbed area and the plantation house. You will be transferred back to your hotel at the end of the visit.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Private transfer from Cusco to Puerto Maldonado.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will pick you up from the hotel and will transfer you to a restaurant of your choice and bring you back at the established time.<br/></p>
<p>Roundtrip transfer from the selected hotel to the restaurant for lunch or dinner. </p><p><br/></p>
<p>Entrance to Maria Reiche Museum.</p><p><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, and at the agreed time, a transportation service will transfer you to the bus station, where a representative will help you catch your bus to Cusco. On the way, you’ll make a number of interesting stops. The first is the town of Pucara and the Pucara Lithic Museum. This was the first urban settlement on Lake Titicaca, established between 100 and 300 A.D. After a brief break at La Raya, the highest point of the trip between Puno and Cusco, at the dizzying altitude of 4,313 metres above sea level, the bus stops at Sicuani, where lunch is served. We then head on to Racchi, home to the Wiracocha Temple, an Inca site built in honour of a powerful Andean god. The rectangular construction boasts thatched rooftops 20 metres above the ground; these are thought to be the highest in the Inca Empire. Later, visit Andahuaylillas, a town located around 40 kilometres away from Cusco. The main attraction here is the local Church, known as the &#34;Sistine Chapel of America&#34; owing to the high quality of art found inside. The church was built during the 16th century on top of Wari remains. Afterwards, a last 40 kilometre drive gets you to Cusco, today’s destination.</div><p></p>

You will have a guide available for 2 and a half hours, while visiting Machu Picchu. If needed, the guide will also accompany you to Intipunku, Wayna Picchu (subject to space), the Temple of the Moon or the Inca Bridge.
A vehicle will be waiting for you at the train station of Ollantaytambo to transfer you to your hotel in Cusco.

<p><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Arrive to the rural community of Huilloc in the province of Urubamba, to participate in the process of making garments using traditional techniques and learn the meaning of their iconography. The local interpreter, which is a resident of the community, will welcome you with traditional music and invite you to the house of a villager who will wait for you to show the Huilloc typical clothing weaving process. The homeowner will welcome you, presenting the members of his family and invite you a muña infusion. In addition, he will give you a demonstration of how different clothing is made so you can perform this process using traditional tools and techniques with the supervision of a community member. Then you will dress entirely in Huilloc clothes and take pictures and videos to remember this remarkable experience. Finally, you can purchase a beautiful Huilloc garment, contributing to the spiritual strengthening and identity of this Inca village. The interpreter will greet you on behalf of the community and farewell you accompanied by musicians.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p>#1<br/></p><p>After an early breakfast, we pick you up from your hotel and leave the city of Cusco in the morning. On the route we take a short break in Mollepata before ascending a winding mountain road to a place called Soraypampa (12,690 ft.) Here, we meet our emergency horse and begin the trek toward Salkantay Pass 4,638 m. (15,213 ft.). At the pass we stop enjoy the views of snowcapped peaks of the Vilcabamba Mountain Range in every direction, with the glaciated south face of Salkantay towering above us where Andean condors can be seen. From the pass we descend towards Wayracmachay to enjoy our lunch and then continue descending along the left bank of the Salkantay River. Observe the landscape become increasingly green, changing from highland to jungle or cloud forest. Feel the warm air rising from the jungle, accompanied by colorful butterflies and striking orchids, while arriving to the second camp at Andenes (9,481ft.), a beautiful spot surrounded with vegetation<br/></p><p>#2<br/></p><p>Today we hike along the Santa Teresa River Valley, through more populated rural areas. We pass through banana, granadilla, avocado and coffee. A hot picnic lunch is served along the way. After lunch, we hike for about 2 hours before a private vehicle meets us for a short drive to the beginning of the Llactapata Inca Trail (30 minutes or so). From the head of the newly restored Inca trail, we easily make our way to the last campsite at Lucmabamba. In the evening we will have a coffee taste demonstration with our host, a local family. Dinner will be served and then spend the night in the camp.</p><p>#3<br/></p><p>After breakfast, we will head uphill for 2-3 hours towards Llactapata Pass (8,974ft.) and descend to the archaeological complex of the same name. Besides, we will enjoy a distant but spectacular view of Machu Picchu Sanctuary from the southeast, a view few travelers get the chance to admire. Lunch is served in a scenic viewpoint, looking out to Machu Picchu. After that, begin the final descent to the Aobamba River through lush bamboo forests, orchards and coffee plantations. Finally we will get to the Hydroelectric Power Station, to board the train to Aguas Calientes and spend the night. There you can stay in the hotel of your choice.</p><p>#4<br/></p><p>After an early breakfast, we make our way to the bus station for the ride up  to Machu Picchu. The guided tour of the sanctuary lasts two hours and at the end, you shall have free time to explore the place at your leisure, or simply relax and meditate. We will return by bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch and board the train to Ollantaytambo. From this point, a private vehicle will drive us back to Cusco and you will be transferred to your hotel. You arrival to Cusco will be approximately at 7 or 8 pm.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">------------------</span><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">------</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/>Level: Moderate<br/>Maximum altitude: 4650 m a.s.l.<br/>Walking distance: 40 km - 24,85 miles</p><p></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour will start in the Pueblo Libre district, to visit the Larco Museum, founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle en 1926. The museum, located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century, houses the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver artifacts as well pieces of erotic art. You will then visit the Main Square, where Lima was first founded as &#34;The City of Kings,&#34; in 1535, and it was already known by the name of Lima when Peru proclaimed its independence from the Spanish Crown. There you may see the Presidential Palace, the Archbishop's Palace and the Lima Cathedral. You will then visit the Aliaga House, a viceregal construction delivered by Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga) when he founded the city. You will return to your hotel after enjoying lunch at its facilities.</div><p><br/></p>

<p>The Larco Museum is located in the traditional district of Pueblo Libre. It was founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926 and is based inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century. The museum has a complete pre-Hispanic collection of pieces of gold and silver, as well as erotic art that you can visit in a one-hour tour.The Larco Museum is located in the traditional district of Pueblo Libre. It was founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926 and is based inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century. The museum has a complete pre-Hispanic collection of pieces of gold and silver, as well as erotic art that you can visit in a one-hour tour with a private curator.<br/></p>

<p>Enjoy a nice cocktail with aperitifs at the Larco Cafe Cafe. Located on the terrace in front of the beautiful gardens of the museum, discover the best Peruvian and international cuisine in a cozy atmosphere.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will pick you up from <span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">the airport of Lima </span>hotel at the established time and will transfer you to your hotel.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Your day will begin with a tour of approximately an hour and a half to the archaeological sites of Moray and Maras. The archaeological remains of Moray consist of mysterious concentric circular terraces that for some scholars would be an agricultural research center. External platforms, more elevated, woud be at higher temperatures than the inmates and could simulate the conditions of the different ecological floors of the empire. On the other hand,  Maras is surreal sight. It is an impressive complex of salt mining. These are large deposits of salt that were used to trade for other products with other regions of the empire. After the tour return to your hotel.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">We recommend visiting the Intipunku (&#34;Sun Gate&#34;), which is the last stop before entering the Inca sanctuary. Another interesting option is the Inca Bridge, which is part of the Inca Trail and is made of vegetable fiber or straw. The bridge is woven with ancient techniques and each year several area communities come together to renew and keep this wonderful tradition.<br/></p>

Upon your arrival in Iquitos City, a transportation service will transfer you to the local pier, where you will board a boat to be transferred to the Ceiba Tops lodge, located 40 kilometers from Iquitos navigating the Amazon River. This exclusive accommodation has air conditioned rooms, private bathrooms with hot water and beautiful tropical gardens. You will enjoy lunch upon your arrival at the lodge and in the afternoon you may take a walk to get acquainted with the flora fauna of the area. We recommend that you visit the Toucan Bar in the evening, where there will be flute and guitar music and some tourist guide will decide to tell the legends that populate the  collective imagination of the Amazonian societies. You will have breakfast at the lodge in the morning, and then you will visit a community of the Yagua ethnic group, where you will see how the cerbatana (blowgun), the Amazonian traditional hunting tool, is used, and then you may barter some items manufactured by the community members. After lunch, you will take a motor boat tour to the Indiana town, founded by the Franciscan religious community. You must pay attention to the river along the route, as it is not unusual to spot the presence of curious pink dolphins. You will be transferred back to the lodge and the end of your visit. Today you will explore the surroundings of the river in a canoe and catch fish in that area. The rivers and lagoons of the Amazon jungle have more than three thousand fish species and they concentrate in flooded jungle areas. It is also possible to catch piranhas; this fish species has earned a bad reputation, but it is generall harmless and it is included in the local populations  daily diet. While you navigate the river you may also see spectacular birds, such as the shansho, which has a prehistoric appearance and populates the river banks and lagoons. At the end of your tour, you will be transferred back to the lodge. After having breakfast, today you may explore some of the jungle that surrounds the lodge. You must pay attention to the sound of the jungle and inspect each corner of the area: thousands of river, flying and terrestrial animal species surround it. You will return to Iquitos after an early lunch and you will be then transferred to the airport to catch your departure flight.
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Aranwa Colca Resort &amp; Spa<br/></p>
Entrance to Cahuachi, pyramid and ceremonial center of Nasca Civilization.

A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel in Paracas and transfer you to the camp at km. 253 of the Panamericana Sur Highway, to board a boogie and enter the desert half an hour before sunset. Ride through the beautiful dunes to reach the camp equiped with carpets and cushions, located at the top of a dune with a panoramic view of the desert to watch the sunset. Once there, you will be offered wine or champagne, accompanied by some snacks, fruit, soft drinks and juices, enjoying a musical background while you appreciate the beautiful sunset. Finally, return to camp and be taken back to your hotel.
<p>Transfer from the airport to the Titilaka hotel<br/></p>

You will start your tour at Perus National Museum of Anthropology, Archaeology and History, located in the Pueblo Libre district. It is the oldest national museum, founded in 1826, and it covers the Peruvian history from the beginning of the local civilization until the republican times. It houses a huge collection of pottery, textiles and gold and silver artifacts from various Peruvian cultures. You will then visit the Larco Museum, located in the same district. Founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926, the museum is located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century, houses the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver artifacts as well pieces of erotic art. Over 45,000 archaeological artifacts bear witness of five thousand years of Peruvian history.

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">D<span style="font-size: 13px;">ay 1</span></div><p><span style="text-align: left;float: none;"></span><div style="font-size: 13px;text-align: justify;">Begin your adventure by travelling south through the Callejón de Huaylas valley. Along the way, appreciate the Querococha lagoon and the Kahuish tunnel (considered the highest in the world) before arriving at Chavín de Huántar, the centre of one of the most advanced pre-Inca cultures in South America and named the World Heritage Site by Unesco. Its pyramids, known as the Old Temple and the Castle, are connected by stairs and platforms of carved stone. Chavín de Huántar stands out for its underground galleries and labyrinths. A must-see in the Old Temple is the Lanzon, a 4.5 meters high monolith carved in stone with human iconography and animals such as jaguars, anacondas and eagles. The walls of Chavín were decorated with carved heads or cabezas clavas, embedded in the stone, today only one remains in its original place. Continue the trip with a visit to the museum, where you will find ceramics, lithic sculptures, more cabezas clavas and the famous Tello Obelisk. Enjoy lunch at a typical restaurant in the area.</div><div style="font-size: 13px;text-align: justify;">After lunch, the driver transfers you to the small town of Castillo where the trek begins with a three-hour ascent in the Qhapaq Ñan. Have a walk surrounded by trees and beautiful countryside, until you reach the campsite in Soledad de Tambo for tea time. Later, visit the beautifully restored &#34;ushnu&#34; (ceremonial platform) and the nearby archaeological remains of the Pinkush and Inca culture accompanied by an archaeologist who will show you some of the latest discoveries.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><font color="#000000"><span style="font-size: 13px;"><br/></span></font></div><font color="#000000"></font><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Day 2</span></div><font color="#000000"></font><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">The Qhapaq Ñan adventure continues in one of its best-preserved sections. Relive the Inca past as you walk the stone stairs up to the Wagapunta pass, at 4,400 meters above sea level, and be amazed by the panorama of the Andean peaks that will leave you breathless, surrounded by ¨apachetas¨, small towers made with rocks and with great religious importance for the Incas. Arrival at the camp in Qhenhuajirca in the afternoon, in time to visit the workshop of master weaver Juan Araujo, who will explain about his traditional work.</span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><font color="#000000"></font><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Day 3</span></div><font color="#000000"></font><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Begin your day descending through a picturesque valley to the village of Ayash, from where you begin to ascend again following the Qhapaq Ñan to the community of Huamanín. Walk through a 4,500 meters high pampa and discover the rock formations around you. Then descend to the Taparaco river basin, where the camp awaits, surrounded only by nature and some shepherds houses in the distance. Not far away is Tambo Grande, an old Inca tambo, now home to dozens of wild rabbits. Sleep under the sparkle of the Milky Way and the constellations that defined the Inca cosmology.</span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><font color="#000000"></font><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Day 4</span></div><font color="#000000"></font><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Let yourself be conquered by the beauty of the valley of Taparaco while you walk along a section magnificently delimited by almost intact Inca walls. Relax as you walk by silent mountains, listening only to the sound of water flowing down the river at your side and the singing of birds, an experience reserved for few adventurers. The camp waits for you in San Lorenzo de Isco, a small community next to the river. Lunch and dinner at camp. </span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><font color="#000000"></font><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Day 5</span></div><font color="#000000"></font><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">The last day begins heading to the town of Colpa. From there, a transportation takes you to Huánuco Pampa, one of the most important administrative centres of the Empire and considered the capital of Chinchaysuyo (northern region of Tahuantinsuyo). In Inca times, there were more than 800 hectares of Huánuco Pampa built area, with thousands of colcas (deposits), kallankas (large rooms for meetings and ceremonies) and homes of the settlers. Today, the 10-meter high ushnu and the Incawasi (Incas house) are the main buildings that still stand. After a guided tour of two hours, enjoy a tasty experience, where you will try a Pachamanca, prepared especially for you while you learn about the traditions of the town. In the end, a transportation service takes you back to the hotel in Huaraz.</span></div></p><p></p>
<p>Bus ticket from Machu Picchu to Aguas Calientes.<br/><br/></p>
<p>Bus ticket from Aguas Calientes to Machu Picchu.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Tour the Sacred Valley from up high in a splendid helicopter ride. The experience starts at the international airport of Cusco, where you will receive a brief safety talk before takeoff. Once in the air, you will head towards the Sacred Valley, specifically to two of the most magnificent Inca constructions: Maras and Moray. Maras is a salt extraction centre that has been operating since the time of the Incas. It is made up of thousands of rectangular water deposits located on the side of a mountain, which together form an out-of-this-world landscape. You will also pass over Moray, an enormous construction of concentric circles that worked as an agricultural laboratory.<br/></p>
<p>We will transfer you from Ollanta station to the hotel in Cusco.<br/></p>
<p>We will transfer you from your hotel in Cusco to Ollantaytambo train station where a representative will assist you boarding for your visit to Machu Picchu.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel in Cusco and take you to Ollanta train station. A representative will assist you to board your train.</p>
<p>A transportation service will transfer you from Rio Sagrado Hotel to your hotel in Cusco (includes a representative).<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and will transfer you to the  Ollantaytambo train station. A representative will contact youupon your arrival and will help you board the train for your visit to Machu Picchu. Upon your return, a representative will help you with your return train to Cusco.</p>
A transportation service will transfer you from Trujillo airport to the selected hotel.

<p>You will be transferred from the airport to the Sacred Valley where you will visit Awanakancha (&#34;The palace of fabrics&#34;), South American textile and camelid exhibition center. It is considered a living museum and you will be able to appreciate the Andean camelids, learn the techniques to take advantage of their wool and acquire finely spun garments. Then, you will follow the road to your hotel in Urubamba. Upon arrival, a representative will assist you with your check-in and accommodation.<br/></p>
A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel at the established time and will transfer you to Poroy train station.
<p style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will be waiting for you at the airport, to transfer you to your hotel.</p>
<p>Transfer from hotel to the pier.  Assistance on departure.</p>
A transportation service will pick you up from the airport and will transfer you to your hotel.
A transportation service will pick up from your hotel at the established time and will transfer you to the agreed bus station.
<p>Once you land in Arequipa, a transportation service will take you to your hotel. Then, a representative informs you about the attractions and services the city has to offer.<br/><br/></p>
<p>Night transfer from the airport to the hotel in shared service<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During your tour of the valley, visit the Cervecería del Valle Sagrado, where craft beer is produced. A perfect place to rest in a welcoming environment. Here, visit the manufacturing plant and learn about the brewery process and supplies used to make the local beer. The visit concludes in the tasting room, where different varieties of beers are tried.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you will arrive at Cala, a restaurant that has a privileged view as it is located right next to the sea. Here, participate in a preparation class of the delicious Causa and the famous Pisco Sour. At the end of the class, lunch is served on the terrace of Cala, from where you will have a spectacular view of the Pacific Ocean while enjoying a typical Peruvian dish of your choice.</div><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you will arrive at Cala, a restaurant that has a privileged view as it is located right next to the sea. Here, participate in a preparation class of the delicious Tiradito and the famous Pisco Sour. At the end of the class, lunch is served on the terrace of Cala, from where you will have a spectacular view of the Pacific Ocean while enjoying a typical Peruvian dish of your choice.</div><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Visitors will embark on one of the most amazing routes in the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful ¨Andean Explorer¨. The train has two dining cars, lounge wagon and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the Sierra. It also has elegant suites and cabins where passengers can rest comfortably. The journey begins leaving Cusco towards Puno. Passengers will enjoy a delicious lunch and then make the first stop in Raqchi; admirable archaeological site with remains of Inca temples and buildings. The journey will continue and after teatime, be amazed by a breathtaking sunset at La Raya, the limit between Cusco and Puno, surrounded by towering mountains. Dinner will be served in the train and you will reach Lake Titicaca station at night.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will enjoy the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and breakfast will be served on board. During the day you can optionally choose to visit the Uros Islands on a typical boat, visit the Taquile Island, lunch in a private area on the beach or a show of traditional dances and crafts demonstration. </p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will travel the Peruvian highlands on a journey that will take you through wonderful landscapes in Cusco, Puno and Arequipa. During the morning, the train will leave Cusco in direction of Puno and will you have lunch on board. The first stop will be at the archaeological site of Raqchi, also known as Temple of Viracocha where Inca storages and parts of the main building can be seen. In the afternoon you will be served tea and enjoy the sunset in La Raya, border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by imposing mountains. Then you will have a cocktail and dinner on board. You will arrive at the station of Lake Titicaca around 10p.m.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Witness the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and continue your journey. Breakfast will be served on board and after this you will visit the Islands of Uros, a set of floating islands made of totora, plants that grow in the lake. Then visit Taquile Island, from where you will have a wonderful view of the lake. Lunch will be served at Collata Beach, which is part of the island and you will enjoy a demonstration of typical dances and craftsmanship of the inhabitants of the island. Afterwards, you will be transferred to the station of Lake Titicaca. You will be served a tea and will continue the trip heading to Arequipa. After a cocktail, dinner will be <span style="text-align: justify;">served on board.</span></p><p>#3</p><p>You will have breakfast in the Saracocha Lagoon in the Puno region and later you will visit the Sumbay caves in Arequipa, where cave paintings from the Paleolithic period representing human and animal figures can be seen on the walls. Disembark in km. 99 to visit the Colca Canyon, more than 4 kilometers deep in its deepest part. After this visit you will have lunch on board and take a panoramic tour that will take you through the main buildings of the &#34;White City&#34;. Finally, you will be transferred to Arequipa´s airport.</p><p></p>

<p>#1<br/>During the morning you will arrive at the Biological Station, a place of learning for young people and professionals interested in conservation, research and ecotourism. After lunch and the check-in, you will take a tour through the trails of the station, surrounded by an impressive forest of vast biodiversity, with trees more than 35 meters high where birds, amphibians, primates and vegetation can be seen on day and night. During the<br/>evening you will go on a river tour.<br/><br/>#2<br/>In the morning you will ascend to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway, where you can walk through the tree tops over 30 meters high, where hundreds of species have built their habitats. Afterwards, you will visit Sandoval Lake, a protected natural area that hosts flora and fauna.<br/><br/>#3<br/>During the morning, you will go on a tour to see some of the most exotic birds in the world from a sandbank on the Madre de Dios River. After lunch you will visit a &#34;native chacra&#34; (farm) where products such as herbs and<br/>tropical plants are grown using natural fertilisers. Finally, visit the ¨Selva Oculta¨ (Hidden Forest), a wildlife sanctuary.<br/><br/>#4<br/>You will be transferred to Puerto Maldonado.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p><span>At arrival in the airport of Puerto Maldonado, you will be transferred to the fluvial port to reach Posada Amazónica in a two-hour boat trip. You will be able to see the great diversity of birds while touring the Madre de Dios River. The Madre de Dios River flows along 1150 kilometers, crossing most of the national Manu and Tambopata National Reserves. The Tambopata National Reserve, which has an area of one million and a half hectares, has been the subject of several TV documentaries due to its exuberant flora and fauna, which makes it a unique region in the world. After arriving at the lodge and getting installed in your bungalow, you will take a short tour to get acquainted with the area that surrounds the lodge. Have dinner and then rest to make the most out of the next day.</span></p><p><span>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). </span>During your stay, larger luggage<span> </span>will remain stored.<span></span></p><p>#2</p><p><span>After having breakfast, you will board a motorboat heading for Lake Tres Chimbadas in a 15-minute ride. Once at the lake, take a canoe trip through its shores to have a close look at the fauna that populates it. The most outstanding animals are giant otters, turtles and shanshos, a bird with a prehistoric appearance. Unlike rivers, lakes attract a different diversity of animals, as there is no need to fight the current and it represents safe, calm water for marine, terrestrial and flying animals. You will then return to the lodge to have lunch and later tour the neighboring paths. This will enable you to watch a great variety of insects and - if you are lucky - vipers, deer and other larger animals from a very short distance. Then, take a night tour after dinner to watch a variety of animals that only leave their lairs at night.</span></p><p></p><p>#3</p><p><span>After breakfast, a motor boat will transfer you to the airport of Puerto Maldonado to take your next flight.</span></p><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">#1 </div><div style="text-align: justify;">Visitors will embark on one of the most amazing routes in the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful Andean Explorer. The train has two dining cars, lounge wagon and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the Sierra. It also has elegant suites and cabins where passengers can rest comfortably. The journey begins leaving Puno towards Cusco. Enjoy a delicious lunch made of fresh, local ingredients, designed by the chefs of Belmond Hotel Monasterio. Then visit La Raya, the border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by towering mountains. Dinner will be served on board and arrival to the Cusco region is set at night. </div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2 </div><div style="text-align: justify;">During the second and last day of the cruise, you will enjoy breakfast on board and arrive to Cusco.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>#1 </p><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and will transfer you to Punos principal pier. Here you will board a motor boat to go to the floating islands of the Uros. Uros are an ancestral society that inhabits a series of artificial islands built with totora, a reef that grows in the lake. After touring the islands, you will continue your trip to the Taquile Island. Unlike other islands, inhabited by Aymara-speaking people in the lake, its residents speak Quechua. You will receive a short explanation about the island ad then you will sail until the Suasi Island, located two and a half hours from Puno City. Suasi is a private island on which it is possible to watch all types of highland camelids, birds, and typical crops, and participate in traditional celebrations. After enjoying a barbecue, you can take part in the activities suggested for the afternoon and you will spend the night at the facilities of the island.</div><div><br/></div><div>#2</div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After enjoying a buffet breakfast, you can sail Lake Titicaca on board of a canoe, the worlds highest navigable lake and part of the border between Bolivia and Peru, countries that share the Aymara population indistinctly. It has an area of over 8,500 kilometers square and various communities that maintain their ancestral attires and customs until today have developed on its islands. You will depart for Puno at noon.</div><p><br/></p></div>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Departure early in the morning (either by private transport or by regular bus) to the town of Paracas to start the excursion. (approximately 4 hours of travel). At arrival, you will be transferred to the chosen hotel. You can rest and enjoy the hotel facilities. Today you will tour the huge dunes of the desert in a tubular vehicle. These lightweight but powerful four-wheel drive cars are capable of climbing slopes of loose sand and offer you an adrenaline-filled experience. The pilots and their machines will take you to places where other vehicles can´t reach, turning the ride into an authentic adventure. Also, you can practice sandboarding, an adventure sport that will test your balance and ability while riding the dunes on a board. An experience that you can´t miss.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Depart in the morning to the boarding point for your boat ride to the Ballestas Islands inhabited by pelicans, sea lions and Humboldt penguins. On the way, you can see the still inexplicable lines drawn in the sand forming a chandelier. After approximately 02 hours of travel, return to the Port of Paracas. At the indicated time, head back to Lima by regular bus service. (approximately 4 hours of travel)<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">The tour starts on the road to Ica Regional Museum, which has an important collection of pre-Hispanic artefacts from the Ica, Paracas, Nazca, Wari and Inca cultures that have been exhibited in different parts of the world. After that you will visit a producer of wine and pisco, products that have made the valleys of the Ica desert famous. Thereafter is a visit to the sanctuary of the Lord of Luren, a dark-skinned saint from Ica, whose carved image was made in Spain in the year 1556. On the third Monday of October each year the image is taken from the church and carried through the streets of Ica accompanied by hundreds of devotees. Finally, you will visit Lake Huacachina. Originally an oasis in the middle of the desert, the lake is one of Ica´s major attractions. It is surrounded by large sand dunes and offers a variety of optional services: from good food to an adrenalin-stimulating sand buggy ride through the desert. <br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">This  classic  old-style cafe,  a  Lima institution  since 1937, is decorated  with  cool  enamel doors  and  staffed  by waitresses in quaint red-and-white dresses.  Its  best  known  for its exquisite pastries,  cakes,  breads,  and gourmet food shop,  but &#34;the little white store&#34; is  also  a good little restaurant, perfect to enjoy a variety of gourmet dishes.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Your tour will start en route to the Paracas National Reserve, three hours South of Lima. Paracas is an extraordinary place due to its multiple attractions. It is a natural reserve with beautiful geological formations, impressive fauna and vestiges of a pre-Inca culture that dominated the desert to install a successful society in adverse conditions. Next, you will go to the pier to sail to the Ballestas Islands, home to a large population of sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species found in the archipelago, an ideal place for their reproduction and coexistence. On your way back, you will be transferred to the Pisco City airport, where you will board a light aircraft to fly over the Nazca Lines (subject to weather conditions). Nazca is a small city. It may be overlooked in the map if it were not for the mysterious lines engraved over the desert that have made it world famous. You will return to Lima after having lunch. <br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour will start en route to the Paracas National Reserve, three hours South of Lima. Paracas is an extraordinary place due to its multiple attractions. It is a natural reserve with beautiful geological formations, impressive fauna and vestiges of a pre-Inca culture that dominated the desert to install a successful society in adverse conditions. Next, you will go to the pier to sail to the Ballestas Islands, home to a large population of sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species found in the archipelago, an ideal place for their reproduction and coexistence. On your way back, you will be transferred to the Pisco City airport, where you will board a light aircraft to fly over the Nazca Lines (subject to weather conditions). Nazca is a small city. It may be overlooked in the map if it were not for the mysterious lines engraved over the desert that have made it world famous. You will return to Lima after having lunch.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p></p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#1</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Arrival to the city of Iquitos. A representative waits for your arrival at the airport. From here, the journey starts towards Nauta, a small riverside town on the banks of the Marañón River. Once there, you are welcomed at the private port. When boarding, enjoy a fantastic panoramic view from the observation area. Participate in an orientation talk and get to know the entertainment centre that offers movies, as well as an extensive library of books about flora and fauna of the area. The ship also has a fully equipped bar where you can try any regional or international combination of liquors and aromas. Dinner is served during the afternoon.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#2</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Wake up early to appreciate one of the most beautiful areas of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve: Fundo Casual. The walk through this area will take you to the depths of the Terra Firme jungle (un-flooded forest) where you can appreciate the local fauna. After lunch on the boat, cross through the Yanayacu and Pucate rivers, passing through different streams and lagoons. This is one of the best preserved and protected areas of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve. If you want to interact directly with the waters of the Amazon, go out in one of the kayaks or paddle boards; and you will even have the opportunity to swim with the curious pink dolphins. Return to the boat and dinner on board.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#3</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, pass through the Samiria River and its tributaries, where wildlife such as blue and yellow parrots, red parrots, toucans, umbrella birds, monkeys, capybaras and more abound. This is also a place where dolphins usually feed, so you can see them up close. After lunch, visit Nauta Caño where you have the opportunity to sail in kayak. Also go fishing for the famous piranha. Return to the boat where dinner is served.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#4</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">During the morning disembark in the port of Nauta and head towards the airport. On the way, visit the Floating Mammals Rescue and Rehabilitation Centre to learn about manatees and other rescued species, as well as plans for the species conservation. Finally, arrive at the airport. You are accompanied by a representative who assists you on the way to your departure from the city.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;"><b></b></p><p></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">The tour starts in Libertador Paracas Hotel early in the morning. Here, a guide will receive the participants and provide a briefing on safety measures, paddling techniques, and the rowing equipment will be handed over.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">When the activity starts, enjoy the tranquillity of the sea and observe the birds and marine fauna. Once on the opposite shore, disembark to get to know the shellfish hatchery, and receive an explanation about its breeding in this area. Also, enjoy a delicious tasting of parmesan seashells and clams, a typical appetiser, accompanied by beer or drink. Once the visit finishes, return in the canoe to the Hotel Libertador.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">We pick you up early in the morning from your hotel and drive you to the train station to board the train that will take us to km. 104 (the starting point of the hike). After crossing the suspension bridge over the Urubamba River we visit the archaeological site of Chachabamba (2,150m / 7,052ft). From here, we will hike upwards for about 8km (approximately 4 hours) and get to the magnificent archaeological site of Wiñayhuayna (2,700m), an impressive complex made up of an agricultural center with numerous terraces, a religious sector as well as an urban sector. On the way we will appreciate the vegetation of the forest. After having the box lunch we will continue for about one hour further onto Inti Punku (The Sun gate) at 2,730m (8,792ft), from where you will have your first dramatic and impressive view of Machu Picchu (2,430m / 7,972ft). As we are going down, we will take a side path off the trail to take a bus that transports us to Aguas Calientes where you will check into your accommodation.</div><p><br/></p><p>#2</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After an early breakfast we will take the bus back up to Machu Picchu. A walking tour is given by a guide through the different sectors of this great Inca legacy, considered Cultural Heritage. In the afternoon, we will take a bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch and then board the train back to Cusco.</div><p><br/></p><p>------------------------</p><p><br/></p><p>Level: Moderate</p><p><br/></p><p>Maximum altitude: Warmi Wañusca 13776 feet / 4200 m a.s.l.</p><p><br/></p><p>Walking distance: 28,59 miles - 46 km</p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Arrival at Refugio Amazonas. To explore the Amazon up-close, choose any &#34;a la carte&#34; activity according to your interests. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities. </div><p></p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Explore the Amazon up-close, choosing an &#34;a la carte&#34; activity according to your interests. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities. Schedules are available at the lodge.</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Explore the Amazon up-close, choosing an &#34;a la carte&#34; activity according to your interests. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities. Schedules are available at the lodge.</p><p>#4<br/><br/>Check-out of Refugio Amazonas.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Virtuoso benefits: welcome letter, upgrade (subject to availability), early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), welcome amenity in the room, 20% discount on bar consumption, 20% discount on meals on the menu and 20% discount on spa services.<span id="selectionBoundary_1553613076728_5589335825096615" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Virtuoso benefits: welcome letter, upgrade (subject to availability), early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), welcome amenity in the room, 20% discount on bar consumption, 20% discount on meals on the menu and 20% discount on spa services.</div><p><br/></p>
<p>Virtuoso benefits: VIP Express check-in, upgrade (subject to availability), early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 1-hour massage per guest.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Virtuoso benefits: upgrade (subject to availability), early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 50-minute massage per guest.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Virtuoso benefits: upgrade (subject to availability), early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 40-minute massage per guest.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Transfer from the hotel to the airport, and assistance on departure.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Drive to Porcon Farm, located 30 km from the city of Cajamarca. On the way stop at the village of Huambocancha Alta, 8.5 km from the city of Cajamarca, where, on both sides of the road, inhabitants-artisans give life to the stone. Here, entire families are dedicated to the art of sculpting forms under the sky, among the dust released by their mini grinders and chisels with which they do their work. Admire their handicrafts in stone, before</div><div style="text-align: justify;">heading to the Porcon Farm, a rural and religious community that counts with pine forests, livestock, fish, mini-zoo and beekeeping. Also, this farm offers a program of agrotourism in the mountains north of cajamarca, including crops and livestock. Among the activities this farm offers visitors we have the planting and harvesting of potatoes and other root crops and vegetables, seed selection and soil preparation, hand milking of cows and sire selection, program management and rehabilitation of pine trees, which is being done by a successful project. You will have the opportunity to stroll in the countryside or just relax and enjoy the peace and tranquility of the beautiful landscapes that surround the community. After the visit, transfer back to Cajamarca.</div><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Visit Cumbemayo complex, discovered in 1937, it is surrounded by an interesting  stone forest  that seems  to mimic  the silhouettes of pious friars (for which it is popularly called &#34;frailones&#34; or giant friars). In addition,  you find the Aqueduct (1000 B.C.),  a unique work  of hydraulic  engineering, the Sanctuary,  a cliff that looks like  a giant human  head,  and  the  Caves,  which  include  cave paintings and carvings.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Visit of the Inca Baths, located 6 km from the city of Cajamarca. Originally called Pultamarca, it is here, supposedly, that Atahualpa was resting just before the confrontation with Pizarro. Here you find hot springs of mineral water with a maximum temperature of 72ºC (158ºF). It is believed that they possess therapeutic properties for the treatment of bone and nervous system disorders. Discover the natural healing power of these hot springs through sessions of hydrotherapy and massage to help reduce muscle fatigue caused by physical exertion. After this visit, transfer back to the hotel.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Visit the 'Ventanillas de Otuzco', located just 7 km. from the city, standing out amidst the countryside. The site is a pre-Inca cemetery which pre-dates the Caxamarca culture, probably influenced by the Wari empire. Hundreds of galleries and individual niches resembling windows were hewn into the volcanic stone. The niches probably were originally sealed with gravestones carved with figures in haut-relief, judging  by fragments found nearby. The niches lead into a network of dark and mysterious galleries which appear to have no end, giving rise to myths of secret tunnels that linked Cajamarca to Cuzco. At the end of the visit, transfer back to the hotel.</div><p><br/></p>
<p>Virtuoso benefits : welcome letter, early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 40-minute massage per guest and 10% discount in the bar and restaurant of all Belmond hotels.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Virtuoso benefits: welcome letter, early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 40-minute massage per guest and 10% discount in the bar and restaurant of all Belmond hotels.</span><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Lunch at Plaza San Martin restaurant. </span><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Virtuoso benefits: welcome letter, early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 40-minute massage per guest and 10% discount in the bar and restaurant of all Belmond hotels.</span></div><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Virtuoso benefits: welcome letter in the room, welcome amenity in the room, upgrade (subject to availability), early check-in and late check-out (subject to availability), 20% discount at the bar, 20% % discount in Market 770 restaurant, 20% discount in the Heavenly Spa by Westin and thermal circuit for two people.</span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Your trip to Machu Picchu begins with a train trip to Aguas Calientes in about two hours. This city is also known as Machu Picchu Pueblo and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you will find the railway station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories, for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and be able to climb early. Finally, after a short transfer you arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and refuge for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1471). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you can also visit, optionally, Intipunku (Puerta del Sol), Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Puente de Inca. In the afternoon return by train.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Tour of the main attractions in the city of Cajamarca. Visit the Hill of Santa Apolonia, natural vantage point in the center of the city from where you can appreciate the whole city and the valley of Cajamarca. Here you also find the so called Silla del Inca, which was a pre-Inca ceremonial altar. Then proceed to the Monumental Complex of Belen, with the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Piedad. Then continue to the &#34;Cuarto del Rescate&#34;, where the Inca Atahualpa, last king of the Incas, was held prisoner. Then visit  the exteriors of the temple of San Francisco, built in 1562 and inhabited by the first Franciscans. Also visit the Plaza de Armas, and the exterior of the Cathedral, built in the seventeenth century, with a facade that  is a refined sample of Baroque art, standing out its main altar made completely of gold leafs. After the visit, transfer back to the hotel.<br/></p>

<p>Virtuoso benefits: welcome letter, early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 40-minute massage per guest and 10% discount in the bar and restaurant of all Belmond hotels.</p><p><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 10.0pt;color: #332f2e;">Virtuoso benefits: welcome letter, early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 40-minute massage per guest and 10% discount in the bar and restaurant of all Belmond hotels.</span></p>

<p>Virtuoso benefits: welcome letter, early check-in, late check-out (subject to availability), 40-minute massage per guest and 10% discount in the bar and restaurant of all Belmond hotels.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Our staff will welcome you at the airport in Puerto Maldonado and drive you through the city to the boat dock on the Tambopata River. Here we board a motorized canoe and head towards the nearby confluence of the mighty Madre de Dios River to the mouth of the Heath River, which is the natural border of Peru and Bolivia. In the haven of this important Amazonian tributary we  have a vision of the diversity of the riverside environment; cliffs of red earth with outreaching forest are interspersed with thick banks of Cercopia trees and couch grass. After brief formalities at border crossings, we take the boat through the narrow waters for a couple of hours, enjoying the intimacy of the mysterious forest on both sides. Occasional sightings of native children splashing along the banks are interspersed with long, silent stretches where we can locate herons, hawks, cormorants, geese, orinoco, and perhaps a family of Capybaras, which is the worlds largest rodent can weigh up to 55Kg. After this interesting trip we arrive at our simple, but comfortable accommodation of the Heath River Wildlife Center; just in time for dinner.</p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p>#2</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Today we start very early to visit the most spectacular attraction of this area: the parrot and macaw clay lick along the river. Here these colorful birds gather to eat the clay in the cliffs on the river banks to neutralize certain toxins in their daily diet (poisonous berries and hallucinogenic plants). Sometimes they congregate in the hundreds, pushing and squabbling over the best place to eat. This noisy and unforgettable show can go on for two or three hours, and can gather many varieties of parrots, parakeets, Chestnut Fronted Macaws and their larger cousins, the Red and Green Macaw. This extraordinary display occurs in only a handful of places in the Upper Amazon Basin. Our floating platform provides us with comfort and is completely hidden, so here we will enjoy a full breakfast during the show. We make land back down the river will walk back along a section of the extensive jungle trails. Here we will find huge Chestnut, Kapok and fig trees; along with the dark strangler fig whose strategy of life is as sinister as its name implies. Our guide will point out and explain the medicinal use and trade of dozens of plants and trees, while keeping eyes and ears open for birds or one of the eight species of monkeys found in this region. We could run into a small herd of the two species of wild pigs that are common in this area. In order to mark their territory they use scent glands so powerful that they can be smelt long before being seen. After lunch we hike along the trail leading to the point where the forest abruptly gives way to the vast plains of the Pampas of Heath. This unique land is a result of poor soil and extreme climatic cycles of droughts and floods. It is the largest intact tropical savanna in the Amazon is the habitat of endemic birds and mammals, such as the Fork-Tailed Hummingbird and the Manned Wolf. Just beyond the edge of the forest you can climb to an elevated platform that allows for a great view of this vast expanse of grasslands and shrubs, dotted with palm trees. The palm tree Mauritia Flexuosa produces nuts rich in palm oil and dry hollow stems that provide vital food and shelter for nesting pairs of Red Bellied Macaws and the rarer Blue and Yellow Macaws. We aim to arrive around sunset, when the parrots are returning from their daily search for food to gather in this place. We return to the lodge at night using headlamps and flashlights, and perhaps stopping here and there in total darkness to listen to the ever-changing sounds of frogs, insects, and other animals; the magic of the jungle at night. We may run into frogs the size of small rabbits, homes of hairy</div><div style="text-align: justify;">tarantulas or night monkeys hanging from the trees; there is a huge and unpredictable collection of nocturnal creatures in the night. After dinner some guests may choose to visit the lick of mammals, with the hope of seeing the Lowland Tapir, the largest mammal in the jungle.</div><p></p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">On our second full day in at the lodge we can choose from a wide range of activities available in this diverse and unique tropical environment. Many people choose to make a second visit to the macaw clay lick or spend more time on the trails. Later we can take a canoe tour around the Cocha Platanillal, an oxbow lake which is located a short distance down river from the lodge. We return by boat after dusk, most likely to caimans; a crocodile cousin that lives in the Amazon. This region is home to the Black Caiman which is endangered, and almost always distinguishable along the rivers edge with its glowing orange eyes.</p><p>#4</p><p style="text-align: justify;">We leave at dawn for the return journey downstream. The mornings are the peak of wildlife activity, so keep a sharp eye on the banks of the river where we may see families of Capybaras and maybe be rewarded with a rare glimpse of a Jaguar or Tapir swimming through the stream. We arrive at Port Sandoval where we will walk to Sandoval Lake, which is protected by Tambopata Reserve. Along the trail there are birds and butterflies; and after 3km (2 miles) the trail leads to a narrow canal lined boats. This flooded forest leads to the open waters of Sandoval Lake. In the golden light of afternoon our crew will row the boats across to the lodge (motors are banned here). We can see the appearance of turbid lake while a huge Paichebreaks the surface (an Amazonian fish that can weigh up to 100Kg (220 lbs.). Or you may hear strange and unsettling screams and see heads peering from the surface of the lake, which will mark our first encounter with Pteronura brasiliensis, the Giant Otter of the Amazon. After dinner we can finish the day with a short night walk, spotting some nocturnal creatures along one of the trails near the lodge.</p><p>#5</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at dawn we cross the lake, perhaps finding a family of macaws leaving their roost or a troop of monkeys waking in the morning light. We will walk back to the river where our motorized canoe quickly leads upstream to the boat dock in Puerto Maldonado, and then on to the airport.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#1</span></p><p style="text-align: justify;">We pick you up early in the morning from your hotel and drive you to the train station to board the train that will take us to km. 104 (the starting point of the hike). After crossing the suspension bridge over the Urubamba River we visit the archaeological site of Chachabamba (2,150m / 7,052ft). From here, we will hike upwards for about 8km (approximately 4 hours) and get to the magnificent archaeological site of Wiñayhuayna (2,700m), an impressive complex made up of an agricultural center with numerous terraces, a religious sector as well as an urban sector. On the way we will appreciate the vegetation of the forest. After having the box lunch we will continue for about one hour further onto Inti Punku (The Sun gate) at 2,730m (8,792ft), from where you will have your first dramatic and impressive view of Machu Picchu (2,430m / 7,972ft). As we are going down, we will take a side path off the trail to take a bus that transports us to Aguas Calientes where you will check into your accommodation.</p><p>#2<br/></p><p style="text-align: justify;">After an early breakfast we will take the bus back up to Machu Picchu. A walking tour is given by a guide through the different sectors of this great Inca legacy, considered Cultural Heritage. In the afternoon, we will take a bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch and then board the train back to Cusco.</p><p><!--StartFragment--><span style="float: none;">------------------------</span><!--EndFragment--><br/>Level: Moderate<br/>Maximum altitude: Warmi Wañusca 13776 feet / 4200 m a.s.l.<br/>Walking distance: 28,59 miles - 46 km</p><p></p>
