<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">The employees of the lodge will pick you up from the airport and will transfer you to the Puerto Maldonado fluvial port, to take a 25-minute boat trip to the port of entry to the path that leads to Lake Sandoval. after trekking for about half an hour, you will arrive at the shores of the lake, from where you will board a canoe to be transferred to the Sandoval Lake Lodge. After checking in and enjoying lunch, you can board a catamaran to visit the Western side of the lake, and then the arrival of the macaws that approach to spend the night. There will be a night tour after dinner to look for black caimans, whose eyes stick up from the water and shine when then are lit up by the flashlights.</p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will visit the lake in the early morning. Lake Sandoval is actually a meander abandoned by the river and, due to its particular ecosystem of calm waters; it is home to an exuberant fauna, which includes, among others, giant otters, caimans and ´shanshos´, an exotic bird with a prehistoric appearance. After having breakfast, you will take a tour through the jungle to see wild chestnut trees, as this local nut represents an important income for native communities. There you will learn the harvesting, packaging and trading processes. You will then return to the lodge to have lunch and visit the lake to watch monkey species, especially the capuchin monkey. You will take a nighttime tour to watch the night fauna.</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will get up to watch the sunrise from the shore of the lake. Next, you will board the catamaran to explore the Western end of the lake, to look for a family of giant river otters, an endangered mammal that has found peace in these calm waters due to the absence of predators. Upon your return to the lodge to have breakfast, you will explore the neighbouring paths together with a resident naturalist guide. After lunch, you will have a free afternoon to rest or explore the surroundings on your own, and you will subsequently board the catamaran to watch the sunset from the water.</p><p>#4</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After having breakfast, a motor boat will take you to Puerto Maldonado and the employees of the lodge will assist you with your check-in to catch your flight.</p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">The employees of the lodge will pick you up from the airport and will transfer you to the Puerto Maldonado fluvial port, to take a 25-minute boat trip to the port of entry to the path that leads to Lake Sandoval. You will take a nighttime tour to watch the night fauna, among which stand out insects and reptiles, such as caimans, which stick out their eyes above the surface and shine when the flashlights light them up.</p><p>#3</p><div>After having breakfast, a motorboat will take you to Puerto Maldonado and the employees of the lodge will assist you with your check-in to catch your flight.</div>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Upon arrival to Puerto  Maldonado, you will be transferred to the pier. Enjoy  a  twenty-five-minute  motor boat ride down  the Madre de Dios River to the starting point of an exciting two-hour trek.  After arriving at a small channel, board a dugout canoe for a short trip to Lake  Sandoval.  The journey continues aboard a catamaran. After lunch, your vessel cruises over to the west side of the lake. Early in the evening, observe colorful macaws painting the sky as they fly to the spot where they spend the night. After dinner, canoe expedition in search of the impressive  black caiman.  Overnight at Sandoval Lake Lodge.</p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p>#2</p><p>After breakfast, return to Puerto Maldonado from where you will be transferred to the airport. Assistance on departure flight.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from your hotel to Callao. Get to know the district of La Punta on a 20-minute panoramic tour and then arrive at Monumental Callao: a space that promotes artistic and cultural development through art, dance, theatre and more. It has art galleries, craft and clothing stores that offer the work of local artists. Walk through these streets full of history, urban art, cuisine and local culture in a must-see route when visiting the Peruvian capital. Enjoy lunch at a local restaurant and return to your hotel.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Enjoy the Virtuoso benefits by receiving a welcome kit which includes: a passport holder, an 8gb data chip to stay connected and decorative Andean pompoms.</span><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">The Inti Raymi festival, which worships the Sun, is reenacted on June 24 of each year, during the winter solstice. You tour will start at the Coricancha, where the Inca and his imperial entourage will salute the Sun. From there you will start your trip to Sacsayhuamán (this includes a snack) where the ritual will be reenacted, including the simulated sacrifice of a llama amidst a mise-en-scène that has been rarely seen. This includes the participation of hundreds of Cusco residents who wear typical attires and accessories manufactured for the reenactment of the ritual. You will be transferred to your hotel at the end of this activity. <br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Arrive in Puerto Maldonado, the biodiversity capital of Peru. After a reception at the airport, ride a bus from the center of the city to the Capitania Port to embark downstream of the Madre de Dios River, toward the hostel. During your trip, you will see alligators, turtles, herons and other animals along the shore. When arriving at the hostel, you will be greeted with a delicious nectar of Amazon tropical fruits and taken to your comfortable room (bungalow). Once installed, a delicious lunch will be waiting in a cozy dining room of rustic design. In the afternoon, will be led by a specialized guide through a trail to the Cocha Cayman, a small lagoon where you will have the opportunity to observe both black and white caimans. At the end of the evening, witness the spectacular sunset in the Amazon, a great experience full of vivid and intense colors. Then return to the lodge for a delicious dinner and have free time to share unforgettable moments in the bar with an excellent game room and provided with all the drinks you can imagine.</div><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p></p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After a delicious breakfast, you will hike to the deep forest. During the walk, you will appreciate the variety of flora and fauna in the wild. Identify species of fruit trees, forest and medicinal trees that support animal and human populations in the Amazonian forests. You can also spot various species of mammals, rodents, primates, reptiles, birds and butterflies. You will reach the majestic Apu Victor Lake surrounded by immense swamps and gigantic trees. There you will find the Amazon Viewpoint (huge observation deck), from which you can enjoy a panoramic view over the lake and the various animals such as alligators, turtles, fish, otters, parrots, toucans. Back at the hostel, a delicious lunch will be waiting. In the afternoon, accompanied by an expert guide, you will visit Monkey Island located just minutes from the lodge. During the walk on the island, observe extraordinary scenery and exuberant flora that shelters a variety of monkeys in their natural habitat, such as the black spider monkey, capuchin monkeys, tamarins, squirrel monkeys and South American coati, as well as a lot species of birds. Finally complete your visit to the island, enjoying the most amazing sunset in the jungle. Back at the lodge, enjoy an exquisite farewell dinner.</p><p>#3 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">After a delicious breakfast, you will hike to the deep forest with a local guide. Walk through trails and cross temporary water courses called pipes, across bridges in good condition and will witness the widest range of flora and fauna. Observe a variety of ornamental plants of incomparable beauty and identify species of fruit trees, forest and medicinal trees that support animal and human populations in the Amazonian forests. There you can spot mammals like the jaguar or otorongo, tapir and huangana, rodents such as the giant otter, capybara, paca and armadillo; primates as the howler monkey and the puffin, reptiles like the iguana and the pallet; amphibians like the toad and birds as guan, partridge, golden parakeet and the red, blue and yellow macaw. Once in the Lost Lake (Cocha Perdida), will go up to a huge viewing platform. You will appreciate the huge area covered by this magical place where you will see two types of palm swamps: dense one and mixed one. Palm swamps are wetlands that provide shelter for many species of wildlife. In the afternoon you will have free time to take pictures around the lodge and also have the opportunity to visit the botanical garden. In the evening, enjoy a delicious dinner and then have fun in the bar and games room. </p><p>#4</p><p>After a magnificent breakfast you will be transferred to the Puerto Maldonado Airport.<br/></p>

When arriving at the hostel, you will be greeted with a delicious nectar of Amazon tropical fruits and taken to your comfortable room (bungalow). In the afternoon, will be led by a specialized guide through a trail to the Cocha Cayman, a small lagoon where you will have the opportunity to observe both black and white caimans. At the end of the evening, witness the spectacular sunset in the Amazon, a great experience full of vivid and intense colors. Then return to the lodge for a delicious dinner and have free time to share unforgettable moments in the bar with an excellent game room and provided with all the drinks you can imagine.</p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p>#2</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After a delicious breakfast, you will hike to the deep forest with a local guide. Walk through trails and cross temporary water courses called pipes, across bridges in good condition and will witness the widest range of flora and fauna. Observe a variety of ornamental plants of incomparable beauty and identify species of fruit trees, forest and medicinal trees that support animal and human populations in the Amazonian forests. There you can spot mammals like the jaguar or otorongo, tapir and huangana, rodents such as the giant otter, capybara, paca and armadillo; primates as the howler monkey and the puffin, reptiles like the iguana and the pallet; amphibians like the toad and birds as guan, partridge, golden parakeet and the red, blue and yellow macaw. Once in the Lost Lake (Cocha Perdida), will go up to a huge viewing platform. You will appreciate the huge area covered by this magical place where you will see two types of palm swamps: dense one and mixed one. Palm swamps are wetlands that provide shelter for many species of wildlife. Back at the lodge, will be waiting for a delicious lunch. In the afternoon, accompanied by an expert guide, you will visit Monkey Island located just minutes from the lodge. During the walk on the island, observe extraordinary scenery and exuberant flora that shelters a variety of monkeys in their natural habitat, such as the black spider monkey, capuchin monkeys, tamarins, squirrel monkeys and South American coati, as well as a lot species of birds. Finally complete your visit to the island, enjoying the most amazing sunset in the jungle. Back at the lodge, enjoy an exquisite farewell dinner.</div><p><br/></p><p>#3</p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">After a magnificent breakfast you will be transferred to the Puerto Maldonado Airport.</span><br/></p><p></p>

<p>#1 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Arrive to Puerto Maldonado, the biodiversity capital of Peru. After a reception at the airport, ride a bus from the center of the city to the Capitania Port to embark downstream of the Madre de Dios River, toward the hostel. During your trip, you will see alligators, turtles, herons and other animals along the shore. When arriving at the hostel, you will be greeted with a delicious nectar of Amazon tropical fruits and taken to your comfortable room (bungalow). Once installed, a delicious lunch will be waiting in a cozy dining room of rustic design. In the afternoon, will be led by a specialized guide through a trail to the Cocha Cayman, a small lagoon where you will have the opportunity to observe both black and white caimans. At the end of the evening, witness the spectacular sunset in the Amazon, a great experience full of vivid and intense colors. Then return to the lodge for a delicious dinner and have free time to share unforgettable moments in the bar with an excellent game room and provided with all the drinks you can imagine. </div><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p></p><p>#2 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After a delicious breakfast, you will hike to the deep forest. During the walk, you will appreciate the variety of flora and fauna in the wild. Identify species of fruit trees, forest and medicinal trees that support animal and human populations in the Amazonian forests. You can also spot various species of mammals, rodents, primates, reptiles, birds and butterflies. You will reach the majestic Apu Victor Lake surrounded by immense swamps and gigantic trees. There you will find the Amazon Viewpoint (huge observation deck), from which you can enjoy a panoramic view over the lake and the various animals such as alligators, turtles, fish, otters, parrots, toucans. Back at the hostel, will be waiting for a delicious lunch. In the afternoon, accompanied by an expert guide, you will visit Monkey Island located just minutes from the lodge. During the walk on the island, observe extraordinary scenery and exuberant flora that shelters a variety of monkeys in their natural habitat, such as the black spider monkey, capuchin monkeys, tamarins, squirrel monkeys and South American coati, as well as a lot of species of birds. Finally complete your visit to the island, enjoying the most amazing sunset in the jungle. Back at the lodge, enjoy an exquisite farewell dinner.</div><p></p><p>#3 </p><p>After breakfast, you will be transferred to the Puerto Maldonado Airport.<br/></p>
<p>Story tale Nasca The adventures of Achiq and the Golden Mask.<br/><br/></p>
<p>Story Tale: Machu Picchu de Dioses y Hombres<br/></p>
<p>Caral stories Adventures in the sacred city.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1<br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Upon arrival from Lima or Cusco, you will be welcomed at the airport and taken to the shelters headquarters in Puerto Maldonado. You will only take the necessary gear for the next days, and leave the rest at the safe deposit to keep the boats and cargo light. After travelling 20 kms you will reach the Tambopata River Port. Sail two and a half hours on a motorboat to Refugio Amazonas through the Infierno Native Community, and beyond the checkpoint of the Tambopata National Reserve, a wonderful 1.3 million hectares conservation unit. On the way, you will enjoy a box lunch. Upon arrival, the lodge manager will welcome you and give important navigation and security tips. After dinner, sail on a boat up the Tambopata River with flashlights scanning the shores with headlamps and flashlights to catch the red gleams of reflection from caiman eyes. Finally, you will return to sleep in the shelter.</div><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p>#2</p><div style="text-align: justify;">After a delicious breakfast, you will make a half-hour walk from the shelter that will take you to the 25-meter high canopy tower, which has been built upon high ground, therefore increasing your horizon of the continuous primary forest extending into the Tambopata National Reserve. From here you can see different species of birds such as toucans, macaws, and raptors. Then sail four and half hours by boat from the refuge to the Tambopata Research Center found in the middle of the reserve. After an hour and a half, arrive to the confluence with the Malinowski River, leaving human presence behind. There you will watch capybara, caiman, geese, macaws and other species of animals. Three hours from the shelter, deep in the Tambopata National Reserve you will stop at the Chuncho Claylick. After a brief walk, watch dozens of large macaws feeding on the special sodium-rich clays of the riverbank. The Chuncho claylick probably attracts more large macaws than any other claylick in the world and the sight of many macaws taking flight is truly unforgettable. However, the stopover will depend on the weather and the amount of macaw activity. There you will be offered a box lunch. Upon arrival, the lodge manager will welcome you and brief important navigation and security tips. A three to five-kilometer hike will lead you to observe magnificent views of the Tambopata winding its way into the lowlands. The forest on this trail is good for watching howler monkey and dusky titi monkey. After having dinner, scientists will provide an in-depth look at the biology of macaws, their feeding habits, the theories for clay lick use, their breeding and the threats to their conservation. Finally, overnight at Tambopata Research Center.</div><p><br/></p><p>#3</p><div style="text-align: justify;">At sunrise, you will visit the parrot clay lick where you can see several species of large macaws like Green-winged, Scarlet and Blue-and-gold, as well as hundreds of parrots that congregate on this large clay wall into a spectacle of colors and sounds which inspired a cover story in National Geographic. You will be located discreetly from very early in the morning, about 50 meters from the cliff. Then return to the lodge for breakfast. The next activity is a five-kilometer walk in a part of the rainforest with immense trees criss-crossed by creeks and ponds. Among the figs, ceibas and shihuahuacos, you will seek for squirrel, brown capuchin, and spider monkeys, as well as peccaries. After lunch, sail ten minutes upriver from the lodge where there is a small pond with a platform in the middle. It is a great place to spot aquatic birds such as ducks, herons and hoatzins along with woodpeckers, orioles, flycatchers and parakeets that have found their home around this pond. Return to enjoy a delicious dinner and then you will have the option of hiking out at night, when most of the mammals are active but rarely seen. Much easier to find are frogs with shapes and sounds as bizarre as their natural histories. Finally, you will return to sleep at the Tambopata Research Center.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p>#4</p><div style="text-align: justify;">On your last day, after having breakfast you will be transferred by boat from the Research Center to Tambopata River Port. From there you go to the shelters headquarters in Puerto Maldonado for a short stop and continue to the airport. Depending on departure times of flights, a very early dawn departure might be required.</div><p></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Get acquainted with Peru's Capital City, its 2000 years of history and Colonial heritage. Experience the artistic traditions and contemporary lives of its residents. Tour Lima's Historical Center* Visit Casa Aliaga, America's best preserved colonial mansion, which has been occupied by the same family since 1535. The Gold Museum, reveals the treasures in gold from a number of Peru's different pre-Columbian cultures.</div><p><br/></p><p>*UNESCO World Heritage Site</p><p><br/></p>
<p>You will be transferred from Huaraz to Lima by bus, approximately 8 hours.<br/></p>
<p>You will be transferred from Lima to Huaraz by bus, approximately 8 hours.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>You will be transferred from Lima to Huaraz by bus, approximately 8 hours.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Bus ticket round trip from Aguas Calientes / Machu Picchu / Aguas Calientes</p><p><br/></p>
<p>You will be transferred from your hotel to the Callao Port, where you will receive assistance to embark.<br/></p>
<p>You will be transferred from Huaraz to Lima by bus, approximately 8 hours.</p><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Arrive at ´Casona San Marcos´, a cultural centre and historical building where different artistic expressions like dance, theatre, music, and others take place. The visit begins with a briefing of the past and present of this important site and its surrounding areas. After this, a staging with characters from the colonial era wearing the traditional clothing from that time will interact with the visitors. Later, the guide will explain about the origins and tradition of Afro-Peruvian dancing, after which you will enjoy the performance of local professional dancers. After this demonstration, participate in a brief lesson to learn the basic steps of this typical dance.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">To get to Explora, you can be transferred from the Cusco airport to the Sacred Valley, or be transferred from your hotel in Cusco to the Valley. Enjoy four days and three nights at the charming Hotel Explora in the Sacred Valley. Located in a former corn plantation and located in a very quiet place in the Valley, where the guest will enjoy all the tranquility and rest options, such as the spa service, which this offers. Also, the traveler can enjoy exquisite dishes prepared especially to feel agile and light during their explorations, prepared with products of excellent quality that will give you the energy to travel throughout the Sacred Valley. The hotel offers excursions to make from the morning, where you can learn about the history, customs and other attractions of Pisac and its surroundings. These include hikes through Urubamba, Quenqo, Moray, Huarocondo, Las Salineras, among others; as well as bike rides that will take you to enjoy incredible views of the valley and archaeological sites. The excursions have a duration of half a day or of the whole day and it is guaranteed that in which you choose you will spend incredible moments.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">To know a place through its stories is to understand its culture and traditions. This storytelling experience accomplishes precisely this, through stories told in a playful and entertaining way. Enjoy this activity near the hotel, in a setting surrounded by trees and the clear sky. Participants will find blankets and comfortable cushions where they will sit to listen to the various stories of the coast, mountains and jungle of Peru around a bonfire. At the end of the storytelling, there will be a brief exchange of questions and answers after which the activity ends.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Lima is a privileged city as it has the sea right in front of it, and its proximity allows to practice different water activities. Today, navigate the entire Lima Bay on a tour where you will have a privileged view of the city and the Pacific Ocean. End this experience in front of the traditional Regatas Club, where you will observe the magnificent sunset over the city.</span><br/></p><p>----------------<br/><!--StartFragment-->Level: Easy<br/>Duration: 4 hours <br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Enjoy a delicious dinner at Doce restaurant.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Café La Paz.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Café La Paz.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">To know a place through its stories is to understand its culture and traditions. This storytelling experience accomplishes precisely this, through stories told in a playful and entertaining way. Enjoy this activity near the hotel, in a setting surrounded by trees and the clear sky. Participants will find blankets and comfortable cushions where they will sit to listen to the various stories of the coast, mountains and jungle of Peru around a bonfire. At the end of the storytelling, there will be a brief exchange of questions and answers after which the activity ends.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you are picked up from your hotel and transferred north of Lima, where you will live an off-road experience. Make a stop in the city of Huaral for breakfast. Continue to the Sacred City of Caral, where the oldest civilisation in America developed more than 5000 years ago. Get up close to this impressive archaeological site, its buildings and squares. Caral was declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO, and its discovery rewrote the history of what was known about life in America up to that time. Then, enjoy lunch at La Empedrada Lodge. Later, start the return to Lima, but first, go through a natural formation of sand known as &#34;El Tubo&#34;, and travel through the desert and its spectacular dunes in a 4x4 vehicle. Once this activity is finished, return to the highway and arrive in Lima.</p><p>----------------<br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="text-align: left;float: none;">Level: Easy<br/>Duration: 10 hours<br/>Maximum altitude: 1149 feet / 350 m a.s.l.</span><br/><br/></p><p></p>
<p>Transfer from the hotel to the bus station. Assistance on departure.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Go on one of the most spectacular trekking excursions in the Andes Mountain Range, towards Lagoon 69: a peaceful turquoise lagoon next to imposing snow-capped peaks. A true natural spectacle. Immerse yourself in the stunning scenery, culture and vast history of this area. Exit the city of Huaraz until you reach Cebollapampa, starting point of the walk. The lagoon is easily accessible and the road is full of impressive Andean landscapes. Once in the lagoon, you will have time to explore the surroundings and appreciate the scenery. You will have a box lunch after which you will start the return to the town of Cebollapampa. Once there, a vehicle will transfer you back to the city of Huaraz.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Take one of the most spectacular trekking excursions in the Andes, to lagoon 69: a peaceful turquoise lagoon next to imposing snow-capped peaks, a true natural spectacle. Immerse yourself in the stunning scenery, culture and vast history of this area. Exit the city of Huaraz until you reach Cebollapampa, starting point of the walk. The lagoon is easily accessible, and the road is full of impressive Andean landscapes. Once in the lagoon, you will have time to explore the surroundings and appreciate the scenery. You will have a box lunch after which you will start the return to the town of Cebollapampa. Once there, a vehicle will transfer you back to the city of Huaraz.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Depart  in  direction of Yungay for an excursion to Llanganuco, conformed by the  beautiful  lakes  of  Orkoncocha  and  Warmicocha, of intense turquoise waters,  located  over  3.800  meters above sea level and surrounded by lush forests  of  queñuales. Llanganuco is located in a narrow glacial valley and its  lakes  are flanked by mounts Huascaran (the highest in Peru, with 6.800 meters of altitude), Huandoy, Pishqo, Chapraraju, Yanapaccha and Chopicalqui of the Cordillera Blanca, the path of Maria Josefa and the Quebrada Demanda, forming  in  conjunction  the  Huascaran  National  Park  and  the Huascaran Reserve. On the way pass through the picturesque villages of Carhuaz, Yungay and Caraz. In the afternoon, return to Huaraz and the hotel.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up very early in the morning from your hotel in the Sacred Valley, to Piscacucho (Km. 82), from where the hike to the Inca trail begins. On the way, we will have a stop in the town of Ollantaytambo for 15 to 20 minutes. Once we arrive in Piscacucho, we will start with the trek until we reach our camp in Wayllabamba. Lunch will be served en route. This day we will walk between 5 - 6 hours (7.7 miles). In the camp, dinner will be served and we will spend the night.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, we will start with the most difficult part of the walk, which consists of ascending towards the highest point of the road, the Abra de Warmi Wañusca (4,200mts / 13,776ft). Then we will begin to descend towards our second camp in Pacaymayo, where lunch and dinner will be served, and we will spend the night. This day we will walk between 6 - 7 hours (7.2 miles).</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">This day is the longest, but the most impressive and interesting, due to the amount of archaeological complexes and cloud forest that we will find while we walk. From Pacaymayo, we will ascend to the Runkurakay pass, and then descend to Chaquicocha, to enjoy a delicious and refreshing lunch. At the end, we will continue ascending until we reach the Phuyupatamarca pass (3,700mts / 12,073ft), we will walk in the cloud forest until we reach our last Wiñayhuayna camp (2,679mts / 8,792ft). This day we walk between 8 - 9 hours (10 miles). In the camp, dinner will be served and we will spend the night.</p><p>#4 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Very early we will restart the walk after having breakfast, until we reach the Intipunku (Puerta del Sol), entrance door to Machu Picchu. With the first rays of the sun, we will appreciate in all its magnitude the historic sanctuary and then descend until we reach the citadel. For two hours, the guide will explain this Inca wonder, and then have free time to explore and enjoy Machu Picchu. At the end of the tour you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes. Lunch at? Café Inkaterra? Machu Picchu Town. In the afternoon, we will board the train that will transport us to Ollantaytambo and then be transferred to the chosen hotel in the city of Cusco.</p><p></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;"> Your passengers will have mobile connection with the LITO Data Card. These will be delivered at their arrival in Peru, and installed on their mobile phones in a quick and easy way. 8GB chips are available.</div><p><br/></p>
<p>Upon arrival in Huaraz,  assistance at the bus station and transfer to the hotel.</p><p><br/></p>

<p>Today, you will visit the snow-covered Pastoruri, the most easily accessible mountain in the Cordillera Blanca located within the Huascarán National Park. On the way you will pass through a forest of puyas de Raimondi, a plant species endemic to Peru and Bolivia that grows between 3200 and 4800 meters above sea level. The puja of Raimondi, relative of the pineapple, can reach 12 meters high and produce more than 5 thousand flowers during the inflorescence stage. They tend to concentrate on forests above 4,000 meters high, which is a spectacle rarely seen. Then you will arrive at Pastoruri, one of the most popular tourist attractions of Huaraz since its accessibility allows whole families to enjoy the snow that is on the glacier although this has regressed considerably during the last years as a result of global warming.</p><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Today, you will visit the snow-covered Pastoruri, the most easily accessible mountain in the Cordillera Blanca located within the Huascarán National Park. On the way you will pass through a forest of puyas de Raimondi, a plant species endemic to Peru and Bolivia that grows between 3200 and 4800 meters above sea level. The puja of Raimondi, relative of the pineapple, can reach 12 meters high and produce more than 5 thousand flowers during the inflorescence stage. They tend to concentrate on forests above 4,000 meters high, which is a spectacle rarely seen. Then you will arrive at Pastoruri, one of the most popular tourist attractions of Huaraz since its accessibility allows whole families to enjoy the snow that is on the glacier although this has regressed considerably during the last years as a result of global warming.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Today you will cross the magnificent Callejón de Huaylas, a valley fed by the Santa River and located between the Cordillera Blanca and Cordillera Negra Mountain Ranges. On the way, you will visit the towns of Carhuaz, Mancos and Yungay, and while ascending towards the Cordillera Blanca the mountainous landscape will become increasingly spectacular. The granite walls of Huandoy (6395 masl) and Huascarán Mountains (6768 masl) make up a ´U´-shaped canyon, with the two turquoise lagoons of Llanganuco. You will find yourself in the heart of the Huascaran National Reserve, a massive laboratory of natural history where it is possible to learn about the tropical species of the Andes and their ecosystems. You will visit the Orcocha and Chinacocha lagoons (3850 masl), where queñuales forests and Quishuar trees can be seen. After lunch, you will be transferred back to Huaraz.</div><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Depart  in  direction of Yungay for an excursion to Llanganuco, conformed by the  beautiful  lakes  of  Orkoncocha  and  Warmicocha, of intense turquoise waters,  located  over  3.800  meters above sea level and surrounded by lush forests  of  queñuales. Llanganuco is located in a narrow glacial valley and its  lakes  are flanked by mounts Huascaran (the highest in Peru, with 6.800 meters of altitude), Huandoy, Pishqo, Chapraraju, Yanapaccha and Chopicalqui of the Cordillera Blanca, the path of Maria Josefa and the Quebrada Demanda, forming  in  conjunction  the  Huascaran  National  Park  and  the Huascaran Reserve. On the way pass through the picturesque villages of Carhuaz, Yungay and Caraz. In the afternoon, return to Huaraz and the hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Visit to  the  archaeological  sites  of  Wilcahuain  and Antoaoco, remains from the  Wari-Tiwanacu (600-800 DC) period.  Here we observe funerary  chambers  built   in   various  levels  surrounded by the magnificent andean panorama of the Callejon de Huaylas Valley. Also visit to the  Regional  Archaeological  Museum, where  displays  ofpottery, mummies,stone sculptures can be seen. These artifacts show different periods of the  regional  development  with  cultures  as Recuay, Huaraz and Wari clutures. Ride  up the Cordillera  Negra to enjoy the magnificent sight of  the  Cordillera Blanca, the worlds highest tropical mountain  range  with  awesome  iced peaks between 18,000 and 22,000  feet  above   sea  level, deep   gorges, glacier tongues and alluvial valleys.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Lima is a privileged city as it has the sea right in front of it, and its proximity allows to practice different water activities. Depart from the district of Barranco and sail through the Lima bay, between Miraflores and Chorrillos, in a journey of 1 hour and 30 minutes where you will have a privileged view of the city and the Pacific Ocean. Choose between one of several daily departures in the morning or in the afternoon.</div><p><br/></p><p><span style="float: none;">---------------</span></p><p>Level: Easy</p><p><span style="float: none;"><span style="text-align: left;float: none;">Duration: <span style="text-align: left;float: none;">1 ½</span><span style="float: none;"> </span>hours </span></span></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will leave the city through Yura, a district characterized by impressive geographical features that include gullies, mountain ranges and canyons. You will enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain populated by vicuñas, native and tamable Andean camelids that are a national symbol. You will then go to Patahuasi, where you will enjoy a coca leaf tea, and then you will head to Vizcachani, the Toccra and Patapampa (4800 masl-meters above sea level) Pampas (plains, in Quechua), from where you can see the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa.  You will have lunch at the legendary Chivay town and in the afternoon you will rest at the hotel of your choice or you may optionally visit the La Calera hot springs.<br/></p><p style="text-align: justify;">#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will go to the Condor Cross, from where you may appreciate the Colca Valley (one of the world's deepest canyons with over four kilometers at its deepest side) to then  daydream: you will see the huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque towns, and the Antahuilque and Choquetico scenic viewpoints. After lunch, you may optionally visit the Coporaque town and the Chininia Colcas, incredible pre-Inca constructions on vertical natural walls. Then you will go to the Ocolle Scenic Viewpoint, the Amphitheater and you may take an optional short hike to the identified yet little explored Uyo-Uyo archaelogical remains. You will return to Chivay in the afternoon.</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p>#3</p><p><br/></p><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will return to Arequipa through Patapampa, the Toccra Pampas and Vizcachani, until you reach Patahuasi, where you will stop for to refresh yourself and enjoy a coca leaf tea. You will then continue to Pampa Cañahuas and Arequipa.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><div style="text-align: justify;">In Peru, many celebrations show the best of each region through dance, music and gastronomy. A festival that stands out for its tradition, beautifully framed by the Andes and the charm of a small town, is the celebration of the Virgen del Carmen in Paucartambo, Cusco. You will be picked up from your hotel in Cusco and transferred to Paucartambo, 110 km (68 miles) away. Enjoy the impressive Andean landscapes as you pass through the Huacarpay and Rumiccollcca lagoons. Continue the trip and make a brief stop at the Chullpas de Ninamarca, mortuary monuments in the shape of circular towers of up to 2.50 metres, built for important members of pre-Inca cultures. After this visit, you will arrive to Paucartambo, where you will have lunch. After lunch, visit the town and the Temple, the Site Museum, and the picturesque streets, while you learn about the history of this place. After dinner, visit the Alba dancers to witness a demonstration of typical dances. Finally, head to the camp, where you will spend the night. </div><div><br/></div><div>#2 </div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Today is the main day of the celebration of the Virgen del Carmen, so the inhabitants of the town and visitors from different places arrive in Paucartambo. After breakfast go out to the streets to witness the various activities that take place this day: dances, live music, the mass, among others happening in the streets and squares. After lunch, observe the Virgen del Carmen procession, which runs through the streets and the beautiful bridge of the town. This experience allows you to witness and participate in a magical celebration with a broad social meaning, where you can see first-hand the identity of a community in all its splendour.</div><p><br/></p></div>

<p>#1 </p><div><br/><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Arrive in the city of Chiclayo, capital of the Lambayeque region. These lands were the birthplace of the impressive Mochica culture, which is why it is currently one of the most interesting archaeological destinations in the</div><div style="text-align: justify;">country. Upon arrival, you are transferred to your hotel to rest and start activities the next day. </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div>#2 </div><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Start the day by visiting Túcume, an impressive archaeological complex formed by a vast network of pyramidal structures, royal tombs, stonework, houses and palaces whose origins go back to ancient Peruvian civilizations. Then head to Lambayeque to see the magnificent Museum of Royal Tombs of Sipan. After the extraordinary discovery in 1987 of several tombs full of gold ornaments, armaments and jewels of the Moche Culture, the museum was established to honour this civilisation. See many of the impressive objects, which are magnificently displayed to replicate the life and wealth of the Moche people, and especially its most famous attraction, The Lord of Sipan. </div><div><br/></div><div>#3 </div><p><span style="text-align: justify;"><br/></span></p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">Leave the hotel after breakfast and start your journey to Trujillo for a full day of sightseeing and adventure. On the way, make a stop at Magdalena de Cao and El Brujo Archaeological Complex. This was a very important place for the Moche culture and houses pyramids, an excellent museum and the famous Dama de Cao mummy. Continue the trip to Trujillo and go on a city tour through its essential buildings and colonial centres, which include, among others, the Main Square, the Archbishops Palace and the 17th-century Cathedral, which houses numerous Baroque works of art from Cusco and Lima. </span><br/></p><div><br/></div><div>#4 </div><div><br/></div><div>Start the day by witnessing the pyramids and petroglyphs of the Temple of the Sun and the Moon. Then visit the Huacas de Moche museum, located a few meters from the temples. Here, get closer to the Mochica world, learn about its traditions and history. Afterwards, you are transferred to Chan Chan; the largest pre-Columbian city in South America. This impressive complex of multiple citadels and urban settlements has an area of 36 square km and is one of the highlights of any visit to Peru. Finally, arrive in Huanchaco; an old centre of Moche Totora boats elaboration, and now a famous beach. At the end of this visit, you are transferred to the Trujillo airport.</div><div><p><br/></p></div></div></div>
<p>Departure from Machu Picchu station at 15:20 on the Belmond train (34), to Ollantaytambo station with arrival time at 17:08.<br/></p>
<p>Departure from Machu Picchu at 15:48 pm on the Vistadome train (304), to Ollantaytambo station with arrival time at 17:29 pm.<br/></p>
<p>Transfer from Poroy Station at 7:35 am. aboard the train Vistadome (31) to the station of Aguas Calientes (Machu Picchu Pueblo) with arrival time at 10:52 pm.</p>
<p>You will be collected from the bus station and taken to your hotel.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious <span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">dinner at D´Paso Hacienda restaurant.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/></p>

<p>#1<br/><br/>Arrival at Refugio Amazonas. To explore the Amazon closely, choose the activity &#34;a la carte&#34; according to your interest. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities.<br/><br/>#2<br/><br/>During the second day, continue exploring the Amazon, choosing an activity according to your interest. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities. Schedules are available at the lodge.<br/><br/>#3<br/><br/>After breakfast choose one of the various activities offered in the jungle. Some of them are walks through the woods, bird watching, among others. Take advantage of the day and choose the ones of your interest starting in the morning. Schedules are available at the lodge.<br/><br/>#4<br/><br/>The 4th day is the last day to explore the jungle and should be exploited to the fullest. Choose a new activity in which you have not participated the previous days or an experience that you want to repeat. Schedules are available at the lodge.<br/><br/>#5<br/><br/>Check-out of Refugio Amazonas.<br/></p>

<p>#1<br/>Arrive in the airport of Puerto Maldonado and begin your transfer to Posada Amazonas. The first stop will be at the hotel´s offices, five minutes away from the airport, where you can store your extra luggage*. From here, take a bus to the fluvial port in the Infierno community, 45 minutes away, from where you will take the boat for another 45 minutes until reaching Posada Amazonas. The Madre de Dios River flows along 1150 kilometers, crossing most of the national Manu and Tambopata National Reserves. The Tambopata National Reserve, which has an area of one million and a half hectares, has been the subject of several TV documentaries due to its exuberant flora and fauna, which makes it a unique region in the world. After arriving at the lodge and getting installed in your bungalow, you will take a short tour to get acquainted with the area that surrounds the lodge. Have dinner and then rest to make the most out of the next day.<br/></p><p><span>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges </span>of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p><br/>#2<br/>The 2nd day is the last day to explore the jungle and should be exploited to the fullest. Choose a new activity in which you have not participated the previous days or an experience that you want to repeat. Schedules are available at the lodge.<br/><br/>#3<br/>Check-out of Refugio Amazonas.<br/></p>
<p>Handing of metal bottle.<br/><br/></p>
