<div><span id="selectionBoundary_1550093338304_540837926389381" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel in Cusco to leave for Pallata, making a short stop in Ollantaytambo. The walk to Choquechaca begins with the ascent from the town of Pallata (3,100 masl) through several ecological floors that allow you to see the changes and colours of flora and fauna generated by the cloud forest phenomenon, where the humidity of the valley is retained and drizzles all year round. In Choquechaca (3,800 m.m.) you will meet the friendly families that live in this small town. Learn about their traditions and culture and then enjoy lunch on site. On the way back, take a detour to the archaeological site of Pumamarca, an Inca site with the largest quantity of terraces throughout the valley. From there, the bus takes you back to your hotel.</div><p><br/></p><p>Level: Challenging</p><p>Duration: 7 - 8 Hours <br/></p><div>Maximum altitude: Choquechaca 12467 feet / 3800 m a.s.l. </div><div>Walking distance: 4,3 miles / 7 km<span id="selectionBoundary_1550093338304_6396667844397734" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span></div><p><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Cabos Restaurant.</p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><div><br/><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Arrival at Tree House Lodge. To explore the Amazon closely, choose an activity &#34;a la carte&#34; according to your interests. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family and classic activities.</div><div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div>#2</div><div><br/><div style="text-align: justify;">During the second day, continue exploring the Amazon, choosing an activity according to your interest. Start your day early and choose the experiences of your interest. Schedules are available at the lodge.</div></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div>#3</div><div><br/><div style="text-align: justify;">The third day is the last day to explore the jungle and should be exploited to the fullest. Choose a new activity in which you have not participated the previous days or an experience that you want to repeat. Schedules are available at the lodge.</div></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div>#4</div><p><br/></p><p>Check-out of Tree Houe Lodge.<br/></p><div><p><br/></p></div></div></div>

<div style="text-align: justify;">The adventure begins with a transfer from your hotel in Cusco to the starting point of the trek to the impressive Palcoyo mountain range. Alike Vinicunca, the 7-colour mountain, Palcoyo acquires its colouration due to minerals such as iron, magnesium, calcium, among others, accumulated over millions of years, which now offer a visual spectacle. Unlike Vinicunca, the arrival to the Palcoyo mountain range is of a moderate and accessible level since it is a walk without great slopes, reaching a maximum elevation of 4,900 metres above sea level. Upon arrival, enjoy the beautiful view of the mountain range and take a few minutes to appreciate the surroundings calmly thanks to the lower traffic of visitors in the area. After this visit, start the descent and enjoy a picnic-lunch while continuing to enjoy the view. When finished, you are transferred back to your hotel in Cusco.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Around 100 km southeast of Cusco, rests one of the most beautiful wonders of nature: the 7-Colour Mountain. This place, also known as the Rainbow Mountain, has an altitude of 5,200 metres above sea level. You are picked up from the hotel early in the morning and transferred to Cusipata, where the hike begins. The trek is surrounded by beautiful sceneries of the Andes and guarded by the majestic Ausangate Mountain, one of the most important mountains of Cusco, considered a sacred entity since the times of the Incas. Once you reach the viewpoint of the 7-Colour Mountain, appreciate the surroundings from up high. After some minutes, begin your descend to Cusipata, from where you are transferred back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><div>Arrival at Tree House Lodge. To explore the Amazon closely, choose an activity &#34;a la carte&#34; according to your interests. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family and classic activities. </div><div><br/>#2</div><div>During the second day, continue exploring the Amazon, choosing an activity according to your interest. Start your day early and choose the experiences of your interest. Schedules are available at the lodge.</div><div><br/>#3</div><div><span id="selectionBoundary_1548883664267_10742432287256753" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>Check-out of Tree House Lodge.<br/><p><br/></p></div>

<p>#1 </p><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Pick up from your hotel during the morning to be transferred to km 60, north of Lima. Upon arrival, travel through the desert and its spectacular dunes in a 4x4. Once this activity finishes, breakfast is served en route. Continue the journey to the north and enjoy lunch at La Empedrada Lodge. Free afternoon to know the surroundings, rest or use of the hotel facilities. Dinner is served during the afternoon. Overnight at La Empedrada Lodge. </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div>#2 </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, head to the Sacred City of Caral declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO, where the oldest civilisation in the Americas, developed more than 5000 years ago. Explore up close to this impressive archaeological site and then start the return to Lima.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p></div>

<p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">The street photography workshop deviates from the traditional touristic circuit of Cusco to get closer to the less known corners of the city. The tour is guided by the talented Peruvian photographers Adriana Peralta and Miguel Palomino, who provide support and recommendations to obtain the best photographs. The journey lasts 3 hours starting at the Main Square and continues to Plazoleta Espinar, Plaza San Francisco and San Pedro; sceneries filled with colour and identity present in food stalls, people and local businesses. After this tour, the group returns downtown for a break in the imposing Coricancha Temple. The tour ends with a visit to the San Pedro market.</span><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Enjoy this transfer surrounded by beautiful Andean landscapes on the way from Arequipa to Puno. A private vehicle picks you up from your hotel in Colca. The first stop occurs at Patapampa´s Viewpoint, an elevated area on the highway from where several volcanoes can be seen. After a few minutes here, continue to the town of Patahuasi and drink a hot coca tea. The journey continues to Lagunillas lagoon, in the Puno region, where you stop again to observe the beautiful landscape and some of the 25 species of birds that live here. Return to the vehicle to travel the last section of the road and finally arrive in Puno.</span><br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes, arriving in approximately two hours. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu pueblo&#34; - the town of Machu Picchu - and is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you can find a train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer, reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca ruler Pachacuti. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the New7Wonders of the World. Once in the citadel, you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Return to Aguas Calientes for lunch at Café Inkaterra.</div><p><br/></p><p>*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).</p><p><br/></p><p>#2 </p><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and see the attractions of the citadel. Perhaps visit the Intipunku, the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. The sun rises every morning through this door if viewed from the most important buildings of the citadel. Then perhaps climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34; as opposed to the &#34;old&#34; Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is the Temple of the Moon, a complex of underground constructions likely to have been used by the elite. Finally, see the daunting Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and has a gap of several meters halfway which is joined by two large wooden boards. The system allowed the Incas to isolate the city by simply withdrawing the planks and thus remain protected. In the afternoon, head to the Aguas Calientes train station to take the train back to Ollantaytambo and start the return to Cusco from there. Check in to your hotel in Cusco upon arrival.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours time. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu pueblo&#34; and is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes are the train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacuti. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by the UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.</div><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p>#2 </p><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today you can return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions of the citadel. Visit the Intipunku, which in Castilian means &#34;Sun Gate&#34; and is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. It was kind of a checkpoint control and the sun rises every day through this door if viewed from the most important buildings of the citadel. You may climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34; as opposed to Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is  the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, a complex of underground constructions  likely to have been used by the elite. Finally you will be able to know the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and halfway has a gap of several meters above which were two large wooden tables.  Thus the Incas could isolate the city by simply withdrawing them and thus remain protected. In the afternoon head to the train station of Aguas Calientes and from there take the train back to Cusco.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours time. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu pueblo&#34; and is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes are the train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacuti. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by the UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.</div><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p>#2 </p><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions of the citadel. Visit the Intipunku, which means &#34;Sun Gate&#34; and is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. It was kind of a checkpoint control and the sun rises  every day through this door if viewed from the most important  buildings of the citadel. You may climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34; as opposed to Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is  the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, a complex of underground constructions  likely to have been used by the elite. Finally you will be able to know the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and halfway has a gap of several meters above which were two large wooden tables.  Thus the Incas could isolate the city by simply withdrawing them and thus remain protected.  In the afternoon head to the station of Aguas Calientes to take train back to the Ollanta station and finally to your hotel in Urubamba.</div><p></p></div>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Your tour begins with a train ride to Machu Picchu. In about an hour and a half reach the town of Aguas Calientes, also known as &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo&#34;. This is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. Finally you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. You may also optionally visit Intipunku, Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take (1) one small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p>#2 </p><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions of the citadel: the Intipunku (&#34;Sun Gate&#34;) from where the sun rises every day. You can climb the Huayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34;. Its ascent takes about an hour and along the way you can find the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, a complex of underground constructions probably intended for the use of the elite. Finally, visit the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone construction that served as an entrance or exit to the citadel. Transfer to the station of Aguas Calientes in the afternoon to take your train back to the Ollanta or Poroy station, and from there you will be taken to your hotel in Cusco.<br/></p>

In the morning you will be picked up by a transportation service that will take you to the dock at Los Órganos Beach where this adventure begins for 3 hours. Go sailing on a coastal boat ride from the dock to el Ñuro. On this tour you can contemplate the beautiful scenery of the area passing through Punta Veleros, the famous Enchanted Hill and Ñuro Bay to enjoy doing snorkeling with green turtles. During this activity you will be accompanied by a guide who will teach you to make the experience in a safe and respectful way with sea turtles: amazing animals of the Peruvian coast.

A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and transfer you to the Larco Museum, located in the traditional district of Pueblo Libre. It was founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926 and is located inside a colonial mansion built on top of a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century. In it you will find the most complete collection of pre-Hispanic pieces of gold and silver as well as erotic art. There are more than 45,000 archaeological objects that have been silent witnesses of three thousand years of Peruvian history. A guide of the museum will accompany you in the entire tour and once the visit is finished you will be taken back to your hotel.

<div style="text-align: justify;">The best way to participate in an activity is by interacting with the elements or the people that make it up. Complement the experience to the Ballestas, a group of islands that is home to birds, fish and mammals, with a conversation with a fisherman. He will explain to you about the elements he uses for his daily work and even about the fish that inhabit the sea of Paracas. He will also tell you about his boat and the traditional fishing activity in the area.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Feed your adventurous spirit with this experience that includes moments of adrenaline on board a 4x4. Head south on the Pan-American Highway in a vehicle driven by an experienced pilot. Ride the ups and downs of the huge dunes of the desert. Then, continue the journey to the town of Chilca, to slide through the sand in a fun sandboarding activity. At the end of this activity, start the return to the city of Lima.</p><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p><p>------------------------<br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="text-align: left;float: none;">Level: Moderate<br/>Duration: 4 hours </span></p><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour in the village of Chinchero, which was one of the most important populations during the Inca empire. However, as much of its annexes, it was also a victim of the &#34;extirpation of idolatries&#34; action that replaced the traditional deities by Catholic ones in order to transform popular beliefs. Then visit the archaeological remains of Maras, which is an impressive complex of salt mining. They are large deposits of salt that are used for exchange with other products with other regions of the empire. After lunch in selected restaurant visit the town of Ollantaytambo, a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited like in the past. You will find in here a great archaeological complex  called &#34;fortress&#34; although it was a &#34;tambo&#34; town for resting and lodging for long distance travellers. Finally return to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">This elegant restaurant, is located  at the northern extreme of the Miraflores  district,  takes  a  thoughtful approach to traditional Peruvian  cooking.    The  restaurant  has  several  dining  rooms, including a  piano bar  and  an enclosed terrace that overlooks the coast.  Many  of  the  dishes  come served in earthenware pots. The daily lunch buffet, with its almost overwhelming lineup of cold and hot plates, is the perfect way to sample a wide variety of Peruvian dishes, some spicier than others.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Transport is provided to take you from your hotel for a nocturnal tour of the Historic Center of Lima, a Cultural Heritage Site of the World inscribed by UNESCO in 1991, where the center of the Spanish colony was established in the sixteenth century and the first buildings were erected. See the San Martín Plaza, its balconies and old houses, the Main Square and the most important buildings like the Government Palace, the Cathedral of Lima, the Archbishops and Municipal Palace, Desamparados Train Station, among others. Finally, enjoy dinner and show in a restaurant, before returning to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Transport is provided to take you from your hotel for a nocturnal tour of the Historic Center of Lima, a Cultural Heritage Site of the World inscribed by UNESCO in 1991, where the center of the Spanish colony was established in the sixteenth century and the first buildings were erected. See the San Martín Plaza, its balconies and old houses, the Main Square and the most important buildings like the Government Palace, the Cathedral of Lima, the Archbishops and Municipal Palace, Desamparados Train Station, among others. Finally, enjoy dinner and show in a restaurant, before returning to your hotel.<br/></p>

<div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">A transportation will pick you up from the hotel and will transfer you to the Callao constitutional province. You will set sail to the Palomino Islands, located in front of the Lima coast, from the La Punta. On your way, you will stop at the San Lorenzo Island,which is eight kilometers long and has played an important role in the history of Lima. You will visit the Palomino Islands, two islands that are part of a special ecosystem, where an extraordinary fauna - composed of sea lions, local and migratory birds - coexists. The lack of predators makes it possible to dive with the lions and penguins at a short distance and in complete harmony. You will finally return among islets inhabited by guano birds and the second largest colony of Humboldt penguins in Peru, while you enjoy a snack.</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;"><br/></div><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">A transportation will pick you up from the hotel and will transfer you to the Callao constitutional province. You will set sail to the Palomino Islands, located in front of the Lima coast, from the La Punta. On your way, you will stop at the San Lorenzo Island,which is eight kilometers long and has played an important role in the history of Lima. You will visit the Palomino Islands, two islands that are part of a special ecosystem, where an extraordinary fauna - composed of sea lions, local and migratory birds - coexists. The lack of predators makes it possible to dive with the lions and penguins at a short distance and in complete harmony. You will finally return among islets inhabited by guano birds and the second largest colony of Humboldt penguins in Peru, while you enjoy a snack.</div><!--EndFragment--><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">The adventure begins with your transfer from your hotel to the starting point of the hike to the impressive coloured mountain range of Palcoyo. Like the 7-colour mountain of Vinicunca, Palcoyo acquires this colouration due to minerals such as iron, magnesium, calcium, among others, accumulated over millions of years and that nowadays offer a visual spectacle. Unlike Vinicunca, getting to the top of the Palcoyo mountain range is of a moderate level and very accessible since it is a 2-hour walk without significant slopes, reaching a maximum elevation of 4,900 meters above sea level. Upon arrival, enjoy the beautiful view of the mountain range and take some minutes to calmly appreciate the surroundings thanks to the little flow of visitors in the area in comparison with the Vinicunca Mountain. This experience is carried following the established routes and regulations for its conservation, collaborating with the sustainability of this natural wonder.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Start your tour with a walk through the districts of Barranco and Chorrillos. Then, a vehicle transports you to the pre-Inca religious centre of Pachacamac, located half an hour from the city of Lima. This archaeological complex originally belonged to the Ichma culture (although it was erected mostly by the Wari) and is located 30 kilometres south of Lima. The religious centre, which houses several temples in its interior in which Pachacamac was worshipped, remained in force for centuries until the arrival of the Spaniards. After lunch, a transportation service takes you to the city to visit the Love Park, located over the Miraflores cliffs, which boasts a spectacular view of the bay of Lima. From there, you are transferred to Huaca Pucllana, a 25-metre high pre-Hispanic truncated pyramid built by the Lima culture between 200 and 800dc. The complex houses plazas, ramps and staircases, as well as deposits of objects that were offered to the deities. Continue to the district of San Isidro to visit the El Olivar park, an old hacienda from the colonial era where olive trees were cultivated on a large scale and which over time became a public park surrounded by impressive mansions of the 20th century. From there, you are transferred to Lima´s historic centre, declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. Arrive at the San Martín Square, the scene of the most important social and political events of the 20th century Lima, and then continue towards the Main Square, the most important plaza in the country. From there, walk to the Church of San Francisco, one of the most impressive religious architectural complexes of the Viceroyalty built in 1546. It holds an astonishing collection of old books and a network of catacombs open to the public. At the end of your tour you are transferred back to your hotel. </div>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Start your tour with a walk through the districts of Barranco and Chorrillos. Then, a vehicle transports you to the pre-Inca religious centre of Pachacamac, located half an hour from the city of Lima. This archaeological complex originally belonged to the Ichma culture (although it was erected mostly by the Wari) and is located 30 kilometres south of Lima. The religious centre, which houses several temples in its interior in which Pachacamac was worshipped, remained in force for centuries until the arrival of the Spaniards. After lunch, a transportation service takes you to the city to visit the Love Park, located over the Miraflores cliffs, which boasts a spectacular view of the bay of Lima. From there, you are transferred to Huaca Pucllana, a 25-metre high pre-Hispanic truncated pyramid built by the Lima culture between 200 and 800dc. The complex houses plazas, ramps and staircases, as well as deposits of objects that were offered to the deities. Continue to the district of San Isidro to visit the El Olivar park, an old hacienda from the colonial era where olive trees were cultivated on a large scale and which over time became a public park surrounded by impressive mansions of the 20th century. From there, you are transferred to Lima´s historic centre, declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. Arrive at the San Martín Square, the scene of the most important social and political events of the 20th century Lima, and then continue towards the Main Square, the most important plaza in the country. From there, walk to the Church of San Francisco, one of the most impressive religious architectural complexes of the Viceroyalty built in 1546. It holds an astonishing collection of old books and a network of catacombs open to the public. At the end of your tour you are transferred back to your hotel.</p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Shared transfer round trip from the hotel to the dock.</span><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour by the modern Miraflores and San Isidro districts, the shopping centers of the Peruvian capital. You will tour the San Isidro Financial Center and Golf Course to reach El Olivar (the Olive Grove Park), a beautiful and huge area that used to be an old colonial plantation, in which olive trees were grown on a large scale. With the passing of time, it became a public space and today it is a beautiful park protected by impressive mansions of the past century. You will then continue to the Pucllana Temple in a panoramic visit, a 25-meter high pre-Hispanic truncated pyramid built by the Lima culture between 200 and 700 AD. The complex includes squares, ramps and staircases, in addition to warehouses of artifacts that would have been offered to the deities. Next, you will go to Limas Historic Downtown, a World Heritage Site listed by UNESCO. You will stop at the  Main Square, where Lima was first founded as &#34;The City of Kings,&#34; in 1535, and it was already known as the capital of the Republic of Peru when the country proclaimed its independence from the Spanish Crown in 1821. It is located in front of the Presidential Palace, the Archbishops Palace and the Lima Cathedral, and several colonial buildings. Next, you will go to Saint Francis Church, which has an enviable collection of old books and a network of catacombs open to the public. After visiting the church, you will be transferred to the traditional Pueblo Libre district, to visit Perus National Museum of Anthropology, Archaeology and History, founded in 1826, which covers the Peruvian history from the beginning of the local civilization until the republican times. It houses a huge collection of pottery, textiles and gold and silver artifacts from various Peruvian cultures. At the end of the tour, you will have the option of staying at the Indian Market, Larcomar, or returning to your hotel.</div>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#1 </span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Tarapoto is a city located in the northeastern jungle of Peru, known for its festivities and excellent cuisine. Upon arrival, you are transferred to the chocolate plant where the cacao grown by the local rural population is processed to obtain one of the best chocolates in the country. Then, head to Pumarinri Amazon Lodge, located in a privileged location on the banks of the Huallaga River. Enjoy your stay in this lodging surrounded by forests, rivers and streams. </span></p><p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#2 </span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">During the morning visit Chasuta, known as the &#34;chocolate jungle&#34;, and walk around the area to see the origin of cacao and taste its fruit. This place also has a strong identity and a rich cultural legacy, manifested through its ancestral pottery, preserved in the museum and workshop of Wasichay, where local artisans keep this tradition alive. On the way back, enjoy lunch and then participate in the Coca Route, an interpretative tour in the surroundings of Pumarinri that goes through streams that hide remains of a maceration pot of this plant. </span></p><p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#3 </span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Participate in a walk full of nature, landscapes and living culture towards Mushuk Lamas. Leave early to ascend the hillsides next to the Huallaga River until you reach the village of Mushuk Lamas. This place is surrounded by forests, under whose shadow grows one of the best coffee beans in the country. Between February and May the coffee plantations are in full harvest and the red color stands out among the green of the high forest. </span></p><p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#4 </span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Depart early in the morning towards the waterfalls of Pucayaquillo. Access to this wonderful place by a path of 1.8km in length where the visitor encounters a great variety of typical plants and trees. On the way, there are also different animals like frogs and isulas ants, known for their large size. Finally, get to the waterfall to take a refreshing swim in the crystal clear waters. Finally, you are transferred to the airport to take the flight back to Lima.</span><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Upon arrival at the airport, you are transferred to the river port to board the Amazonas cruise. Once there, you are welcome with a complimentary cocktail. After a safety talk, enjoy lunch. In the afternoon, visit the Kukama native community and learn about their customs, ancestral traditions, their music and dances. Return to the cruise for a well-deserved rest and during the afternoon participate in a cocktail class by the experienced bartender. Enjoy dinner at the end of the day. </div><br/>#2 <br/><br/><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, you are transferred to Isla de Monos (Monkey Island), an animal rescue centre. Here, find a paradise that houses different species of monkeys, which are rescued, rehabilitated and reinserted in their natural habitat. Return to the boat for lunch. During the afternoon visit the native community Yagua, where the local chief kindly shows you around. Put your aim into practice with the pucuna, a blowgun that locals still use to hunt their food. Before leaving, participate in a planting ceremony, and collaborate with the forest´s reforestation. Back on the boat, participate in a cooking class by the experienced chef. After dinner, a local shaman visits the boat to perform a ritual and bless the rest of your trip. </div><br/>#3 <br/><br/><div style="text-align: justify;">Start the day early to appreciate the impressive jungle sunrise. After that, take a short trip to the picturesque town of Indiana. Visit its market and discover exotic fruits and the catch of the day. Return to the cruise for breakfast. Then, get to know Morphosapi, a butterfly farm located in the community of San Rafael where you can see a great variety of butterfly species and their distinctive colours. Return to the boat for lunch, while the cruise moves towards the Nanay River. During the afternoon, leave in the small boat to appreciate the magical sunset over the waters of the Momón River. After dinner, head to the third deck for a farewell toast of this memorable tour. </div><br/>#4 <br/><br/><div style="text-align: justify;">Check out and disembark from the cruise. A transport service takes you from the port to the Manatee Rescue Center, a space dedicated to the rehabilitation of these animals and conservation as a form of education. After this interesting visit, you are transferred to the airport.</div><p><br/></p></div>

<p>#1 </p><div><span style="text-align: justify;">Upon arrival at the airport, you are transferred to the river port to board the Amazonas cruise. Once there, you are welcome with a complimentary cocktail. After a safety talk, enjoy lunch. In the afternoon, visit the Kukama native community and learn about their customs, ancestral traditions, their music and dances. Return to the cruise for a well-deserved rest and during the afternoon participate in a cocktail class by the experienced bartender. Enjoy dinner at the end of the day. </span><br/></div><div><br/></div><div>#2 </div><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, you are transferred to Isla de Monos (Monkey Island), an animal rescue centre. Here, find a paradise that houses different species of monkeys, which are rescued, rehabilitated and reinserted in their natural habitat. Return to the boat for lunch. During the afternoon visit the native community Yagua, where the local chief kindly shows you around. Put your aim into practice with the pucuna, a blowgun that locals still use to hunt their food. Before leaving, participate in a planting ceremony, and collaborate with the forest´s reforestation. Back on the boat, participate in a cooking class by the experienced chef. After dinner, a local shaman visits the boat to perform a ritual and bless the rest of your trip.</div><div><br/>#3 </div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, disembark in the town of Orellana, a historical place explored since the XVI century. Once in the town´s main square, the guide explains the stories and legends of the community. Afterwards, board the small boat to spend a pleasant morning fishing, then return to the cruise for lunch while sailing back to the Yagua community. Have the afternoon free to relax and appreciate the surroundings. Try cocktails with local flavours and dine during the afternoon.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div></div><div>#4 </div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Start the day early to appreciate the impressive jungle sunrise. After that, take a short trip to the picturesque town of Indiana. Visit its market and discover exotic fruits and the catch of the day. Return to the cruise for breakfast. Then, get to know Morphosapi, a butterfly farm located in the community of San Rafael where you can see a great variety of butterfly species and their distinctive colours. Return to the boat for lunch, while the cruise moves towards the Nanay River. During the afternoon, leave in the small boat to appreciate the magical sunset over the waters of the Momón River. After dinner, head to the third deck for a farewell toast of this memorable tour. </div></div><div><br/></div><div>#5 </div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Check out and disembark from the cruise. You are transferred from the port to the Manatee Rescue Center, a space dedicated to the rehabilitation of these animals and conservation as a form of education. After this</div><div style="text-align: justify;">interesting visit, you are transferred to the airport.</div><p><br/></p></div>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, take part in an activity that invites participants to learn about the living culture of Ollantaytambo from their own point of view. You will do this through photography, with the assistance of a professional photographer. The activity begins with a presentation in the Ollantaytambo main square. Then, you’ll be transferred to the Pakaritampu pyramid to snap some shots of the excellent view of the landscape and the valley. Later, visit the town of Ollantaytambo, uninterruptedly inhabited since the time of the Incas, to explore its historical streets, meet its inhabitants and capture its colours, architecture and environment in your photos. Lastly, visit a chichería to share some moments with the manufacturers of a local craft drink, who’ll explain the history and importance of these centres in the Andean world. </div><p></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious box lunch from the Cicciolina restaurant.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Charge your batteries with breakfast at the hotel before leaving the city via Yura, a district characterised by its rugged scenery, hills and cliffs. Then enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive grass plain where vicuñas (an Andean camelid and a national symbol) can be spotted. Head through Vizcachani and Pampas de Toccra up to the crater of the Chucura volcano. From there, continue one last leg of the way to reach the Mirador de los Andes at Patapampa, a stunning viewpoint 4,800 metres above sea level with impressive views of the volcanoes surrounding Arequipa. Lunch is served in the town of Chivay and in the afternoon you can rest in the hotel.</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2 </div><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">O<span id="selectionBoundary_1553035942500_4323402001670291">&#65279;</span>nce breakfast is over, head to Condor’s Cross, from where you can contemplate the view of the Colca Valley (one of the world´s deepest canyons) and see the large, majestic condor soaring over the valley, only a few metres away from where you stand. Back in Chivay, visit the towns of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque, and in between snap some more memorable photos at the Antahuilque and Choquetico viewpoints. Then later on continue to Puno with a detour that takes you through Patahuasi. Along the way, observe the amazing stone forests and sky-blue lagoons populated by cranes that fill this area 4,000 metres above sea level. Arrive at Puno after this amazing journey and enjoy a well-deserved rest at the hotel.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Chuncho Restaurant.</p>
<p>Dinner at Casa Andina Standard Machu Picchu Hotel.<br/></p>
Lunch buffet in Ayasqa restaurant.

<p>#1</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Today you will travel the Peruvian highlands on a journey that will take you through wonderful landscapes in Cusco, Puno and Arequipa. During the morning, the train will leave Cusco in direction of Puno and will you have lunch on board. The first stop will be at the archaeological site of Raqchi, also known as Temple of Viracocha where Inca storages and parts of the main building can be seen. In the afternoon you will be served tea and enjoy the sunset in La Raya, border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by imposing mountains. Then you will have a cocktail and dinner on board. You will arrive at the station of Lake Titicaca around 10p.m.</div><p><br/></p><p>#2</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Witness the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and continue your journey. Breakfast will be served on board and after this you will visit the Islands of Uros, a set of floating islands made of totora, plants that grow in the lake. Then visit Taquile Island, from where you will have a wonderful view of the lake. Lunch will be served at Collata Beach, which is part of the island and you will enjoy a demonstration of typical dances and craftsmanship of the inhabitants of the island. Afterwards, you will be transferred to the station of Lake Titicaca. You will be served a tea and will continue the trip heading to Arequipa. After a cocktail, dinner will be served on board.</div><p><br/></p><p>#3</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">You will have breakfast in the Saracocha Lagoon in the Puno region and later you will visit the Sumbay caves in Arequipa, where cave paintings from the Paleolithic period representing human and animal figures can be  seen on the walls. Disembark in km. 99 to visit the Colca Canyon, more than 4 kilometers  deep in its deepest part. After this visit you will have lunch on board and take a panoramic tour that will take you through the main buildings of the &#34;White City&#34;. Finally, you will be transferred to Arequipa´s airport.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will travel the Peruvian highlands on a journey that will take you through wonderful landscapes in Cusco, Puno and Arequipa. During the morning, the train will leave Cusco in direction of Puno and will you have lunch on board. The first stop will be at the archaeological site of Raqchi, also known as Temple of Viracocha where Inca storages and parts of the main building can be seen. In the afternoon you will be served tea and enjoy the sunset in La Raya, border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by imposing mountains. Then you will have a cocktail and dinner on board. You will arrive at the station of Lake Titicaca around 10p.m. </p><p>#2 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Witness the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and continue your journey.Breakfast will be served on board and after this you will visit the Islands of Uros, a set of floating islands made of totora, plants that grow in the lake. Then visit Taquile Island, from where you will have a wonderful view of the lake. Lunch will be served at Collata Beach, which is part of the island and you will enjoy a demonstration of typical dances and craftsmanship of the inhabitants of the island. Afterwards, you will be transferred to the station of Lake Titicaca. You will be served a tea and will continue the trip heading to Arequipa. After a cocktail, dinner will be served on board.</p><p>#3 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will have breakfast in the Saracocha Lagoon in the Puno region and later you will visit the Sumbay caves in Arequipa, where cave paintings from the Paleolithic period representing human and animal figures can be seen on the walls. Disembark in km. 99 to visit the Colca Canyon, more than 4 kilometers deep in its deepest part. After this visit you will have lunch on board and take a panoramic tour that will take you through the main buildings of the &#34;White City&#34;. Finally, you will be transferred to Arequipa´s airport.<br/></p>

<p>#1 <br/><br/>Start your journey arriving in Puerto Maldonado, where our staff will welcome you and provide the necessary information for your stay. Navigate by outboard motorized canoe along the Madre de Dios River to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepción for approximately 25 minutes. Enjoy lunch with organic ingredients and native flavors. After lunch, take a walk through Concepcion Trails, inside property nature trails, where you will discover and learn about primary and secondary rainforest, its components, uses, human impact and its relevance for the world. At dusk navigate by outboard motorized canoe, at the Twilight River excursion. By sailing on the Madre de Dios River, you will learn about its ecosystem, Amazon creeks, nocturnal animals behaviour and the southern constellations. In the evening, enjoy dinner at Casa Grande´s dining room. </p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p><br/>#2 <br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">New experiences are ahead and after taking a nutritious buffet breakfast, get ready for a long trek through terra firma into the Tambopata National Reserve towards Lake Sandoval. Here you will understand why Madre de Dios is called the biodiversity capital of Peru, while learning about the Reserve´s conservation efforts and the lake´s formation. Navigate by dugout canoe across this mirror-like oxbow lake that is home to the endangered giant river otter (Pteronura brasiliensis), blue and yellow macaw (Ara ararauna),red howler monkey (Alouatta seniculus), black caiman (Melanosuchus niger), and one of the worlds biggest fresh water scaled fish, the paiche (Arapaima gigas). Back to the lodge for lunch, and later the INKATERRA CANOPY WALKWAY &amp; ANACONDA WALK await for you. Take a 20-minute ride by outboard motorized canoe to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway Interpretation Center where you will learn about its construction and the conservation projects of ITA-Inkaterra Asociación (NGO). Ascend one of the two 98-foot towers (30 m.) and cross the7 hanging bridges that connect the treetops at 91 ft. high (28 m). Enjoy the breathtaking vistas and get the chance to watch white-throated toucans(Ramphastos tucanus), woodpeckers, trogons, squirrel monkeys (Saimiri boliviensis) and the three-toed sloth (Choleopus hoffmani). After descendingkeep on feeling the incredible primary rainforest energy going along the 200-meter wooden bridge over the swamps (aguajales). You will learn aboutthe fauna and flora of this flooded ecosystem. At night get ready for an adventure during the RAINFOREST BY NIGHT excursion, where you will explore the Amazon rainforest that gives way to nocturnal animals who display intriguing behavior patterns, and enhanced senses as adaptation mechanisms. Listen to nocturnal living nature sounds, and penetrate this hidden world. End up your day with a pleasant dinner. </div><p><br/></p><p>#3 </p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p>After breakfast buffet, take a ride back to Puerto Maldonado by outboard motorized canoe. Arrive at the Butterfly House where you may wait for your flight back home or to your next destination, while visiting this beautiful BUTTERFLY HOUSE and learning about the fascinating transformation of these colorful insects.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a  train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu pueblo&#34; and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes are the railway station, a crafts market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. In the afternoon return by train.</div><p></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Enjoy a delicious dinner at Pachapapa restaurant.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from your hotel to Callao. Get to know the district of La Punta on a 20 minute panoramic tour and then arrive at Monumental Callao: a space that promotes artistic and cultural development through art, dance, theatre and more. It has art galleries, craft and clothing stores that offer the work of local artists. Walk through these streets full of history, urban art, cuisine and local culture in a must-see route when visiting the Peruvian capital. Enjoy lunch at a local restaurant and return to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p>#1<br/><br/>Your tour will start on the way to the Paracas National Reserve, two and a half hours south of Lima. Paracas is an extraordinary site due to the many attractions. It is a natural reserve of beautiful geological formations, impressive animal fauna and remnants of a pre-Inca culture that dominated the desert to install a successful society in adverse conditions. You will spend the night in the bay of Paracas.<br/><br/>#2<br/><br/>You will head to the port to sail to the Ballestas Islands, home to a large population of sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species found in the archipelago, an ideal place for their reproduction and coexistence. It is, in turn, an impressive natural scenery, the consequence of thousands of years of wind and water erosion. On your way back, you will visit the Regional Museum of Ica, which houses an important collection of pre-Hispanic remains that have been exhibited in different parts of the world. In the afternoon he will move to Nazca.<br/><br/><br/>#3<br/><br/>Nazca is a small city. Perhaps it would go unnoticed on a map if it were not for the mysterious lines engraved on the desert, which have given it worldwide fame. You will be transferred to the airport to board a plane to fly over the Nazca lines (subject to weather conditions). Then they will be transferred to the department of Arequipa after a lunch in Puerto Inca, an archaeological complex located on the seafront where it is believed that marine resources were administered for the empire.<br/></p>

<p>Trastienda restaurant, just meters away from the ocean. You will participate in a cooking and cocktail class where you will learn to prepare typical dishes and drinks such as famous Pisco Sour. Finally, you will have lunch viewing the beach and board the vehicle again to be driven to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">A transportation unit will pick you up from your hotel in Puno, to transfer you to the town of Chivay. The road is a magical journey through high plateaus, stone forests and sky-blue lagoons, where you will be able to watch typical Andean fauna such as alpacas, llamas, vicuñas and a diversity of birds such as caracaras and flamingos. You will make a stop at Patahuasi and its geological formations, where you can enjoy a coca leaf tea at a small restaurant and will meet the group with which you will visit the amazing Colca Valley and Canyon. Then, you will pass by Vizcachani, the Toccra Pampas and will skirt the crater of the Chucura Volcano to reach the Los Andes Lookout in Patapampa (4,800 m.a.s.l.), from where you can watch the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa. Then, you will have lunch at the town of Chivay and rest at the hotel of your choice or you can visit the local hot springs. </p><p>#2 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will go to the Condor Cross, from where you can see the Colca Valley (the second deepest canyon in the world with over 4 kilometers at its deepest side) and then you will be able to gaze at a huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the towns of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma, Yanque and several lookouts, from where you can watch the beautiful landscape of the Colca Valley and its terraces. After lunch, you will visit the town of Coporaque and the Chininia colcas, amazing pre-Inca constructions on natural vertical walls. From there, you will go to the Ocolle Lookout, the Amphitheater and will take a short hike to the identified, but hardly explored Uyo-Uyo archaeological remains. Finally, you will return to Chivay in the afternoon. </p><p>#3 </p><p>Today your will enjoy the last lunch in the Colca Canyon before you leave Chivay and head for the city of Arequipa. After traveling for approximately three and a half hours, you will be transferred to your hotel in the city of Arequipa.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Costa del Sol Wyndham Arequipa Hotel.</p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Enjoy a delicious lunch at Costa del Sol Wyndham Arequipa Hotel.</span><br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">A transportation unit will pick you up from your hotel in Puno, to transfer you to the town of Chivay. The road is a magical journey through high plateaus, stone forests and sky-blue lagoons, where you will be able to watch typical Andean fauna such as alpacas, llamas, vicuñas and a diversity of birds such as caracaras and flamingos. You will make a stop at Patahuasi and its geological formations, where you can enjoy a coca leaf tea at a small restaurant and will meet the group with which you will visit the amazing Colca Valley and Canyon. Then, you will pass by Vizcachani, the Toccra Pampas and  will skirt the crater of the Chucura Volcano to reach the Los Andes Lookout in Patapampa (4,800 m.a.s.l.), from where you can watch the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa. Then, you will have lunch at the town of Chivay and rest at the hotel of your choice or you can visit the local hot springs. </div><p></p><p>#2 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">You will go to the Condor Cross, from where you can see the Colca Valley (the second deepest canyon in the world with over 4 kilometers at its deepest side) and then you will be able to gaze at a huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the towns of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma, Yanque and several lookouts, from where you can watch the beautiful landscape of the Colca Valley and its terraces. After lunch, you will visit the town of Coporaque and the Chininia colcas, amazing pre-Inca constructions on natural vertical walls.
