Finally, you will return to Chivay in the afternoon. </div><p></p><p>#3 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today your will enjoy the last lunch in the Colca Canyon before you leave Chivay and head for the city of Arequipa. After traveling for approximately three and a half hours, you will be transferred to your hotel in the city of Arequipa.<br/></p>

<p># 1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up from your hotel in Puno for the trip to the Colca Valley.  On the way you will pass through Santa Lucía, Lagunillas, Imata and Patahuasi, on the Andean plateau, which includes petrified forests and sky-blue lakes more than 4,000 metres above sea level. After a journey of six and a half hours you arrive in the valley where you will spend the night. </p><p>#2 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Visit to Cruz del Cóndor to see the Colca Valley (the second deepest canyon in the world, more than 4 kilometres at its deepest part) followed by an astonishing sight: the great and majestic condor flying only a few metres</div><div style="text-align: justify;">away. In the afternoon you may visit the villages of Chivay, Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque, and the vantage points at Antahuilque and Choquetico.</div><p></p><p>#3 </p><p>Travel to the city of Arequipa. On the way you enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain where you may see vicuñas, the Andean camelid that is considered a national symbol, and pass through the district of Yura, characterised by its rugged geography of ravines, hills and canyons.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up from your hotel to start the trip to the Colca Valley. During the journey you will pass through the city of Juliaca, known for its commercial movement, and on the way you will enjoy great volcanic landscapes (stone forests) and lakes, in the area of Lagunillas where you will be able to see a lot of fauna of the Andes as alpacas, llamas, vicuñas and a variety of birds, including abundant Kara-Kara, flamingos, etc. After four hours of travel we will make a stop at Patahuasi, where you can enjoy a coca tea. At this point the transshipment will be carried out, which will join you with the group whith whom you will travel through the Colca Valley. Continuing with the tour, you will pass by Vizcachani, the Pampas de Toccra and border the crater of the Chucura volcano to reach the Andes viewpoint in Patapampa (4800 masl) from where you will see the different volcanoes surrounding Arequipa. You will have lunch in the mythical village of Chivay and in the afternoon you will rest in the hotel of your choice or you can visit the local thermal baths.</div><p><br/></p><p></p><p>#2 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">New day for visual treats. You will go to the Cruz del Condor, from where you can see the Colca Valley (whose canyon is the second deepest in the world with more than 4 kilometers on its deepest side), and the huge and majestic condor fly a few meters away. You will visit the villages of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma, Yanque and the viewpoints of Antahuilque and Wayrapunko, back to Chivay. After enjoying lunch, you will leave for the city of Arequipa arriving between 16:30 and 17:00 hours.</div><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>#1 </p><div><span style="text-align: justify;"><br/></span></div><div><span style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel in Puno and transfer you to the  Colca Valley. You will cross the Lagunillas and Imata areas in a magical tour that crosses high plains, stone forests and sky-blue lagoons above 4000 masl. You will enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain where vicuñas, native and tamable Andean camelids considered a national symbol, can be seen before arriving to the Colca, where you will spend the night. </span><div><br/></div><div>#2 </div><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">You will go to the Condor Cross, from where you can see the Colca Valley (which is one of the world?s deepest canyons with over four kilometers at its deepest side) and then you will daydream: you will see the huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque towns, and the Antahuilque and Choquetico scenic viewpoints. You will finally return to Arequipa.</div><div><p><br/></p></div></div>

<p>#1 </p><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up from your hotel to start the trip to the Colca Valley. During the journey you will pass through the city of Juliaca, known for its commercial movement, and on the way you will enjoy great volcanic landscapes (stone forests) and lakes, in the area of ??Lagunillas where you will be able to see a lot of fauna of the Andes as alpacas, llamas, vicuñas and a variety of birds, including abundant Kara-Kara, flamingos, etc. After four hours of travel we will make a stop at Patahuasi, where you can enjoy a coca tea. At this point the transshipment will be carried out, which will join you with the group whith whom you will travel through the Colca Valley. Continuing with the tour, you will pass by Vizcachani, the Pampas de Toccra and border the crater of the Chucura volcano to reach the Andes viewpoint in Patapampa (4800 masl) from where you will see the different volcanoes surrounding Arequipa. You will have lunch in the mythical village of Chivay and in the afternoon you will rest in the hotel of your choice or you can visit the local thermal baths.</div><br/>#2 </div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">New day for visual treats. You will go to the Cruz del Condor, from where you can see the Colca Valley (whose canyon is the second deepest in the world with more than 4 kilometers on its deepest side), and the huge and</div><div style="text-align: justify;">majestic condor fly a few meters away. You will visit the villages of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma, Yanque and the viewpoints of Antahuilque and Wayrapunko, back to Chivay. After enjoying lunch, you will leave for the city of Arequipa arriving between 16:30 and 17:00 hours.</div><p><br/></p></div>
<p>Almuerzo en el Restaurante Qincha<br/></p>

<p></p><p>Enjoy a journey from Cusco to Puno passing through some of the most amazing landscapes of the Peruvian highlands. The train has a capacity for 132 passengers and 4 dining cars, a car bar and an observatory car. Along the way you will enjoy a gourmet lunch prepared with exquisite Andean ingredients and there will be music and dance shows on board.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/><!--EndFragment--></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Train tickets Hiram Bingham (one way) and Vistadome (return) to Machu Picchu for full day tour. From/to the Poroy station (from may to december).</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Train tickets Hiram Bingham (one way-from Poroy) and Vistadome (return-from Ollanta) to Machu Picchu for full day tour. <br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Train tickets to Machu Picchu in Expedition service for a full day tour. From / to Poroy station.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Train tickets to Machu Picchu in Expedition service for a </span><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">2d/1n package</span><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">. From / to Poroy station.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p style="font-size: 12px;"><span style="text-align: justify;">A transport mobility will pick you up from your hotel and take you to the Ollantaytambo station, where your trip to Machu Picchu begins with a train trip to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours. This city is also known as Machu Picchu Pueblo and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you will find the railway station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories, for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and be able to climb early. Finally, after a short transfer you arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and refuge for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1471). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you can also visit, optionally, Intipunku (Puerta del Sol), Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Puente de Inca. In the afternoon return by train</span><br/></p><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p>You will start your tour en route to the Pisac market, which has scores of stands that exhibit and sell products manufactured by the residents of the area everyday. Beautiful alpaca textiles, musical instruments, paintings, carvings, necklaces and replicas of old Inca pieces stand out. You will continue to the Pisac archaeological vestiges, which as the largest complex of terraces in the area and a masterpiece of the agriculture of the Inca Empire. Next, you will have lunch at the previously agreed restaurant and then you will visit the Ollantaytambo Park, making a series of tops at points of interest. The Ollantaytambo town, a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas, whose residents still live in accordance with their old traditions. Pachacútec (1438 1471) conquered the town, destroyed it and built a new one, absorbing it as part of his empire. It exhibits one of the most remarkable complexes of the Inca Empire. After lunch at Rancho Wayra you will return to your hotel.<br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Visit the community of Willoc.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Complement the experience of visiting the highlands in the company of a shaman, a master who has deep knowledge of the mysticism of the Andes. The shaman performs a ´payment to the earth´ ceremony, coca leaf reading or both, during the visit that is carried out. The coca leaf reading can also be done at the hotel in Cusco. Get closer to the Andean spirit with the help of the shaman through these millenary traditions.<br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Enjoy an outdoor picnic and a beautiful view in Tiobamba in the Sacred Valley of the Incas. Start with a welcome drink and try the variety of appetisers at your disposal, all made with fresh ingredients grown in the region like quinoa, potato, corn and much more. Finish the delicious lunch with a dessert and coffee or tea.</span><br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will take your luggage to Cusco City<br/></p>
<p>Private transfer from Ollanta station to the hotel in Sacred Valley<br/></p>
<p>Private transfer from the Ollanta station to the hotel in Cusco<br/></p>

<p>During the morning you will head towards the picturesque neighbourhood of San Blas, specifically to the house of Sabino, a talented Andean musician. He will welcome you and explain the history of the typical Andean<br/>instruments. You will be able to learn and play the drums, ¨quenas¨ (Andean flutes), among others. Also, not only will you play the most representative Andean instrument, the zampoña (pan flute), but you will make one. Finally, you will play together like the local bands.<br/></p>

<p>A transportation service accompanied by a transfer person, will pick you up from your hotel to take you to dinner to the chosen restaurant. Upon arrival, the transfer person will help you accommodate and coordinate the<br/>pick up time. Finally, you will be taken back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;"><span id="selectionBoundary_1516117767736_051092968605494615" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>Additional transfer from/to the airport (Includes a representative) </span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/></p>
<p>Private transfer from the hotel to the airport with representative<br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;"></span>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel in Cusco and take you to your hotel in the Sacred Valley.<br/><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">A mobility will pick you up from your hotel at the scheduled time and you will be taken to the train station. A guide will accompany you to the boarding gate and on the way will explain your trip to Puno, the highland city that has Titicaca, the highest navigable lake in the world.<br/></p>

<p>One-way transfer from Ollanta station at 10:30 am on the Sacred Valley train (91), to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 13:34 pm. Departure from Machu Picchu at 7:30 p.m. Sacred Valley Train (92) towards Olllanta at 21:00 p.m.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Melia hotel.</p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Mantra Indian Cuisine.</p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Dinner at Kingdom Hotel.</span><br/></p>

<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Alfresco Restaurant which has great experience cooking the best seafood dishes, staying true to Peruvian tradition, thanks to renowned chef Jose Alredo and his team.<br/></p>

<p>Enjoy a delicious, varied, light and homemade lunch at Museo Larco Cafe. Located on the terrace in front of beautiful gardens, discover the best Peruvian and international cuisine in a cosy<span id="selectionBoundary_1543267894326_9585774509112754" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span> atmosphere.</p>
<p>Additional mobility will be at your disposal to transfer you to the airport or from the airport to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p><span style="text-align: justify;">We start the bike route in the Miraflores district and head to the seafront where we will see the spectacular view of the bay of Lima. Then we will go to taste a delicious and exquisite marine menu. We continue to delight in more local delicacies, one of the main dishes of Peru: our &#34;Causa peruana&#34;. Continue the adventure in the third restaurant, where we will try the &#34;lomo saltado&#34; or a special dish of Peruvian delicatessen. Then, you can choose between an artisan ice cream or &#34;picarones&#34; (Peruvian doughnuts) made of sweet potato, pumpkin, cloves and cinnamon, sweet and delicious. Finally, some craft beers will be waiting for us for the return.</span><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><br/></span></div><p style="text-align: justify;"><!--StartFragment-->You will be picked up from your hotel and transferred by the Pan-American Highway en route to the South toward Pachacamac. Located thirty minutes from Lima City, this archaeological complex, built in 200 AD, is one of the most important Pre-Inca religious centers on the Peruvian coast. It originally belonged to the Ichma culture (although it was mostly built by the Wari people and subsequently conquered by the Incas); it was a place where Pachacámac, the god who created the world, was worshipped. The religious center, which houses several temples, operated for centuries until the arrival of the Spaniards, when the fall of the Inca Empire finally started. You will return to your hotel at the end of the tour.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Enjoy a bicycle group tour through the city. This journey starts on Larco Avenue, located across from the well-known Kennedy Park, from where you will continue along Arequipa Avenue. Make the first stop in the market of Surquillo; have a fresh fruit juice and explore the immense variety of local products. You will continue with the route to the Huaca Pucllana, a pre-Inca ceremonial neighbourhood in the middle of the district of Miraflores, and later you will visit El Olivar Forest, a beautiful and historical recreation area in the district of San Isidro that has around 1600 olive trees. Afterwards, enjoy a drink with a view of the Pacific Ocean from the Miraflores waterfront near the Larcomar shopping centre, from where you will return to Larco Avenue to finish the tour.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/>-------------------<br/><!--StartFragment-->Level: Easy<br/>Duration: 3 hours approximately<br/><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour starts downtown, where you will visit the Aliaga House, an ancient viceregal house delivered by Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga) after the foundation of the city. The construction of this house started in 1535. This genuine jewel of the colonial architecture is currently inhabited by his descendants. You will then get to know the Main Square, where Lima was first founded as &#34;The City of Kings,&#34; in 1535, and it was already known by the name of Lima when Peru proclaimed its independence from the Spanish Crown in 1821. There you can see (panoramic view) the Presidential Palace, the Archbishops Palace and the Lima Cathedral. Next, you will go to Saint Francis Church, which has an enviable collection of old books and an authentic network of catacombs open to the public. Then you will go to the Pueblo Libre district to visit the Larco Museum, located in the same district, founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926. The visit to this museum allows understanding over five thousand years of the high degree of development achieved by the cultures that flourished before the Inca Empire. The museum, located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century, shows the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver artifacts as well pieces of erotic art.</div><p></p>

<p>During the morning you will be picked up from your hotel and transferred to the district of Barranco. From there, you will start the kayak trip towards Chorrillos, a traditional fishing district, to the south of the city. Lima is the only capital of South America located on the seafront and the fusion of nature and city make this a unique experience. During the trip you will appreciate the immensity of the sea as well as the enormous city that rises on the imposing cliffs. If you are lucky you will appreciate some marine fauna. After approximately 5km, you will return to the shore and be transferred to your hotel.<br/></p><p><br/></p><p>-----------------</p><p><br/></p><p><span style="text-align: left;float: none;">Level: Easy</span></p><p><br/></p><p><span style="text-align: left;float: none;">Duration: 3 ½ hours approximately</span></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Today you can choose what you most want to know about the city of Lima and have a guide available to accompany and guide you for three hours.<br/></p>
A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel at the established time and will transfer you to the bus station.
<p><span style="font-size: 11pt;"></span>Additional pick up &amp; drop off of equipment for biking tours.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;"></span><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Day 1</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Visitors will embark on one of the most incredible routes of the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful Andean Explorer. The train has 2 dining cars, a lounge car and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the mountains. It also has elegant suites and cabins where the passenger can rest comfortably. The crossing begins leaving Cusco in the direction of Puno. You will enjoy a delicious lunch and then make the first stop in Raqchi; admirable archaeological site with remains of Temples and Inca constructions. The trip will continue and after tea time, you will be delighted with the sunset at La Raya, the border between Cusco and Puno, surrounded by imposing mountains. Dinner will be served on board and you will arrive at the Lake Titicaca station at night.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Day 2</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Enjoy the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and breakfast will be served on board. During the rest of the day you can optionally choose between visiting the Uros Islands in a typical boat, visiting the Taquile Island, having lunch in a private area on the beach or show of typical dances and demonstration of handicrafts.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Visitors will embark on one of the most amazing routes in the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful ¨Andean Explorer¨. The train has two dining cars, lounge wagon and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the Sierra. It also has elegant suites and cabins where passengers can rest comfortably. The journey begins leaving Cusco towards Puno. Passengers will enjoy a delicious lunch and then make the first stop in Raqchi; admirable archaeological site with remains of Inca temples and buildings. The journey will continue and after teatime, be amazed by a breathtaking sunset at La Raya, the limit between Cusco and Puno, surrounded by towering mountains. Dinner will be served in the train and you will reach Lake Titicaca station at night.<br/></p><p>#2<br/></p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">You will enjoy the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and breakfast will be served on board. During the day you can optionally choose to visit the Uros Islands on a typical boat, visit the Taquile Island, lunch in a private area on the beach or a show of traditional dances and crafts demonstration.</span></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Enjoy lunch with the original flavors of the Imperial City; with a contemporary and stylish proposal, using local products, Chullpi Machu Picchu guarantees a unique culinary experience.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour with a visit to the Coricancha, also known as the &#34;Temple of the Sun&#34; (or &#34;Premises of the Gilded One&#34;), the construction of which was ordered by Inca Pachacutec (1438-1471) and was used as base for the construction of the Santo Domingo Convent upon the arrival of the Spaniards. This is when the Sun (Inti, in Quechua) was worshipped during the Inca Empire and at the time of the Incas, its walls were fully covered with gold sheets and its courtyards were planted with golden corn and life-size llamas. You will then go to the Cathedral, the most important building on the Main Square and one of the most splendid Spanish colonial churches in America, which is shaped as a Latin Cross and houses about 400 colonial paintings from the Cusco art school, and impressive goldsmith, silversmith and precious stones works. Its construction lasted almost a century; it started in 1560 and ended in 1654. The Ecclesiastical Chapter ordered to extract and transport hundreds of stones from the Sacsayhuaman fortress for this purpose.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at La Casona restaurant.</p>

<div><p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes, arriving in approximately two hours. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu pueblo&#34; - the town of Machu Picchu - and is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you can find a train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer, reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca ruler Pachacuti. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the New7Wonders of the World. Once in the citadel, you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability)  and the Inca Bridge. Return to Aguas Calientes for lunch at Café Inkaterra.</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).. </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2 </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and see the attractions  of the citadel. Perhaps visit the Intipunku, the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from  Ollantaytambo. The sun rises</div></div><div style="text-align: justify;">every morning through this door if viewed from the most important buildings of the citadel. Then perhaps climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34; as opposed to the &#34;old&#34; Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is the Temple of the Moon, a complex of underground constructions likely to have been used by the elite. Finally, see the daunting Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and has a gap of several meters halfway which is joined by two large wooden boards. The system allowed the Incas to isolate the city by simply withdrawing the planks and thus remain protected. In the afternoon, head to the Aguas Calientes train station to take the train back to Ollantaytambo and start the return to Cusco from there. Check in to your hotel in Cusco upon <span style="text-align: justify;">arrival.</span><p></p></div>
<p>Train tickets to Machu Picchu in Inca Rail First Class for a 2d/1n package. From / to Ollanta station.<br/></p>
<p>Ticket de tren en servicio Voyager (Frecuencia 44)<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">This  classic  old-style cafe,  a  Lima institution  since 1937, is decorated  with  cool  enamel doors  and  staffed  by waitresses in quaint red-and-white dresses.  Its  best  known  for its exquisite pastries,  cakes,  breads,  and gourmet food shop,  but &#34;the little white store&#34; is  also  a good little restaurant, perfect to enjoy a variety of gourmet dishes.<br/></p>
<p>Departure from Ollantaytambo station at 7:45 am on the Expedition train (83), to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 9:15 am.</p><p><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Learn to prepare some of the most representative cocktails of Peru in a participatory class at the elegant Hotel B. The class is carried out by an experienced bartender who explains the history of the drinks and the elements used for each one. Each cocktail will be accompanied by a tasty pairing. Take advantage of this experience to learn about local cocktails in this luxurious hotel, located in a mansion of the early twentieth century.     </div><p></p>
<p>Departure from Ollantaytambo station at 10:32 am pm on the Vistadome train (203), to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 12:11 pm.<br/></p>

<p>Your passengers will have mobile connection with the LITO Data Card. These will be delivered at their arrival in Peru, and installed on their mobile phones in a quick and easy way. 8GB chips are available.</p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">Arrival at Refugio Amazonas. To explore the Amazon closely, choose the activity &#34;a la carte&#34; according to your interest. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities.</span></p><p></p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p></p><p>#2</p><div><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">The 2nd day is the last day to explore the jungle and should be exploited to the fullest. Choose a new activity in which you have not participated the previous days or an experience that you want to repeat. Schedules are available at the lodge.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/>#3</div><div><br/></div><div>Check-out of Refugio Amazonas.<br/></div><div><p></p></div>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel in Puno and transfer you to the  Colca Valley. You will cross the Lagunillas and Imata <span id="selectionBoundary_1542226458284_12280627293544977" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>areas in a magical tour that crosses high plains, stone forests and sky-blue lagoons above 4000 masl. You will enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain where vicuñas, native and tamable Andean camelids considered a national symbol, can be seen before arriving to the Colca, where you will spend the night. </p><p>#2 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will go to the Condor Cross, from where you can see the Colca Valley (which is one of the worlds deepest canyons with over four kilometers at its deepest side) and then you will daydream: you will see the huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque towns, and the Antahuilque and Choquetico scenic viewpoints. You will finally return to Arequipa.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel in the Sacred Valley and transferred to the ´Potato Park´. In this place, diverse Andean communities preserve hundreds of native potato varieties, using knowledge and traditional farming techniques inherited from their ancestors. Tour the area and learn about the communities and the agricultural legacy that is kept alive. Finally, you are transferred to your hotel in the city of Cusco.</div><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Complement the experience of visiting the highlands in the company of a shaman, a master who has deep knowledge of the mysticism of the Andes. The shaman performs a ´payment to the earth´ ceremony, coca leaf reading or both, during the visit that is carried out. The coca leaf reading can also be done at the hotel in the Sacred Valley. Get closer to the Andean spirit with the help of the shaman through these millenary traditions.</div><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve by skirting the volcanoes that surround Arequipa City. This is a broad plain populated by vicuñas, native and tamable Andean camelids that are a national symbol. You will then visit Vizcachani, the Toccra Pampas and will skirt the crater of the Chucura Volcano to reach the Los Andes Scenic Viewpoint in Patapampa (4910 masl), from where you can see the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa. You will enjoy lunch at the legendary Chivay town and in the afternoon you will rest at the hotel of your choice or you may visit the La Calera hot springs. </p><p>#2 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will visit the Condor Cross, from where you may appreciate the Colca Valley (which is one of the world's deepest canyons with over four  kilometers at its deepest side) to then daydream: you will see the huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque towns, and the Antahuilque and Choquetico scenic viewpoints. You will return to Arequipa after lunch.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Participate in this demonstrative experience in the cosy atmosphere of the Pisco Museum, located in the centre of Miraflores, with a privileged view to the district´s main park. Learn about the history, the varieties and the culture around this national drink. The activity of approximately one hour includes a demonstration of the preparation of a cocktail by an experienced bartender and the tasting of tapas, to accompany the drink. You can choose between one tapas option and one cocktail option.  Take advantage of this activity to complement and close the day after your previous visits in the city. This experience is recommended for private tours.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p>Departure from Ollantaytambo station at 10:32 am pm on the Vistadome train (203), to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 12:11 pm. You will return from the station of Aguas Calientes (Machu Picchu Pueblo) at 16:43 pm. aboard the train Vistadome (32), to Poroy Station with arrival time at 20:23 pm.</p>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">You will leave the city through Yura, a district characterized by impressive geographical features that include gullies, mountain ranges and canyons. You will enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain populated by vicuñas, native and tamable Andean camelids that are a national symbol. You will then visit Vizcachani, the Toccra Pampas and will skirt the crater of the Chucura Volcano to reach the Los Andes Scenic Viewpoint in Patapampa (4800 masl) from where you can see the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa. You will have lunch at the Chivay town and in the afternoon you will rest at the hotel of your choice or you may visit the local hot springs.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">A new day for visual delights. You will go to the Condor Cross, from where you may appreciate the Colca Valley (one of the world?s deepest canyons with over four kilometers at its deepest side)  and you will see the you will see the huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque towns, and the Antahuilque and Choquetico scenic viewpoints. From there, you will go to Puno City through the detour to Patahuasi. You will daydream once again: stone forests, sky blue lagoons populated by cranes above 4 masl. You will arrive in Puno after a 6-hour trip.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Drive  to Barranco and stroll around this quaint  and traditional  district,considered to be the city´s most  romantic and bohemian, being the home  and working  place  of many of  Peru's leading artists, musicians, designers and photographers.  Stop  at  the  Park  of  Barranco to degustate the exquisite traditional  Peruvian  anticuchos, made with beef heart and seasoned with garlic, vinegar, cumin, and aji panca, a mild red chile pepper  with a smoky flavor.  Have a taste also of the typical picarones, a fabulous dessert of sweet flavor, crispy and warm, dripping with syrup. Return to your Hotel.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Head to the pier of your hotel to board the launch for the Ballestas Islands. 45 minutes of the coast of Paracas, the Ballestas Islands attract an impressive quantity of marine animals: chiefly seabirds and penguins, as well as large populations of seals, which swim and jump close to the boat.<br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will transfer you to Ica City, Paracas and back to Lima.<br/></p>

<p><span style="text-align: justify;">A transport mobility will pick you up from your hotel and take you to the Ollantaytambo station, where your trip to Machu Picchu begins with a train trip to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours. This city is also known as Machu Picchu Pueblo and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you will find the railway station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories, for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and be able to climb early. Finally, after a short transfer you arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and refuge for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1471). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you can also visit, optionally, Intipunku (Puerta del Sol), Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Puente de Inca. In the afternoon return by train</span><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><br/></span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><br/></span></div><p></p>

<p>#1<br/><br/>Arrival at Refugio Amazonas. To explore the Amazon closely, choose the activity &#34;a la carte&#34; according to your interest. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities.</p><p><span>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). </span><span>During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</span></p><p><br/>#2<br/><br/>During the second day, continue exploring the Amazon, choosing an activity according to your interest. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities. Schedules are available at the lodge.<br/><br/>#3<br/><br/>After breakfast choose one of the various activities offered in the jungle. Some of them are walks through the woods, bird watching, among others. Take advantage of the day and choose the ones of your interest starting in the morning. Schedules are available at the lodge.<br/><br/>#4<br/><br/>The 4th day is the last day to explore the jungle and should be exploited to the fullest. Choose a new activity in which you have not participated the previous days or an experience that you want to repeat. Schedules are available at the lodge.<br/><br/>#5<br/><br/>Check-out of Refugio Amazonas.<br/></p><div><div><div><p><br/></p></div></div></div>

<p><span style="font-size: 9.0pt;color: #332f2e;">#1</span><span style="font-size: 9.0pt;color: #332f2e;"><br/> <span style="float: none;">Arrival at Refugio Amazonas. The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p><span style="font-size: 9.0pt;color: #332f2e;"> <span style="float: none;">#2</span><br/> <span style="float: none;">During the second day, continue exploring the Amazon, choosing an activity according to your interest. The experiences include science, adventure, river excursions, family activities and classic activities.
