<p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Enjoy a delicious dinner at Del Pilar Ollantaytambo Hotel.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Hotel Del Pilar Ollantaytambo.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Take part in a spectacular 32 km bicycle tour through the Sacred Valley. Start the tour leaving your hotel heading southeast, on the way to Huayllabamba district, passing through beautiful fields of quinoa, corn and potatoes while enjoying the view of the Urubamba River. On the way, make a stop at a chichería. These places were declared Cultural Patrimony of the Nation for their cultural importance and preservation of the tradition of preparation of chicha , the sacred drink of the Incas. You can also taste some of the more than three thousand varieties of native potatoes that grow in Peru. Continue the bike tour for one more stretch and then start the return to the hotel. The bicycles are perfectly suited for this trail and the activity does not imply a great physical effort.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p style="font-size: 12px;">#1<br/><br/>You will land at the International Airport of Iquitos and after having collected your luggage, you will board an air-conditioned bus to be transferred to the main port of Iquitos where the cruise awaits you. While settling in your spacious room, the ship will begin the journey across the Yanayacu River, deep in the Amazon. A meeting will be held on the upper deck to inform about safety on board. Next, the Cruise Director and naturalist guides will give a brief orientation talk about life on board, about the places you will go and what you will see during your trip through the Amazon. Then, enjoy the welcome dinner while watching the river and surroundings through panoramic windows. Before retiring to rest, you can have a cocktail on the upper deck, under the stars.<br/><br/><br/>#2<br/><br/>You will wake up early to enjoy the beautiful view of the sunrise over the Amazon River. After a delicious breakfast, with a wide variety of dishes, Peruvian specialties and fresh fruit juices, board a comfortable boat to sail to the Yacapana Islands, called &#34;the Islands of the Iguana&#34;, for the large number of these reptiles that inhabit in them. You will walk very close to the islands and you will have the opportunity to take good pictures of the flora and fauna that you will observe along the way. You can also do sport fishing for the famous &#34;piranhas&#34;, for which our nature guides will carry the proper implements. While the ship continues its journey through the Amazon River, you will have free time to rest or take a nap before lunch. Back to the ship, you will enjoy a light lunch in an air conditioned dining room and then the guides will give a talk about flora and fauna. As the sun sets, you can photograph the gray and pink freshwater dolphins, and there is also the possibility of observing some macaws, toucans, sloths and monkeys on the way. Finally, you will return to the ship for dinner, but before we recommend you drink as a snack a Pisco Sour on the upper deck, while watching the sunset.<br/><br/><br/>#3<br/><br/>After breakfast, the naturalist guide will tell you about sustainable development projects in the Amazon. You will visit one of the projects, where villagers plant palm trees to obtain oil. Your guide will also show you the artificial nests created in the sand to protect turtle eggs. Then you will enter the jungle to find wildlife and learn firsthand about the value of medicinal plants in the jungle. You will see the famous Ceiba trees, the highest in the Amazon. Then, you will return to the cruise for lunch and after taking a nap, you will start an expedition to appreciate the two different types of freshwater dolphins and a great variety of primates. Among the 13 species of monkeys resident in the national park, there are squirrel and howler monkeys, marmosets and pygmy monkeys. Observe the lizards and keep your binoculars ready as you will see more than 200 species of birds. Finally, you will return to the cruise for dinner.<br/><br/><br/>#4<br/><br/>After breakfast, you will navigate the boundaries of the vast Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, a dense vegetation that covers five million acres in the tributaries of the Amazon basin. Get up early and be ready in your boat at 5:30 am to see one of the biggest shows in the southern hemisphere: sunrise over the source of the Amazon River. Here, at the junction of the two largest tributaries, the Ucayali and the Marañón, the greatest waterfall in the world is formed. Your morning excursion will be accompanied by abundant bird sounds, while you take a boat ride, crossing riverbanks populated by long-beaked swallows, laughing hawks, exotic short-tailed parrots and dozens of other birds. You will travel by boat to the native town of Puerto Prado, where you can meet and interact with local residents of this remote part of the world, as well as learn a little more about their culture, traditions and folklore. You can also exchange goods for local crafts. After returning to the ship for lunch, you will go to Nauta Caño, part of the Pacaya Samiria Reserve, also known as the forest of mirrors, due to the black waters of its lagoons, which reflect one of the most beautiful scenery in the world. nature. In this area we will observe different species such as: toucans, macaws, iguanas, etc. Return to the cruise, you will enjoy one of the most incredible sunsets reflected in the black waters of the flooded forest. While having dinner, you will start the journey through the Marañón River.</p><p style="font-size: 12px;"><br/></p><p style="font-size: 12px;">#5<br/><br/>Tras desayunar, subirá a la lancha para explorar el fascinante río Marañón. Los guías le hablarán acerca de la selva y sobre la ´galería de la selva´, como lo llaman los científicos, debido a su tupida vegetación, creando un túnel de hojas. Mientras flota por este túnel, podrá observar fácilmente loros, guacamayos, y peludos monos frailes amazónicos muy cerca de las lanchas. Luego de regresar al crucero para almorzar. iniciará una caminata con los guías hacia poblados nativos, en donde podrá apreciar a los habitantes efectuando sus labores diarias y le enseñarán cómo ellos aprendieron a cultivar plantas salvajes y árboles para generar sus ingresos. Dentro de la selva primaria, los guías también le enseñarán el valioso aporte de los árboles frutales y las plantas medicinales que se encuentran ocultos en la selva. Finalmente, regresará al crucero para cenar.<br/><br/><br/>#6<br/><br/>Tras desayunar, el guía naturalista le contará acerca de proyectos de desarrollo sostenible en la Amazonía. Visitará uno de los proyectos, en donde los pobladores plantan palmeras para obtener aceite. Su guía también le mostrará los nidos artificiales creados en la arena para proteger los huevos de tortugas. Luego se adentrará en la jungla para encontrar vida salvaje y aprender de primera fuente acerca del valor de las plantas medicinales en la Selva. Observará los famosos árboles de Ceiba, los más altos de la Amazonía. Luego, regresará al barco. Ese día hay otra opción en las actividades. Tras desayunar, realizará la misma caminata, pero luego regresará a almorzar al barco, seguido de una siesta. En la tarde, a la caída del sol, podrá observar animales silvestres incluyendo a los monos frailes, a los monos capuchinos y a los monos aulladores. Cuando el sol caiga, usted escuchará la segunda sinfonía de la Selva Amazónica, cuando otros animales nocturnos emitan sus estridentes llamados a través de las copas de los árboles. De regreso al barco verá criaturas nocturnas, incluso, tal vez, grandes caimanes. Observe a los lagartos y mantenga sus binoculares listos ya que verá más de 200 especies de aves. A continuación, se servirá la cena.<br/><br/><br/>#7<br/><br/>Al salir el sol, subirá a la lancha para apreciar un verdadero tesoro de vida salvaje, incluyendo loros, guacamayos, tucanes y gran variedad de monos, así como algunas especies que, estamos seguros, nunca habrá visto antes. Disfrute de un desayuno ligero a bordo, compuesto de emparedados y jugo fresco. Hará una parada cerca a un lugar de alimentación de delfines donde podrá observar un espectáculo inigualable mientras disfruta de su merienda. Toallas refrescantes esperan por usted, mientras se traslada de regreso al barco para continuar su viaje por el río Amazonas. Tendrá tiempo libre para descansar o tomar una siesta antes de almorzar y luego disfrutará de un almuerzo ligero en un comedor con aire acondicionado. Esta tarde abordará una lancha para navegar desde el río Amazonas a través del atajo del canal de Huaysi y explorar el río de aguas oscuras: el Tahuayo. Chalecos salvavidas, ponchos, botas de goma, y agua fresca estarán siempre disponibles en las lanchas. A medida que navegue a través del río Tahuayo, observará a algunos pescadores remando en sus canoas. Mantenga sus binoculares listos para ver una gran variedad de vida salvaje: golondrinas, aves, halcones de cuello negro, monos y osos perezosos. Luego de llegar al lago Charo, podrá hacer uso de sus instrumentos de pesca para tratar de atrapar alguna de las feroces pirañas, así como otras clases de peces. Mientras pesca, los guías naturalistas le contarán la historia del lago y de sus habitantes. De regreso al barco, habrá una charla a cargo de los guías naturalistas y disfrutará su cena final. Después de la cena, los guías le presentarán a algunos de los miembros de la tripulación que usted no ha conocido aún, quienes se han encargado del trabajo tras bastidores de esta inolvidable experiencia.</p><p style="font-size: 12px;"># 8<br/><br/>After breakfast, and if the weather and weather permit, you will have one last chance to observe how the Amazonian peoples live visiting the riverside community of Bethlehem aboard a boat. Here the houses and shops are floating on the river for months. You will find the traditional food market where all the local inhabitants of the region meet in this area to sell and buy goods. Return to the cruise for lunch and leave your luggage in the room ready to be picked up. That afternoon, they will take him for a walk around Iquitos. You will visit the Amazon Wildlife Rescue Center, a sort of animal orphanage where manatees and Amazonian dolphins receive and provide them with specialized attention to save them and in the future reinsert them into their natural habitat. In addition, you will stop at the Mercado Indio Amazónico de San Juan, where you can buy some local crafts before leaving. Then, you will be transferred to the airport where the guides will help you with the registration and will remain with you until you have boarded your flight back to Lima.<br/></p><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">A vehicle takes you from Cusco to the Community of Huilloc in a journey full of impressive Andean landscapes. This community is known for being hospitable and sharing its impressive cultural legacy with visitors. This 2D/1N experience allows you to participate in the daily activities of the inhabitants of the town such as agriculture, weaving and cooking, among others. At the end of the visit, you are transferred back to your hotel in Cusco.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>
<p>You will be transferred from Lima to Ica by bus, approximately 5 hours.</p><p><br/></p>

<p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment-->You are picked up from your hotel at the Sacred Valley and transferred to the Ollantaytambo archaeological centre stopping at various points of interest along the route. Ollantaytambo is a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas, whose residents still live in accordance with their old traditions. It is considered one of the most remarkable archaeological complexes of the Inca Empire. Then you will be transferred back to your hotel in the Sacred Valley.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Entrance to Acueducto &amp; Paredones<br/></p>
<p>We will transfer you from the bus station to the hotel in Paracas.<br/></p>
<p> Enjoy  a delicious Dinner at La hacienda Hotel.<br/></p>
<p>Entrance to Chauchilla cemetery.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Adventures with Allco in Machu Picchu&#34; is a dynamic 4-hour experience for children between 6 and 11 years old, where they explore the magnificent Inca citadel in a fun and educational way. The children receive the ¨Explorer Kit¨ that contains a map, a hat, a magnifying glass and ¨Allco¨ a stuffed Peruvian dog. Accompanied by a guide exclusively for them, the children can explore Machu Picchu in a personalised and entertaining way. Parents will also have their guide during the tour. <br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Adventures with Allco in Machu Picchu&#34; is a dynamic 4-hour experience for children between 6 and 11 years old, where they explore the magnificent Inca citadel in a fun and educational way. The children receive the ¨Explorer Kit¨ that contains a map, a hat, a magnifying glass and ¨Allco¨ a stuffed Peruvian dog. Accompanied by a guide exclusively for them, the children can explore Machu Picchu in a personalised and entertaining way. Parents will also have their guide during the tour. <br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Adventures with Allco in Machu Picchu&#34; is a dynamic 4-hour experience for children between 6 and 11 years old, where they explore the magnificent Inca citadel in a fun and educational way. The children receive the ¨Explorer Kit¨ that contains a map, a hat, a magnifying glass and ¨Allco¨ a stuffed Peruvian dog. Accompanied by a guide exclusively for them, the children can explore Machu Picchu in a personalised and entertaining way. Parents will also have their guide during the tour. </div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Transfer from the pier to the hotel with a representative.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Hotel Royal Inn Puno.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Royal Inn Puno Hotel.</p><p><br/></p>

<p>One-way transfer from Ollanta station at 10:30 am on the Sacred Valley train (91), to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 13:34 pm. Departure from Machu Picchu at 7:30 p.m. Sacred Valley Train (92) towards Olllanta at 21:00 p.m.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">During the morning leave your hotel in the Sacred Valley and head to Ollantaytambo, a town and the archaeological site inhabited uninterruptedly since the time of the Incas. The Ollantaytambo fortress, formed by a series of platforms and temples next to the mountain, is one of the most emblematic buildings of the Incas. Then, head to a local restaurant for lunch. Later, you are transferred to Huilloc, a community that maintains the Inca traditions of its ancestors. Participate in an experiential activity with the members of the community. Upon finishing this experience, you are transferred to your hotel in the city of Cusco.</span></div><p></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#1</span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Embark on a unique 3-day journey, in which you will travel from the Peruvian coastal desert to the highlands, to reach Huancaya (3554 m.a.s.l), one of the most beautiful natural refuges in the country. Located within the Nor Yauyos-Cochas Landscape Reserve, this area holds one of the most fascinating high Andean landscapes in Peru, with numerous waterfalls and emerald lagoons formed by the headwaters of the Cañete River, and typical Andean flora and fauna, including Andean foxes, vicuñas, Andean ducks and herons. A perfect place to rest, relax and get in touch with nature just a few hours away from the city of Lima. This 4x4 adventure starts departing from your hotel in Lima. The first stop happens at km. 101 of the Pan-American highway, in the valley of the district of Asia to explore Huaca Malena, a ceremonial centre developed between 700-1100 AD, where funerary bales of pre-Inca cultures have been found. The trip continues with an up and down high-speed ride through the dunes of Asia. The tour follows through the provinces of Cañete and Lunahuaná and finally arrives at Huancaya. After lunch take some time to acclimatize while enjoying the amazing view. During the afternoon dinner is served at the hotel.</span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#2</span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">During the second day, after breakfast, start the exciting full day tour of the Huancaya reserve. Learn about the attractions of this natural refuge on an off-road route through the districts of Vilca, Tanta and the majestic Pariacaca snow-capped mountain, which rise to a height of 5750 meters above sea level. Also, contemplate the surrounding areas where you can appreciate the region´s typical vegetation and geography. Enjoy a picnic en route surrounded by nature and the calm currents of water of the reserve before returning to your hotel in Huancaya.</span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#3</span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">During the final day on site, visit the neighbouring district of Laraos, a place with a vast history that dates back to pre-Inca times and which a farmer community currently inhabits. Visit the Inca terraces, which show the presence of the Empire on this location, and that remain in use to this day, a great example of the traditional agricultural knowledge that has been inherited for generations. After this visit, start the trip back to the city of Lima where the trip ends.</span><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="text-align: left;">Participating in a cooking class in Peru is not limited to the preparation of a dish; Learning to prepare Peruvian recipes means knowing the history, the cultural heritage and the representative flavours of a whole country. The culinary experience in Marcelo Batata shares precisely this with its guests, offering a true participative, entertaining and personalised immersion in Peruvian cuisine in a professional environment designed exclusively for cooking classes. The afternoon starts with a tasting of different varieties of Pisco to whet the appetite and continues with an introduction to the local culinary culture where the chef shares knowledge of history, biodiversity and geography. Continue with the participatory preparation of ´alpaca Lomo Saltado´ or ´Ceviche´. The chefs will also prepare Neo-Andean, Creole and contemporary dishes to taste during the afternoon. At the end of the class, each participant enjoys the delicious lunch prepared by themselves. All in all, a delightful culinary experience and an opportunity to learn more about Peru through gastronomy.</span></div><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from your hotel in the Sacred Valley to the community of Huilloc with guide available. Participate in an experiential activity with the members of the community. Upon completion, you are transferred back to your hotel in the Sacred Valley.<br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><br/></span></div><p></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from your hotel in Cusco to the community of Huilloc with guide available. Participate in an experiential activity with the members of the community. Upon completion, you are transferred back to your hotel in Cusco.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Take part in a spectacular 32 km bicycle tour through the Sacred Valley. Start the tour leaving your hotel heading southeast, on the way to Huayllabamba district, passing through beautiful fields of quinoa, corn and potatoes while enjoying the view of the Urubamba River. On the way, make a stop at a chichería. These places were declared Cultural Patrimony of the Nation for their cultural importance and preservation of the tradition of preparation of chicha , the sacred drink of the Incas. You can also taste some of the more than three thousand varieties of native potatoes that grow in Peru. Continue the bike tour for one more stretch and then start the return to the hotel. The bicycles are perfectly suited for this trail and the activity does not imply a great physical effort.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">During the morning leave your hotel in Cusco and head to Ollantaytambo, a town and the archaeological site inhabited uninterruptedly since the time of the Incas. The Ollantaytambo fortress, formed by a series of platforms and temples next to the mountain, is one of the most emblematic buildings of the Incas. Then, head to a local restaurant for lunch. Later, you are transferred to Huilloc, a community that maintains the Inca traditions of its ancestors. Participate in an experiential activity with the members of the community. Upon finishing this experience, you are transferred to your hotel in the city of Cusco.</span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>The journey from Arequipa to Puno passes through almost surreal scenery. You leave the city via Yura, a district characterised by its rugged scenery, through the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain where you can see vicuñas, an indigenous Andean camelid. At Patahuasi you take the turn-off to Sillustani via Imata, Lagunillas and Santa Lucía.  At Sillustani, a peninsula jutting into Lake Umayo, you can visit a necropolis that is one of the largest in the world, with more than 90 &#34;chullpas&#34;: huge and primitive stone mausoleums more than 12 metres high, with their tops wider than their bases.  The road to Puno is a magical journey across the Andean plateau, through petrified forests and emerald lakes.</p><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">A mobility will pick you up from your hotel in Urubamba and you will be transferred to the Ollanta train station. Your trip to Machu Picchu will begin with a train trip to the town of Aguas Calientes. In Aguas Calientes there is a market for handicrafts, restaurants, and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb up early. After a short trip, you will arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resting place for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means Old Mountain, is considered a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the new seven wonders of the modern world. At the end of the tour, you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes. Finally, you will be transferred from the Ollanta train station to your hotel in Cusco.</div><p><br/></p>

<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel at the established time and will transfer you to the airport of Lima. Includes the assistance of an english speaking representative.<br/></p>
<p>Today you can choose any attraction you prefer from Cusco City and a guide will be at your disposal to accompany and guide you for an additional hour.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Enjoy an authentic culinary experience in a typical house in the village of Maras. Prepare a &#34;Timp&#34; (boil in Quechua), a delicious soup made from boiling a piece of beef, as well as tubers (potatoes, ocas, cassava) and vegetables (cabbage, carrots, peas). Sharing the table with a marasina family is certainly something you will not forget.<br/></p>

<p>Mrs. Grandonia and Mrs. Álvarez, the renowned hatters of Maras, run an attractive crafts shop that produces top hats. They want to share their art with the travelers who visit their town. Dare to learn this ancient Andean activity.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you will be transferred to the imposing fortress of Kuelap, the main archaeological site of the region built by the Chachapoyas culture around the year 500a.d. It has 505 enclosures in its interior distributed in diverse levels; its imposing walls are 600 meters long and its interior architecture shows its function as a formerly inhabited space. The fortress is located at 3000masl and to reach it, you will be transported in a modern cable car that will take you to the complex in just 20 minutes. Afterwards, you will be transferred to Leymebamba to visit the Leymebamba Museum which houses more than 200 mummies recovered from the Condor Lagoon, which can be appreciated along with their funeral offerings. The design of the building recreates the local architecture, using local materials. The beautiful garden surrounding the museum contains more than 100 varieties of orchids. After this visit you will return to Chachapoyas.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour at the Ollanta train station and head to the park of Ollantaytambo with a series of stops at places of interest. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited like in the past. Pachacutec (1438 - 1572) captured the town, destroyed it and built a new one absorbing it as part of his empire, and exhibits one of the most remarkable archaeological sites of the empire. At the end of the tour return to your hotel in Cusco.<span id="selectionBoundary_1538153914546_7886677294057047" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours time. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu pueblo&#34; and is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes are the train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacuti. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by the UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.</div><p><br/></p><p>*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).</p><p><br/></p><p>#2</p><p></p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions of the citadel. Visit the Intipunku, which means &#34;Sun Gate&#34; and is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. It was kind of a checkpoint control and the sun rises every day through this door if viewed from the most important  buildings of the citadel. You may climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34; as opposed to Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is  the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, a complex of underground constructions  likely to have been used by the elite. Finally you will be able to know the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and halfway has a gap of several meters above which were two large wooden tables.  Thus the Incas could isolate the city by simply withdrawing them and thus remain protected.  In the afternoon head to the station of Aguas Calientes to take train back to the Ollanta station and finally to your hotel in Urubamba.</div><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Ollantaytambos train station is located close to the site where the old Inca hanging bridge crossed the Urubamba. Move along the tracks following the rivers course into the jungle lowlands to Aguas Calientes also known as Machu Picchu Pueblo. This is the last stop before getting to the famous archaeological complex. Find crafts, books, restaurants and array of hotels in this buzzling town. The bus zig-zags up the steep side of the mountain for nearly 20 minutes. The prehispanic engineering marvel unveils itself gradually; this was the site Inca Pachacutec chose as royal retreat and where his &#34;malqui&#34; or mummified remains were to be worshiped. Machu Picchu or &#34;Old Mountain&#34; is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the New Seven Wonders yet the traveler senses a beauty unmatched by these or any other title. How was such a work accomplished? Granite stone buildings that seem to naturally sprout from the earth instead of being constructed on it. Andean mountain aesthetics can truly be appreciated at Machu Picchu; explore the site at your own pace. Visit the Royal Mausoleum or take the path to the Inca Bridge, or the central flight of steps to reach Intihuatana. Or simply contemplate the sacred geography all around; the Urubamba nearly encircling the site and Mount Salkantay (&#34;Wild&#34;), overlooking the citadel from the southeast. Enjoy the evening train back.</p><p style="text-align: justify;"><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Join a gastronomic tour that includes a visit to the Chocomuseo, the Coffee Museum, and the Pisco Museum; three ideal places in which to learn about the production process of each of these staples, from raw ingredient to end product. To make things even more enticing, in each one of them you will get to enjoy a taste! At the end of the tour, return to your hotel.</div><p><br/></p>

<p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">Be part of this entertaining family activity offered by the Larco Museum where participants will learn about Ai Apaec, an ancestral deity of the Mochica culture. According to the legend, this character had the power to cross through different worlds and was revered as the creator and provider of water, food and military triumphs. You will also have an approach to the worldview of this impressive civilisation. The visit continues with a fun, creative workshop, where participants design their own comic strip and it concludes with an exciting treasure hunt inside the museum. An exceptional cultural and entertainment experience to share with the family!</p><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Participate in a chocolate preparation workshop in Chocomuseo. This two-hour workshop takes you through all the steps of chocolate manufacturing process from the cacao harvest, including the history of its manufacture in Peru. For the preparation, use black or milk chocolates. Also, you have the option of preparing cacao tea or hot chocolate.</span></div><p><span style="font-size: 15px;text-align: left;float: none;"><br/></span></p><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>The tour starts at Sacsayhuaman, one of the most emblematic constructions of the Incas (located just minutes from the city, it is a World Cultural Heritage Site listed by UNESCO) built by Pachacutec in the 15th century. It is a huge fortress made of large stones which transportation is still a mystery. Return to the city to continue your tour along the San Blas neighborhood, originally inhabited by the Inca nobility who built impressive stone constructions within walking distance of the Plaza de Armas. Unmistakable Inca walls can still be seen at the base of many buildings. Today the neighborhood is a delightful cluster of cobblestone streets popular among artisans who sell their handcrafts on the street. Continue to the cathedral. This is the most impressive monument in the Plaza de Armas. Its construction began in 1560 and ended only in 1654. It took nearly a century to complete. For this purpose hundreds of stones were extracted and transported from the fortress of Sacsayhuaman under the command of the Ecclesiastical Council and the burden of thousands of Indians. The tour ends at the Coricancha also known as &#34;The Temple of the Sun&#34;(or Quri Kancha 'Golden Temple'), was built by Inca Pachacutec becoming the base of the Santo Domingo Convent at the arrival of the Spaniards. At the Coricancha homage was payed to the Sun (&#34;Inti&#34;) during the Inca Empire, a time when the whole building was covered with gold.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">During the afternoon you will visit the Planetarium Cusco. This is a centre of cultural interpretation on Inca astronomy, astronomical observatory and a beautiful place surrounded by nature, all in one experience. Upon arrival, you will have a talk about Inca astronomy and then a virtual projection of the southern sky in the Dome, where you will see the constellations. Finally, you will participate in an observation activity with a telescope.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">During the afternoon you will visit the Planetarium Cusco. This is a centre of cultural interpretation on Inca astronomy, astronomical observatory and a beautiful place surrounded by nature, all in one experience. Upon arrival, you will have a talk about Inca astronomy and then a virtual projection of the southern sky in the Dome, where you will see the constellations. Finally, you will participate in an observation activity with a telescope.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious american breakfast at Cafe del Museo Larco.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Muña Restaurant.</p>
<p>Alojamiento por 2 noches en la comunidad de Huilloc</p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at IK Restaurant.</p>

<p><span>A vehicle takes you from Cusco to the Community of Huilloc in a journey full of impressive Andean landscapes. This community is known for being hospitable and sharing its impressive cultural legacy with visitors. This 3D/2N experience allows you to participate in the daily activities of the inhabitants of the town such as agriculture, weaving and cooking, among others. At the end of the visit, you are transferred to the Ollantaytambo Station. <span>    </span></span></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up from your hotel at the established time and transferred to the Pucará Lithic Museum. It is located in the Pucará town (3910 masl), at 61 kilometers from Juliaca City. The museum has a collection of zoomorphic sculptures in addition to steles and monoliths with sophisticated details. Pucará was the first urban settlement proper of the lacustrine altiplano and it developed between 100 and 300 BC.  Then you will make a short stop at Raya, the highest point of the road between Puno and Cusco above 4338 masl. After enjoying lunch in the Sicuani town, you willcontinue your tour until the Racchi ruins, to visit the Temple of Wiracocha, the Sun God of the Incas. The next stop will be Andahuaylillas, a town located 40 kilometers from Cusco and its top attraction is the Andahuaylillas Church, also known as &#34;The Sistine Chapel of the Americas&#34;, due to the high quality of the art works its houses. The structure was built in the 16th century over the vestiges of buildings that could have belonged to the Wari culture. It now has an original  pipe organ that has been recently restored. You will arrive in Cusco after approximately 7 and a half hours.</div><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Half day tour to enjoy a unique ride through one of the driest deserts in the world, aboard a comfortable 4X4 van,  prepared especially for this type of routes and led by one of our experts. Enjoy a tour full of emotion and</div><div style="text-align: justify;">adrenaline, and  the views of the spectacular dunes of Paracas. The tour concludes with a delicious dinner in the desert.</div><p></p>
<p>Ticket de tren en servicio Expedition (Frecuencia 74). <br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Start with a scenic tour of the boardwalk, crossing the bohemian district of Barranco and the modern district of Miraflores, passing by Love Park where you can admire an imposing view of the Pacific Ocean. After the tour, arrive at a thematic restaurant and participate in a cocktail class, learning how to prepare the famous pisco sour. Then, enjoy a cooking class, learning how to make a delicious, typical Peruvian dish. After both classes, enjoy a tasty lunch overlooking the sea. At the end of your tour, transfer back to your hotel.</div>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Enjoy a delicious l</span><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">unch at La Casona restaurant</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious l<span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">unch at La Casona restaurant</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Enjoy a delicious lunch at Tunupa restaurant.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Today you can choose any attraction you prefer from Cusco City and a guide will be at your disposal to accompany and guide you for an additional hour.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Tunupa restaurant.<br/></p>
<p>Departure from Ollantaytambo station at 6:40 am on the Incarail train (41) voyager service, to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 8:01 am.<br/></p>
<p>Ticket de tren en servicio Voyager (Frecuencia 41)<br/></p>
<p>Ticket de tren en servicio Voyager (Frecuencia 44)<br/></p>
<p>Ticket de tren en servicio Expedition (Frecuencia 83)<br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Ticket de tren en servicio Expedition (Frecuencia 84)</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Departure from Machu Picchu at 18:20 pm on the Expedition train (84), to Ollantaytambo station with arrival time at 20:05 pm.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Ticket de tren en servicio Expedition (Frecuencia 34)<br/></p>
<p>Departure from Machu Picchu at 15:20 pm on the Expedition train (34), to Poroy with arrival time at 19:05 pm.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Departure from Poroy  at 6:40 am on the Expedition train (33), to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 9:54 am.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Ticket de tren en servicio Expedition (Frecuencia 33)<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from Urubamba to the Ollantaytambo station. From there, board the train that takes you to the town of Aguas Calientes, last stop before the citadel Machu Picchu. Upon arrival in Aguas Calientes, you are assisted to leave your bags at the hotel and then enjoy lunch (according to the arrival of the train the box lunch option is evaluated). Upon completion, you are transferred to Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and at the same time a resting place for Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, was named a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. After the tour, you are transferred back to your hotel in Aguas Calientes.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">* Baggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to baggage restrictions aboard trains to and from Machu Picchu, passengers may only carry one (1) small package that weighs no more than 5 kg (11 lbs.)</div><div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div>#2</div><div><br/><div style="text-align: justify;">Enter again to the archaeological complex of Machu Picchu. You can choose between visiting the citadel, the Inca Bridge or Huayna Picchu mountain, (subject to space availability) the adjacent hill whose name means &#34;Young Mountain&#34;, as opposed to Machu Picchu. Its ascent takes approximately one hour and the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, which is a complex of underground constructions probably destined for the use of the elite, is its main attraction. In the afternoon you are transferred to the train station to start the return to the city of Cusco.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p></div></div>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Some of the best memories you will take from Peru are its flavours, but with this experience, you can take the recipes to continue enjoying them. Participate in a ceviche hands-on class, where you will learn to prepare this famous dish with an experimented chef. Then, complement your chosen lunch or dinner with a Pisco Sour tasting, the national cocktail made with pisco, lemon and egg white. The meal ends with a delightful dessert. </span></div><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Take the alternate route to the infamous Machu Picchu Archaeological Site, and create unforgettable moments at every amazing stop along the way. Depart from your hotel early in the morning and make your way to the train station in a private vehicle. As the train moves along </span>though<span style="font-size: 13px;"> the green valley, towards Aguas Calientes, you will get off at km. 104, from where the hike begins. Cross the suspension bridge over the roaring Urubamba River and arrive at the Chachabamba Archaeological Site (2,150m / 7,052ft). From here, the real hike begins as you make your way about 8km (approximately 4 hours) up, through increasingly lush vegetation, until arriving at the magnificent Wiñayhuayna Archaeological Site (2,700m). Upon encountering the impressive Inca agricultural centre, which features numerous terraces, a worship area, and an urban area, you will see that the hours-long uphill climb was well worth it! </span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;"><br/></span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">The rewards continue as you make time for a quick, but tasty box lunch before continuing on to Inti Punku (The Sun gate) at 2,730m (8,792ft). Inti Punku is your introduction to Machu Picchu (2,430m / 7,972ft), and what an introduction it is! The views from the Sun Gate are dramatic and spectacular. From here, descend down a side path that takes you off the main trail, and towards the bus stop, where you will board a bus that takes you down the mountain, to the town of Aguas Calientes. Upon arriving in the town, check into your hotel, and spend the rest of the evening relaxing. Fully enthralled and </span>having<span style="font-size: 13px;"> enjoyed this, the world’s most unmissable travel experience, board the train for a picturesque journey to Cusco this evening.</span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p><br/></p><p></p>
<p>Empanada service in Pisac.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Upon arrival in Puno, you are transferred to Amantica Lodge located in Amantaní Island, on the shores of Lake Titicaca. Check-in while you receive a welcome cocktail and a snack. After lunch you have free time to rest,</div><div style="text-align: justify;">acclimatize and get to know the place. Afterwards, participate in a Mother Earth or Pachamama ceremony and/or a reading of coca leaves. Then enjoy tea time and appetisers before dinner. Overnight at the lodge.</div><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Wake up early in the morning to go fishing on the lake while enjoying the spectacular sunrise. Return for breakfast and then choose between a cooking class and kayak or paddle on the lake. At the end of the selected activity, have lunch and take a walk during in the evening through Pachatata and Pachamama, high spots of the island where you can find ceremonial centres built by the pre-Inca cultures that inhabited the area and from where you have a privileged view of the landscape. When returning, enjoy an aperitif and tea time before dinner. Overnight at the lodge.</p><p style="text-align: justify;">#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, visit two of the most representative islands of Lake Titicaca: Uros and Taquile. Uros is a set of floating islands built by the inhabitants of the lake using totora plant. Then visit the Taquile Island, which was inhabited by the Incas and where constructions of this civilisation remain. Enjoy lunch or picnic and after resting meet members of local communities, who will share their lifestyle and traditions. Finally, dine and spend the night in the lodge.</p><p style="text-align: justify;">#4</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, enjoy free time or participate in activities according to time availability. After check-out, you are transferred to the Sillustani cemetery to see the impressive tombs belonging to the Kollpa culture and/or</div><div style="text-align: justify;">to the village of Lampa to appreciate the beautiful architecture of its churches and temples.</div><p></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Breakfast at the hotel. A transportation service takes you from the selected hotel to the bus station. A representative assists you during the journey. Then you are transferred from Lima to Paracas by bus. When finished, you are driven to the hotel in Paracas, where you have the rest of the day off.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Breakfast at the hotel. A transportation service takes you to the dock; boarding point for the boat tour to the Ballestas Islands. This is a natural reserve of beautiful geological formations, impressive animal fauna and lags of a pre-Inca culture, which dominated the desert to install a successful society in adverse conditions. The Ballestas Islands are inhabited by migratory birds, pelicans, sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species found in the archipelago. Along the way, you can see the still inexplicable lines drawn in the sand forming a candlestick. At the end of the tour, you are transferred to the bus station to continue the trip to the city of Nasca.</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Breakfast at the hotel. You are transferred to the airfield where you go on a 30-minute plane flight over the desert and the mysterious Nasca Lines, which can only be appreciated from the air. Nasca is a small town that might go unnoticed on a map if it werent for the mysterious lines engraved on the desert that have given it worldwide fame. Although they are centuries old, how and why they were made still constitute an enigma. Large shapes of a monkey, a spider, a condor, can be seen from the sky. If weather conditions permit, the recent findings in Palpa are overflown. After flying over the desert the tour ends, and upon landing, you are transferred to the bus station, from where you depart by regular bus for a 10-hour journey to the Arequipa city. Upon arrival, you are transferred to the hotel.</p><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">The restaurants of Gastón Acurio, the main driver of the gastronomic revolution in the country, have become an unmissable destination in Lima. Together, they make up a network of establishments where you can try and get to know the conjunction of flavours and cultures that are part of our cuisine.</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Among the most outstanding offers one can find Tanta, with the typical flavour of Peruvian homemade food; Madam Tusan, with a variety of Chinese dishes and snacks; La Mar, which presents the emblematic dishes of the coast such as ceviche, tiradito or ¨tiger milk¨; Panchita, sharing the Creole seasoning in large portions; or Los Bachiche, offering Italo-Peruvian style dishes.</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Probably the best known restaurant of Gastón Acurio is Astrid &amp; Gastón, and its impressive tasting menu that changes every season. The restaurant was chosen at number 39 on the Worlds 50 Best list and is located in an old 300-year-old hacienda house in the district of San Isidro. Do not miss trying the best of Lima during your visit!</p><p><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will be at your disposal to visit some city attractions for a half day. You may visit the Main Square, the very center of the city, where the cathedral and other important building are located. You may visit the 12-angle stone, an example of Inca meticulousness in cutting the large stones used to build their walls. You may also visit the San Blas quarter, originally used as dwellings for the Inca nobles, which built impressive stone constructions at a short distance of what used to be Huacaypata, and it is the current place of the Main Square. The unmistakable rigor of the Inca walls is still evident at the base of many constructions.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">Today the quarter is a conglomerate of charming small cobblestone streets very popular among the craftspeople who sell their works on the street. Other places you may visit are the Regocijo Square, Saint Francis Square, the Loreto street and the Coricancha, also known as the &#34;Temple of the Sun&#34;, the construction of which was ordered by Inca Pachacútec (1438-1471) and was used as base for the construction of the Santo Domingo Convent upon the arrival of the Spaniards. This is where the Sun was worshipped during the Inca Empire and it was once full with gold artifacts. You may end your tour visiting the San Cristóbal and Cristo Blanco scenic viewpoints.</div><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Start  with a visit to the Coricancha, also known as the &#34;Temple of the Sun&#34;, the construction of which was ordered by Inca Pachacútec (1438-1471) and was used as base for the construction of the Santo Domingo Convent upon the arrival of the Spaniards. This is when the Sun (Inti, in Quechua) was worshipped during the Inca Empire and at the time of the Incas its walls were fully covered with gold sheets and its courtyards were planted with golden corn and life-size llamas. You will then go to the Cathedral, the most impressive building on the Main Square. Its construction lasted almost a century, it started in 1560 and ended in 1654. The Ecclesiastical Chapter ordered to extract and transport thousands of stones from the Sacsayhuamán fortress for this purpose. You will then visit Sacsayhuamán, a magnificent building conceived and built by Inca Pachacútec in the 15th century, The Sacsayhuamán complex was built with megalithic blocks, the transportation and erection of which continue to be a fascinating mystery. The Inti Raymi Festival, which worships the Sun, is reenacted on June 24 of each year, during the winter solstice. You will then go to Qenqo, located four kilometers away from Cusco City, an archaeological complex mainly used for religious purposes, and it is believed that agricultural rituals took place there. You will finally explore Puka Pukara (also known as the &#34;Red Fortress&#34;), located seven kilometers away from Cusco. It is an architectural complex presumably used for military purposes; it has many rooms, squares, bathrooms, aqueducts, walls and towers, and it is believed that the Inca's entourage used it while he rested at the neighboring Tambomachay. You will finally reach Awanakancha (en route to Pisac), after a scenic trip to the valley. It is a farm of South American camelids and showroom of textiles, where local families show the entire traditional process of weaving and making garments.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Early in the morning, we will pick you up from your hotel and drive you through the mountains and valleys, combining the Andean landscapes, local culture and traditional villages. We`ll go first to Pisac ruins to have a</div><div style="text-align: justify;">short visit to this amazing Inca remain. After this we take the bus down to visit Pisac Market (here we have free time to relax and walk around). After a car ride, we will bike by a dirt road crossing an impressive landscape to Taray, Calca and other small villages. Finally, return to hotel in Sacred valley.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><span id="selectionBoundary_1550858245131_5316807004418895" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span><br/></p>
<p>Oxygen service will be provided in the room by Marriott Convento Hotel.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you are transferred to Awanakancha, South American textile and camelid exhibition centre. This is the perfect place to get close to different Andean camelids, learn the traditional weaving techniques and acquire finely spun garments. Later, you are transferred to the viewpoint of Taray, from where can appreciate one of the best views of the Sacred Valley. Then, descend to the famous Pisac artisan market; today it remains as a great window into the past of the Sacred Valley. After lunch at a local restaurant, take a tour of the impressive Ollantaytambo Fortress, famous for its emblematic agricultural terraces, its location on the mountain and its excellent level of preservation. Then visit Chinchero, a picturesque town, known for its fine crafts markets and old agricultural terraces. Chinchero has enchanted visitors for many years, in part because of its wonderful mix of colonial architecture such as the Plaza and church of the early seventeenth century, living history and culture through its markets, its perfectly preserved Inca structures, and unbeatable views of the Sacred Valley. From here start your return to the hotel.</div><p><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Experience a traditional andean ritual to the Mother Earth, carried out by a local shaman.  The indigeneous communities keep alive this ancient ceremony  to honour the Pachamama and express their genuine gratitude for the benefites they have received from her. Share this unique  and unforgettable  experience, while  you  think about your most desired wishes.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p></p>
<p>Reading coca leaf with Lucas Checa<span id="selectionBoundary_1535119795517_30092123610188803" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Sonesta Cusco Hotel</p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Mantra Indian Cuisine.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">Visitors will embark on one of the most amazing routes in the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful Andean Explorer. The train has two dining cars, lounge wagon and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the Sierra. It also has elegant suites and cabins where passengers can rest comfortably. The journey begins leaving Puno towards Cusco. Enjoy a delicious lunch made of fresh, local ingredients, designed by the chefs of Belmond Hotel Monasterio. Then visit La Raya, the border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by towering mountains. Dinner will be served on board and arrival to the Cusco region is set at night. </span><br/></p><p>#2 <br/></p><p>During the second and last day of the cruise, you will enjoy breakfast on board and arrive to Cusco.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Entrance to Sandoval Lake <span id="selectionBoundary_1534945348206_19828004068270255" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span> </p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious box lunch at Casa Andina Classic Colca hotel</p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious box lunch at Colca Lodge</p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious box lunch at Refugio Hotel</p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">You leave the city via Yura, a district characterised by its rugged scenery, hills and ravines. You enter Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain where you can see vicuñas, the indigenous Andean camelid that is a national symbol. After that you go to Vizcachani, Pampas de Toccra, up to the crater of the volcano Chucura and on to Mirador de los Andes at Patapampa (4800 m.a.s.l.) where the volcanoes surrounding Arequipa can be seen. Lunch will be served in the village of Chivay and in the afternoon you can rest in the hotel, or visit the thermal springs at La Calera.</p><p><br/>#2</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Visit to Cruz del Cóndor to see the Colca Valley (the second deepest canyon in the world, more than 4 kilometres at its deepest part) followed by an astonishing sight: the great and majestic condor flying only a few metres</div><div style="text-align: justify;">away. On the way back to Chivay you will pass through the villages of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma, Yanque and the vantage points at Antahuilque and Choquetico. After lunch you can visit the village of Coporaque and the Colcas at Chininia, incredible pre-Inca constructions on natural vertical faces. From there you go to the vantage point at Ocolle and the amphitheatre, followed by a short walk to the little-explored ruins at Uyo-Uyo. Return to Chivay in the afternoon.</div><p></p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p>#3</p><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">From Chivay you leave for Puno by taking the turn-off for Patahuasi where you will take the road to Sillustani via Imata, Lagunillas and Santa Lucía. <span id="selectionBoundary_1534794709098_1574723924803687" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>At Sillustani, a  peninsula jutting into Lake Umayo, you can visita necropolis that is one of the largest in the world, with more than 90 &#34;chullpas&#34;: huge and primitive stone mausoleums more than 12 metres high, with their tops wider than their bases.  From there you leave for Puno<br/></p>
<p>A private transportation, accompanied by a representative, will transfer you from the selected hotel to Lima airport.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">A transportation unit will transfer you to Lima?s Historic Downtown, a World Heritage Site listed by UNESCO. Once you arrive there, you will walk to the Aliaga House, an ancient viceregal house delivered by conquistador Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga), upon the foundation of the city; it is a genuine jewel of the Spanish colonial architecture and is currently inhabited by members of the Aliaga family. Then, you will walk around the Main Square taking pictures and listening to an interesting explanation, followed by a visit to the Lima Cathedral located on the square, which is Peru?s most important church built between 1542 and 1622, and is one of the various beautiful colonial buildings that are an important part of the city's identity. Next, you will be transferred to the Larco Museum - located in the traditional Pueblo Libre district -, founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926. The museum, located in a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century, houses the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver artifacts as well pieces of erotic art. It contains more than 45,000 archaeological pieces that are silent witnesses of three thousand years of Peruvian history. Finally, you will be transferred to the Miraflores district to enjoy a scenic tour, making a brief stop at the Love Park before heading back for your hotel.</div><p></p>
<p>A transportation unit with a transfer person will pick you up from Lima airport to take you to your hotel. The service includes tips in the hotel and airport.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour visiting the archaeological remains of Pisac which are the largest complex of terraces in the area and a masterpiece of agriculture of the Inca empire. After lunch at the agreed restaurant drive to the town of Ollantaytambo located 60 kilometers from the city of Cusco. This is a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited, as in the past. Pachacutec (1438 - 1572) captured the town, destroyed it and built a new one absorbing it as part of his empire. It bears in its slopes  a great archaeological complex  called  &#34;tambo&#34; (&#34;fortress&#34;), which was a resting place with accommodation for travelers of long distances, located in the town of the same name. It is without doubt one of the most monumental works of the empire. After the visit return to Cusco.<br/></p>
<p>Transfer from the station of Aguas Calientes (Machu Picchu Pueblo) at 16:43 pm. aboard the train Vistadome (32), to Poroy Station with arrival time at 20:23 pm.<br/></p>
<p>Departure from Ollantaytambo station at 15:37 pm pm on the Vistadome train (603), to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 17:02 pm.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious box lunch from the Cicciolina restaurant.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Hotel Las Dunas<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes, arriving in approximately two hours. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu pueblo&#34; - the town of Machu Picchu - and is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you can find a train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer, reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca ruler Pachacuti. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the New7Wonders of the World. Once in the citadel, you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Return to Aguas Calientes for lunch at Café Inkaterra.</div><br/>*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).<br/><br/>#2<div><br/><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and see the attractions of the citadel. Perhaps visit the Intipunku, the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. The sun rises</div><div style="text-align: justify;">every morning through this door if viewed from the most important buildings of the citadel. Then perhaps climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34; as opposed to the &#34;old&#34; Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is the Temple of the Moon, a complex of underground constructions likely to have been used by the elite. Finally, see the daunting Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and has a gap of several meters halfway which is joined by two large wooden boards. The system allowed the Incas to isolate the city by simply withdrawing the planks and thus remain protected. In the afternoon, head to the Aguas Calientes train station to take the train back to Ollantaytambo and start the return to Cusco from there. Check in to your hotel in Cusco upon arrival.</div><p><br/></p></div></div></div>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo&#34; and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you will find the railway station, a crafts market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. In the afternoon return by train.</div><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Today you will experience a unique culinary experience overlooking the citys stunning historic downtown from the 11th floor at Rooftop Kitchen. The kitchen is equipped with modern utensils and contains individual kitchen stations where guests will learn to prepare typical Peruvian dishes with the freshest regional ingredients, experiencing a new way of knowing the incredible history and culinary tradition of Peru. Rooftop Kitchen offers cooking classes for lunch and dinner including pick-up from the hotel and a guided tour through the traditional San Pedro market where the class purchases will be made. You will begin with the preparation of Pisco Sour and will continue with the elaboration of the first dish based on fish. Continue with the preparation of the main dish: Quinotto de Quinua or Lomo Saltado. Then prepare the dessert and enjoy the main course accompanied by a glass of wine. A magnificent opportunity to know the country through its flavors while enjoying the wonderful view of the Imperial City. At the end of this gastronomic experience, you will return to your hotel.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>You will be picked up from your hotel and taken to Ica Regional Museum, which possesses an important collection of pre-Hispanic relics from the Ica, Paracas, Nazca, Wari and Inca cultures that have been exhibited in different parts of the world.<br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel at the established time and will transfer you to the agreed bus station in about ten minutes. <br/></p>
<p>You will be collected from your hotel and taken to the airport.<br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will take you from your hotel to the airport.<br/></p>
<p>A transport will pick you up you from your hotel in Urubamba and transfer you to hotel Aranwa Sacred Valley. (Only driver).<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Tambo del Inca Luxury Collection hotel.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Belmond Rio Sagrado Hotel.<br/></p>
<p>You will travel from Cusco to Puno for about seven hours. You will be transferred to your hotel upon your arrival.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious Lunch at Huacatay Restaurant.</p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious Lunch at Hacienda Orihuela.</p>

<p>#1<br/><br/>Enjoy two days and one night in the charming Titilaka hotel. The hotel is located on a private peninsula of Lake Titicaca and has a spectacular view. At arrival, you will be transferred from the airport to the hotel where you will enjoy a delicious lunch. Then you will have free time in which you can choose one of the various activities offered, which include paddling through the lake, biking, walking or navigate; you can also choose guided tours on the lake and land, where you can explore the ecological and cultural diversity that characterizes this region. You can visit the Acora Market, Lampa, Sillustani and the Atuncolla Community, or participate in bird watching, trekking to Kolla (private) and visit temples such as Juli and Pomata. Excursions to Tiahuanaco (Bolivia) and Isla del Sol and the Moon have an additional cost. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and spend the night.<br/><br/>#2<br/><br/>After breakfast, you will be transferred to the airport.<br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p>Enjoy two days and one night in the charming Titilaka hotel. The hotel is located on a private peninsula of Lake Titicaca and has a spectacular view. At arrival, you will be transferred from the airport to the hotel where you will enjoy a delicious lunch. Then you will have free time in which you can choose one of the various activities offered, which include paddling through the lake, biking, walking or navigate;  you can also choose guided tours on the lake and land, where you can explore the ecological and cultural diversity that characterizes this region. You can visit the Acora Market, Lampa, Sillustani and the Atuncolla Community, or participate in bird watching, trekking to Kolla (private) and visit temples such as Juli and Pomata. Excursions to Tiahuanaco (Bolivia) and Isla del Sol and the Moon have an additional cost. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and spend the night.</p><p>#2</p><p>After breakfast, you will be transferred to the airport.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up from your hotel early in the morning and transferred to the kayak departure point in Capachica. From there you will start the 5km stretch to Llachón. Halfway of the route you will stop at a sand beach where you will enjoy a picnic based on Andean products as well as coca and muña tea. You will be able to rest and appreciate the beautiful landscape that surrounds you. Lunch will be a Pachamanca; ancestral way of cooking products like potatoes, sweet potatoes, chicken, corn, meat and more with hot stones inside a hole in the ground. After lunch, you will return to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="font-size: 12px;">#1</p><p style="font-size: 12px;">Enjoy two days and one night in the charming Titilaka hotel. The hotel is located on a private peninsula of Lake Titicaca and has a spectacular view. At arrival, you will be transferred from the airport to the hotel where you will enjoy a delicious lunch. Then you will have free time in which you can choose one of the various activities offered, which include paddling through the lake, biking, walking or navigate; you can also choose guided tours on the lake and land, where you can explore the ecological and cultural diversity that characterizes this region. You can visit the Acora Market, Lampa, Sillustani and the Atuncolla Community, or participate in bird watching, trekking to Kolla (private) and visit temples such as Juli and Pomata. Excursions to Tiahuanaco (Bolivia) and Isla del Sol and the Moon have an additional cost. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and spend the night.</p><p style="font-size: 12px;">#2</p><p style="font-size: 12px;">After breakfast, you will be transferred to the airport.</p><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at hotel Pozo del Cielo<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Killawasi lodge</p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Casitas del Colca</p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious dinner at Colca Lodge.<br/></p>
<p>Assistance and transfer to Pisco airport.<br/></p>
<p>Lunch or dinner for guide.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will transfer from your hotel to the Lima Art Museum (MALI), one of the main museums of Peru, which promotes art and culture. It is located in the ¨Palacio de la Exposición¨ (Exhibition Palace) and home to more than fourteen thousand works; the most important collection of Peruvian art. You can visit its various showrooms, then have a coffee, eat a snack and go through the store to buy a beautiful souvenir (additional costs not included). Then you will go to the Water Circuit; a modern system of pools (certified as the world's largest by Guinness World Records) built in the Exhibition Park, which attract crowds throughout the year. Finally,you will be taken back to your hotel.</div><p></p>

<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and will transfer you to the Indian Market, located near the Miraflores Central Park, where you will find many handicrafts that are ideal to buy as souvenirs.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at La Casa de Barro Lodge &amp; Restaurant.<br/></p>
<p>Transfer from selected hotel in Chachapoyas or Cocachimba to Jaen airport.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">The tour starts on the road to Ica Regional Museum, which has an important collection of pre-Hispanic artefacts from the Ica, Paracas, Nazca, Wari and Inca cultures that have been exhibited in different parts of the world.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">After that you will visit a producer of wine and pisco, products that have made the valleys of the Ica desert famous. You will also visit Lake Huacachina. Originally an oasis in the middle of the desert, the lake is one</div><div style="text-align: justify;">of Ica´s major attractions. It is surrounded by large sand dunes and offers a variety of optional services: from good food to an adrenalin-stimulating journey by dune buggy. Finally, a tour of Ica with emphasis on the old and modern cities.</div><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">The tour starts on the road to Ica Regional Museum, which has an important collection of pre-Hispanic artefacts from the Ica, Paracas, Nazca, Wari and Inca cultures that have been exhibited in different parts of the world.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">After that you will visit a producer of wine and pisco, products that have made the valleys of the Ica desert famous. Thereafter is a visit to the sanctuary of the Lord of Luren, a dark-skinned saint from Ica, whose carved</div><div style="text-align: justify;">image was made in Spain in the year 1556. On the third Monday of October each year the image is taken from the church and carried through the streets of Ica accompanied by hundreds of devotees. Finally, you will visit Lake Huacachina. Originally an oasis in the middle of the desert, the lake is one of Ica´s major attractions. It is surrounded by large sand dunes and offers a variety of optional services: from good food to an adrenalin-stimulating sand buggy ride through the desert.</div><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you will travel through the Chachapoyas forest for about an hour in direction of the Sarcophagi of Karajía. These sarcophagi are elaborate two-meter high clay sculptures that rest on the edge of a rocky cliff of limestone, looking towards the abyss. The visit in the place will last approximately 40 minutes after which you will head back to Chachapoyas.<br/></p>
