If you or your guests do not wish to contribute, kindly instruct your LimaTours Specialist to deduct the above amount from the quotation. If you or your guests wish to contribute additional amounts, please advise your Specialist. Thank you!</div><div style="text-align: justify;">(*) Wallata is the Quechua name for the Andean Goose, an endemic bird which inspired a traditional courtship dance, now a ritual in Ollantaytambo feasts.</div><p></p>

<p>Day #1<br/>The tour begins at Ollantaytambo station, where passengers board the train to Machu Picchu Pueblo, a route full of breathtaking landscapes that will be a preamble of the arrival to the Sanctuary. Reaching the town, bags are left at the hotel and you will take a bus to Machu Picchu where you will have a guided tour where you will learn about the history and every corner of the citadel. After lunch, passengers return to the town and accommodate at the hotel. The rest of the afternoon is free.<br/><br/>*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).<br/><br/>Day #2<br/>During the morning, you can re-enter the citadel where you can choose between visit it again or climb Huayna Picchu mountain, a challenging road route from where you will have a unique view of the citadel. In the ascent of Huayna Picchu trekkers come across Inca archaeological sites such as the Temple of the Moon, an important place of worship of the time. In the afternoon, passengers will be transferred to the station to take the corresponding train for the return to the city.<br/></p>

<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel to enjoy a tour around the city of Cusco, which begins at the Coricancha Temple, also called the &#34;Temple of the Sun&#34;. It was built by Inca Pachacútec and the Spanish conquistadores used it as foundation for the construction of the Monastery of Santo Domingo. During the time of the Incas, a tribute was paid to the Sun (&#34;Inti&#34;). You will then be transferred to the Main Square to visit the Cathedral, with its fantastic design, followed by a stop in San Cristobal to enjoy a panoramic view of Cusco. Continue towards Sacsayhuaman Fortress, a colossal Inca construction north of Cusco, built with huge stones. The next place you will visit is Qenqo, an archaeological center located in the Sacred Valley, that had religious purposes. The tour will continue to Puca Pucara, a military construction composed of large walls, terraces and stairways. At the end, you will be transferred to the Tambomachay archaeological complex, which was used to worship water and was a leisure place for the Inca.  Finally, you will return to your hotel in Cusco. There are departures in the morning and afternoon.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your day will begin with a tour where you will visit the salt mines of Maras, the terraces of Moray and the archaeological site of Ollantaytambo. Located on the side of a mountain, Maras presents a surreal landscape. It is</div><div style="text-align: justify;">an impressive complex of saline extraction composed of large deposits of pink salt that was used to exchange for other products of other regions of the Empire during the Inca period and that continues to produce salt today. Then visit Moray, mysterious concentric circular platforms, for which some investigators would have been an agricultural research center. The elevated external platforms would be at a higher temperature than the internal ones and could simulate the conditions of the different ecological floors of the Empire. You will enjoy lunch and then head to Ollantaytambo. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas and is still inhabited as it was in the past. Pachacutec conquered the village, destroyed it and built a new one by absorbing it as part of its Empire. On its slopes is the Fortress of Ollantaytambo. A great archaeological complex that although receiveing the name of &#34;fortress&#34; was a &#34;tambo&#34;, city for rest and lodging for entourages that traveled long distances. Finally, you will head towards the Ollantaytambo station where you will board the train.</div><p></p>

<div><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your day will begin with a tour where you will visit the salt mines of Maras, the terraces of Moray and the archaeological site of Ollantaytambo. Located on the side of a mountain, Maras presents a surreal landscape. It is</div><div style="text-align: justify;">an impressive complex of saline extraction composed of large deposits of pink salt that was used to exchange for other products of other regions of the Empire during the Inca period and that continues to produce salt today. Then visit Moray, mysterious concentric circular platforms, for which some investigators would have been an agricultural research center. The elevated external platforms would be at a higher temperature than the internal ones and could simulate the conditions of the different ecological floors of the empire. You will enjoy lunch and then continue to Ollantaytambo. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas and is still inhabited as it was in the past. Pachacutec conquered the village, destroyed it and built a new one by absorbing it as part of its Empire. On its slopes is the Fortress of Ollantaytambo. A great archaeological complex that although receiveing the name of &#34;fortress&#34; was a &#34;tambo&#34;, city for rest and lodging for entourages that traveled long distances. Finally, you will head towards the Ollantaytambo station.</div><p></p></div>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you are picked up from your hotel in Cusco and transferres to the Pisac archaeological complex; an elevated, well-preserved site of agricultural terraces, ceremonial platforms and simple house structures peppered across the mountainside. The complex is another impressive example of Inca architecture which is placed in a location of astrological significance, as with many structures in the region. Then, you can optionally visit one of the local markets. Later, begin the trip to Urubamba, where you have free time for lunch. After lunch, continue the journey to the town and archaeological complex of Ollantaytambo; a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas that is still inhabited as it was in the past. The imposing Fortress of Ollantaytambo is also located here; a great archaeological complex that served as resting and lodging site for entourages that traveled long distances. Finally, you are transferred back to your hotel in Cusco.</div><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Our guide will pick you up from the hotel in Puno very early in the morning, to take a  two-hour motor excursion on the waters of Lake Titicaca to the unique Floating &#34;Islands&#34; of the Uros. The Uros are an ancient culture that inhabits a series of artificial islands built on the basis of reeds growing in the lake itself. These are constructed by weaving reed in the areas where it grows thicker and thus forming a natural layer. After  having experienced  some of the ancient traditions of the local people, you continue your motor boat trip to Amantani Island.  This  island  is inhabited  by Quechua-speaking natives still governed  by their ancient traditions, whose main occupations are the  production of  ceramics and textiles. The positive energy of  this Island is  so powerful that it is known as the &#34;Love Island&#34;. Lunch. Upon arrival to the island, you will stay at the house of a local islander to enjoy the hospitality of a typical Amantani home. Dinner included.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, you have  time to  stroll  at leisure  through this colourful island. Then, take a motor-boat  to Taquile Island,  also inhabited  by Quechua-speaking natives who have developed efficient and unique social systems, as well as fine hand weaving techniques, down through the generations. By the afternoon, you will return to Puno  and in  the evening you will arrive to the hotel.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you are picked up from your hotel in the Sacred Valley and transferres to the Pisac archaeological complex; an elevated, well-preserved site of agricultural terraces, ceremonial platforms and simple house structures peppered across the mountainside. The complex is another impressive example of Inca architecture which is placed in a location of astrological significance, as with many structures in the region. Then, you can optionally visit one of the local markets. Later, begin the trip to Urubamba, where you have free time for lunch. After lunch, continue the journey to the town and archaeological complex of Ollantaytambo; a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas that is still inhabited as it was in the past. The imposing Fortress of Ollantaytambo is also located here; a great archaeological complex that served as resting and lodging site for entourages that traveled long distances. Finally, you are transferred back to your hotel in the Sacred Valley.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p>You will begin your tour in the town of Chinchero, one of the most important settlements of the Inca Empire located in an esplanade 3,500 meters above sea level, where beautiful Andean landscapes surround the town, framed by the beauty of the Chicón And Veronica Mountains. Nowadays, every Sunday, the town becomes a center for the exchange of products from nearby communities. From there you will leave for the complex of Moray, mysterious concentric circular platforms, which for some investigators would have been an agricultural research center. The elevated external platforms would be at a higher temperature than the internal ones and could simulate the conditions of the different ecological floors of the Empire. You will enjoy lunch and then head to Ollantaytambo. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas and is still inhabited by the use of the past. Pachacutec conquered the village, destroyed it and built a new one by absorbing it as part of its Empire. On its slopes is the Fortress of Ollantaytambo. A great archaeological complex that although receives the name of &#34;fortress&#34; was a &#34;tambo&#34;, city for rest and lodging for entourages that traveled long distances. Finally, you will head to your chosen hotel in Ollantaytambo.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Let your imagination soar in two magnificent workshops offered by Art Trail, an artistic and cultural development project where Cusco traditions are rescued and given new life in artistic sessions. In the first activity, Cusco Colonial Glazed Ceramics, learn how to make a horse or bull of Pucara, a traditional Cusco figure, and apply fine finishes with a layer of enamel, giving it a glazed effect. In the second, the Colonial Gold Leaf application, enjoy painting with the characteristic colours of the mountain range and then finish decorating your work with gold leaf, a traditional technique that Art Trail has recovered and applied to contemporary crafts. Both workshops are taught by renowned Cusco artists. By the end of the sessions, you’ll have two personalised works that will be delivered to your hotel the following day. After the workshops, return to your hotel.</div>

<p>Departure from Ollantaytambo station at 12:55 pm on the Expedition train (73) to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 2:25 pm.<br/><br/>Departure from Machu Picchu at 14:55 pm on the Expedition train (74) to Ollantaytambo station with arrival time at 4:31 pm.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Departure from Ollantaytambo station at 8:53 am pm on the Vistadome train (501) to Machu Picchu with arrival time at 10:26 am.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Departure from Machu Picchu at 16:43 pm on the Vistadome train (32) to Ollantaytambo station with arrival time at 18:31 pm.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Cafe Inkaterra.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious home-cooked Andean lunch prepared with local products harvested in traditional and sustainable ways. Lunch will take place at a local villagers home.<br/></p>
<p>A transportation service with a guide will pick you up from your hotel at the established time and transfer you to the airport.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Your tour will start in the Pueblo Libre district, where you will visit the  Larco Museum. Founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926, the museum is located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century. The visit to this museum enables to understand over five thousand years of the high degree of development of the cultures that flourished before the Inca Empire. It shows the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver artifacts as well pieces of erotic art. Over 45,000 archaeological artifacts bear witness of five thousand years of Peruvian history. From there you will go to the citys built-up area, a World Heritage listed by UNESCO. You will tour the Main Square to see the Presidential Palace and the Lima Cathedral, impressive constructions that are still being used. You will then visit the Aliaga House, an ancient viceregal construction delivered by Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga) when he founded the city, the construction of which started in 1535. It may be the only house that still belongs to the same family until today. Next, you will visit the Santo Domingo Church, built along forty years and completed at the end of the 16th century. It is also known as the Minor Basilica and Maximum Convent of our Lady of the Rosary. The church has several altars and viceregal sculptures, and exhibits different times and aesthetic expressions. From here you will walk until the San Martín Square, where you will be picked up by your transportation service and transferred to your hotel.</div><p></p>
<p>Lunch at Café del Museo Larco<br/></p>
<p>Additional for transfer from or to the pier<b>.</b><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel and transferred to the Inca Bridge in Ollantaytambo, an ancient Inca building from which the original pillars still stand. Then explore the imposing Ollantaytambo complex; a military, religious and agricultural centre composed of platforms and different attractions such as The Temple of the Sun and the Royal Sun House. As you ascend, appreciate a panoramic view of the town, the only urban centre that has been continuously inhabited since the Inca Empire. Continue the trek to the Cacchicata quarry (3210 masl), a place dedicated to the carving and extraction of stones which were utilised to build Ollantaytambo and other surrounding structures. Observe some of the tired stones; huge stones that were left on the road and that show the magnitude of the constructions that ancient civilisations accomplished. On the way to the quarry, you can choose to take a one-hour hike to IntiPunku or Sun Gate (3925 masl), an imposing stone door on the top of a mountain from where you have a breathtaking view of Veronica Mountain. Enjoy a box lunch in the quarry, and finally, you are transferred from Cacchicata to your hotel in Cusco or the Sacred Valley. </span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><p><br/></p><br/><!--StartFragment--><p style="font-size: 12px;">Level: Extreme. Good physical condition required.</p><p style="font-size: 12px;">Duration: 6-7 hours hiking approximately</p><p style="font-size: 12px;">Maximum altitude: Cachicata 10531 feet</p></div>
<p>Additional for transfer from and to the pier.<br/></p>

<p></p><h6 style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you are transferred from your hotel to the Sacred Valley, where Pisac (3500 masl), is located. This is one of the most important and impressive archaeological complexes in the region, composed of agricultural terraces, aqueducts, a residential settlement, temples and the largest Inca cemetery. Begin the exploration ascending through the platforms from where you can see the different Inca constructions in a walk of approximately 1 hour and 15 minutes. Walk to the aqueducts, the urban area, and the Inca cemetery to learn about the burial methods used at that time. After this visit, descend to the town of Pisac for lunch. Get to know the famous local craft market, where hats, scarves and brightly coloured ponchos have attracted visitors for many hundreds of years. Today, it remains as a truthful window into the past of the Sacred Valley. During the afternoon you are transferred back to your hotel.</h6><p></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Dinner at La Trastienda</span><br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Lunch at La Trastienda</span><br/></p>

<div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#1</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">Upon arrival from Lima or Cusco, you will be welcomed at the airport and taken to the shelters headquarters in Puerto Maldonado. You will only take the necessary gear for the next days, and leave the rest at the safe deposit to keep the boats and cargo light. After traveling 20 kms you will reach the Tambopata River Port. Sail two and a half hours on a motorboat to Refugio Amazonas through the Infierno Native Community, and yond the checkpoint of the Tambopata National Reserve, a wonderful 1.3 million hectares conservation unit. On the way, you will enjoy a box lunch. Upon arrival, the lodge manager will welcome you and give important navigation and security tips. After dinner, sail on boat up the Tambopata River with flashlights scanning<span id="selectionBoundary_1558363463156_22653716801701895" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span> the shores with headlamps and flashlights to catch the red gleams of reflection from caiman eyes. Finally, you will return to sleep in the shelter.</div><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">After a delicious breakfast, you will make a half-hour walk from the shelter that will take you to the 25-meter high canopy tower, which has been built upon high ground, therefore increasing your horizon of the continuous primary forest extending into the Tambopata National Reserve. From here you can see different species of birds such as toucans, macaws, and raptors. Then sail four and half hours by boat from the refuge to the Tambopata Research Center found in the middle of the reserve. After an hour and a half, arrive to the confluence with the Malinowski River, leaving human presence behind. There you will watch capybara, caiman, geese, macaws and other species of animals. Three hours from the shelter, deep in the Tambopata National Reserve you will stop at the Chuncho Claylick. After a brief walk, watch dozens of large macaws feeding on the special sodium-rich clays of the riverbank. The Chuncho clay lick probably attracts more large macaws than any other clay lick in the world and the sight of many macaws taking flight is truly unforgettable. However, the stopover will depend on the weather and the amount of macaw activity. There you will be offered a box lunch. Upon arrival, the lodge manager will welcome you and brief important navigation and security tips. A three to five-kilometre hike will lead you to observe magnificent views of the Tambopata winding its way into the lowlands. The forest on this trail is good for watching howler monkey and dusky titi monkey. After having dinner, scientists will provide an in-depth look at the biology of macaws, their feeding habits, the theories for clay lick use, their breeding and the threats to their conservation. Finally, overnight at Tambopata Research Center.</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#3</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">At sunrise, you will visit the parrot clay lick where you can see several species of large macaws like Green-winged, Scarlet and Blue-and-gold, as well as hundreds of parrots that congregate on this large clay wall into a spectacle of colours and sounds which inspired a cover story in National Geographic. You will be located discreetly from very early in the morning, about 50 meters from the cliff. Then return to the lodge for breakfast. The next activity is a five kilometer walk in a part of the rainforest with immense trees criss-crossed by creeks and ponds. Among the figs, ceibas and shihuahuacos, you will seek for squirrel, brown capuchin, and spider monkeys, as well as peccaries. After lunch, sail ten minutes upriver from the lodge where there is a small pond with a platform in the middle. It is a great place to spot aquatic birds such as ducks, herons and hoatzins along with woodpeckers, orioles, flycatchers and parakeets that have found their home around this pond. Return to enjoy a delicious dinner and then you will have the option of hiking out at night when most of the mammals are active but rarely seen. Much easier to find are frogs with shapes and sounds as bizarre as their natural histories. Finally, you will return to sleep at the Tambopata Research Center.</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#4</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">At sunrise, you will visit the parrot clay lick where you can see several species of large macaws like green-winged, scarlet and blue-and-gold, as well as hundred of parrots that congregate on this large clay wall into a spectacle of colours and sound, which inspired a cover story in National Geographic. You will be located discreetly from very early in the morning, about 50 meters from the cliff. Then return to the lodge for breakfast. The</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">next activity is five kilometre trail in Terra Firme Forest, an entirely different habitat characterized by smaller, thinner trees atop hills and slopes. Saddleback tamarins are frequently found here, and as you walk near the limits of the swamp you must also keep our eyes open for rare tapir tracks. Return to the Research Center to have lunch. Then you will take a trail through the Palm Swamp, which grows on the remains of an oxbow lake and provides both arboreals as well as terrestrial mammals with fruits throughout the year. The aguaje palms are one of the most important food sources in the rainforest. The demand for these fruits and great conditions for planting rice, makes the palm swamp also one of the most threatened habitats. Return to Tambopata Research Center for dinner and overnight.</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#5</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">On your last day, after having breakfast you will be transferred by boat from the Research Center to Tambopata River Port. From there you go to the shelters headquarters in Puerto Maldonado for a short stop and continue to the airport. Depending on departure times of flights, a very early dawn departure might be required.</div><p style="font-size: 12px;"><br/></p><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#1</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">Upon arrival from Lima or Cusco, you will be welcomed at the airport and taken to the shelters headquarters in Puerto Maldonado. You will only take the necessary gear for the next days, and leave the rest at the safe deposit to keep the boats and cargo light. After travelling 20 kms you will reach the Tambopata River Port. Sail two and a half hours on a motorboat to Refugio Amazonas through the Infierno Native Community, and yond the checkpoint of the Tambopata National Reserve, a wonderful 1.3 million hectares conservation unit. On the way, you will enjoy a box lunch. Upon arrival, the lodge manager will welcome you and give important navigation and security tips. After dinner, sail on a boat up the Tambopata River with flashlights scanning the shores with headlamps and flashlights to catch the red gleams of reflection from caiman eyes. Finally, you will return to sleep in the shelter.</div><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">After a delicious breakfast, you will make a half-hour walk from the shelter that will take you to the 25-meter high canopy tower, which has been built upon high ground, therefore increasing your horizon of the continuous primary forest extending into the Tambopata National Reserve. From here you can see different species of birds such as toucans, macaws, and raptors. Then sail four and half hours by boat from the refuge to the Tambopata Research Center found in the middle of the reserve. After an hour and a half, arrive to the confluence with the Malinowski River, leaving human presence behind. There you will watch capybara, caiman, geese, macaws and other species of animals. Three hours from the shelter, deep in the Tambopata National Reserve you will stop at the Chuncho Claylick. After a brief walk, watch dozens of large macaws feeding on the special sodium rich clays of the riverbank. The Chuncho claylick probably attracts more large macaws than any other claylick in the world and the sight of many macaws taking flight is truly unforgettable. However, the stopover will depend on the weather and the amount of macaw activity. There you will be offered a box lunch. Upon arrival, the lodge manager will welcome you and brief important navigation and security tips. A three to five kilometre hike will lead you to observe magnificent views of the Tambopata winding its way into the lowlands. The forest on this trail is good for watching howler monkey and dusky titi monkey. After having dinner, scientists will provide an in depth look at the biology of macaws, their feeding habits, the theories for clay lick use, their breeding and the threats to their conservation. Finally, overnight at Tambopata Research Center.</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#3</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">At sunrise, you will visit the parrot clay lick where you can see several species of large macaws like Green-winged, Scarlet and Blue-and-gold, as well as hundred of parrots that congregate on this large clay wall into a spectacle of colors and sounds which inspired a cover story in National Geographic. You will be located discreetly from very early in the morning, about 50 meters from the cliff. Then return to the lodge for breakfast. The</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">next activity is a five-kilometre walk in a part of the rainforest with immense trees criss-crossed by creeks and ponds. Among the figs, ceibas and shihuahuacos, you will seek for squirrel, brown capuchin, and spider monkeys, as well as peccaries. After lunch, sail ten minutes upriver from the lodge where there is a small pond with a platform in the middle. It is a great place to spot aquatic birds such as ducks, herons and hoatzins along with woodpeckers, orioles, flycatchers and parakeets that have found their home around this pond. Return to enjoy a delicious dinner and then you will have the option of hiking out at night, when most of the mammals are active but rarely seen. Much easier to find are frogs with shapes and sounds as bizarre as their natural histories. Finally, you will return to sleep at the Tambopata Research Center.</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#4</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">At sunrise you will visit the parrot clay lick where you can see several species of large macaws like green-winged, scarlet and blue-and-gold, as well as hundred of parrots that congregate on this large clay wall into a spectacle of colors and sound, which inspired a cover story in National Geographic. You will be located discreetly from very early in the morning, about 50 meters from the cliff. Then return to the lodge for breakfast. The</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">next activity is a five-kilometre trail in Terra Firme Forest, an entirely different habitat characterized by smaller, thinner trees atop hills and slopes. Saddleback tamarins are frequently found here, and as you walk near the limits of the swamp you must also keep our eyes open for rare tapir tracks. Return to the Research Center to have lunch. Then you will take a trail through the Palm Swamp, which grows on the remains of an oxbow lake and provides both arboreal as well as terrestrial mammals with fruits throughout the year. The aguaje palms are one of the most important food sources in the rainforest. The demand for these fruits and great conditions for planting rice makes the palm swamp also one of the most threatened habitats. Return to Tambopata Research Center for dinner and overnight.</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">#5</div><div style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;">On your last day, after having breakfast you will be transferred by boat from the Research Center to Tambopata River Port. From there you go to the shelters headquarters in Puerto Maldonado for a short stop and continue to the airport. Depending on departure times of flights, a very early dawn departure might be required.</div><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Start your journey arriving to Puerto Maldonado, where our staff will welcome you and provide the necessary information for your stay. Navigate by outboard motorized canoe along the Madre de Dios River to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion for approximately 25 minutes. Enjoy lunch with organic ingredients and native flavors. After lunch, take a walk through CONCEPCION TRAILS, our inside property nature trails, where you will discover and learn about primary and secondary rainforest, its components, uses, transformations by the human being, and its relevance for the world. At dusk navigate by outboard motorized canoe, at the TWILIGHT RIVER excursion. By sailing on the Madre de Dios River, you will learn about its ecosystem, Amazonian creeks, nocturnal animals behavior, and the southern constellations. In the evening enjoy a nice dinner in the Casa Grande´s dining room. New experiences are ahead and after taking a nutritious buffet breakfast, get ready for a long trek through terra firma into the Tambopata National Reserve towards LAKE SANDOVAL. Here you will understand why Madre de Dios is called the biodiversity capital of Peru, while learning about the Reserve´s conservation efforts and the lake?s formation. Navigate by dugout canoe across this mirror-like oxbow lake that is home to the endangered giant river otter (Pteronura brasiliensis), blue and yellow macaw (Ara ararauna), red howler monkey (Alouatta seniculus), black caiman (Melanosuchus niger), and one of the world´s biggest freshwater scaled fish, the paiche (Arapaima gigas). Back to the lodge for lunch, and later the INKATERRA CANOPY WALKWAY &amp; ANACONDA WALK await for you. Take a 20-minute ride by outboard motorized canoe to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway Interpretation Center where you willlearn about its construction and the conservation projects of ITA-Inkaterra Asociación (NGO). Ascend one of the two 98-foot towers (30 m.) and cross the7 hanging bridges that connect the treetops at 91 ft. high (28 m.). Enjoy the breathtaking vistas and get the chance to watch white-throated toucans(Ramphastos tucanus), woodpeckers, trogons, squirrel monkeys (Saimiri boliviensis) and the three-toed sloth (Choleopus hoffmani). After descending keep on feeling the incredible primary rainforest energy going along the 200-meter wooden bridge over the swamps (aguajales). You will learn about the fauna and flora of this flooded ecosystem. At night get ready for an adventure at the RAINFOREST BY NIGHT excursion, where you will explore theAmazon rainforest that gives way to nocturnal animals who display intriguing behavior patterns, and enhanced senses as adaptation mechanisms. Listen to nocturnal living nature sounds, and penetrate this hidden world. Return to the lodge and enjoy a nice dinner. The sun rises once again and after an early breakfast you will take our NATIVE FARM excursion where you will be able to learn local techniques of cultivation and taste some of the products during the visit. Then visit the BOTANICAL GARDEN, discover the immense variety of medicinal plants considered part of the natural pharmacy used by the habitants at the amazon region. Compare the medicinal uses of local medicine with traditional remedies. Getting back to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion you will have lunch.Take a rest and get ready for the COCHA water excursion. Enjoy a dugout canoe ride on the Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion natural pond (cocha). Trynative fishing applying the catch and release techniques, as you enjoy the scenery observing hoatzins (Opisthocomus hoazin), a diversity of flycatchers, and gray-necked woodrails (Aramides cajanea). Disembark at the remains of Fitzcarrald steamboat; learn about its history and how itinspired Inkaterra to ask Werner Herzog to film the award-winning Fitzcarraldo, best director at Cannes, 1982. You will be able to participate in the INLAND COLLPA during the evening, where you will see different species and their night routines by observing through our blinds. End up your day with a pleasant dinner. After breakfast buffet, take a ride back to Puerto Maldonado by outboard motorized canoe. Arrive at the Butterfly House where you may wait for your flight back home or to your next destination while visiting this beautiful BUTTERFLY HOUSE and learning about the fascinating transformation of these colorful insects.</div><p><br/></p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p></p>

<p>#1</p><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Start your journey arriving to Puerto Maldonado, where our staff will welcome you and provide the necessary information for your stay. Navigate by outboard motorized canoe along the Madre de Dios River to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepción for approximately 25 minutes. Enjoy LUNCH with organic ingredients and native flavors. After lunch, take a walk through CONCEPCION TRAILS, our inside property nature trails, where you will discover and learn about primary and secondary rainforest, its components, uses, transformations by the human being, and its relevance for the world. In the evening enjoy a nice DINNER in the Casa Grandes dining room.</div><br/>#2</div><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">New experiences ahead and after taking a nutritious buffet BREAKFAST, get ready for a long trek through terra firma into the Tambopata National Reserve towards LAKE SANDOVAL. Back to the lodge for LUNCH, and later on the INKATERRA CANOPY WALKWAY &amp; ANACONDA WALK await for you. You will learn about the fauna and flora of this flooded ecosystem. At night get ready for an adventure at the RAINFOREST BY NIGHT excursion, where you will explore the Amazon rainforest that gives way to nocturnal animals who display intriguing behavior patterns, and enhanced senses as adaptation mechanisms. Return to the lodge and enjoy a nice DINNER.</span></div><div><br/></div><div>#3</div><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">The sun rises once again and after an early BREAKFAST, you will take our NATIVE FARM excursion where you will be able to learn local techniques of cultivation and taste some of the local products. Then visit the BOTANICAL GARDEN, discover the immense variety of medicinal plants considered part of the natural pharmacy used by the habitants at the amazon region. Compare the medicinal uses of local medicine with traditional remedies. Getting back to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion you will have LUNCH. Take a rest and get ready for the COCHA water excursion. Enjoy a dugout canoe ride on the Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion natural pond (cocha). Try native fishing applying the catch and release techniques, as you enjoy the scenery observing hoatzins (Opisthocomus hoazin), a diversity of fly catchers, and gray-necked woodrails (Aramides cajanea). Disembark at the remains of Fitzcarrald steam boat; learn about its history and how it inspired Inkaterra to ask Werner Herzog to film the award-winning Fitzcarraldo, best director at Cannes, 1982. End up your day with a pleasant DINNER. </div><div><br/></div><div>#4</div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">On your last day before leaving Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion, there is still much more to experience. The sun rises once again and after an early BREAKFAST, get ready for the CASTAÑA excursion. Take a walk within the hotel grounds to a small hut where our explorer-guides will teach you theimportance of the Brazil nut Tree in the local economy of Madre de Dios. Learn how the Brazil nut is collected using traditional tools and techniques, and try to open one of them using a machete and a nut cracker, to finally enjoy a fresh Brazil nut. Back to the lodge feed your appetite with a healthy and rich LUNCH. In the afternoon, visit the CACAO plantation at Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion and take part of the chocolate making process. Learn how to select the fresh cacao fruits, discover how the seeds are fermented and sundried. After pealing the toasted cacao seeds and grinding them into an aromatic paste, end the excursion with a selection of treats made from our organic chocolate. You will be able to participate in the INLAND COLLPA during the evening, where you will see different species and their night routines by observing through our blinds. End up your day with a pleasant DINNER.</div><br/></div><div>#5</div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After BREAKFAST buffet, take a ride back to Puerto Maldonado by outboard motorized canoe. Arrive at the Butterfly House where you may wait for your flight back home or to your next destination, while visiting this beautiful BUTTERFLY HOUSE and learning about the fascinating transformation of these colorful insects.</div><p><br/></p><p style="text-align: justify;">*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.<br/></p><p><br/></p></div>

<p>#1<br/>You will be transferred 30 minutes to the outskirts of Lima towards Pachacamac. Pachacamac is a historical place where you can see archaeological sites such as temples and pyramids built by pre-Hispanic civilisations that inhabited the area. Today it offers country restaurants and is a favourite place for ¨Limeños¨ to spend the day out of the city. <span id="selectionBoundary_1532019423391_5306348992344163" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>After this visit, you will be transferred to Paracas. You will arrive at the airport of Pisco for a flight of 1 hour and 30 minutes over the vast desert to observe the mysterious Nasca Lines, composed by figures that form a monkey, a spider, a condor, among others. When finished, you will be taken to the hotel in Paracas. Overnight in Paracas.<br/><br/>#2<br/>During the morning, a transportation service will take you to the boarding point for the boat trip to the Ballestas Islands; a natural reserve of beautiful geological formations, impressive animal fauna and vestiges of pre Inca cultures. The Ballestas islands are inhabited by endemic birds, pelicans, sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species that find refuge in the archipelago. At the end of the tour, you will be transferred back to Lima.<br/></p>

<p>A transportation unit will transfer you to Lima?s Historic Downtown, a World Heritage Site listed by UNESCO, where you will visit the Lima Cathedral located on the Main Square, which is Peru's most important church built<br/>between 1542 and 1622, and is one of the various beautiful colonial buildings that are an important part of the city's identity. Then, you will be able to walk around the square, taking pictures until you arrive at the Aliaga House, an ancient viceregal house delivered by conquistador Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga), upon the foundation of the city; it is a genuine jewel of the Spanish colonial architecture and is currently inhabited by members of the Aliaga family. Next, you will be transferred to the San Isidro and Miraflores districts, to enjoy a scenic tour, making a brief stop at the Love Park before heading back for your hotel.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">The employees of the lodge will receive you upon your arrival at the airport and will transfer you to the fluvial port of the Infierno native community, located at 45 minutes from Puerto Maldonado. From there you will sail for two and a half hours to Refugio Amazonas. During this trip, you will enjoy lunch while you are watching a great variety of local birds, such as black skimmers, crowned egrets and flycatchers. Upon your arrival at the lodge, you will receive a brief introduction about the local wildlife and, after resting, you will take a short hike to a 35-meter high watch tower built with scaffoldings. You will watch the sunset amidst toucans, parrots and macaws, and will return to the lodge to have dinner and rest. After having breakfast, you will hike for approximately one hour to visit Lake Sachavacayoc, a flooded area that houses particular Amazonian species due to its calm waters. During a canoe trip, you will find turtles, caimans, giant otters and riparian birds, among them, the ´shansho´, which has a prehistoric appearance. Next, you will return to the lodge to have lunch, take a short rest, and hike on the Ethnobotanical Path, where you will learn about the daily use of the natural resources of the jungle as medicines, construction and food. After having breakfast, you will take a short hike to a nearby collpa. Collpas are clay deposits that parrots and macaws visit in large quantities to eat this substance, as it helps them clean their stomachs. In this same place, it is possible to watch blue-headed parrots, and Andean cocks-of-the-rocks, a beautiful endemic species. After watching hundreds of birds fluttering around you, you will walk on a path that enters the chestnut forest, as this local nut has become an important income for native communities. After learning how they are harvested and transported, you will return to the lodge to have lunch and then you will visit a parcel of native crops. In the evening, you will have dinner and go to rest. After having breakfast, a motorboat will transfer you to Puerto Maldonado and then to the airport.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p></p>

The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p></p>

<p>A transportation service accompanied by a representative in German, will pick you up from your hotel to take you to Cusco airport. Upon arrival, the representative will assist you to reach the boarding gate.<br/></p>

<p></p><p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment-->#1</p><p>Upon arrival in Puerto Maldonado, you are assisted at the airport and transferred to the pier in a two-hour motorboat trip up the beautiful Tambopata River to Posada Amazonas Lodge, at the eseeja native community. A box lunch is served on board. During the trip, several bird species typical of the river and forest edge may be spotted. During the afternoon, hike the nearby trails visiting a small parakeet clay lick were dozens of these birds of several species gather to eat clay. dinner and accommodation at the lodge.</p><p><span>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). </span><span>During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</span></p><p><br/></p><p>#2</p><p>After breakfast, go on an ecology walk through primary forest passing by the side of a hill where the different forest strata can be clearly seen, ending on a seasonal floodplain forest where a huge ceiba tree stands. Return to the lodge for lunch and during the afternoon, walk around the nearby trails. after dinner at the lodge, go on an optional night walk to observe the nocturnal wildlife, especially amphibians and insects.</p><p><br/></p><p>#3</p><p>After an early breakfast, go on a ten-minute walk to visit another parrot clay lick where parrots, parakeets and large macaws of different species gather to eat clay. Here, you have the opportunity to spot some other spectacular wildlife activity. After lunch at the lodge, visit a native farm to learn how is it that this agriculture system is sustainable over the long term. Finally, return to Posada Amazonas Lodge.</p><p><br/></p><p>#4</p><p>After breakfast, enter the river for a fifteen minute-boat trip followed by a half-an-hour walk to the ¨Tres Chimbadas¨ oxbow lake canoe ride along the edge to admire its fauna, especially giant otters, turtles and hoatzin birds. Later, explore the surroundings or swim at the lake. after lunch at the lodge, hike the nearby trails or relax enjoying the lodge facilities. Dinner at the lodge</p><p><br/></p><p>#5</p><p>After an early breakfast, return trip to Puerto Maldonado and the airport. Assistance on departure flight<!--EndFragment--></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

A box lunch is served on board. During the trip, several bird species typical of the river and forest edge may be spotted. During the afternoon, hike the nearby trails visiting a small parakeet clay lick where dozens of these birds of several species gather to eat clay. dinner and accommodation at the lodge. </div></div><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><div><br/></div><div>#2 </div><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, go on an ecology walk through primary forest passing by the side of a hill where the different forest strata can be clearly seen, ending on a seasonal floodplain forest where a huge ceiba tree stands. Return to the lodge for lunch and during the afternoon, walk around the nearby trails. after dinner at the lodge, go on an optional night walk to observe the nocturnal wildlife, especially amphibians and insects.</div><div><br/>#3 </div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After an early breakfast, go on a ten-minute walk to visit another parrot clay lick where parrots, parakeets and large macaws of different species gather to eat clay. Here, you have the opportunity to spot some other spectacular wildlife activity. After lunch at the lodge, visit a native farm to learn how is it that this agriculture system is sustainable over the long term. Finally, return to Posada Amazonas Lodge.</div><br/></div><div>#4 </div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, enter the river for a fifteen minute-boat trip followed by a half-an-hour walk to the ¨Tres Chimbadas¨ oxbow lake canoe ride along the edge to admire its fauna, especially giant otters, turtles and hoatzin</div><div style="text-align: justify;">birds. Later, explore the surroundings or swim at the lake. after lunch at the lodge, hike the nearby trails or relax enjoying the lodge facilities. Dinner at the lodge</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div></div><div>#5 </div><div><br/></div><div>After an early breakfast, return trip to Puerto Maldonado and the airport. Assistance on departure flight<p><br/></p></div>

<p>A transportation unit will pick you up from your hotel and take you to Moray (11,646ft/3,550m), where you will discover an amazing archaeological complex of circular farming terraces. Then, you will head for the enchanting town of Maras (11,023ft/3,360m), to discover its unique pink salt mines. You will return to Cusco at the end of the visit.<br/></p>

<p>A transportation unit will pick you up from your hotel to enjoy a tour by the city of Cusco that will begin at the Coricancha, also called the &#34;Temple of the Sun&#34; (or the &#34;Premises of the Golden One&#34;).  It was built by Inca Pachacútec and the Spanish conquistadores used it as foundation for the construction of the Monastery of Santo Domingo. This was the place where the Incas worshipped the Sun (Inti, in Quechua) in ancient times, when its walls were completely lined with sheets of pure gold and its courtyards were populated with golden corn cobs and life-size llamas. Next, you will head for the fountain at the Main Square, to visit the Cathedral with its amazing design, followed by a stop at San Cristóbal, to enjoy a scenic view of Cusco. You will continue to the Sacsayhuaman Fortress, a colossal Inca construction located north of the city, built with huge stones. This is followed by the visit to Qenqo, an archaeological center located in the Sacred Valley, built for religious purposes. The tour continues to Puca Pucará, a military construction composed of large walls, terraces and stairways. You will end the tour at the Tambomachay archaeological site, used to worship water and as leisure place for the Inca. Finally, you will be transferred back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p>You will be picked up from your hotel and depart to Huilloc where you will learn about the customs of this community and the ancestral activities of daily Andean life such as working the land, their gastronomy or the process of garment making using traditional techniques. In addition, you can purchase one of these beautiful attires, contributing to the spiritual strengthening and identity of this Inca town, making this a complete and exceptional experience. After lunch you will head to a ¨Chicheria¨, traditional three-centuries-old spaces considered National Cultural Heritage of Peru, where food is served with recipes inherited through generations and are accompanied by ¨chicha¨, drink made from corn. Afterwards, you will visit Chinchero, a small and picturesque Inca town where you will learn about traditional textile and handicraft work in the area. From Chinchero you will see magnificent views of the Sacred Valley from above and visit the beautiful chapel that conserves original pictures of the Cusco School of Art inside. Finally, you will return to the city of Cusco.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">During the morning you will be picked up from your hotel and transferred to Ollantaytambo, a typical example of Inca urban planning, still inhabited by its descendants. The fortress of Ollantaytambo is a great archaeological complex that was a city of rest and lodging for entourages that travelled long distances. After this visit, you will go to the station to board the train to Aguas Calientes. This city is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo&#34; and is the last stop before entering the archaeological site. Upon arrival, you will be transferred to the bus that will take you to the citadel. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, was named a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. At the end of the tour, you will return to Aguas Calientes where you will have lunch and later board the train back to Ollantaytambo. Upon arrival, you will be transferred from the station to the selected hotel in the Sacred Valley.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p>A transportation unit will pick you up from your hotel and transfer you from Puno to the Sacred Valley in Cusco. On the way, you will make stops at amazing places. First, you will visit the Sillustani chullpas, which are pre-Inca tombs. Then, you will stop at La Raya, the highest point on the road at 4,350 m above sea level (m.a.s.l). You will continue to Raqchi, a temple dedicated to Wiracocha, the main god of the Incas, considered the creator of the world. You will also visit Andahuaylillas, which houses a beautiful church known as the &#34;Sistine Chapel of the Americas&#34;.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">#1 </div><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Depart Arequipa after a tasty breakfast at the hotel. Drive through the Yura district, heading towards the Colca Valley. Yura is characterised by a number of dramatic geographical features such as gullies, mountain ranges and canyons. Continue through the Pampa Canahuas Reserve, an extensive plain populated by vicuñas, one of the four indigenous Andean camelids found all over Peru. Cross through the pampas of Viscachani and Toccra, skirting the crater of the Chucura Volcano to reach the Los Andes scenic lookout in Patapampa, found 4,800 metres above sea level. From here, you can see the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa. Lunch is in the picturesque town of Chivay with a transfer in the afternoon to your selected hotel in the Colca Valley. Rest of the day at leisure. <br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="text-align: justify;"> </div></div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, prepare for a day of high drama with a trip to the breathtaking Condor Cross lookout. Take a deep breath and appreciate the sheer vastness of the Colca Valley, which is one of the worlds deepest canyons with a maximum depth of more than 4,160 metres – twice that of the Grand Canyon. The thrill of the experience is enhanced by the natural spectacle of huge condors flying over the canyon. Watch in awe as these graceful birds of prey glide over the canyon, patrolling it with military discipline. Descend to the valley floor to discover some of the rustic towns on the banks of the River Colca such as Chivay, Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque, where quaint colonial churches are as much as part of the scenery as the peaks of the Andes. There are also stops at the lookouts of Antahuilque and Choquetico to admire majestic views of the valley. After lunch, return to Arequipa.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div>

<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel to take you to know the beautiful city of Arequipa in a half-day tour, which includes visits to Carmen Alto Viewpoint, Yanahuara Square, Church of the Company of<br/>Jesus, the Cathedral's facade and the impressive Santa Catalina Monastery. The tour has departures in the morning and afternoon. It should be noted that due to the pedestrianization of the historic downtown, the visits to the Main Square, Cathedral and dome of San Ignacio are made without transportation.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 10.0pt;">Board a boat from the pier in Lake Titicaca and sail until reaching Uros; a group of fascinating islands that have been entirely elaborated with totora plant by the members of the community. Learn about the lifestyle and traditions of the inhabitants of these floating islands. After strolling through this peculiar site, leave for Llachón, a rural community located on the Capachica peninsula. From here you can see beautiful views of the lake, picturesque paths to walk and hills with viewpoints where ancestral archaeological sites can be found. Capachica has a Quechua population of approximately 1,300 inhabitants who live in various towns scattered around the place. Enjoy lunch in Llachon and when finished, return to Puno.</span></p>

<p>#1<br/>After enjoying the tour in the city of Lima and discover its charms, you will be transferred to Paracas in the morning. Paracas has one of Peru's most bountiful seas due to the cold water currents with a high-density planktonic community that feeds a large variety of mammal species such as whales, dolphins and sea lions. You will have a free afternoon and then you will overnight at the hotel.<br/>#2<br/>Today you will enjoy a tour to the impressive Ballestas Islands, located 45 minutes from the coast of Paracas. The islands boast an impressive fauna that includes guano birds, penguins, Inca terns and Peruvian boobies, in<br/>addition to sea lions that curiously approach the tourist boats and jump around them. The existence of this habitat and the wildlife that populates it is an indicator of our bountiful sea. Then you will visit the Huacachina Lagoon, a beautiful oasis surrounded by dunes, palm trees and colorful houses; followed by the Maria Reiche Museum. At the end, you will be transferred to your hotel in Nasca.<br/>#3<br/>Today you will have a free day to know the city of Nasca where you can fly over the renowned Nasca Lines, where you may watch the 12 classic geoglyphs that decorate the desert: the Whale, the Trapezoids, the Astronaut, the Monkey, the Dog, the Condor, the Spider, the Hummingbird, the Gannet/Flamingo, the Parrot, the Hands and the Tree. At the end of the tour, you will be transferred to your hotel in Nasca.<br/>#4<br/>This day you will continue your trip to Arequipa. Upon arrival you will be <span id="selectionBoundary_1531859510582_02115323528498947" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>taken to your assigned hotel in that city.<br/></p>

<p>A tour bus will take you from Arequipa to Puno, making stops at various attractions. First you will pass by the district of Yura, which occupies a large area of beautiful geography. Continue to the National Reserve of Pampa Cañahuas where you can see incredible scenery and then you will go to Patahuasi. After that you will know the district of Santa Lucia, located in the province of Lampa, and continue to Lagunillas, a high Andean lake located in Puno. Finally you will pass the city of Juliaca, before reaching Puno.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;"></span></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Just as important as the natural beauty and archaeological sites of the Sacred Valley are, of course, its people. Here, in this region of Peru, you find communities proud of their traditions, history and heritage, many of whom still live and trade by them in the 21st century. Fittingly then, the first point of interest today is Awanakancha, an authentic Camelid farm who specialize in the weaving of traditional garments made from their animal’s fur. During the tour, witness local culture at work as farmhands raise and care for the Camelids and weavers work their magic on the mill, producing a range of traditional garments – some of which you can take home as souvenirs! From Awanakancha to one of Peru’s most famous craft markets, where brightly coloured bobble hats, scarves and throws have been attracting vendors and bargain hunters alike for many hundreds of years: Pisac. If you are lucky enough to visit on a busy market day, prepare for dozens of stalls selling their wares just as they have done since ancient times which, though heavily populated by tourists today, is still very much a window into the Sacred Valley’s past. Following lunch at a local restaurant, take a tour of the stunning Ollantaytambo Fortress, famous for its iconic agricultural terraces, picturesque mountainside location and excellent level of preservation, making it one of the most recognizable Inca ruins of them all. Then head to Chinchero, a lively high-altitude town known for its fine craft markets and ancient agricultural terraces, as can be seen during this tour of the settlement itself and nearby archaeological complexes. Chinchero has been thrilling visitors for many years, partly down to its enviable mix of colonial architecture in the form of an early 17th century central square and church, living history and culture through its markets and pristine Inca structures, and unbeatable views of the Sacred Valley. From here start your return to the hotel. </div><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>#1</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Pick up from the hotel at 8:00 am and transfer Checacupe, starting point of the trek. Visit Pikillacta on the way, Wari site of constructions that resemble pyramids. Once in Checacupe, explore the area for a few minutes and continue towards Quesoyuni, a small Andean community. Have lunch in Quesoyuni and continue the walk towards the camp in Kairawiri. Go on a walk to acclimatise to the height of the Andes and continue the journey tomorrow. Dinner and overnight at the camp.</div><p><br/></p><p>#2</p><p></p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Have breakfast in the early hours of the morning and start hiking to the impressive 7-Colour Mountain. Enjoy the natural spectacle in front of you as well as the imposing mountains that also fill the landscape with colour.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">After the visit of between 30 and 45 minutes, descend to Quesoyoni for a picnic and lunch. Transfer to Cusco after lunch.</div><p></p><p><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">--------------------------</span><!--EndFragment--><br/></p><p>Level: Challenging</p><p>Duration: 15 hours approximately</p><p>Maximum altitude: Vinicunca 17000 feet</p><p><span style="font-size: 10.0pt;color: black;"></span></p>

<div><p></p><p>During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel to visit the town of Chinchero, a traditional Cusco urban centre located at an elevated point with a beautiful view of the valley. The inhabitants of this area preserve the artisan traditions and the inherited knowledge of the Incas and display them in their clothes and customs. In Chinchero there are also archaeological remains and a beautiful seventeenth-century church, one of the first Catholic buildings in Peru. Then, you are transferred to the Living Museum of Yucay, a centre Andean traditions interpretations, where demonstrations of the elaboration of textile work, adobe, pottery and silverware are carried out in the same way in which they used to in antiquity; an original recreation of Inca traditions. Also, residents of the museum include llamas, sheep and alpacas, and they can be fed! Upon arriving in Ollantaytambo visit its town, inhabited uninterruptedly since Inca times, and climb the iconic fortress, built next to a mountain. Later, have lunch in Ollantaytambo and live a superior Andean gastronomy experience in a restaurant, where the true spirit of the Sacred Valley is transmitted in a cosy atmosphere.! At the end of this activity, you are transferred back to your hotel.</p><p style="text-align: justify;">*On Sundays, also visit the local market.</p></div>

<p style="text-align: justify;"></p><div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel in Cusco to visit the town of Chinchero, a traditional Cusco urban centre located at an elevated point with a beautiful view of the valley. The inhabitants of this area preserve the artisan traditions and the inherited knowledge of the Incas and display them in their clothes and customs. In Chinchero there are also archaeological remains and a beautiful seventeenth-century church, one of the first Catholic buildings in Peru. Then, you are transferred from Ollantaytambo to the Living Museum of Yucay, a centre Andean traditions interpretations, where demonstrations of the elaboration of textile work, adobe, pottery and silverware are carried out in the same way in which they used to in antiquity; an original recreation of Inca traditions. Also, residents of the museum include llamas, sheep and alpacas, and they can be fed! Upon arriving in Ollantaytambo visit its town, inhabited uninterruptedly since Inca times, and climb the iconic fortress, built next to a mountain. Later, have lunch in Ollantaytambo and live a superior Andean gastronomy experience in a restaurant, where the true spirit of the Sacred Valley is transmitted in a cosy atmosphere. At the end of this activity, you are transferred back to your hotel. </div><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">*On Sundays, also visit the local market.</div>

<div style="text-align: justify;">The adventure begins with your transfer from your hotel to the starting point of the hike to the impressive coloured mountain range of Palcoyo. Alike the 7-colour mountain of Vinicunca, Palcoyo acquires this colouration due to minerals such as iron, magnesium, calcium, among others, accumulated over millions of years and that nowadays offer a visual spectacle. Unlike Vinicunca, getting to the top of the Palcoyo mountain range is of a moderate level and very accessible since it is a 2-hour walk without significant slopes, reaching a maximum elevation of 4,900 meters above sea level. Upon arrival, enjoy the beautiful view of the mountain range and take some minutes to calmly appreciate the surroundings thanks to the little flow of visitors in the area in comparison with the Vinicunca Mountain. This experience is carried following the established routes and regulations for its conservation, collaborating to the sustainability of this natural wonder. After the visit, board the transport to head towards Cusco making a stop at the Qeswachaca Bridge, a Cultural Heritage of the Nation. This bridge is made of ichu plant. Every year, the inhabitants of the nearby communities carry out the weaving of a new bridge by hand. This tradition goes back to Inca times, as the Qeswachaca was part of the great network of Inca roads or Qhapaq Ñan. Finish your day arriving at your hotel in Cusco.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p><p>Level: Easy</p><p>Duration: 10 hours</p><p>Maximum altitude: Palcoyo 16000 feet</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Start your journey towards Maras, an impressive Inca salt exploitation complex. These are large salt deposits, still in operation, from which salt was extracted and exchanged for products from other parts of the Empire. Next, visit Moray to contemplate the mysterious circular concentric platforms that were found here. Some scholars believe the place used to be an agricultural research centre: the external platforms would have been at higher temperatures than the internal ones, simulating the conditions of the different ecological floors of the Andes. After this visit, you’ll be transferred to your hotel in the Sacred Valley of the Incas. Then head to a restaurant in the Sacred Valley for lunch and when finished return to your hotel in the valley. </div>

<p>Early morning departure from your hotel in Cusco to drive to the locality of Pachar, located 8 kms. from Ollantaytambo, where you will find the Via Ferrata, a climbing route  300 meters high.  Here you will have the opportunity to climb a mountain with a safety system permanently installed, for which you do not require any previous experience. While climbing,  enjoy the mystical landscapes of the Sacred Valley of the Incas. Upon completing the ascent, enjoy an energyzing snack while you admire the spectacular scenery and recover your energies.  Then climb 100 more mts  until you reach the second cable of the canopy or zip-line, which will allow you to fly over the beautiful landscapes of  the Sacred Valley of the Incas, in a very safe and fun way. The canopy or zip-line, also called  flying fox, zip wire, death slide, or aerial runway, is an unforgettable experience. Through a pulley suspended by 6 cables of 100 to 500 m (with a total of 2000 m), located in an inclination, you hook the cable through a pulley fixed  to your harness and you will be impulsed by gravity, sliding from the top to the bottom, increasingly losing speed as you approach the end of the cable. At the end of your adventure, transfer back to  the hotel.</p>

<p></p><div>Depart from the city of Cusco for one of the most spectacular hiking excursions there is through the Andes Mountains, and immerse yourself in the stunning scenery, rich culture and vast history of this area of the world. Make your way through the archaeological site of Tarawasi near the town of Limatambo (1.5 hours from Cusco), before continuing to the mountain village of Mollepata, taste the delicious locally-grown coffee and then continue to Soraypampa (3850 masl). From this point start the two-hour ascent to the Humantay Lagoon (above 4200 masl) and have lunch at the site. Enjoy one of the most impressive panoramas of the trip, where photo enthusiasts will have the opportunity to get wonderful shots. Finally, begin the descent to Soraypampa for approximately 1.15 hours, where a bus awaits you to return to Cusco.</div><p><span id="selectionBoundary_1550095951415_9608559678919417" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span><br/></p><div><br/></div><p>Level: Moderate</p><p>Duration: 12 hours</p><p>Maximum altitude: Humantay 13780 feet</p><p></p>
<p>A transportation will pick you up from your hotel and transfer you to the airport<br/></p>

<p>You will start your tour to the city, visiting Saint Francis Church and the Witches Market, a shopping area where it is possible to buy all the supplies that local healers use for their rituals. You will then go to Moon Valley, an area with strange clay geological formations caused by wind erosion on a mountain top.<br/></p>

<p>Early in the morning you will be transferred by road to Copacabana Sanctuary to board the catamaran cruise, where you will enjoy a snack while sailing to the Island of Sun, birthplace of the Inca Empire. In this legendary island visit the Garden, Steps and the Fountain of the Inca, and the Inti Wata Cultural Complex, the largest private tourist attraction in Bolivia, which includes the underground museum of Ekako, traditional medicine centers and construction of totora rafts, the Pachamama agricultural Inca terraces, a craft center, the Intica  shelter and the largest variety of Andean camelids in the &#34;Manco Kapac&#34; viewpoint. The visit to the Inti Wata Complex also includes the possibility to sail aboard a huge typical Totora raft and make a tour to the Pilkokaina Inca Palace. Later, return to the catamaran cruise to sail to the Port of Chua and along the way enjoy a buffet lunch. Then you will be transferred by road to the city of La Paz.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#1</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">During the morning you will arrive at the Biological Station, a place of learning for young people and professionals interested in conservation, research and ecotourism. After lunch and the check-in, you will take a tour through the trails of the station, surrounded by an impressive forest of vast biodiversity, with trees more than 35 meters high where birds, amphibians, primates and vegetation can be seen on day and night. During the evening you will go on a river tour.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#2</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">In the morning you will ascend to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway, where you can walk through the tree tops over 30 meters high, where hundreds of species have built their habitats. Afterwards, you will visit Sandoval Lake, a protected natural area that hosts flora and fauna.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#3</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">During the morning, you will go on a tour to see some of the most exotic birds in the world from a sandbank on the Madre de Dios River. After lunch, you will visit a &#34;native chacra&#34; (farm) where products such as herbs and tropical plants are grown using natural fertilisers.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#4</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">During the morning visit Palmetum, a natural area devoted to the conservation of vegetation. Afterwards, you will head to the Aguajales where you will walk through the bridge system from where you can see the imposing trees. After a 20-minute boat ride, you will arrive at the Gaminata Farm where you will enjoy the varied flavours of native fruits and where you will learn about agriculture in the region. Finally, visit the ¨Selva Oculta¨(Hidden Forest), a wildlife sanctuary.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#5</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">You will be transferred to Puerto Maldonado.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p>#1<br/>During the morning you will arrive at the Biological Station, a place of learning for young people and professionals interested in conservation, research and ecotourism. After lunch and the check-in, you will take a tour through the trails of the station, surrounded by an impressive forest of vast biodiversity, with trees more than 35 meters high where birds, amphibians, primates and vegetation can be seen on day and night. During the evening you will go on a river tour.<br/><br/>#2<br/>In the morning you will ascend to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway, where you can walk through the tree tops over 30 meters high, where hundreds of species have built their habitats. Afterwards, you will visit Sandoval Lake, a protected natural area that hosts flora and fauna.<br/><br/>#3<br/>During the morning, you will go on a tour to see some of the most exotic birds in the world from a sandbank on the Madre de Dios River. After lunch, you will visit a &#34;native chacra&#34; (farm) where products such as herbs and tropical plants are grown using natural fertilisers.<br/><br/>#4<br/>During the morning visit Palmetum, a natural area devoted to the conservation of vegetation. Afterwards, you will head to the Aguajales where you will walk through the bridge system from where you can see the imposing trees. After a 20-minute boat ride, you will arrive at the Gaminata Farm where you will enjoy the varied flavours of native fruits and where you will learn about agriculture in the region. Finally, visit the ¨Selva Oculta¨(Hidden Forest), a wildlife sanctuary.<br/><br/>#5<br/>You will be transferred to Puerto Maldonado.<br/></p>

During the</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">evening you will go on a river tour.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#2</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">In the morning you will ascend to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway, where you can walk through the tree tops over 30 meters high, where hundreds of species have built their habitats.
