After lunch you will visit a &#34;native chacra&#34; (farm) where products such as herbs and</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">tropical plants are grown using natural fertilisers. Finally, visit the ¨Selva Oculta¨ (Hidden Forest), a wildlife sanctuary.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#4</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">You will be transferred to Puerto Maldonado.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#1</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">During the morning you will arrive at the Biological Station, a place of learning for young people and professionals interested in conservation, research and ecotourism. After lunch and check-in, you will tour the parks trails, surrounded by an impressive forest of vast biodiversity, with trees over 35 meters high where birds, amphibians, primates and vegetation can be seen during day and night. At night, you will go on a river tour.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#2</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">In the morning you will ascend to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway, where you can walk through 30 metres high tree tops, where hundreds of species have built their habitats. Afterwards, you will visit Sandoval Lake, a protected natural area that hosts flora and fauna. Finally, visit the ¨Hidden Forest¨, a wildlife sanctuary.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">#3</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Transfer to Puerto Maldonado.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p>#1<br/>During the morning you will arrive at the Biological Station, a place of learning for young people and professionals interested in conservation, research and ecotourism. After lunch and check-in, you will tour the parks trails, surrounded by an impressive forest of vast biodiversity, with trees over 35 meters high where birds, amphibians, primates and vegetation can be seen during day and night. At night, you will go on a river tour.<br/>#2<br/>In the morning you will ascend to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway, where you can walk through 30 metres high tree tops, where hundreds of species have built their habitats. Afterwards, you will visit Sandoval Lake, a protected natural area that hosts flora and fauna. Finally, visit the ¨Hidden Forest¨, a wildlife sanctuary.<br/>#3<br/></p><p class="MsoNoSpacing"><span>During the last day visit the Butterfly Farm, the first breeder and butterfly exhibition centre in Peru. Here, discover a wide variety of Amazonian butterfly species as they fly through the garden or feed. After this educational experience, you are transferred to the airport to leave the city.</span></p>
<p>Dinner at Empedrada Lodge. Caral.<br/></p>
<p>Lunch at Empedrada Lodge. Caral.<br/></p>

<p>Pick up from the hotel at 4:00 am to leave towards of Quesoyuni. Visit Japura on the way, a small Andean community and have breakfast there. Hike towards Kairawiri, starting point of the trek to the impressive 7-Colour Mountain. Enjoy the natural spectacle that you have in front as well as the imposing mountains that also fill the landscape with colour. After the visit of between 30 and 45 minutes head down to Kairawiri to have a box lunch. Return by bus to Cusco and transfer to the hotel.</p><p><br/><!--StartFragment--><span> </span><span style="float: none;">Level: Challenging</span><br/><span style="float: none;">Duration: 15 hours approximately</span><br/><span style="float: none;">Maximum altitude: Vinicunca 17000 feet</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1</p><p>Your tour will start en route to Paracas City, located three hours South of Lima. Upon your arrival, you will go to the local airport to fly over the Nazca Desert. Nazca is a small city. It may be overlooked in the map if it were not for the mysterious figures engraved over the desert that have made it world famous. At the end of the flight, you will go to your hotel in Paracas.</p><div><div><br/>#2<br/><br/></div><div>You will start your tour in the early morning toward the Paracas pier to sail until the Ballestas Islands, home to a large population of sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species found in the archipelago, an ideal place for their reproduction and coexistence. You will then go to the Paracas National Reserve, an extraordinary place due to its multiple attractions. It is a natural reserve with beautiful geological formations, impressive coastal fauna and vestiges of a pre-Inca culture that dominated the desert to install a successful society in adverse conditions. You will finally return to your hotel.<br/><br/></div><div>#3<br/><br/></div><div>You will be transferred to Lima City and your hotel in the morning.<p><br/></p></div></div>

<p>Transfer to Parque del Amor, located in Malecon Cisneros in Miraflores, which was inaugurated in February of 1993 on the &#34;Day of Love&#34;, and whose central part exhibits a beautiful sculpture carved by Peruvian artist Victor Delfin, dedicated to love and whose name is &#34;The Kiss&#34;. The park also offers a magnificent view of the Pacific Ocean. Proceed then to the heart of Miraflores and enjoy a panoramic view of the archaeological site of Huaca Pucllana that operated between centuries V - VIII AD.  Proceed then to the Historic Center of Lima, and enjoy the Capital of Peru and its 2000 years of history and colonial wealth. Admire the Plaza Mayor, which has important architectural monuments and buildings, like the Archbishop of Lima with its elegant balconies, the Government Palace and its magnificent bronze fountain dating from 1650. Continue to the convent of San Francisco, a masterpiece of colonial architecture where you can appreciate the beauty of its monastic cloisters  decorated with tiles and remarkable paintings depicting the life of the holy founder of the Order, St. Francisco de Asis. In the underground galleries are impressive catacombs  whose purpose, according to popular histories, remains an enigma. Enjoy a delicious lunch at a local restaurant before proceeding to the district of Pueblo Libre to visit the  Larco Herrera Museum, a colonial mansion built in the eighteenth century which houses an extensive collection of ceramics from pre-columbian art and textiles worked with many materials and tools used  by the ancient Peruvians, as well as gold and silver pieces with semiprecious stones and pieces of erotic art. After the visit, transfer back to the hotel.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">#1</span></div><p><span style="text-align: left;float: none;"></span></p><div style="font-size: 13px;text-align: justify;">Transfer from Lima to Paracas by bus during the morning. After arriving in Paracas, you are transferred to the airport of Pisco to make a 45-minute to an hour flight by plane over the vast desert to observe the Nasca Lines; composed by enigmatic figures such as the monkey, the spider, the condor, among others. When finished, you are transferred back to your hotel in Paracas. Overnight at the hotel.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;"><br/></span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">#2</span><br/></div><p><font color="#000000"></font></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">After breakfast, a transportation service takes you to the boarding point for the boat ride to the Ballestas Islands; natural reserve of beautiful geological formations, impressive animal fauna and lags of pre-Inca cultures. The Ballestas Islands are inhabited by endemic birds, pelicans, sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species that find refuge in this area. At the end of the tour, you are transferred to your hotel and then to the bus station to return to the city of Lima.</span></div><p></p><p></p>

<div>Transfer from your hotel to Sacsayhuaman early in the morning. From here start the walk towards Chacán, known as El Balcón del Diablo or Devil´s Balcony. This place presents a perfect harmony between nature and Inca architecture, with constructions located in the rocks that seem to be one. At the top of the cliff, a crack works as a viewpoint of the whole site. The trek continues to Zone X, known for its natural rock labyrinths. After lunch en route, visit the Temple of the Moon; here you will learn more about the Andean cosmovision and its link with the stars and forces of nature. Later visit the archaeological complex of Cusilluchayoc, and after exploring it, undertake the return to Cusco, following the route of the old Inca road that went to the Antisuyo. Enter the city through San Blas, a neighbourhood known for its talented artisans, where the tour ends.</div><p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment--><span>-------------------------</span></p><p> <span style="float: none;"><b>Level:</b> Moderate</span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Duration: </b>8 hours</span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Maximum altitude: </b>Chacan 12358 feet / 3767 m a.s.l.</span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Walking distance:</b> 6,2 miles / 10 km</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Transfer from your hotel to the pier of Paracas to start the journey to the Ballestas Islands; natural reserve of beautiful geological formations, impressive animal fauna and lags of pre-Inca cultures. The Ballestas Islands are inhabited by endemic birds, pelicans, sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species that find refuge in the archipelago. At the end of the tour, you are transferred back to the dock to return to your hotel.</span></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>Upon arriving at Cusco, a transportation service accompanied by a guide will pick you up from the airport and transfer you to the hotel. The guide will assist you with the check in and provide general information for your<br/>stay.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from your hotel to the Cathedral of Cusco. Dominating the already impressive colonial square of the city, this building is an exquisite example of Spanish architecture both inside and outside, as its enormous facade and imposing iron doors house an altar lined with gold, works of art and carved pillars. After this visit, get to know the amazing Temple of the Sun, known as Coricancha. This impressive complex of several levels is considered one of the best attractions of Peru, thanks to its mix of Inca and Spanish architecture, as well as a fascinating history as the most important temple of the Sun God, Inti; then as an administrative centre and finally as a colonial convent. Then, head to the Chocomuseo of Cusco, learn the process of making this sweet, participate in activities in the workshop and explore all the products that derive from chocolate. Upon finishing this delightful experience, visit the formidable Fortress of Sacsayhuaman. Located a short distance from the city, Sacsayhuaman is a great example of the stonework of the Incas, since they used giant blocks of all shapes, with a weight of up to 300 tons each. These were perfectly placed side by side to create an impenetrable and unmovable front. Inside, there are ancient water reserves, irrigation channels and other attractions that still bring locals and foreigners throughout the year and during the Inti Raymi festival. To end the day, visit the colourful ice cream shop Qucharitas, to taste ice cream, crêpes or natural soft drinks made from local ingredients.<br/></p>
<p>Entrance to Cusco Cathedral.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Entrance to Maras<br/></p>

<p>#1<br/>Today you will make a tour to discover the fascinating Sacred Valley. When finished, you will be taken to board a train to go to the town of Aguas Calientes to visit the impressive Inca citadel of Machu Picchu. At arrival at the station, you will be transferred along with your guide to your hotel where you will spend the night.<br/>#2<br/>Early in the morning you will be picked up from your hotel to go to Machu Picchu. First you will visit the beautiful mountain of Huayna Picchu, followed by the tour of the historic sanctuary of Machu Picchu. Then begin your return to the town of Aguas Calientes, where you will enjoy a delicious lunch at the chosen restaurant. Finally, you will be taken to the station to board the train to Ollantaytambo.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">#1</span></div><p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;"></span></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Depart from Cusco towards the Cloud Forest of Manu where the Andes disappear to make way for the Amazon basin. Observe landscapes with really impressive contrasts. After lunch en route, descend through the fantastic sites and see the transformation of the environment. After some spontaneous stops along the way to observe the local fauna, arrive at the comfortable Cock-of-the-Rock Lodge. At dusk, go in search of the Cock of the Rocks (Rupicola peruvianus), to observe the bustling cortege of the national bird of Peru.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Wake up early to go in search of birds and Capuchin monkeys on a short trail to the river. After breakfast, continue the trip observing how the mountains give way to hills and farmland. Visit the Town of Patria to see the coca plantation, legally grown for the Peruvian market. At noon, arrive at Atalaya, a small river port where the Piñi-Piñi River joins the Alto Madre de Dios. Continue north up the waters of the Manu River, entering the deepest and richest part of the National Park. Go to the Romero Lodge to spend the night while listening to the singing of the tinamou, and the cicadas.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#3</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, travel again by boat to Camp Aguaje or Manu Park Wildlife Center, a simple but comfortable lodge. Its excellent location as well as its well-planned construction, make this a well-camouflaged place with low impact to the environment and almost invisible in the Manu forest. If time permits, take a short walk before dinner to enjoy the first encounter with the virgin forest tomorrow.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#4</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Today visit two lakes near the camp: Lago (Cocha) Salvador and, depending on the schedule, the Lake (Cocha) Otorongo. The Cochas or oxbow lakes are meander lakes that form when the rivers change their course, leaving behind the bend or an isolated channel. Cocha Salvador is the largest lake in the reserve, with a length of 3.5 km, about 2 miles. It is also the habitat of a resident family of giant otters. We surround the lake in a catamaran, which offers a new perspective of the lake and its connection with the jungle. The trees on the banks come alive with monkeys, macaws, herons, alligators, and more that inhabit them.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#5</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Leave at dawn downstream through the Manu River. At this time the opportunities for encounters with the fauna of the Manu are excellent. After 90 minutes downstream, arrive at the Manu Wildlife Center, the final stop on this exciting trip, around lunchtime. Then head to the Observation Tower located 34 meters / 112 feet high to witness a frenetic activity in the jungle canopy. Then continue to the famous Tapir Collpa, considered the most active known throughout the Amazon, where these animals feed on clay. This unusual meal absorbs and neutralises the toxins of the vegetarian diet of the Tapir, the largest terrestrial animal in Latin America. For a better view, enter the 30-meter long platform.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#6</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">After a delicious breakfast, walk down a trail through the woods to find the hideaway of the Macaw Collpa Project to observe scarlet macaw groups, flapping their wings and landing in the treetops. After lunch, rest and then leave for the Cocha or Lago. On the way, observe the fish, birds and mammals that live in the area. Returning to Manu Wildlife Center and before dinner, take advantage of the boat ride to look for alligators, and other nightlife along the riverbank.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#7</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Leave very early for 2 and a half hours by boat on the Madre de Dios River to Boca Colorado. Take advantage of this time to observe the spectacular nature of all the activity of the fauna along the river. This trip also allows us to see a variety of beautiful natural scenes of the lower jungle. Upon arrival disembark and start the trip to the Puerto Maldonado airport.</div><p></p><p></p>
<p>Cena en Qincha<br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Dinner at Paracas Luxury Collection</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Dinner at the Posada del Inca Yucay Hotel Restaurant<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour to Pisac to visit the handicraft market where every day the main square of the town is filled with dozens of stalls that display and sell products created by the inhabitants of the area. Outstanding alpaca textiles, musical instruments, paintings, carvings, necklaces and replicas of ancient Inca pieces. Every Sunday in the ancient church of Pisac the mass in Quechua is celebrated with the assistance of the seven caciques of the nearby communities, who typically dress and carry the &#34;rod&#34; or command scepter for which they are called &#34;Varayocs&#34;. Afterwards, you will have lunch in the agreed restaurant and then you will go to the citadel of Ollantaytambo, a picturesque Andean town that still conserves the Inca urban planning crowned by an imposing fortress with ascending terraces on the steep slope of the hill that supports it. The Inca Pachacutec ordered to burn the old site and rebuild a new one under the scheme of Inca architecture while his army made its way through the jungle to what later became Machu Picchu and Vilcabamba. At the end of your visit you will return to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, your journey to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride Aguas Calientes, also known as &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo&#34;, the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you’ll find a craft market, restaurants and, for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb early in the morning, hotels for all budgets. Take a short bus ride up to Machu Picchu, the architectural and engineering masterpiece that once served as a sanctuary and refuge for the Inca ruler Pachacútec. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is a UNESCO-listed World Heritage Site and one of the seven new wonders of the world. At the end of the tour, take a bus back to Aguas Calientes and have lunch at Café Inkaterra. In the afternoon you’ll be taken to Aguas Calientes station, where you’ll board the train back to Cusco. </div><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel to visit the town of Chinchero*, a traditional Cusco urban centre located at an elevated point with a beautiful view of the valley. The inhabitants of this place preserve the traditions and knowledge inherited from the Incas and display them in their clothes and customs. In Chinchero there are also archaeological remains and a beautiful seventeenth-century church, one of the first Catholic buildings in Peru. After this visit, you are transferred to the Living Museum of Yucay, making a stop at the Raqchi viewpoint. The museum is an interpretation centre of the Andean traditions, where demonstrations of the elaboration of textile work, adobe, pottery and silverware are carried out in the same way in which they used to in antiquity; an original recreation of the Inca traditions. Also, residents of the museum include llamas, sheep and alpacas, and they can be fed! Later, live an experience of superior Andean gastronomy in Chuncho restaurant, located in Ollantaytambo, where the true spirit of the Sacred Valley is conveyed in a cosy atmosphere. Then, visit the town and archaeological site of Ollantaytambo, inhabited uninterruptedly since Inca times, and explore the iconic fortress, built next to the mountain. Finally, be transferred back to your hotel.<br/></p><p>* The visits that take place on Sundays include the Chinchero market.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Arrive to the rural community of Huilloc in the province of Urubamba, where you will have the opportunity to share the educational process from the Andean worldview of children in the community. Approach the culture of parenting, which exists in reciprocity between men, nature and deities. You may also explain your worldview, showing some elements of your own culture such as your original clothes and your vision for the future. The intercultural interpreter will welcome you to the community accompanied by musicians and guide you to the inside of the Huilloc Education Center (Yachaywasi), where you will be received by the director or teacher in charge. There you will meet the children who will join you on your experience. Then go to the Interpretation Center, where children will show you the Path of Clothing. They will explain the role of the garments, its spiritual meaning, iconographic meaning and color. You will appreciate the tools and the original seeds sown in the fields, and food recipes that are prepared based on these products. In this place you can see trout breeding, which is part of the diet of children in the school. You will have the opportunity to acquire samples of bibliographic production to enrich your experience and to encourage and strengthen the educational community to continue producing texts of cultural information. Finally, you can help to enrich the Quqawa (snack) to maintain a good nutrition for children of Huilloc Ayllu Inka.<br/></p>
<p>A mobility will pick you up from your hotel and will be transferred to Tumbes airport.<br/></p>

<p>A vehicle will pick you up from your hotel in Urubamba and transfer you to the Huilloc community, a settlement which has sought to attract Cuscos tourism industry by promoting live experiences. After getting to know the community and its customs you will be transferred back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Your tour begins on the way to Pachar, in the Sacred Valley of the Incas. From there, after instructions from the guides, you will board a boat to travel the river for two hours in an exciting rafting experience. The ride ends at the community of Cachiccata, where you may have a snack. From there, you will begin the return to your hotel.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>Lunch for guide at the Posada del Inca Yucay hotel<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">#1 </div><div style="text-align: justify;">Visitors will embark on one of the most amazing routes in the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful Andean Explorer. The train has two dining cars, lounge wagon and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the Sierra. It also has elegant suites and cabins where passengers can rest comfortably. The journey begins leaving Puno towards Cusco. Enjoy a delicious lunch made of fresh, local ingredients, designed by the chefs of Belmond Hotel Monasterio. Then visit La Raya, the border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by towering mountains. Dinner will be served on board and arrival to the Cusco region is set at night. </div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2 </div><div style="text-align: justify;">During the second and last day of the cruise, you will enjoy breakfast on board and arrive to Cusco.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;"><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Day 1</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Visitors will embark on one of the most incredible routes of the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful Andean Explorer. The train has 2 dining cars, a lounge car and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the mountains. It also has elegant suites and cabins where the passenger can rest comfortably. The crossing begins leaving Cusco in the direction of Puno. You will enjoy a delicious lunch and then make the first stop in Raqchi; admirable archaeological site with remains of Temples and Inca constructions. The trip will continue and after tea time, you will be delighted with the sunset at La Raya, the border between Cusco and Puno, surrounded by imposing mountains. Dinner will be served on board and you will arrive at the Lake Titicaca station at night.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Day 2</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Enjoy the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and breakfast will be served on board. During the rest of the day you can optionally choose between visiting the Uros Islands in a typical boat, visiting the Taquile Island, having lunch in a private area on the beach or show of typical dances and demonstration of handicrafts.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/></span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Upon arrival at the bus station, you will be met by one of our service representatives and transferred to the airport.<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Belmond Sanctuary Lodge.<br/></p>

<p>Lunch at Casa  Hacienda  Orihuela, where the Orihuelas, a family of great collectors, will be your hosts. The house has  a  varied  collection  of popular  and colonial art ceramics, paintings, weapons, photography, ceremonial  vessels  and the  ceramic  bulls  of  Pucara. The environs include a  forest  of eucalyptus trees and other native species and here the famous giant white corn is cultivated for export.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Delight yourself with the surprising variety of recipes prepared under the careful tutelage of renowned chefs. The ingredients used come from the fertile Sacred Valley of the Incas, and are rich in nutrients and flavour.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#1</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Arrival to the city of Iquitos. A representative waits for your arrival at the airport. From here, the journey starts towards Nauta, a small riverside town on the banks of the Marañón River. Once there, you are welcomed at the private port. When boarding, enjoy a fantastic panoramic view from the observation area. Participate in an orientation talk and get to know the entertainment centre that offers movies, as well as an extensive library of books about flora and fauna of the area. The ship also has a fully equipped bar where you can try any regional or international combination of liquors and aromas. Dinner is served during the afternoon.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#2</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Wake up early to appreciate one of the most beautiful areas of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve: Fundo Casual. The walk through this area will take you to the depths of the Terra Firme jungle (un-flooded forest) where you can appreciate the local fauna. After lunch on the boat, cross through the Yanayacu and Pucate rivers, passing through different streams and lagoons. This is one of the best preserved and protected areas of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve. If you want to interact directly with the waters of the Amazon, go out in one of the kayaks or paddle boards; and you will even have the opportunity to swim with the curious pink dolphins. Return to the boat and dinner on board.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#3</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, pass through the Samiria River and its tributaries, where wildlife such as blue and yellow parrots, red parrots, toucans, umbrella birds, monkeys, capybaras and more abound. This is also a place where dolphins usually feed, so you can see them up close. After lunch, visit Nauta Caño where you have the opportunity to sail in kayak. Also go fishing for the famous piranha. Return to the boat where dinner is served.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#4</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">During the morning disembark in the port of Nauta and head towards the airport. On the way, visit the Floating Mammals Rescue and Rehabilitation Centre to learn about manatees and other rescued species, as well as plans for the species conservation. Finally, arrive at the airport. You are accompanied by a representative who assists you on the way to your departure from the city.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;"><b></b></p>

<p>#1<br/><br/>Your tour will start on the way to the bus station from where you will depart to the town of Paracas, in an approximate time of four hours, where you will find the Paracas Natural Reserve, an extraordinary site due to the many attractions. It is a natural reserve of beautiful geological formations, impressive animal fauna and remnants of a pre-Inca culture that dominated the desert to install a successful society in adverse conditions. You will spend the night in the Paracas peninsula.<br/><br/><br/><br/>#2<br/><br/>Early in the morning, you will head to the port to sail to the Ballestas Islands, home to a large population of sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species found in the archipelago, an ideal place for their reproduction and coexistence. In the afternoon you will move to Nasca where you will spend the night.<br/><br/><br/><br/>#3<br/><br/>Nasca is a small town. Perhaps it would go unnoticed on a map if it were not for the mysterious lines engraved on the desert, which have given it worldwide fame. You will be transferred to the airport to board a plane to fly over the Nasca lines (subject to weather conditions). Then it will go to the city of Arequipa.<br/></p>

<p>It will begin its journey in the archaeological remains of Maras an impressive complex of salt exploitation that were used to extract salt and exchange it for other products with other parts of the empire. Then visit Ollantaytambo, a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited as in the past. You will find in it a great archaeological complex that although it receives the name of &#34;fortress&#34; it was a &#34;tambo&#34;, a city of rest and accommodation for comitivas that traveled long distances. After lunch in the agreed restaurant you will visit the citadel of Pisac, a village built in the shape of a partridge known for two reasons: for the handicraft market, established between colonial buildings, and for housing one of the most impressive platforms of the valley. The market consists of dozens of stands that display and sell products worked by the residents of the area. After visiting this point you will return to your hotel in Cusco.<br/></p>

<p>Enjoy a cultural tour to the Barranco district with two shifts, one in the morning and another in the afternoon.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p>AM</p><p><br/></p><p>You will be picked up from your hotel in the morning and taken to Barranco, to visit the Osma Museum where you will see a wide collection of Peruvian colonial art. Then you will go to the Mario Testino Art Gallery (MATE), where the famous Peruvian photographer exhibits his best photographs which have become known worldwide. Then visit Las Pallas, a craft and traditional</p><p><br/></p><p>folk art shop, where you will run out of words with the wide and colorful variety of pieces brought from artisanal communities around Peru. Finally, you will be taken to a restaurant in Barranco for lunch, followed by a magical walk through the romantic Bridge of Sighs, which inspired the great Peruvian singer Chabuca Granda to compose one of her most iconic and well-known songs. Finishing the tour, you will be transferred to your hotel.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p>PM</p><p><br/></p><p>You will be picked up from your hotel in the afternoon and taken to a restaurant in Barranco for lunch, followed by a visit to the Museo de Osma where you will see a wide collection of Peruvian colonial art. Then you will go to the Mario Testino Art Gallery (MATE), where the famous Peruvian photographer exhibits his best photographs which have become known worldwide. Then visit Las Pallas, a craft and traditional folk art shop, where you will run out of words with the wide and colorful variety of pieces brought from artisanal communities around Peru. Finally, you will be taken to the Bridge of Sighs to enjoy a romantic walk in this magical place, which inspired the great Peruvian singer Chabuca Granda in composing one of her most iconic and well-known songs. Finishing the tour, you will be transferred to your hotel. </p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p>Guide Travel Allowance - 3 meals<br/></p>
<p>Transportation at disposal within Cusco city<br/></p>
<p>Today you can choose any attraction you prefer from Cusco City and a guide will be at your disposal to accompany and guide you for half a day.</p><p><br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will pick you up from the Ollantaytambo train station at the established time and will transfer you back to your hotel in Cusco.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p>Enjoy four days and three nights in the charming hotel Titilaka. The hotel is located on a private peninsula of Lake Titicaca and has a spectacular view. Upon arrival, you will be transferred from the airport to the hotel where you will enjoy a delicious lunch. Then you will have free time where you can choose one of the various activities offered, which include paddling around the lake, riding a bicycle, walking or sailing; and has guided excursions by lake and land, where you can explore the ecological and cultural diversity that characterizes this region. You can visit the Market of Acora, Lampa, Sillustani and the Community of Atuncolla, or participate in a bird watching, trekking to Kolla (private) and visit temples such as Juli and Pomata. Excursions to Tiahuanaco (Bolivia) and Isla del Sol and Luna have an additional cost. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and overnight.<br/><br/>#2 </p><p>Breakfast at the hotel and then have free time during the morning where you can choose one of the various activities offered, which include rowing the lake, ride a bicycle, walk or surf; and has guided excursions by lake and land, where you can explore the ecological and cultural diversity that characterizes this region. You can visit the Market of Acora, Lampa, Sillustani and the Community of Atuncolla, or participate in a bird watching, trekking to Kolla (private) and visit temples such as Juli and Pomata. Excursions to Tiahuanaco (Bolivia) and Isla del Sol and Luna have an additional cost. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and overnight. After lunch you can also choose between these activities. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and overnight.<br/><br/>#3 </p><p>Breakfast at the hotel and then have free time during the morning where you can choose one of the various activities offered, which include rowing the lake, ride a bicycle, walk or surf; and has guided excursions by lake and land, where you can explore the ecological and cultural diversity that characterizes this region. You can visit the Market of Acora, Lampa, Sillustani and the Community of Atuncolla, or participate in a bird watching, trekking to Kolla (private) and visit temples such as Juli and Pomata. Excursions to Tiahuanaco (Bolivia) and Isla del Sol and Luna have an additional cost. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and overnight. After lunch you can also choose between these activities. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and overnight. </p><p>#4 </p><p>After breakfast, you will be transferred to the airport.<br/></p>
<p>Transfer from hotel Titilaka to the airport.<br/></p>
<p>Guide Travel Allowance - 2 meals<br/></p>
<p>Today you may return to Machu Picchu with a guide at your disposal, to visit any of the attractions of the citadel.</p><p><br/></p>
<p><span>A guide will be at your disposal today to accompany and guide you.</span></p>
<p>Dinner at La Retama Restaurant<br/></p>
<p>Lunch at La Retama Restaurant<br/></p>
<p>Dinner at Tunupa restaurant<br/></p>
<p>Lunch at Tunupa Restaurant<br/></p>

<p>Located in unparalleled setting within the complex of an adobe pyramid built by the original inhabitants of Lima more than 1500 years ago; It is one of the biggest surprises in the city at dinner time. The restaurant is beautifully designed in a rustic colonial style; You can dine inside or outside, but the best point is without a doubt the covered terrace. The menu is creative, with spicy touches of the classic Peruvian Creole cuisine.<br/></p>
<p>Dinner at Cala Restaurant<br/></p>

<p>La Rosa Nautica, is located on the No. 4 spigon of the Lima beach circuit in the district of Miraflores; is an elegant Victorian style restaurant that offers the most complete selection of Peruvian and international cuisine, as well as the most sophisticated bar on the shores of the Peruvian Pacific. All this combined with an incomparable view and the peace that can only be found where the infinite begins. Enjoy a delicious dinner.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, your journey to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride Aguas Calientes, also known as &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo&#34;, the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you’ll find a craft market, restaurants and, for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb early in the morning, hotels for all budgets. Take a short bus ride up to Machu Picchu, the architectural and engineering masterpiece that once served as a sanctuary and refuge for the Inca ruler Pachacútec. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is a UNESCO-listed World Heritage Site and one of the seven new wonders of the world. At the end of the tour, take a bus back to Aguas Calientes and have lunch at Café Inkaterra. In the afternoon you’ll be taken to Aguas Calientes station, where you’ll board the train back to Cusco. </div><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will participate on a walking tour starting at the Coricancha Temple, one of the most important buildings in the city. It was one of the most important centers of worship to the Sun God of the Incas that was <span style="text-align: justify;">converted into a  convent after the arrival of the Spanish. Then visit the wonderful Cusco Cathedral, built between 1560 and 1664, and end the tour visiting the San Pedro market, a pantry of thousands of products from different areas of the region. Finally, you will return to your hotel.</span></p><div> * The visit to San Pedro market can be replaced by a visit to Casa Concha, an old colonial mansion that houses an impressive collection of Inca pieces from Machu Picchu.<p><br/></p></div>
<p>An additional car will be available to take you to the airport or from the airport to your hotel. </p>

<p>#1</p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel to visit the town of Chinchero*, a traditional Cusco urban centre located at an elevated point with a beautiful view of the valley. The inhabitants of this place preserve the traditions and knowledge inherited from the Incas and display them in their clothes and customs. In Chinchero there are also archaeological remains and a beautiful seventeenth-century church, one of the first Catholic buildings in Peru. After this visit, you are transferred to the Living Museum of Yucay, making a stop at the Raqchi viewpoint. The museum is an interpretation centre of the Andean traditions, where demonstrations of the elaboration of textile work, adobe, pottery and silverware are carried out in the same way in which they used to in antiquity; an original recreation of the Inca traditions. Also, residents of the museum include llamas, sheep and alpacas, and they can be fed! Later, live an experience of superior Andean gastronomy in Chuncho restaurant, located in Ollantaytambo, where the true spirit of the Sacred Valley is conveyed in a cosy atmosphere. Then, visit the town and archaeological site of Ollantaytambo, inhabited uninterruptedly since Inca times, and explore the iconic fortress, built next to the mountain. The tour then finishes and you will take the train service from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes. </span></p><p>* The visits that take place on Sundays include the Chinchero market.<br/></p><p>#2</p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast you will be picked up for your tour of World Wonder, Machu Picchu. We suggest you get the early shuttle bus up the winding road to Machu Picchu Citadel. At 10 am you will meet your guide at the entrance of Machu Picchu (Shared basis). They will pass on articulate insights into the complexities of the architecture, the significance and the fine intricacies of one of the world’s most profound and fascinating civilizations. You will have some free time to wander around the ruins at your leisure. By then you will have work yourselves up an appetite. After lunch and at the designated time you will make your way down on the shuttle bus to Aguas Calientes where you will then take the train back to Ollantaytambo train station, where you will be met and transferred back to your comfortable Cusco hotel where you will overnight.</span></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at your hotel you will be picked up for your full day tour of the Sacred Valley of the Incas (Shared basis). Along the way we will stop at Awanacancha, a small complex to admire Andean camelids such as Alpacas and Llamas. We will learn about dying techniques from the locals. Later we will explore the Pisac Markets - the biggest arts and crafts markets in South America. We will then enjoy lunch at a local restaurant. In the afternoon our tour continues to Ollantaytambo. Ollantaytambo is a small town full of local colour and inhabited since Inca times. We will visit the main temple which was used as a fortress during the Inca resistance. A great place to take some amazing photos. The tour then finishes and you will take the train service from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes. </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p>#2 </p><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast you will be picked up for your tour of World Wonder, Machu Picchu. We suggest you get the early shuttle bus up the winding road to Machu Picchu Citadel. At 10 am you will meet your private guide at the entrance of Machu Picchu. They will pass on articulate insights into the complexities of the architecture, the significance and the fine intricacies of one of the world’s most profound and fascinating civilizations. You will have some free time to wander around the ruins at your leisure. By then you will have work yourselves up an appetite. After lunch and at the designated time you will make your way down on the shuttle bus to Aguas Calientes where you will then take the train back to Ollantaytambo train station, where you will be met and transferred back to your comfortable Cusco hotel where you will overnight.<br/></p>
<p>Dinner at the Ayasqa restaurant in the Sacred Valley<br/></p>

<p>#1<br/><br/>Today he will tour the Peruvian highlands on a journey that will take him through wonderful landscapes in Arequipa, Puno and Cusco. It will leave from Arequipa station in the direction of Puno. You will enjoy a cocktail and later you will have dinner on board.<br/><br/><br/>#2<br/><br/>Witness the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and continue the journey. Breakfast will be served on board and after this, you will visit the Uros Island, a set of floating islands made of totora, plants that grow in the lake. Then you will visit Taquile Island, from where you will have a wonderful view of Titicaca. Lunch will be given at the Collata Beach, belonging to the island and you will enjoy a demonstration of typical dances and the handicraft work of the inhabitants of the island. Later, you will be transferred to the Titicaca Lake station, a tea will be served and you will start your way to Cusco. After a cocktail, dinner will be served on board. During the night you will arrive at the Marangoni station.<br/><br/><br/>#3<br/><br/>After breakfast on board, you will continue your journey departing from Marangoni station in the direction of Cusco. On the way you will make a stop at Raqchi, an archaeological site also known as the Temple of Viracocha, where you can see Inca warehouses and parts of the main building. You will arrive at the Cusco station at about noon.<br/></p>

A transportation service and a guide will pick you up at the bus station at the agreed time to take you to your hotel. The guide will assist you with the check in.

<p>Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a train ride from the Ollanta train station, to the town of Aguas Calientes. In Aguas Calientes you will find the train station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and go up early to it. After a short trip you will arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resting place for the Pachacutec Inca. Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. At the end of the tour you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes. Lunch at Café Inkaterra &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo. You will enjoy free time in the afternoon. Overnight at the hotel of your choice in Aguas Calientes.<br/></p>

<p>You will start your tour en route to the Pisac market, which has scores of stands that exhibit and sell products manufactured by the residents of the area everyday. Beautiful alpaca textiles, musical instruments, paintings, carvings, necklaces and replicas of old Inca pieces stand out. You will continue to the Pisac archaeological vestiges, which as the largest complex of terraces in the area and a masterpiece of the agriculture of the Inca Empire. Next, you will have lunch at the previously agreed restaurant and then you will visit the Ollantaytambo Park, making a series of tops at points of interest. The Ollantaytambo town, a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas, whose residents still live in accordance with their old traditions. Pachacútec (1438-1471) conquered the town, destroyed it and built a new one, absorbing it as part of his empire. It exhibits one of the most remarkable complexes of the Inca Empire. You will return to your hotel in Cusco at the end of the tour.<br/></p>
<p>Bottle of water at Inka Grill<br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Inka Grill restaurant.<br/><br/></p>
<p>Bottle of water at Machu Picchu Pueblo for lunch or dinner.<br/></p>

<p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment-->Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo&#34; and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you will find the railway station, a crafts market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572).  Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. In the afternoon return by train.</p>

<p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment-->You will visit the Chinchero town to participate in a textile workshop. You will meet with native weavers who will share their traditional weaving techniques with you at the Traditional Textile Center. You will attend a private demonstration presented by neighboring towns of weavers affiliated to the Center, which will show you the manufacturing process and the final garment. Each garment is really unique, a gem that is the result of a methodical and meticulous work. The local techniques are old and institutions such as this have committed to promote their preservation for the enjoyment of future generations. Natural elements and landscapes, such as mountains, stars and rivers are shown in opaque colors, exclusively obtained from natural dyes. The wool is carded, spun and dyed to create ponchos, belts and shawls. You will then enjoy the return trip to Cusco among the mountains to Cusco city.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Receive pottery classes by Pablo Seminario, a master craftsman who has studied for many years the techniques and designs of pre-Columbian pottery, incorporating them into his own art, creating unique pieces. The workshop includes tools and materials for you to develop a small piece, besides witnessing a theoretical and practical class that will help discover your artistic side.<span id="selectionBoundary_1559593893098_8596803899742975" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span></div><p><br/></p>
<p>Bottle of water at Inkaterra Hacienda Urubamba.<br/></p>

<p>A transportation service will pick you up from the hotel for your half day tour in Cusco. Start visiting the traditional and bohemian neighborhood of San Blas, residence of artists and craftsmen. Discover its streets, between bars, shops and art galleries. Then you will be transferred to the dynamic market of San Pedro, which has a lot of stalls selling local products such as different varieties of potatoes, traditional Andean food, flowers, among many others. When finished, go to the main square and visit the Cathedral and the Convent of Santo Domingo, which was built on top of the most important temple of the Inca Empire: Coricancha. Finally, visit the impressive fortress of Sacsayhuaman, where the Inti Raymi (Festival of the Sun) is made, on June 24 of each year. At the end of the tour you will be transferred to Inka Grill restaurant for lunch and later back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Pick up from the hotel at 4:00 am towards Pitumarca, starting point of the walk, arriving at around 7:00 am. Once there, have breakfast and when finished, get ready to trek to the impressive 7-Colours Mountain. At arrival, enjoy the natural spectacle in front of you as well as the imposing mountains that also fill the landscape with colour. After the visit of between 45 minutes and an hour descend back to Pitumarca for lunch. Transfer to Cusco during the afternoon.</span></div>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from the hotel to Market 1 of Surquillo, a popular market that offers an immense variety of fruits from different regions of the country. After this visit, the tour continues to the historic centre, starting in Casa Aliaga, an old residence of the Viceroyalty handed over by Francisco Pizarro in 1535 to one of its captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga), after the foundation of the city. Later, stroll through Lima´s Main Square to see its main buildings and walk a short distance to the historic Cordano Bar, a meeting place of presidents, ministers, and congressmen since its founding in 1905, to taste classic appetisers. Transfer to the hotel when finished.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Live a relaxing experience in the Piuray lagoon. Participate in the briefing and then start a 15-minute yoga session, after which the equipment according to the chosen activity (Paddle or Kayak) is delivered. Enter the lagoon and paddle for an hour to an hour and a half through the gentle waters. Bicycles are available for a ride around the lake after the water activities. Later enjoy the Andean buffet based on native potatoes, sweet potatoes, chicken, vegetables and other local ingredients that are cooked in a clay oven, giving them a unique touch.</span></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Transfer from your hotel to downtown Lima. Visit Casa Aliaga, an old residence of the viceroyalty handed over by Francisco Pizarro in 1535 to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga). Then visit the Santo Domingo Convent, built during the 16th century. Transfer to the Casona San Marcos, cultural centre belonging to San Marcos University, the oldest in America, where </span>artistic<span style="font-size: 13px;"> work is exhibited, and dance and music workshops are given. Then enjoy lunch at the Huaca Pucllana restaurant, from where you have a privileged view of the archaeological complex. Transfer to Barranco, the bohemian district of Lima, known for its bars, museums, galleries and urban art. Visit the Dédalo gallery, where craft and artwork by local artists are offered. Afterwards, visit Larco Museum, located in the traditional district of Pueblo Libre. It was founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926 and is located inside a viceregal mansion. The museum has an impressive pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver pieces, as well as erotic art. Transfer to the hotel at the end of the tour.</span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">After a briefing, get on a tubular to travel the dunes of Ica for 25 minutes and live an adventure full of adrenaline. After crossing the ups and downs of the desert, reach one of the highest dunes to appreciate the landscape and take pictures. Also, practice sandboarding in this location. Later, admire the sunset from a prime spot while enjoying iced champagne, snacks, fruit, and drinks, until dusk. If weather permits, enjoy the impressive starry sky. Return to the base at the end of the experience.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">The historic centre of the capital of Peru was declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and keeps an impressive architectural legacy with places such as the landmark Casa Aliaga, the Cathedral and the Plaza Mayor. The tour begins at Casa Aliaga, an old residence of the viceroyalty handed over by Francisco Pizarro in 1535 to one of his captains, Jerónimo de Aliaga, after the city´s foundation. This house is the only one of the time that still belongs to the same family to this day. Continue to the Basilica and Convent of San Francisco to visit its beautiful courtyards and catacombs. Later, head to the historic Cordano bar, a meeting place of presidents, ministers, and congressmen since its foundation in 1905, to taste classic Lima appetisers. Transfer to the hotel when finished.</span></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from the hotel to the Historic Centre of Lima. The tour begins at the Basilica and Convent of San Francisco to visit its beautiful courtyards and catacombs. Later, head to Huaca Pucllana, a ceremonial centre formed by a truncated pyramid and plazas that belonged to the Lima culture. Transfer to the hotel at the end of the tour.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>

<p>#1<br/><br/>You will land at the International Airport of Iquitos and after having collected your luggage, you will board an air-conditioned bus to be transferred to the main port of Iquitos where the cruise awaits you. While settling in your spacious room, the ship will begin the journey across the Yanayacu River, deep in the Amazon. A meeting will be held on the upper deck to inform about safety on board. Next, the Cruise Director and naturalist guides will give a brief orientation talk about life on board, about the places you will go and what you will see during your trip through the Amazon. Then, enjoy the welcome dinner while watching the river and surroundings through panoramic windows. Before retiring to rest, you can have a cocktail on the upper deck, under the stars.<br/><br/><br/>#2<br/><br/>You will wake up early to enjoy the beautiful view of the sunrise over the Amazon River. After a delicious breakfast, with a wide variety of dishes, Peruvian specialties and fresh fruit juices, board a comfortable boat to sail to the Yacapana Islands, called &#34;the Islands of the Iguana&#34;, for the large number of these reptiles that inhabit in them. You will walk very close to the islands and you will have the opportunity to take good pictures of the flora and fauna that you will observe along the way. You can also do sport fishing for the famous &#34;piranhas&#34;, for which our nature guides will carry the proper implements. While the ship continues its journey through the Amazon River, you will have free time to rest or take a nap before lunch. Back to the ship, you will enjoy a light lunch in an air conditioned dining room and then the guides will give a talk about flora and fauna. As the sun sets, you can photograph the gray and pink freshwater dolphins, and there is also the possibility of observing some macaws, toucans, sloths and monkeys on the way. Finally, you will return to the ship for dinner, but before we recommend you drink as a snack a Pisco Sour on the upper deck, while watching the sunset.<br/><br/><br/>#3<br/><br/>After breakfast, the naturalist guide will tell you about sustainable development projects in the Amazon. You will visit one of the projects, where villagers plant palm trees to obtain oil. Your guide will also show you the artificial nests created in the sand to protect turtle eggs. Then you will enter the jungle to find wildlife and learn firsthand about the value of medicinal plants in the jungle. You will see the famous Ceiba trees, the highest in the Amazon. Then, you will return to the cruise for lunch and after taking a nap, you will start an expedition to appreciate the two different types of freshwater dolphins and a great variety of primates. Among the 13 species of monkeys resident in the national park, there are squirrel and howler monkeys, marmosets and pygmy monkeys. Observe the lizards and keep your binoculars ready as you will see more than 200 species of birds. Finally, you will return to the cruise for dinner.<br/><br/><br/>#4<br/><br/>After breakfast, you will navigate the boundaries of the vast Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, a dense vegetation that covers five million acres in the tributaries of the Amazon basin. Get up early and be ready in your boat at 5:30 am to see one of the biggest shows in the southern hemisphere: sunrise over the source of the Amazon River. Here, at the junction of the two largest tributaries, the Ucayali and the Marañón, the greatest waterfall in the world is formed. Your morning excursion will be accompanied by abundant bird sounds, while you take a boat ride, crossing riverbanks populated by long-beaked swallows, laughing hawks, exotic short-tailed parrots and dozens of other birds. You will travel by boat to the native town of Puerto Prado, where you can meet and interact with local residents of this remote part of the world, as well as learn a little more about their culture, traditions and folklore. You can also exchange goods for local crafts. After returning to the ship for lunch, you will go to Nauta Caño, part of the Pacaya Samiria Reserve, also known as the forest of mirrors, due to the black waters of its lagoons, which reflect one of the most beautiful scenery in the world. nature. In this area we will observe different species such as: toucans, macaws, iguanas, etc. Return to the cruise, you will enjoy one of the most incredible sunsets reflected in the black waters of the flooded forest. While having dinner, you will start the journey through the Marañón River.</p><p><br/></p><p>#5<br/><br/>Tras desayunar, subirá a la lancha para explorar el fascinante río Marañón. Los guías le hablarán acerca de la selva y sobre la ´galería de la selva´, como lo llaman los científicos, debido a su tupida vegetación, creando un túnel de hojas. Mientras flota por este túnel, podrá observar fácilmente loros, guacamayos, y peludos monos frailes amazónicos muy cerca de las lanchas. Luego de regresar al crucero para almorzar. iniciará una caminata con los guías hacia poblados nativos, en donde podrá apreciar a los habitantes efectuando sus labores diarias y le enseñarán cómo ellos aprendieron a cultivar plantas salvajes y árboles para generar sus ingresos. Dentro de la selva primaria, los guías también le enseñarán el valioso aporte de los árboles frutales y las plantas medicinales que se encuentran ocultos en la selva. Finalmente, regresará al crucero para cenar.<br/><br/><br/>#6<br/><br/>Tras desayunar, el guía naturalista le contará acerca de proyectos de desarrollo sostenible en la Amazonía. Visitará uno de los proyectos, en donde los pobladores plantan palmeras para obtener aceite. Su guía también le mostrará los nidos artificiales creados en la arena para proteger los huevos de tortugas. Luego se adentrará en la jungla para encontrar vida salvaje y aprender de primera fuente acerca del valor de las plantas medicinales en la Selva. Observará los famosos árboles de Ceiba, los más altos de la Amazonía. Luego, regresará al barco. Ese día hay otra opción en las actividades. Tras desayunar, realizará la misma caminata, pero luego regresará a almorzar al barco, seguido de una siesta. En la tarde, a la caída del sol, podrá observar animales silvestres incluyendo a los monos frailes, a los monos capuchinos y a los monos aulladores. Cuando el sol caiga, usted escuchará la segunda sinfonía de la Selva Amazónica, cuando otros animales nocturnos emitan sus estridentes llamados a través de las copas de los árboles. De regreso al barco verá criaturas nocturnas, incluso, tal vez, grandes caimanes. Observe a los  lagartos y mantenga sus binoculares listos ya que verá más de 200 especies de aves. A continuación, se servirá la cena.<br/><br/><br/>#7<br/><br/>Al salir el sol, subirá a la lancha para apreciar un verdadero tesoro de vida salvaje, incluyendo loros, guacamayos, tucanes y gran variedad de monos, así como algunas especies que, estamos seguros, nunca habrá visto antes. Disfrute de un desayuno ligero a bordo, compuesto de emparedados y jugo fresco. Hará una parada cerca a un lugar de alimentación de delfines donde podrá observar un espectáculo inigualable mientras disfruta de su merienda. Toallas refrescantes esperan por usted, mientras se traslada de regreso al barco para continuar su viaje por el río Amazonas. Tendrá tiempo libre para descansar o tomar una siesta antes de almorzar y luego disfrutará de un almuerzo ligero en un comedor con aire acondicionado. Esta tarde abordará una lancha para navegar desde el río Amazonas a través del atajo del canal de Huaysi y explorar el río de aguas oscuras: el Tahuayo. Chalecos salvavidas, ponchos, botas de goma, y agua fresca estarán siempre disponibles en las lanchas. A medida que navegue a través del río Tahuayo, observará a algunos pescadores remando en sus canoas. Mantenga sus binoculares listos para ver una gran variedad de vida salvaje: golondrinas, aves, halcones de cuello negro, monos y osos perezosos. Luego de llegar al lago Charo, podrá hacer uso de sus instrumentos de pesca para tratar de atrapar alguna de las feroces pirañas, así como otras clases de peces. Mientras pesca, los guías naturalistas le contarán la historia del lago y de sus habitantes. De regreso al barco, habrá una charla a cargo de los guías naturalistas y disfrutará su cena final. Después de la cena, los guías le presentarán a algunos de los miembros de la tripulación que usted no ha conocido aún, quienes se han encargado del trabajo tras bastidores de esta inolvidable experiencia.</p><p># 8<br/><br/>After breakfast, and if the weather and weather permit, you will have one last chance to observe how the Amazonian peoples live visiting the riverside community of Bethlehem aboard a boat. Here the houses and shops are floating on the river for months. You will find the traditional food market where all the local inhabitants of the region meet in this area to sell and buy goods. Return to the cruise for lunch and leave your luggage in the room ready to be picked up. That afternoon, they will take him for a walk around Iquitos. You will visit the Amazon Wildlife Rescue Center, a sort of animal orphanage where manatees and Amazonian dolphins receive and provide them with specialized attention to save them and in the future reinsert them into their natural habitat. In addition, you will stop at the Mercado Indio Amazónico de San Juan, where you can buy some local crafts before leaving. Then, you will be transferred to the airport where the guides will help you with the registration and will remain with you until you have boarded your flight back to Lima.<br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">The historic centre of the capital of Peru was declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and keeps an impressive architectural legacy with places such as the landmark Casa Aliaga, the Cathedral and the Plaza Mayor. The tour begins at the Basilica and Convent of San Francisco to visit its beautiful courtyards and catacombs. Later, head to the historic Cordano bar, a meeting place of presidents, ministers, and congressmen since its foundation in 1905, to taste traditional Lima appetisers. In the afternoon, at sunset, you will be able to observe wild animals including monkeys, monkeys, capuchin monkeys and howler monkeys. This afternoon you will board a boat to navigate from the Amazon River through the Huaysi channel shortcut and explore the dark water river: El Tahuayo. Life jackets, ponchos, rubber boots, and fresh water will always be available on boats. As you sail through the Tahuayo River, you will observe some fishermen paddling in their canoes. In addition, you will stop at the Mercado Indio Amazónico de San Juan, where you can buy some local crafts before leaving.
