<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Travel through the jaw-dropping landscapes of the highland plateau. Stop en route at the Pukara complex, which dates from 200BC to 300AD. Have lunch at a local restaurant. After lunch, head towards Raqchi to admire the archaeological site. There is one more stop before Cusco at Andahuaylillas, a picturesque town famed for the beautiful 16th century Church of St Peter the Apostle. It boasts a gilded baroque interior and fresco paintings so delicate that it is known as the ‘Sistine Chapel of America’. After this visit, arrive at your hotel in the Sacred Valley.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p>Day 1 Bus to Paracas<br/>Take a four hour bus ride from Lima to Paracas, a beach town where the desert meets the ocean. Transfer to your hotel and enjoy the beautiful sunset over the sands of southwestern Peru.<br/><br/></p><p>Day 2 IslaS Ballestas and Paracas<br/>Take a short bus ride to El Chaco, the beach and fishing village near Paracas village. Join a boat ride to explore the arches and caves of the islands that play home to thousands of sea birds. You will also see lounging and active sea lions. From here you will continue on to Paracas National Reserve, where you will view even more of the many species of sea birds that grace the area. Flamingos can often be viewed feeding in Paracas Bay. You will get an opportunity to visit the fishing village of Launillas and the Paracas museum. Transfer to Nazca by Royal Class Bus Lines (4 hour bus ride) where a private vehicle transfers you to your hotel.<br/><br/></p><p>Day 3 Nazca Lines. Return to Lima<br/>You may take the option of the flight over the Nazca Lines or wait for the others on the ground. These mysterious lines date back to the Nazca period, between 900 BC and 600 AD. The images of the monkey, hummingbird and lizard are so large that they can only be fully viewed from the air. From here you will go to Cauchillas Cemetery where countless mummies are preserved in the desert. Pieces of cloth and ceramics, dating back to the time of the Nazca culture, rest near each of the mummies. Transfer back to Lima with a 8 hour bus ride, and then transfer to your hotel or the airport. Alternatively, travelers can continue from Nazca to Arequipa by overnight bus.<br/></p>

<p>#1<br/>Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a two-hour trip by train until the Aguas Calientes town. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo&#34; and it is the final stop before entering the vestiges of the most important Peruvian archaeological complex. In Aguas Calientes you will find a train station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodations of many different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb to the top in the early morning. After a short trip, you will finally arrive in Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture, which is believed to have been used as a sanctuary and leisure residence for Inca Pachacútec (1438-1471). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is a World Cultural Heritage listed by UNESCO and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.  You will spend the night in Aguas Calientes.<br/>#2<br/>Today you can return to Machu Picchu and you can optionally visit any attraction that the citadel has to offer. You can visit the Intipunku, which means &#34;Gateway of the Sun&#34; in Spanish, and it is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca Trail from Ollantaytambo. It was a sort of guardhouse and the Sun rises every day through that door if it is contemplated from the most important buildings of the citadel. At the same time, you can climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34;, as opposed to Machu Picchu. It takes around an hour to reach the top, and its main attraction is the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, an underground building complex probably conceived for the entertainment of the nobles. Finally, you will visit the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone construction which served to  enter and exit the citadel, and which shows a large void in the middle, over which the Incas used to place two wooden boards. Thus, the Incas were able to isolate the city by just removing those boards, protecting themselves. You will spend the night <span id="selectionBoundary_1519940224152_04029723932799767" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>in Aguas Calientes.<br/>#3<br/>Today, you will have the option of visiting the Intipunku (Gateway of the Sun), the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space) and the Inca Bridge. In the afternoon, you will be transferred back to the Aguas Calientes train station and you will take your return train.<br/></p>

<p>#1<br/>Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours time. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu pueblo&#34; and is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes are the train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by the UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world.  Overnight in Aguas Calientes.<br/>#2<br/>Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions of the citadel. Visit the Intipunku, which in Castilian means &#34;Sun Gate&#34; and is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. It was kind of a checkpoint control and the sun rises every day through this door if viewed from the most important buildings of the citadel. You may climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34; as opposed to Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is  the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, a complex of underground constructions  likely to have been used by the elite. Finally you will be able to know the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and halfway has a gap of several meters above which were two large wooden tables.  Thus the Incas could isolate the city by simply withdrawing them and thus remain protected.  In the afternoon head to the train station of Aguas Calientes and from there take the train back.<br/></p>

<p>Day 1 Arequipa<br/><br/>Located 7,750 feet above sea level and surrounded by snowy peaks, Arequipa delights visitors with its colonial charm and friendly inhabitants. Upon arrival at the Arequipa airport, you driver will meet you and take you to your hotel. The rest of the day is yours to explore the city of Arequipa on your own, as this day is unguided. Visit the Santa Catalina Monastery (a 4.5 acre maze of gardens, plazas, and churches in the town center), people-watch in the main plaza or just relax in our colonial hotel.<br/><br/><br/>Day 2-3 Colca Canyon<br/><br/>As rich in history as the Cusco area, Colcas pre-Inca terraces rise straight up the canyon and ancient tombs can be found perched on vertical canyon walls. Youll leave Arequipa early this morning for the deepest canyon in the world. The drive takes you back in time, past herds of llamas and alpacas and through 400-year old colonial towns where residents raise potatoes and tend sheep according to the rising and setting of the sun. During your two nights in the canyon take ample time to relax in the hot springs! One morning youll head to the Cruz del Condor where youll have your best chance to see the Andean condor. In the afternoon youll walk along the canyon edge to the pueblo of Cabanaconde for a taste of village life in the Colca.<br/><br/>An optional one-night homestay on Day 2 with a local family is available for travelers wanting to experience the daily and communal life in a Peruvian village. Travelers will return to the Colca Lodge for the night of Day 3.<br/><br/><br/>Day 4 Return to Lima</p><p><br/>Return to Arequipa in the afternoon, and travelers can either catch a flight to Lima or continue overland to Puno to visit Lake Titicaca. Alternatively, stay an extra night in Arequipa and fly to Cusco the next morning.<br/><br/></p>

<p>Day 1 Arrive Puno<br/></p><p><br/></p><p>A luxury-class bus takes us from Cusco to the shores of Lake Titicaca. Along the way, enjoy visits to the &#34;Sistine Chapel&#34; of Peru and a pre-Incan site, as well as a huge lunch featuring traditional Peruvian cuisine. </p><p><br/></p><p>Arrive at mystical Lake Titicaca in the early afternoon. Legend has it that the first Inca rose out of its azure waters. Transfer to your hotel and explore on your own this afternoon.</p><div><br/>Day 2 Islands of Lake Titicaca<br/><br/>Depart this morning with your guide to thet the floating islands of the Uros people. Perus floating islands are built using the buoyant totora reeds that grow along Lake Titicaca, and these reeds are interwoven to make homes, boats, and crafts.<br/>We continue to Amantani Island, a quiet island known for its hilltop ruins that honor Pachamama and Pachatata (Mother and Father Earth). Here we meet our host families whose generosity is apparent with their open arms (and open doors to their homes) and smiles. Explore this island with your guide and then settle in to share a short day in the lives of the Aymara.</div><div><br/>Day 3 Taquile Island | Return to Puno<br/><br/>After breakfast and bidding farewell to your host family, travel to Taquile Island. Climb the steps to the plaza at the center of the island for breathtaking views of the lake and surrounding mountains.<br/>In the afternoon, return to Puno and relax this evening on the shores of Lake Titicaca.</div><div><br/>Day 4 Fly Lima<br/><br/>Catch a flight to Lima today. For travelers choosing this Lake Titicaca extension prior to your visit to Cusco, the Sacred Valley, and Machu PIcchu, transfer by bus to Cusco.<br/></div>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Day 1</span></div><p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;"></span><div style="text-align: justify;">Arrive at the city of Iquitos and transfer to the port of Nauta, making a stop at the Amazon Rescue Center (CREA), a foundation dedicated to the rescue, rehabilitation and release of manatees. Once in Nauta, participate in an orientation and security talk and tackle the Amazon Star.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">During the afternoon, explore the Pawachiro stream by boat and enjoy the flora variety of the place. Upon return, the crew will present a musical activity. While eating dinner, the activities for the following day will be presented.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">Day 2</div><div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, enjoy a complete experience taking breakfast in a picnic on the boat by the Pucate River. The excursion begins with a boat exploration, observing the monkeys, birds, and the immense variety of fauna that inhabits the area. Then, you can enter the river to swim with pink dolphins, a unique species that live in the Amazon. As an optional activity, youll be able to fish piranhas. </div><div style="text-align: justify;">Return to the boat for lunch and listen to the guides conference about the source of the Amazon River, where you will find out the root of the most significant river in the world. During the afternoon, explore the Nauta Caño, with the option of travelling the stream in a kayak. Then, you will have dinner and participate in the informative talk preparing you for the next day. When the sun falls, you will leave on a night excursion.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">Day 3</div><div style="text-align: justify;">Breakfast on board. During the morning, go on a walking tour to have direct contact with nature. See the Victoria Regias, a unique plant from the Amazon and visit the local shaman of Puerto Prado, who will perform a ritual to charge you with good energy. Return to the boat for lunch outdoors and have a cooking class to learn more about the local gastronomy: its ingredients and preparation of its dishes. As an optional activity, you can have an origami class with towels. </div><div style="text-align: justify;">The last boat ride will depart during the afternoon to enjoy the colourful sunset while toasting with champagne. Back in the cruise, the crew will be presented; theyll perform a musical presentation and then you will have a farewell dinner where the itinerary for the next day will be explained.</div><div style="text-align: justify;">Day 4</div><div style="text-align: justify;">Wake up at 5:00 in the morning. Have breakfast at 5:15 and disembark in Iquitos by 6:00 am.</div></p><p></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Day 1<br/>Arrive at the city of Iquitos. Once there, begin the tour to some attractions in the area such as the Amazon Rescue Center (CREA), a foundation dedicated to the rescue, rehabilitation and release of manatees; the Museum of Amazonian Indigenous Cultures and Casa Morey, built between 1910 and 1913, an example of the ostentatious architectural style carried out during the rubber boom era.<br/>After the tour, transfer to the port from where the Amazon Star will set sail. Participate in an orientation and safety talk, and begin to navigate the Amazon River. In the afternoon, enjoy a welcome dinner while listening to the instructions of the activities for the following day.<br/>Day 2<br/>Early in the morning, you can head to the deck for an optional bird watching activity. Peru contains the second largest number of bird species in the world, and the Amazon is home to most of them. After breakfast, go on a boat exploration along the Amazon River and be impressed by the local vegetation and wildlife.<br/>Have lunch upon return and listen to a talk about life in the Amazon by local naturalists. Depart on a boat exploration on the Yarapa River, a tributary of the Ucayali River and a nature reserve protected by a local community. Then, the crew will offer a live musical presentation in the boat. Finally, have dinner and participate in the briefing for next day´s activities.<br/>Day 3<br/>Start the day at 6:00 a.m. with an optional bird watching activity from the deck. Then visit Yanallpa, a beautiful stream near the Ucayali River where you can appreciate the vegetation and local fauna. After breakfast, visit a school and a local town where you will learn about the culture and traditions in the Amazon. You can also acquire beautiful handicrafts and help the sustainability of the community. Back at the boat, have lunch on the deck while enjoying a panoramic view of the river. Later, a naturalist will offer a talk of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve. Enjoy a night walk in El Dorado stream, where you can see the nocturnal fauna. Then, have dinner and participate in the briefing for next day´s activities.<br/>Day 4<br/>Wake up at 6:00 am to start a boat exploration of El Dorado River and fish the most famous one in the Amazon: the piranha. Have a unique picnic breakfast in the boat. Back in the Amazon Star, have lunch and participate in a cooking class. The next exploration by boat will take you to the Supay stream, which you can also optionally explore by kayak. Then, you will have dinner and participate in the informative talk of the next day.<br/>Day 5<br/>Wake up at 5:00 in the morning. Have breakfast at 5:15 and disembark in Nauta by 6:00 am.</span><br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Enjoy a delicious dinner at Villa Urubamba Sacred Valley.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Enjoy a delicious lunch at Villa Urubamba Sacred Valley.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a two-hour trip by train until the Aguas Calientes town. This town is also known as &#34;Machu Picchu Pueblo&#34; and it is the final stop before entering the vestiges of the most important Peruvian archaeological complex. In Aguas Calientes you will find a train station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodations of many different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb to the top in the early morning. After a short trip, you will finally arrive in Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture, which is believed to have been used as a sanctuary and leisure residence for Inca Pachacútec (1438-1471). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is a World Cultural Heritage listed by UNESCO and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.  You will spend the night in Aguas Calientes.</p><p>#2 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today you can return to Machu Picchu and you can optionally visit any attraction that the citadel has to offer. You can visit the Intipunku, which means &#34;Gateway of the Sun&#34; in Spanish, and it is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca Trail from Ollantaytambo. It was a sort of guardhouse and the Sun rises every day through that door if it is contemplated from the most important buildings of the citadel. At the same time, you can climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34;, as opposed to Machu Picchu. It takes around an hour to reach the top, and its main attraction is the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, an underground building complex probably conceived for the entertainment of the nobles. Finally, you will visit the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone construction which served to  enter and exit the citadel and which shows a large void in the middle, over which the Incas used to place two wooden boards. Thus, the Incas were able to isolate the city by just removing those boards, protecting themselves. You will spend the night in Aguas Calientes. </p><p>#3 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Today, you will have the option of visiting the Intipunku (Gateway of the Sun), the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space) and the Inca Bridge. In the afternoon, you will be transferred back to the Aguas Calientes train station and you will take your return train.</p>
<p>Dinner at Inkaterra Hacienda Urubamba Hotel.<br/></p>

<p>Your tour will start going to the Sacred Valley, where you will visit Awanakancha, a farm of South American camelids and textiles showroom, where local families show the entire traditional process of weaving and making garments. You will then visit the Pisac market, which has stands that exhibit and sell products manufactured by area residents. Beautiful alpaca textiles, musical instruments, paintings, carvings, necklaces and replicas of old Inca pieces are sure to catch your eye. Next, have lunch at a local restaurant, followed by a visit Ollantaytambo, making a series of stops at points of interest. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of Incan urban planning,whose present-day residents still live in accordance with their old traditions. Inca ruler Pachacuti conquered the town, destroyed it and built a new one, absorbing it as part of his empire. This town exhibits one of the most remarkable complexes of the Inca Empire. Transfer to Hacienda Huayoccari for lunch and return to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="font-size: 12.8px;">#1</p><p style="font-size: 12.8px;">The employees of the lodge will pick you up from the airport and after a short stop<span style="font-size: 12.8px;"> at the Corto Maltés (Corto Maltese) offices, you will board a </span>boat<span style="font-size: 12.8px;"> that will take you to the Corto Maltés Lodge during a 40-minute trip. </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">After enjoying a welcome drink and getting installed in your bungalow, you </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">will have lunch and then you will hike for three hours to see a great </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">variety of medicinal </span>plats<span style="font-size: 12.8px;">, tropical trees and insects. You will go to the </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">scenic viewpoint before dusk to watch the toucans, and you will take a </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">nighttime tour after the sunset to look for caimans.</span></p><p style="font-size: 12.8px;"></p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p></p><p style="font-size: 12.8px;"><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">#2 </span></p><p style="font-size: 12.8px;">Wake up at 5:30 am for some coffee or tea before leaving for Lake Sandoval. <span style="font-size: 12.8px;">Your guide will hand out a breakfast snack for the excursion. After roughly </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">20 minutes of navigation on the river Madre de Dios</span>,arrival<span style="font-size: 12.8px;"> at the landing </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">stage of Lake Sandoval. During a 3 km hike through the exuberant vegetation, </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">we will enter the territory of Tambopata National Reserve, where you will </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">have the chance to admire giant trees, snakes, monkeys (squirrel monkeys, </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">howler monkeys and capuchin monkeys) and various birds (notably the Hoatzin </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">also known as the stinkbird, kingfishers and snakebirds). Once on Lake </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">Sandoval, navigate in small rowing boats, searching for the giant otters. We </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">will also have the possibility to spot caimans, piranhas and a variety of </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">birds. Stop at a rest area, a construction with hammocks, lavatories and a </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">bar, where you can have breakfast, rest and gain energy to continue the trek </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">to Monkey Island (optional visit subject to seasonal availability). Return </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">to the Lodge for lunch. Visit of one of the two lookout towers of Corto </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">Maltès Amazonía, with a height of 20 and 42 meters and designed for </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">birdwatching and viewing of other types of animals living at this altitude. </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">In addition, you will enjoy an incredible view of the sunset. Have some free </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">time to enjoy the pool or have a massage, get refreshed at the bar or visit </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">the souvenir shop. Finally, dinner will be served.</span></p><p style="font-size: 12.8px;">#3</p><p style="font-size: 12.8px;">After a 20 minute boat trip down the river, we arrive at the chacra, a local <span style="font-size: 12.8px;">farm owned by a typical family of the agricultural region, where domestic </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">plants are carefully cultivated, without the use of chemicals, and which are </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">later offered at the main market in Puerto Maldonado. We will distinguish </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">and even taste different tropical fruits of the season and other domestic </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">products (cacao, banana, </span>cocona<span style="font-size: 12.8px;">, breadfruit, noni, manioc, lime). </span>Next<span style="font-size: 12.8px;"> we </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">are received by a native family, who will show us some of their customs and </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">demonstrate their main activities, that is fishing, hunting and handicraft, </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">passed on by their ancestors from various indigenous tribes of the </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">Rainforest. You will learn about some natural products and how they are </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">used to manufacture tools, dwellings, clothing and ornaments, playing an </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">important role in the family´s everyday life. The visit ends with an outdoor </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">lunch, being </span>an unique<span style="font-size: 12.8px;"> experience, both for the family and their guests. </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">Return to Corto </span>Maltes<span style="font-size: 12.8px;">. Finally, dinner will be served. </span></p><p style="font-size: 12.8px;"><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">#4</span></p><p style="font-size: 12.8px;">Wake up at 5:30 am for some coffee or tea before leaving for a 20 minutes <span style="font-size: 12.8px;">walk to the clay lick, where you will have the possibility to observe 3 to 5 </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">types of parrots. The parrots come to the clay lick to ingest a particular </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">kind of clay, to be found in certain areas of this region, and vital for the </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">regulation of their metabolism. A </span>colorful<span style="font-size: 12.8px;"> and veritable delightful and </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">exciting event. After breakfast, head to the city of Puerto Maldonado. </span>One <span style="font-size: 12.8px;">there, visit the local market where the population supplies itself with food </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">and other products. During a short visit, you will be able to appreciate the </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">different typical products of the area. We will then head to the airport for </span><span style="font-size: 12.8px;">the check-in and registration of your respective flights. </span></p>
<p>Full day private walk through Choquechaca, arriving in Pumamarca.<br/></p>

<p>Full day private hike to Huchuy Qosqo.<br/></p><p><br/></p><p>------------------------------</p><p></p><p>Level: Challenging</p><p>Duration: 8 hours</p><p>Maximum altitude: Puka Ccasa 14557 feet / 4437 m a.s.l.</p><p></p><p><span lang="EN-US">Walking distance:</span><span lang="EN-US"> 90,7 miles / 14,6 km</span></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Pick up from your hotel and transfer to Pachar in the morning. After a safety talk, receive the equipment to climb and begin the ascent to the Skylodge, a unique capsule-shaped hotel that hangs next to a mountain in the Sacred Valley of the Incas. Upon arrival, enjoy a lunch made from local ingredients and an unmatched view of the Valley. Then start the descent according to the type of activity chosen (zip-line or via ferrata). A transportation service awaits for you to take you back to the city of Cusco.</span></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>Surprise yourself with the largest complex of electronic sources in the world, certified by Guinness World Record. You will enjoy a wonderful show of water, light, music and images, presented at the famous Parque de la Reserva, for 3 magnificent hours.<br/></p>
<p>Departure from the Ollantaytambo station at 16:36 p.m. in the Incarail executive service train, towards Machu Picchu with arrival time at 6:09 p.m.<br/></p>
<p>Departure from Ollantaytambo station at 19:04 pm on the Expedition train, towards Machu Picchu with arrival time at 20:45 pm.<br/></p>

<p>Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a transfer to the train station of Ollanta, from where you will take the train that will take you to the town of Aguas Calientes in an approximate time of 1 hour and 30 minutes. In Aguas Calientes you will find the train station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and go up early to it. After a short trip you will arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resting place for the Pachacutec Inca. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. At the end of the tour you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes. In the afternoon, you will return by train to the city of Cusco.<br/></p>
<p>Entrance ticket for Colca Valley and Canyon<br/></p>
<p>Guide at disposal for one additional hour, within the city of Arequipa.<br/></p>
<p>Guide at disposal within the city of Arequipa, for three hours.<br/></p>
<p>Transportation at disposal, for three hours, within the city of Arequipa.<br/></p>

<p>#1<br/>You will enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve by skirting the volcanoes that surround Arequipa City. This is a broad plain populated by vicuñas, native and tamable Andean camelids that are a national symbol. You will then visit Vizcachani, the Toccra Pampas and will skirt the crater of the Chucura Volcano to reach the Los Andes Scenic Viewpoint in Patapampa (4910 masl), from where you can see the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa. You will enjoy lunch at the legendary Chivay town and in the afternoon you will rest at the hotel of your choice or you may visit the La Calera hot springs.<br/>#2<br/>You will visit the Condor Cross, from where you may appreciate the Colca Valley (which is one of the world's deepest canyons with over four kilometers at its deepest side) to then daydream: you will see the huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque towns, and the Antahuilque and Choquetico scenic viewpoints. You will return to Arequipa after lunch.<br/></p>
<p>You will be picked up and taken to and from the restaurant at the agreed times.<br/></p>

<p>You will be picked up from your hotel at the agreed time and taken to the chullpas at Sillustani. Sillustani is a peninsula jutting into Lake Umayo and you can visit a necropolis that is one of the largest in the world, with more than 90 &#34;chullpas&#34;: huge and primitive stone mausoleums more than 12 metres high, with their tops wider than their bases. Thereafter you will return to your hotel in Puno.<br/></p>
<p>Today you can choose any attraction you prefer from Cusco City and a guide will be at your disposal to accompany and guide you for half a day.</p>
<p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment-->Full day with guide at disposal in the Sacred Valley.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/><!--EndFragment--></p><p><br/></p><p></p>
<p>Entrance ticket to Koricancha.<br/></p>

<p>This day you will have a new entrance to Machu Picchu and enjoy some time at the Citadel. We recommend visiting the Intipunku (&#34;Sun Gate&#34;), which is the last stop before entering the Inca sanctuary. Another interesting option is the Inca Bridge, which is part of the Inca Trail and is made of vegetable fiber or straw. The bridge is woven with ancient techniques and each year several area communities come together to renew and keep this wonderful tradition.<br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Entrance to Machu Picchu citadel.</span><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p>You will start your tour in the Chinchero town, one of the most important towns of the Inca Empire. You will visit its colorful indigenous fair where every Sunday the town becomes a lively and colorful market, one the few in which the ancient barter system is practiced; the exchange of products from the area for other products, utensils and items brought from neighboring communities. You may bargain with the salespersons for a variety of handicrafts. You will then visit the archaeological vestiges in Maras and Moray. Maras is a surreal view. It is near Moray and it is an impressive complex for the exploitation of salt mines. Its huge salt deposits were used to trade them for other products with other parts of the empire. Moray's archaeological vestiges are composed of mysterious concentric circular terraces which, according to some scholars, would be an agricultural research center. Next, you will visit Ollantaytambo, a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas, whose residents still live in accordance with their old traditions. You will find a large archaeological complex which, although it is called a fortress it was actually a tambo, a leisure city used to accommodate the delegations that traveled long distances. You will enjoy your snack and will finally visit the Pisac citadel, a town shaped as a partridge, which is known for two reasons: for the craft market established between colonial buildings and for housing one of the most impressive complex of agricultural terraces of the valley. The market has scores of stands that exhibit and sell products manufactured by the residents of the area. You will return to you hotel in Cusco after visiting this place.<br/></p>

<p>You will be picked up from your hotel and depart towards Huilloc where you will learn about the customs of this community and will participate in a demonstration of one of the ancestral activities of Andean life: the process of making garments using traditional techniques, understanding the meaning of their iconography. A resident will show you the weaving process of typical Huilloc clothing. In addition to the opportunity to appreciate this traditional activity, you can buy one of these beautiful garments, contributing to the spiritual strengthening and identity of this Inca people, making this a complete and special experience. Then, it will depart towards the archaeological site of Pumamarca, composed of structures such as platforms, roads and canals. He will have lunch on the road. Later visit the town of Ollantaytambo which has been continuously inhabited since the time of the Inca Empire. Finally, you will be transferred from Ollantaytambo to your hotel in Cusco or the Sacred Valley.</p><p><span id="selectionBoundary_1550090367274_8591016898748307" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">------------------------</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;"><b><b>Level: </b></b>Moderate</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;"><b><b>Duration:</b></b> 6 - 7 hours approximately</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;"><b><b>Maximum altitude:</b></b> Huilloc 12467 feet / 3800 m a.s.l.</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;"><b><b>Walking distance:</b></b> 8,7 miles / 14 km</span></p>

<p>You will be picked up early in the morning from your hotel and transferred to the archaeological site of Moray. Moray consists of mysterious concentric circular platforms, which for some investigators would be an agricultural research centre. The higher external platforms would be at a higher temperature than the internal ones and could simulate the conditions of the different ecological floors of the Empire. Then you will stop at Maras, an impressive saline extraction complex composed of large deposits of pink salt that was used to exchange for other products with different parts of the Inca Empire and from which salt is still being extracted to this day. Then, descend to the village of Pichingoto, a Quechua community that maintains the traditions of its ancestors and whose homes are built as part of the mountain in the form of a small cave. After visiting this picturesque village, you will be transferred to your hotel in Cusco or the Sacred Valley.</p><p><br/><!--StartFragment--> <span style="float: none;">------------------------</span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Level:</b> Moderate</span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Duration: </b>7 - 8 hours</span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Maximum altitude: </b>Moray 11482 feet / 3500 m a.s.l.</span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Walking distance: </b>7,4 miles / 12 km </span></p>

<p></p><p>During the morning you will be transferred from your hotel in Cusco to Chinchero. This is a small and picturesque Inca town where you will learn about the traditional textile and craft work of the area. In the same way, you will be able to observe wonderful views of the Sacred Valley from the top and get to know the beautiful chapel that preserves original paintings of the Cusco school inside. From Chinchero, you will start a walk to Urquillos, a town located at 2980 meters above sea level where Inca terraces, a temple and colonial haciendas are preserved. You will enjoy a box lunch along the way. After knowing the place you will be transferred back to your hotel in Cusco or in the Sacred Valley.</p><p>-------------------------------------------------<br/></p><p><span style="float: none;"><b>Level:</b> Moderate, mostly down hill </span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Duration:</b> 6 - 7 hours (2 ½ hours of hike approximately) </span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Maximum altitude:</b> Chinchero 12040 feet / 3670 m a.s.l.</span><br/><span style="float: none;"><b>Walking distance:</b> 4,9 miles / 8 km</span><!--EndFragment--> <br/><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>
<p>Upon arrival at Ollanta, we will transfer you to the hotel in the Urubamba  Valley.<br/></p>
<p>A mobility will transfer you from your hotel to the bus station.<br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will pick you up from the Ollantaytambo train station at the established time and will transfer you back to your hotel in Cusco City. </p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will go to the pier of your hotel to board a boat and go to the Ballestas Islands. Located 45 minutes from the coast of Paracas, the Ballestas Islands congregate an impressive animal fauna made up of guano birds, penguins, tendrils and pikemen, as well as large populations of sea lions that curious to approach the boats and jump around them.<br/></p>

<p>Start your journey to the Center of Lima, declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. Once there you will stop at the Church of San Francisco, one of the most impressive religious architectural complexes of the Viceroyalty built in 1546. It holds an enviable collection of old books and has a network of catacombs open to the public. Continue on foot to Casa Aliaga, former colonial residence given by Francisco Pizarro to one of its captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga) after the founding of the city. It is perhaps the only house of the time that still belongs to the same family to this day. Then you will go to the district of Miraflores to visit the Huaca Pucllana, a pre-Hispanic truncated pyramid 25 meters high built by the Lima culture between 200 and 800dc. The complex houses squares, ramps and staircases, as well as deposits of objects that would have been offered to the deities. It is presumed, based on the human remains found, that human sacrifices would have been made by choosing young women to do so. At the end of your tour you will be transferred back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p>You will be picked up from your hotel to go to the district of Surquillo where you can get to know the local market. It is a classic Lima market that offers a variety of fruits, vegetables, cuts of meat and fish. An authentic visual and olfactory experience. From there, it will go to the Plaza de Armas, where the &#34;City of the Kings&#34; was founded in 1535, and the independence of Peru was proclaimed in 1821 with the name of Lima. From there you can see the Presidential Palace, the Archbishopric and the Cathedral of Lima. Finally, you will go on foot to the Cordano bar, classic bar of the old Lima bohemian that opened its doors in 1905 and through it passed the most important figures of national arts and politics. It has become known for its traditional sandwiches and drinks.<br/></p>

<p><br/><span style="font-size: 16px;text-align: left;float: none;">During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel in Urubamba to the town of Chinchero, meeting point for the Sacred Valley tour. </span><br/><br/></p>
<p><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Dinner at Casa Andina Colca</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Shared transfer from the hotel to the aerodrome and vice versa with overflight assistance</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Lunch at Café del Museo Larco.</p>

<p>Your tour will start in downtown Lima, where you will visit the Main Square, where Lima was first founded as &#34;The City of Kings,&#34; in 1535, and it was already known by the name of Lima when Peru proclaimed its independence from the Spanish Crown in 1821. There you may see the Presidential Palace, the Archbishop's Palace and the Lima Cathedral. You will then visit the Aliaga House, a viceregal construction delivered by Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga) when he founded the city. You will then go to Saint Francis' Church, which has an enviable collection of old books and an authentic network of catacombs open to the public. Next, you will go to Pueblo Libre district to visit the Larco Museum, founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle en 1926. The museum, located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century, houses the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver artifacts as well pieces of erotic art.</p>

<p>Transportation seat-in-coach from Chivay to Puno, amid the scenery of the highland plateau.  Enjoy the magnificent view of clear blue skies and unusual highland vegetation, known as ichu.  Pass by small villages such as Imata, Lagunillas, Santa Lucia and Cabanillas.</p>

<p></p><p><span>Transportation seat-in-coach from Chivay to Puno, amid the scenery of the highland plateau.<span>  </span>Enjoy the magnificent view of clear blue skies and unusual highland vegetation, known as ichu.<span>  </span>Pass by small villages such as Imata, Lagunillas, Santa Lucia and Cabanillas.</span></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>Day 1:</p><p>Your tour will start on board of a private transportation service on route to the Paracas Bay, located near Pisco City, in the buffer zone of the Paracas Natural Reserve. The reserve is a protected area with beautiful geological formations, impressive fauna and vestiges of a pre-Inca culture that dominated the desert to install a successful society in adverse conditions. Once you are there, you can optionally visit the Ballestas Islands, home to a large population of sea lions, Humboldt penguins and other species found in the archipelago, an ideal place for their reproduction and coexistence. You will then take a tour by the reserve, to admire the fauna that lives there, and at the end of your visit, you will be transferred to the hotel of your choice.</p><p>Day 2:</p><p>You will continue your trip to Ica City. Ica has become known for its desert and fertile valleys in which large grape and wine producing farms have been established. There you will visit the Ica Regional Museum, which houses an important collection of pre-Hispanic vestiges of the local cultures that have been exhibited in different parts of the world. When you finish your visit to the museum, you will go to Nasca City, where you can optionally board a light aircraft to fly over the Nasca Lines (subject to weather conditions). At the end of the flight, you will be transferred to your hotel in said city, where you will stay overnight.</p><p>Day 3:</p><p>In the morning, you will visit the Antonini Museum that houses recent discoveries made at the Cahuachi Ancient Ceremonial Center by Italian archaeologist Giuseppe Orefici. There you will understand the evolution of the Nasca culture and appreciate beautiful pieces of pottery, weavings, mummies, trophy heads, and many other vestiges.<span>  </span>You will then be transferred to Arequipa City, for a trip that takes approximately nine hours, and you will be subsequently transferred to your hotel.<span> </span></p>
<p>Today you can choose what you most want to know about the city of Lima and have a guide available to accompany and guide you.<br/></p>
<p>Today you will have at your disposal a transport in the city of Lima, to move anywhere you wish, for an additional hour.<br/></p>
<p>Today you can choose what you most want from the city of Lima and have a vehicle available to take you where you want for three hours.<br/></p>
<p>Additional mobility will be at your disposal to transfer you to the airport or from the airport to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p>Visit the Lima Center, declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO, where you will know the Cathedral of Lima, located in the Plaza Mayor, the most important church in Peru built between 1542 and 1622, and one of several beautiful colonial buildings which are an important part of the identity of the city. Then you will walk to the Casa Aliaga -house of the Viceroyalty handed over by Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga) when he founded the city- an authentic jewel of colonial architecture currently inhabited by family members. Continue your tour visiting the Church of Santo Domingo, built at the end of the sixteenth century, which concentrates several altars and sculptures of the Viceroyalty, exposing different eras and aesthetic manifestations. Then you will be transferred to the districts of San Isidro and Miraflores to take a panoramic walk, until you make a brief stop at the Parque del Amor before continuing on your way to the hotel.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#1 </span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">You will be picked up from your hotel and transferred to Pachar at noon. After a safety talk, you will receive the equipment to climb and begin the ascent to the Skylodge, a unique capsule-shaped hotel that hangs next to a mountain in the Sacred Valley of the Incas. Upon arrival, you will be greeted with coffee or coca leaf tea and will participate in a safety and operation talk of the hotel. Then you will enjoy dinner. Overnight in the Skylodge. </span></p><p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">#2 </span></p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Early in the morning, you will receive a coffee or coca leaf tea in your suite. Then you will have breakfast and later start the descent according to the type of activity chosen (zip-line or via ferrata). A transportation service will be waiting for you to take you back to the city of Cusco where you will arrive around 1 in the afternoon.</span></div><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>Today, together with a private escort, he will visit the Magical Circuit of Aguas de Lima, a modern pool system (certified as the largest in the world by the Guinness World Records) built in the Exhibition Park that attracts crowds during summer and Winter consolidating as one of the new attractions of Lima. After your visit you will be transferred back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Transportation will pick you up from your hotel in Cusco and you will be transferred to the Ollanta train station. Your trip to Machu Picchu will begin with a train trip to the town of Aguas Calientes. There you can find a handicrafts market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb up early. After a short trip you will arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resting place for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means Old Mountain, is considered a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the new seven wonders of the modern world. At the end of the tour, you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes. Later, take the train back to Ollanta and be transferred to your hotel Cusco.</div><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p>A transportation service will take you to the &#34;Wayra&#34; restaurant to have lunch and then you will   go to the Sacsayhuamán  archaeological complex, one of the most emblematic constructions of the Incas (located a few minutes away from the city) built by Inca Pachacútec in the 15th century. It is an enormous fortress erected with large stones, and the manner in which they were removed and cut remains a mystery. The Inti Raymi Festival, which worships the Sun, is re-enacted on June 24 of each year, during the winter solstice. You will be transferred to your hotel in Cusco city after a guided visit.<br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">Assitance in Lima airport</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>
<p>Assitance in Lima airport<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">At night, a transportation service accompanied by a representative will pick you up from your hotel to take you to Lima airport. The transfer lasts between 35-45 minutes, depending on the location of the hotel.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p></p><p>At night, a transportation service accompanied by a representative will pick you up from Lima airport to take you to your selected hotel. The transfer lasts between 35-45 minutes, depending on the location of the hotel.</p><p></p>
<p>A transportation service, accompanied by a representative, will pick you up from your hotel in Lima to transfer you to the airport.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;"><span id="selectionBoundary_1543266452091_2994731887588433" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>A transportation service with a guide will pick you up at the airport at the established time and will transfer you to your hotel. Lima is a modern metropolis full of history, which has ten million inhabitants and is going through an exciting process of cultural and economic changes.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><span id="selectionBoundary_1543266452091_3187420777847758" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span><br/></p>

<p style="font-size: 12px;">Enjoy a journey from Puno to Cusco passing through some of the most amazing landscapes of the Peruvian highlands. The train has a capacity for 132 passengers and 4 dining cars, a car bar and an observatory car. Along the way you will enjoy a gourmet lunch prepared with exquisite Andean ingredients and there will be music and dance shows on board.</p><!--EndFragment--><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>
<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Entrance to Mateo Salado Archaeological Complex.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will pick you up from the hotel for your half day tour in Cusco. Start visiting the traditional and bohemian neighborhood of San Blas, residence of artists and craftsmen. Discover its streets, between bars, shops and art galleries. Then you will be transferred to the dynamic market of San Pedro, which has a lot of stalls selling local products such as different varieties of potatoes, traditional Andean food, flowers, among many others. When finished, go to the main square and visit the Cathedral and the Convent of Santo Domingo, which was built on top of the most important temple of the Inca Empire: Coricancha. Finally, visit the impressive fortress of Sacsayhuaman, where the Inti Raymi (Festival of the Sun) is made, on June 24 of each year.</div><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">You will be transferred from Chachapoyas to Kuelap to visit the imposing fortress, one of the most significant lithic monuments in Peru, which rises 3000 meters above the Cerro Barreta. It has 505 enclosures in its interior distributed in different levels. Its impressive walls and its interior architecture show its role as a housing complex. The ascent and descent are carried out in the new cable car installed in the area, which makes possible reaching the fortress in just 20 minutes.</div><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">During the morning you will be transferred from Chachapoyas to the town of Cruzpata. From there, you will walk in the direction of the sarcophagi of Karajía. These sarcophagi are elaborate two-meters high clay sculptures that rest on the edge of a rocky cliff of limestone, looking towards the abyss. The visit will last approximately 40 minutes. From there you will walk to Cruzpata for approximately 40 minutes, where a transport will be waiting to take you to the village of Lamud to enjoy lunch. Then you will visit the Cave of Quiocta, a 550 meters long cave long that was used as a cemetery by pre Columbian civilizations. Enjoy the surreal underground landscape made up of stalagmites and stalactites.</p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">The tour will start during the morning departing from Esteves Island at 9:30 am. Participate in a briefing offered by a guide and then board the Polynesian canoe that will take you on a wonderful tour of Lake Titicaca, heading to Uros, a set of floating human-made islands from totora plant. Visit the islands to share moments with its inhabitants and learn about their lifestyle, traditions, customs and the art they produce in crafts and textiles. After the visit you will return to Esteves Island. During the time on board you will be able to see up close the great diversity of wildlife and native vegetation of the area around the lake.</div><p><br/></p><p>----------------<br/><!--StartFragment-->Level: Easy<br/>Duration: 3 hours approximately<span id="selectionBoundary_1550701660666_8691608344691326" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span><br/></p><p></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">The tour will start during the morning departing from Esteves Island at 9:30 am. Participate in a briefing offered by a guide and then board the Polynesian canoe that will take you on a wonderful tour of Lake Titicaca, heading to Uros, a set of floating human-made islands from </span>totora<span style="font-size: 13px;"> plant. Visit the islands to share moments with its inhabitants and learn about their lifestyle, traditions, customs and the art they produce in crafts and textiles. After the </span>visit<span style="font-size: 13px;"> you will return to Esteves Island. During the time on </span>board<span style="font-size: 13px;"> you will be able to see up close the great diversity of wildlife and native vegetation of the area around the lake.</span></div><div><p><br/></p><span style="text-align: left;float: none;">-----------------</span></div><div><span style="text-align: left;float: none;">Level: Easy<br/>Duration: 3 hours approximately</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/><p><br/></p><p><!--EndFragment--></p><p><br/></p></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>
<p>Oxygen service will be provided in the room by the Monasterio Hotel.</p>
<p>You will be assisted at the airport.<br/></p>
<p>You will be assisted at the airport.</p>

<p><br/></p><p style="font-size: 12px;">#1</p><p style="font-size: 12px;">Your tour begins with a train ride to Machu Picchu. In about an hour and a half reach the town of Aguas Calientes, also known as &#34;Machu Picchu&#34;. This is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. Finally you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. You may also optionally visit the Intipunku, the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.</p><p style="font-size: 12px;"><br/></p><p style="font-size: 12px;">#2</p><p style="font-size: 12px;">Today you can return to Machu Picchu optionally and visit any of the attractions that the citadel keeps: the Intipunku (&#34;Puerta del Sol&#34;) was a kind of control gate, where the sun rises every day through this door. You can climb the Huayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34;. Its ascent takes about an hour and it is the &#34;Temple of the Moon&#34;, a complex of underground constructions probably intended for the use of the elite. Finally, you can visit the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone construction that served as an entrance or exit to the citadel. In the afternoon you will transfer to the Aguas Calientes train station to take your train back to the Poroy station, and from there you will be taken to your hotel in Cusco.</p><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">To get to Explora, you can be transferred from the Cusco airport to the Sacred Valley, or be transferred from your hotel in Cusco to the Valley. Enjoy four days and three nights at the charming Hotel Explora in the Sacred Valley. Located in a former corn plantation and located in a very quiet place in the Valley, where the guest will enjoy all the tranquility and rest options, such as the spa service, which this offers. Also, the traveler can enjoy exquisite dishes prepared especially to feel agile and light during their explorations, prepared with products of excellent quality that will give you the energy to travel throughout the Sacred Valley. The hotel offers excursions to make from the morning, where you can learn about the history, customs and other attractions of Pisac and its surroundings. These include hikes through Urubamba, Quenqo, Moray, Huarocondo, Las Salineras, among others; as well as bike rides that will take you to enjoy incredible views of the valley and archaeological sites. The excursions have a duration of half a day or of the whole day and it is guaranteed that in which you choose you will spend incredible moments.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">You will leave from Chachapoyas in direction of the town of San Bartolo from where the walk of approximately 30 minutes to the Revash mausoleums begins. Revash is a funerary complex composed of &#34;chullpas&#34; (funerary towers) of one and two floors of height, painted of ocher color. Afterwards, you will be transferred to Leymebamba to visit the Leymebamba Museum which houses more than 200 mummies recovered from the Condor Lagoon, which can be appreciated along with their funeral offerings. The design of the building recreates the local architecture, using local materials. The beautiful garden surrounding the museum contains more than 100 varieties of orchids. After this visit, you will return to Chachapoyas.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Todays tour will go through some of Limas most celebrated attractions. You will be transferred from the hotel to San Martín Square and then to the Main Square of Lima to appreciate the colonial architecture that forms the identity of the historic centre. Then, you will visit a pre-Columbian complex located in the middle of the district of Miraflores: Huaca Pucllana, composed of a pyramid of more than 20 meters high. The tour will continue at the ¨Love Park¨, an attractive space surrounded by art and an impressive view of the ocean. After this visit you will be transferred to a nearby restaurant to enjoy the renowned Peruvian gastronomy. Later, you will head to the Larco Museum, located in the traditional district of Pueblo Libre. It was founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926 and is located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century. The museum has a complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver pieces, as well as erotic art. Finally, you will be transferred to your hotel.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Today you will hike to Cocachimba, a paradise in the middle of the jungle that houses different waterfalls, including Gocta, one of the highest waterfalls in the world. Walk along a beautiful route full of vegetation and wild animals. You will enjoy wonderful views and the imposing waterfall where the strength of the water and the pleasant sensation of the dew will renew you.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Lima’s historic old town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is awash with fascinating cultural sites, colonial architecture and a varied history which reflects the many faces of this intriguing city, with <span id="selectionBoundary_1527022222786_8694371130904683" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>a guided walking tour being the best way of getting to grips with the Peruvian capital. First up is Plaza de Armas which is dominated by a central 16th-century Cathedral, as well as grand colonial buildings (the Presidential Palace, City Hall and Archbishop’s Palace among them), fountains and even native palm trees, making it the perfect introduction to Old Lima. Then, a visit to one of Lima’s most prestigious colonial mansions which has since turned into a centre of historical interest – the Larco Museum, home to many pieces or fine pre-Columbian art, gold and silver artefacts, in a building constructed on the site of a 7th-century pyramid, no less!</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">You will be picked up to start your tour through Barranco a Miraflores boardwalks, passing by tourist attractions such as the ‘Love Park’ (“El Parque del Amor) and enjoying a beautiful view of the Pacific Ocean. Then you will be transferred to Qincha restaurant, just meters away from the ocean. There you will participate in a cooking and cocktail class where you will learn to prepare typical dishes and drinks such as famous Pisco Sour. Finally, you will have lunch viewing the beach and board the vehicle again to be driven to your hotel.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">The Urban Tour is the perfect combination between the discovery of interesting cultural sites and the tranquillity of riding a bicycle. We will start heading to ´Huaca Pucllana´, an archaeological pre-Inca site, then we will pass through the Basilica of San Isidro and beautiful parks. Take advantage of the splendid view along the Miraflores seafront, and continue to the Surquillo market, where you will have the opportunity to try several exotic fruits. Finally, have a delicious coffee in a restaurant with a beautiful view of the Pacific Ocean before the tour ends.</div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p>

<p><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12.8px;float: none;">You will start your tour in the village of Chinchero, which was one of the most important populations during the Inca Empire. However, as much of its annexes, it was also a victim of the &#34;extirpation of idolatries&#34; action that replaced the traditional deities by Catholic ones, in order to transform popular beliefs. Then visit the archaeological remains of Maras and Moray. Maras is a surreal sight. It is close to Moray and is an impressive complex of salt mining. They are large deposits of salt used for exchange for other products with other regions of the empire. On the other hand, the archaeological site of Moray consists of mysterious concentric circular terraces that for some investigators would be an agricultural research center. Enjoy lunch at the agreed restaurant and then visit Ollantaytambo, a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited like in the past. You will find here a great archaeological complex called &#34;fortress&#34; although it was a &#34;tambo&#34;, a resting and lodging place for long distance travellers. Finally visit the citadel of Pisac, a town built in the form of partridge known for two reasons: the crafts market, established between colonial buildings, and for hosting one of the most impressive complexes of terraces in the valley. The market has dozens of stalls displaying and selling goods crafted by residents of the area. After the visit return to your hotel in Cusco.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Today you will enjoy a special trip through the Nasca desert. Nasca is a small town. Perhaps it would go unnoticed on a map if it were not for the mysterious lines engraved on the desert that have given it worldwide fame. A monkey, a spider, a hummingbird, all of great dimensions, can be seen from the sky in a flight in small planes so that you try to decipher the mystery. However, this flight travels the desert like no other on the more than twelve geoglyphs of the Nazca desert plus the recently discovered in Palpa. After flying over its mountains, where marine fossils of up to 150 million years old are found, it will end its journey.</div><p><br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">This morning a mobility will pick you up from your hotel to transfer you to the wholesale fruit market, which houses the most precious treasures of the coast, mountains and jungle. You can enjoy delicious and fresh fruit juices before continuing your journey to the market of Surquillo, located in the limits of that district and Miraflores. There, a wide variety of vegetables and fruits are offered, along with all the fresh aromatic herbs, such as dill, marjoram, and others. Then you will go to the traditional district of Pueblo Libre to visit the Larco Museum. founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926. The museum is located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century and it is not possible to find the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver pieces as well as portrait and art ceramics erotic. There are more than 45 thousand archaeological objects silent witnesses of three thousand years of Peruvian history. Later you will delight with a demonstration of Peruvian cuisine and Creole lunch that will allow you to taste a variety of national dishes. At the end of the tour you will be transferred back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will enjoy a special trip through the Nasca desert. The town of Nasca is small and perhaps would go unnoticed on a map, if not for the enigmatic lines recorded on the desert that have given it worldwide fame. A monkey, a spider, a condor, all large, can be seen from the sky in a flight in small planes for you to try to decipher the mystery. If the weather conditions or airspace permit permit, the recent findings in Palpa will be overflown. You will enjoy a panoramic view of the mountains, where marine fossils up to 150 million years old are found.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will tour the huge sand dunes that draw the horizon of the Ica desert in sand cars, lightweight but powerful four-wheel drive cars capable of climbing slopes of loose sand and offer an adrenaline experience. The pilots and their machines will move it through places where other vehicles do not arrive, converting the ride into an authentic adventure.<br/></p>

<p>A mobility will pick you up from the hotel at the scheduled time and you will be directed to the chulpas of Sillustani. In Sillustani, a peninsula of the Umayo lagoon, you can visit one of the largest necropolises in the world where more than 90 &#34;chullpas&#34; stand out, gigantic and primitive stone mausoleums more than 12 meters high with the upper part wider than the base. Upon completion you will be transferred to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p>You will be picked up from the lake port of Puno and will go to the floating islands of the Uros. The Uros are an ancestral culture that populates a series of artificial islands built on totora that grows in the same lake. These are made by weaving totora in the areas where it grows busier forming a natural layer. On it they build their houses and cook outdoors to avoid fires. After a journey of two hours, you will sail back to Puno.<br/></p>

<p>You will be picked up from your hotel in Cusco followed by a twenty minute car trip to the starting point. The trip lasts two and a half hours, and will take you through fields, Andean-style villages and views of sites and Inca ruins. You will enjoy a wonderful view of the city of Cusco on your way to the Temple of the Moon, where the horses will be left and will embark on the trip by car back to Cusco. Return to the hotel in Cusco. You are not allowed to visit the archaeological sites, while you are riding on horseback.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your journey in Tipón, perfectly preserved archaeological sites located 27 kilometers from the city of Cusco. Close to the town of the same name, where stands an impressive rural colonial mansion (1650), there are 12 terraces still cultivated, some enclosures and an irrigation system considered a masterpiece of hydraulic engineering still used by the surrounding population. Then continue to Pikillacta, considered one of the best preserved pre-Inca cities of Peru and corresponds to the Wari culture, ancestor of the Incas. Among its 700 structures, covering more than 3 thousand hectares, there are stone and mud buildings of two and three floors, fine andenería and the head of a condor carved in a rock. It is estimated that in this city, whose streets can still be traveled, about 10,000 people lived. Finally, you will visit Andahuaylillas, a town located 40 kilometers from Cusco that has the Church of Andahuaylillas as the most attractive. Also called &#34;The Sistine Chapel of America&#34;, due to the high quality of the pieces of art found in it, the structure was built in the sixteenth century on sites of possibly Wari buildings. Today it has an original pipe organ recently restored. From here, he will return to Cusco.<br/></p>

<p>Start a tour of two of the most famous &#34;huacas&#34; (ceremonial centres or temples) of Lima. First, you will visit Huaca Pucllana in the district of Miraflores, formed by a 25-meter high truncated pyramid of adobe and a set of patios and plazas. Also, the complex has an exhibition room and a restaurant. Then, you will be transferred to Huaca Mateo Salado in the district of Pueblo Libre. This is one of the most important ¨huacas¨ in Lima due to its connection with the Qhapaq Ñan, the Great Inca Trail that united the entire empire. Initially Mateo Salado was a Yschma civilization administrative-ceremonial centre, but then it was occupied by the Incas, being they who enabled the section that united this ¨huaca¨ with Pachacamac, south of Lima. Finally, you will be transferred back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<p>Your tour will start in the center of the city where you will make a panoramic tour of the town, declared a World Heritage Site by UNESCO. Arrive at the Plaza Mayor from where you can see the Presidential Palace and the Cathedral of Lima, imposing buildings still in use. Next, you will go on foot to the Church of San Francisco, where you will find an enviable collection of old books and a network of catacombs open to the public. From here you will move to the district of San Isidro to visit the Casa Luna museum, where you will find an exquisite collection of births. Finally, you will go to the district of Miraflores where you will visit the Parque del Amor, located in Miraflores cliffs with a spectacular view of the bay of Lima. Then be transferred to a restaurant and after lunch you will be taken back to your hotel.<br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Pick up at your hotel and short drive to the Surquillo Market. The citys market is probably the best place to sample local produce and observe how different goods coming from the coast, highlands and jungle converge in one place. Then you are transferred to El Bolivariano tavern, where you can taste different types of Pisco and traditional snacks. Drink a Pisco Sour while the ceviche demonstration is carried out by the local chef. The tasting experience continues at another renowned restaurant. Visit the residential and busy streets of Miraflores and San Isidro. Stroll around San Isidro´s Financial Centre, Golf Club and Olive Park; the citys oldest natural site. Continue your tour in Miraflores, where ancient inhabitants of the valley built the adobe pyramid of Huaca Pucllana. Pass through the Parque del Amor (The Love Park) and enjoy the view of the Pacific Ocean. Then you are transferred to Lima´s Historic Center, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Its long, wooden balconies were once used by flirty women called &#34;tapadas&#34; to see while not being seen. Colonial buildings surrounding the Main Square and the Cathedral stand out impressively. The Presidential Palace, City Hall and the Archbishops Palace complete a nice sight. Later, visit the Santo Domingo Convent, a 16th century building that has an imposing bell tower and altar. Finally, you will transferred to your hotel.</div><p><br/></p>

<p>The north coast of Peru, noted for housing amazing archaeological complexes; evidence of the fascinating history of our country. Today he will visit the remains of Caral; the oldest civilization in America. A mobility will pick you up from your hotel and will head north of Lima for approximately 3 hours. After passing through the cities of Chancay, Huacho, Huaura and Supe, Caral will finally arrive, the civilization that gave rise to the oldest urban complex (of the same name) in America and that redefined the history of societies when it was discovered that it has 5 thousand years old, exactly the same as China, Egypt, India and Mesopotamia. In Caral you can find great pyramids, squares, atriums and altars where different sacred ceremonies and offerings to deities were performed. After your visit to the archaeological complex you will go to the city of Huacho to see the Pittaluga House, the University Archaeological Museum and the Malecón Playa Chorrillos, before returning to Lima.<br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel in Sacred Valley and transfer you to the hotel in Cusco.</span><br/><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;"></span>During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel to visit the town of Chinchero*, a traditional Cusco urban centre located at an elevated point with a beautiful view of the valley. The inhabitants of this place preserve the traditions and knowledge inherited from the Incas and display them in their clothes and customs. In Chinchero there are also archaeological remains and a beautiful seventeenth-century church, one of the first Catholic buildings in Peru. After this visit, you are transferred to the Living Museum of Yucay, making a stop at the Raqchi viewpoint. The museum is an interpretation centre of the Andean traditions, where demonstrations of the elaboration of textile work, adobe, pottery and silverware are carried out in the same way in which they used to in antiquity; an original recreation of the Inca traditions. Also, residents of the museum include llamas, sheep and alpacas, and they can be fed! Later, live an experience of superior Andean gastronomy in Chuncho restaurant, located in Ollantaytambo, where the true spirit of the Sacred Valley is conveyed in a cosy atmosphere. Then, visit the town and archaeological site of Ollantaytambo, inhabited uninterruptedly since Inca times, and explore the iconic fortress, built next to the mountain. Finally, be transferred back to your hotel.</p><p><br/></p><p>* The visits that take place on Sundays include the Chinchero market.<br/><br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">This day you will have a new entrance to Machu Picchu and enjoy some time at the Citadel. We recommend visiting the Intipunku (&#34;Sun Gate&#34;), which is the last stop before entering the Inca sanctuary. Another interesting option is the Inca Bridge, which is part of the Inca Trail and is made of vegetable fiber or straw. The bridge is woven with ancient techniques and each year several area communities come together to renew and keep this wonderful tradition.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>
<p>Entrance to Machu Picchu<br/></p>
