<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Entrance ticket to Machu Picchu and Huayna Picchu, which has different rates depending on the age of the visitor. Machu Picchu is a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed served as a sanctuary and retreat for Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1471). On the other hand, Huayna Picchu is located north of the citadel of Machu Picchu. It is a mountain of great beauty that displays in its summit a group of terraces apparently used for religious and aesthetic purposes, and an incomplete temple. On the northern flank is found the Temple of the Moon.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><p></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 11pt;">Hike the Machu Picchu mountain for two and a half hours from the archaeological site, and reach the Inca sanctuary. This trail connects parts of the trail that leads to the Sun Gate, before climbing to the highest point of the mountain. From that point, you'll appreciate the citadel and other major attractions in the area, such as mountains Putukusi, Wayna Picchu, Salkantay and the Vilcanota River. After the tour, enjoy free time in the citadel.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment-->You will be picked up from Puno´s lake port and transferred to the floating islands of the Uros. Uros are an ancestral society that inhabits a series of artificial islands built with totora, a reed that grows in the lake. The islands are built by weaving totoras in the places where they grow the thickest and form a natural layer. After a one and a half-hour tour, you will continue your trip to the Taquile Island. Unlike other islands, inhabited by Aymara-speaking people in the lake, its residents speak Quechua. They will give you a brief presentation about the island. Finally, return to Puno after lunch.</p><p><br/><br/></p><p>* Transfers are made from the following hotels:<br/>- Libertador Lake Titicaca<br/>- Casa Andina Private Collection<br/>- Inn of the Inca<br/>- Jose Antonio<br/>- Eco Inn<br/></p>

<p><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 10pt;">The museum shows the 360 pieces of Machu Picchu returned by Yale University, which can be appreciated by Cusco and tourists. They also show in pedestals and showcases in two rooms of the second level ceramic, lithic, metallic pieces and skeletal remains which were found in the excavations made at Casa Concha and some of the goods of the Inca museum.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">Arrive to the rural community of Huilloc in the province of Urubamba, to participate in an ancient Inca practice called Willaq Uma that will allow you to enter the spiritual world through a sacred ritual where you express your wishes and desires to join the harmony of the cosmos. The intercultural interpreter will welcome you the community accompanied by musicians, and guide you to the house of a resident that will welcome you with a muña infusion. With great respect and in a spiritual environment, the wise man of the community prepares the elements for the offering. You will appreciate and recognize these elements, its symbolic meaning and how they complement between the spiritual and natural forces. After that, you will walk to the archaeological site of Marka Qucha (Markaqocha) and in the road you will ask for the Apus? blessing with coca leaves and respect to the sacred place of Sutuq Machay. This place is a cave at the foot of the Quri Vara hill and inside there is an altar in which the ritual will be held as the Incas did. The offering will be entrusted to a messenger who will deliver it to a sacred place in the Aya Urqu Mountain. Then you will share a hug with all participants, as a sign of affection and satisfaction. Finally, the villagers will give you a proper farewell with ancient music wishing you a good trip.</div><p><span id="selectionBoundary_1560194804371_7176201481512061" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span><br/></p>
<p><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Lunch at Balcon de Zacarias restaurant.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">Your tour will start in the Pueblo Libre district, where you will visit the Larco Museum. Founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926, the museum is located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century. The visit to this museum enables to understand over five thousand years of the high degree of development of the cultures that flourished before the Inca Empire. It shows the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver artifacts as well pieces of erotic art. Over 45,000 archaeological artifacts bear witness of five thousand years of Peruvian history. From there you will go to the citys built-up area, a World Heritage listed by UNESCO. You will tour the Main Square to see the presidential Palace and the Lima Cathedral, impressive constructions that are still being used. You will then visit the Aliaga House, an ancient viceregal construction delivered by Francisco Pizarro to one of his captains (Jerónimo de Aliaga) when he founded the city, the construction of which started in 1535. It may be the only house that still belongs to the same family until today. Next, you will visit the Santo Domingo Church, built along forty years and completed at the end of the 16th century. It is also known as the Minor Basilica and Maximum Convent of our Lady of the Rosary. The church has several altars and viceregal sculptures, and exhibits different times and aesthetic expressions. From here you will walk until the San Martín Square, where you will be picked up by your transportation service and transferred to your hotel.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p><br/><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">You will head to the Wayra Ranch Restaurant for lunch and enjoy a show of passing horses. Then visit the Park of Ollantaytambo with a series of stops at points of interest. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited as in the past. Pachacutec (1438 - 1471) conquered the village, destroyed it and built a new one by absorbing it as part of its empire considered today as one of the most remarkable archeological complexes of the Incanate. At the end of your tour you will be transferred to the Sacred Valley.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">You will begin your tour in the town of Chinchero, one of the most important settlements of the Inca Empire located in an esplanade 3,500 meters above sea level, where beautiful Andean landscapes surround the town, framed by the beauty of the Chicón And Veronica Mountains. Nowadays, every Sunday, the town becomes a center for the exchange of products from nearby communities. From there you will leave for the complex of Moray, mysterious concentric circular platforms, which for some investigators would have been an agricultural research center. The elevated external platforms would be at a higher temperature than the internal ones and could simulate the conditions if the different ecological floors of the Empire. You will enjoy lunch and then head to Ollantaytambo. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas and is still inhabited by the use of the past. Pachacutec conquered the village, destroyed it and built a new one by absorbing it as part of its Empire. On its slopes is the Fortress of Ollantaytambo. A great archaeological complex that although receives the name of &#34;fortress&#34; was a &#34;tambo&#34;, city for rest and lodging for entourages that traveled long distances. Finally, you will head to your chosen hotel in Ollantaytambo.</div><p><br/></p>

<p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;">A transportation service will pick you up and take you to visit Chinchero and its archaeological site, followed by Moray, where you will discover its amazing archaeological circular platforms. When finished, you will be taken to the selected restaurant for lunch, and then go to the Inca city of Ollantaytambo, to visit its extraordinary archaeological complex, which served as a resting place for the Inca forces. The tour ends at the Ollanta train station.</span><!--EndFragment--><br/><br/><br/></p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 13px;">Your day will begin with a tour where you will visit the salt mines of Maras, the terraces of Moray and the archaeological site of Ollantaytambo. Located on the side of a mountain, Maras presents a surreal landscape. It is an impressive complex of saline extraction composed of large deposits of pink salt that was used to exchange for other products of other regions of the Empire during the Inca period and that continues to produce salt today. Then visit Moray, mysterious concentric circular platforms, for which some investigators would have been an agricultural research center. The elevated external platforms would be at a higher temperature than the internal ones</span><br/></div><p><span style="font-size: 13px;text-align: left;float: none;"></span><div style="text-align: justify;">and could simulate the conditions of the different ecological floors of the empire. You will enjoy lunch and then continue to Ollantaytambo. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas and is still inhabited as it was in the past. Pachacutec conquered the village, destroyed it and built a new one by absorbing it as part of its Empire. On its slopes is the Fortress of Ollantaytambo. A great archaeological complex that although receiveing the name of &#34;fortress&#34; was a &#34;tambo&#34;, city for rest and lodging for entourages that traveled long distances. Finally, you will head towards the Ollantaytambo station.</div></p><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">The tour will start at the Sacred Valley, where you will visit Awanakancha, a farm of South American camelids and textiles showroom, where local families show the traditional process of weaving and making garments. You will then visit Pisac, a town shaped as a partridge, which is known for two reasons: the craft market established between colonial buildings, and for housing one of the most impressive complex of agricultural terraces of the valley. The market has stands that exhibit and sell products manufactured by the residents of the area. You will then have lunch at the agreed restaurant and will arrive in Ollantaytambo, a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas, whose residents still live in accordance with their old traditions. Inca Pachacutec conquered the town, destroyed it and built a new one, absorbing it as part of his empire. The Ollantaytambo Fortress is a huge archaeological complex which, although bears the name of fortress, it was actually a ´tambo´, a leisure city used to accommodate the delegations that traveled long distances. Without a doubt, it is one of the most grandiose works of the Inca Empire.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour in Awanakancha, an exhibition center of textiles and South American camelids located at kilometer 23 of the road to Pisac. It is considered a living museum and here you can see Andean camelids, learn about the techniques to take their wool and finally acquire finely spun garments. You wil then visit the Pisac market, consisting of dozens of stalls displaying and selling goods crafted by residents of the area. You can find textiles, carvings, and replicas of ancient Inca pieces. Located 33 kilometers from the city of Cusco, it is a town built as a partridge, also known for hosting one of the most important archaeological sites in the Sacred Valley on the slopes of the hills. It is perhaps the largest complex of platforms in the area. Free time to visit the area on your own. Once you finish the tour, the guide will recommend a place to enjoy lunch and in the afternoon you will be transferred to your hotel located in Cusco or in the Sacred Valley of the Incas.<br/></p>

<p>Your journey will begin towards the market of Pisac, which consists of dozens of stalls that display and sell products worked by the inhabitants of the area.You can find textiles, carvings and replicas of old Inca pieces.Then you will go to the restaurant agreed The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited as in the past. Pachacútec (1438 - 1471) conquered the town of Ollantaytambo Town, destroyed it and built a new one by absorbing it as part of its empire considered today as one of the most remarkable archaeological complexes of the Incanate. At the end of your tour you will be transferred to the Sacred Valley.<br/></p>

<p>Your journey will begin towards the market of Pisac, which consists of dozens of stalls that display and sell products worked by the inhabitants of the area. Textiles, carvings and replicas of ancient Inca pieces can be found. Then you will head to Huilloc for lunch and then visit the Ollantaytambo Park with a series of stops at points of interest. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of urban planning of the Incas still inhabited as in the past. Pachacutec (1438 - 1471) conquered the village, destroyed it and built a new one by absorbing it as part of its empire considered today as one of the most remarkable archaeological complexes of the Incanate. At the end of your tour you will be transferred to the Sacred Valley.<br/></p>

<p><span style="text-align: justify;">#1</span></p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel at the established time and will travel for about twenty minutes the eleven kilometers that separate Poroy from the city. The Hiram Bingham train will depart from the Poroy station to Aguas Calientes, the town located before the entry to Machu Picchu. This trip takes three and a half hours, and the Hiram Bingham train will transfer you through fantastic landscapes of the Cusco highlands, gradually descending to the subtropical jungle where the Machu Picchu citadel is located. This train has elegant finishings and large windows and offers Peruvian gourmet food (fresh trouts are cooked and chocolate cheesecakes are baked on board) and a bar located beside the scenic wagon, in which musical concerts are eventually performed. Upon your arrival to Aguas Calientes, you will take a bus to Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture which is believed to have been used as a sanctuary and leisure residence for Inca Pachacútec. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is a World Cultural Heritage listed by UNESCO and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. After your visit, you will enjoy an English tea and then you will be transferred to your hotel in Aguas Calientes, to rest for the rest of the day.</span></p><p>*Luggage: Please pack an overnight bag for this journey. Due to luggage restrictions aboard the trains to/from Machu Picchu, passengers may only take one (1) small handbag weighing no more than 5 kg (11 lbs).<br/><br/><span style="text-align: justify;">#2</span></p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">After having breakfast today, you may optionally make a second visit to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions that the citadel has in store. You may visit the Intipunku, which means &#34;Gateway of the Sun&#34; in Spanish and it is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu, if the Inca trail is followed from Ollantaytambo. It was a sort of guardhouse and the Sun rises everyday through this gate if it is observed from the most  important buildings of the citadel. You may also climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space), which means &#34;Young Mountain&#34; as opposed to Machu Picchu. Its ascent takes approximately one hour and its top attraction is the  &#34;Temple of the Moon,&#34; a complex of underground constructions probably used for the elite. You may finally go to the Inca Bridge, an amazing stone construction which served to enter or exit  the citadel that has an empty space of several meters in the middle, on which they placed two large wooden boards. Thus, the Incas could isolate the city and protect themselves by removing them. In the afternoon, you will go the Aguas Calientes train station to take your train back to Cusco City.</span></p>

<p>#1<br/>Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a train trip from the Ollanta train station to the town of Aguas Calientes. There you will find the train station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb early to it. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.<br/>#2<br/>Breakfast at the hotel. After a short trip you will arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resting place for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, meaning &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered a World Heritage Site by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. At the end of the tour you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch. In the afternoon you will transfer to the train station of Aguas Calientes to take your train back to Ollanta station.<br/></p>

<p>#1 </p><p>Visitors will embark on one of the most incredible routes of the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful Andean Explorer. The train has 2 dining cars, a lounge car and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the mountains. It also has elegant suites and cabins where the passenger can rest comfortably. The crossing begins leaving Puno in the direction of Cusco. You will enjoy a delicious lunch based on local ingredients and fresh, designed by the chefs of the Belmond Hotel Monasterio. Then, you will visit La Raya, the border between Puno and Cusco, surrounded by imposing mountains. Dinner will be served on board and you will arrive to the Cusco region at night.</p><p>#2 </p><p>During the second and last day of crossing, you will enjoy breakfast on board and arrive in the city of Cusco.</p>

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">#1</div><div style="text-align: justify;">A mobility will pick you up from your hotel in Urubamba and you will be transferred to Ollanta station. Your trip to Machu Picchu will begin with a train trip to the town of Aguas Calientes. There you will find a market of handicrafts, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb up early to it. After a short trip you will arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resting place for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means &#34;Old Mountain&#34;, is considered World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. At the end of the tour you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes, where you will enjoy a delicious lunch in the Coffee Inkaterra Machu Picchu Pueblo. You can enjoy free time in the afternoon. Overnight at hotel of your choice in Aguas Calientes.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="text-align: justify;">You can optionally return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions that keeps the citadel: the Intipunku ( &#34; Puerta del Sol &#34;) was a kind of checkpoint, where the sun rises every day through this door. You can climb Huayna Picchu (subject to availability of spaces), which means &#34; Young Mountain &#34;. Its assenso takes about an hour and in it is the &#34; Temple of the Moon &#34;, a complex of constructions</div><div style="text-align: justify;">underground areas likely to be used by the elite. Finally, you will be able to see the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel. In the afternoon you will transfer to Aguas Calientes train station to take your train back to Ollanta station, and from there you will be taken to your hotel.</div><p></p>

Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a two-hour train trip to the Aguas Calientes town. In Aguas Calientes you will find a train station, a handicraft market, restaurants and accommodations of many different categories for those who would prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb to the top in the early morning. Finally, after a shorttrip, you will arrive in Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture which is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resort for Inca Pachacútec (1438-1471). Machu Picchu means 'Old Mountain' and UNESCO has listed it as a World Heritage and it is one of the new Seven  Wonders ofthe World. You will spend the night in Aguas Calientes. Today you can return to Machu Picchu and you can optionally visit any attraction that the citadel has to offer. You can visit the Intipunku, which means  'Gateway of the Sun'  in Spanish, and it is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca Trail from Ollantaytambo. It was a sort of guardhouse and the Sun rises through this gateway every day if it is contemplated from the most important buildings of the citadel. You can also climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space), the 'Young Mountain', as opposed to Machu Picchu. It takes around an hour to reach the top, and its main attraction is the 'Temple of the Moon', an underground building complex probably conceived for the entertainment of the nobles.Finally, you will visit the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone construction which served to enter and exit the citadel, and which shows a large void in the middle, over which the Incas put two wooden boards. Thus, the Incas were able to isolate the city by just removing those boards, protecting themselves. You will return to the Aguas Calientes train station in theafternoon to board your train back to the Ollantaytambo station.

You will leave the city via Yura, a district characterised by its rugged scenery, hills and cliffs. Then, enter Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain where vicuñas, Andean camelids and national symbol, can be seen. Then head to Vizcachani, Pampas de Toccra, up to the crater of the volcano Chucura and on to Mirador de los Andes at Patapampa (4800 m.a.s.l.) where the volcanoes surrounding Arequipa can be seen. Lunch will be served in the town of Chivay and in the afternoon you can rest in the hotel, or visit the thermal springs at La Calera. Visit Cruz del Cóndor to see the Colca Valley (one of the deepest canyons in the world) followed by an astonishing sight: the great and majestic condor flying only a few metres away. On the way back to Chivay you will go through the villages of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma, Yanque and the vantage points at Antahuilque and Choquetico. After lunch you can visit the village of Coporaque and the Colcas at Chininia, incredible pre-Inca constructions. From there, you will head to the vantage point at Ocolle and the amphitheatre, followed by a short walk to the little-explored ruins at Uyo-Uyo. Return to Chivay in the afternoon. Head to Puno from Chivay by taking the turn-off to Patahuasi. After a brief stop at Lagunillas or Cabanillas for lunch, continue the magical tour of the Andean plateau, stone forests, sky-blue lakes populated by white flamingoes flying over 4,000 metres above sea level. You will arrive at Puno after this amazing journey.

<p>#1</p><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">You will leave the city through Yura, a district characterized by impressive geographical features that include gullies, mountain ranges and canyons. You will enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain populated by vicuñas, native and tamable Andean camelids that are a national symbol. You will then visit Vizcachani, the Toccra Pampas and will skirt the crater of the Chucura Volcano to reach the Los Andes Scenic Viewpoint in Patapampa (4800 masl) from where you can see the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa. You will have lunch at the Chivay town and in the afternoon you will rest at the hotel of your choice or you may visit the local hot  springs.</div><div><br/></div><div>#2</div><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">You will go to the Condor Cross, from where you may appreciate the Colca Valley (which is one of the world's deepest canyons with over four kilometers at its deepest side)  and then you will daydream: the you will see the huge and majestic condor flying at a few meters of distance. Back in Chivay, you will visit the Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque towns, and the Antahuilque and Choquetico scenic viewpoints. After lunch, you will <span style="text-align: justify;">visit the Coporaque town and the Chininia Colcas, incredible pre-Inca constructions on vertical natural walls. Then you will go to the Ocolle Scenic Viewpoint, the Amphitheater and you will take an optional short hike to the identified yet little explored Uyo-Uyo archaelogical remains. You will return to Chivay in the afternoon.</span></div><div><br/></div><div>#3</div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">From Chivay you will go to Puno City through the detour to Patahuasi. The road is a magical tour by the high plains, stone forests and sky blue lagoons populated by cranes that dry their wings in the icy wind above 4 masl. You will arrive in Puno after a 6-hour trip. </div><p><br/></p></div>

<div style="text-align: justify;">#1 </div><div style="text-align: justify;">Charge your batteries with breakfast at the hotel before leaving the city via Yura, a district characterised by its rugged scenery, hills and cliffs. Then enter the Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive grass plain where vicuñas (an Andean camelid and a national symbol) can be spotted. Head through Vizcachani and Pampas de Toccra up to the crater of the Chucura volcano. From there, continue one last leg of the way to reach the Mirador de los Andes at Patapampa, a stunning viewpoint 4,800 metres above sea level with impressive views of the volcanoes surrounding Arequipa. Lunch is served in the town of Chivay and in the afternoon you can rest in the hotel.<br/></div><p><br/></p><div style="text-align: justify;">#2 </div><div style="text-align: justify;">Once breakfast is over, head to Condor’s Cross, from where you can contemplate the view of the Colca Valley (one of the world´s deepest canyons) and see the large, majestic condor soaring over the valley, only a few metres away from where you stand. Back in Chivay, visit the towns of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque, and in between snap some more memorable photos at the Antahuilque and Choquetico viewpoints. Then later on continue to Puno with a detour that takes you through Patahuasi. Along the way, observe the amazing stone forests and sky-blue lagoons populated by cranes that fill this area 4,000 metres above sea level. Arrive at Puno after this amazing journey and enjoy a well-deserved rest at the hotel.</div>

<p>#1 </p><p>Departure from Arequipa by Yura, passing by the skirts of Chachani, Pampa de Arrieros, National  Reserve  of  Pampa  Cañahuas  where  the  slender vicuña is appreciated. Continue on to Vizcachani, Pampas de Toccra where  a variety of  birds  are  observed.  Then  border  the  crater of Chucura to arrive at Patapampa  (4800  masl) and the lookout of the  Andes where  you can observe the  volcanoes  that  surround the city of Arequipa. We  will  return  visiting  the  towns  of Pinchollo,  Maca,  Achoma,  Yanque  and  the  lookouts  of  Antahuilque  and Choquetico. Afternoon free. </p><p>#3 </p><p>Morning free.  Halfway  through  the day you will be picked up at your hoteland  transferred  to  Chivay to enjoy a delicious and repairing lunch. After lunch we return to Arequipa, arriving in the late afternoon.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">#1</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Depart Arequipa after a tasty breakfast at the hotel. Drive through the Yura district, heading towards the Colca Valley. Yura is characterised by a number of dramatic geographical features such as gullies, mountain ranges and canyons. Continue through the Pampa Canahuas Reserve, an extensive plain populated by vicuñas, one of the four indigenous Andean camelids found all over Peru. Cross through the pampas of Viscachani and Toccra, skirting the crater of the Chucura Volcano to reach the Los Andes scenic lookout in Patapampa, found 4,800 metres above sea level. From here, you can see the various volcanoes that surround Arequipa. Lunch is in the picturesque town of Chivay with a transfer in the afternoon to your selected hotel in the Colca Valley. Rest of the day at leisure. </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, prepare for a day of high drama with a trip to the breathtaking Condor Cross lookout. Take a deep breath and appreciate the sheer vastness of the Colca Valley, which is one of the worlds deepest canyons with a maximum depth of more than 4,160 metres – twice that of the Grand Canyon. The thrill of the experience is enhanced by the natural spectacle of huge condors flying over the canyon. Watch in awe as these graceful birds of prey glide over the canyon, patrolling it with military discipline. Descend to the valley floor to discover some of the rustic towns on the banks of the River Colca such as Chivay, Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma and Yanque, where quaint colonial churches are as much as part of the scenery as the peaks of the Andes. There are also stops at the lookouts of Antahuilque and Choquetico to admire majestic views of the valley. After lunch, return to Arequipa.</div>

<p>#1 </p><p>Leaving the city via Yura, you will enter Pampa Cañahuas Reserve, an extensive plain where you can see vicuñas, the indigenous Andean camelid that is a national symbol. Then go to Vizcachani, Pampas de Toccra, up to the crater of the Chucura volcano and on to Los Andes Viewpoint at Patapampa (4800 m.a.s.l.), where the volcanoes surrounding Arequipa can be seen. Lunch will be served in the village of Chivay and in the afternoon you can rest in the hotel or visit the thermal springs at La Calera. </p><p>#2 </p><p>After having a delicious breakfast at the hotel, you will visit the Condor´sCross to see the Colca Valley (the second deepest canyon in the world, with more than 4 kilometers at its deepest part) followed by an astonishing sight: the great and majestic condor flying only a few meters away. On the way back to Chivay you will pass through the villages of Pinchollo, Maca, Achoma, Yanque and the view points at Antahuilque and Choquetico. Then you return to Arequipa after lunch.</p>

<p>#1 </p><p>Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours time. This town is also known as Machu Picchu pueblo and is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means Old Mountain, is considered a World Heritage by the UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Overnight in Aguas Calientes. </p><p>#2 </p><p>Today you can optionally return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions of the citadel. Visit the Intipunku, which in Castilian means Sun Gate and is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. It was kind of a checkpoint control and the sun rises every day through this door if viewed from the most important buildings of the citadel. You may climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means Young Mountain as opposed to Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is the Temple of the Moon, a complex of underground constructions likely to have been used by the elite. Finally you will be able to know the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone building that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and halfway has a gap of several meters above which were two large wooden tables. Thus the Incas could isolate the city by simply withdrawing them and thus remain protected. In the afternoon head to the train station of Aguas Calientes and from there take the train back.</p>

<p>#1 </p><p>Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes. This town is also known as Machu Picchu pueblo and is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes there is a train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means Old Mountain, is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.. </p><p>#2 </p><p>Today you can return to Machu Picchu and you can optionally visit any attraction that the citadel has to offer. You can visit the Intipunku, which means Gateway of the Sun in Spanish, and it is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca Trail from Ollantaytambo. It was a sort of guardhouse and the Sun rises through this gateway every day if it is contemplated from the most important buildings of the citadel. You can also climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space), the Young Mountain, as opposed to Machu Picchu. It takes around an hour to reach the top, and its main attraction is the Temple of the Moon, an underground building complex probably conceived for the entertainment of the nobles. Finally, you will visit the Inca Bridge, an incredible stone construction which served to enter and exit the citadel, and which shows a large void in the middle, over which the Incas put two wooden boards. Thus, the Incas were able to isolate the city by just removing those boards, protecting themselves. You will return to the Aguas Calientes train station in the afternoon to board your train back to the Ollantaytambo station.</p>

<p></p><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">#1 </p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, a transportation service will pick you up and transfer you to the Ollanta Station to board the train to Aguas Calientes, the nearest town to Machu Picchu. Following a scenic journey through dramatic Andean landscapes, arrive at the station in Aguas Calientes. Here you board a coach for a 25-minute ride to Machu Picchu. The ‘Lost City of the Incas’ is believed to have been built in the 1450s by Inca emperor Pachacuti as a holiday retreat, yet within a century the city would be abandoned following the Spanish conquest. The Spaniards never found it, so it was spared the destruction they brought on other Inca sites. Instead, it was slowly swallowed up by the jungle and hidden from harm. A guided tour explains the origins behind this awe-inspiring citadel, presenting its most prominent landmarks such as the monumental Main Square, the Royal Quarters, the Temple of the Three Windows, the Circular Towers, the Sacred Sun Dial, and the Burial Grounds. Return to Aguas Calientes for lunch at the famous Café Inkaterra, a beautiful thatched-roof restaurant hemmed in between the railway and a mountain.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2 </div><p></p><p style="text-align: justify;">The delicious breakfast at the hotel is followed by a relaxing morning at leisure. We strongly recommend waking up early today to see the breathtaking sunrise over Machu Picchu. This is a must-have experience! The best views are from Huayna Picchu, which is about 360 metres above Machu Picchu, boasting an underground temple complex, and from Inti Punku, which offers even better views from about 390 metres above the citadel. Another option is the Inca Bridge, a secret military trail, which controlled the access to the citadel. In the afternoon, transfer to the Aguas Calientes station and take the train back to Ollanta station. From here you will be transferred to your hotel in Cusco. The second entrance to Machu Picchu is not included.<br/></p>

Your tour begins with a train ride to Machu Picchu. In about an hour and a half reach the town of Aguas Calientes, also known as 'Machu Picchu'. This is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. Finally you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary andretreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the newseven wonders of the world. You may also optionally visit the Intipunku, the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnightin Aguas Calientes. Head to the train station of Aguas Calientes and from there take the train back to Ollanta station.

<p>#1 </p><p>Arrival and welcome cocktail at the exclusive Amantica Logde, located at the shores of Lake Titicaca. You will go on a hike to Pachatata, the highest point in the island, from where you will be able to observe the beauty and gratness of the lake. You will have lunch and then participate in a coca leaves reading; ancestral Andean tradition executed by a shaman. Finally, you will enjoy dinner. </p><p>#2 </p><p>During the morning you will enjoy breakfast and then visit Uros, a group of floating islands built with ¨totora¨, an acuatic plant that grows in the lake. You will have lunch and then participate of a ¨Pachamama¨ Ceremony; a ritual that consists of giving offerings to Mother Earth, generating reciprocity between the human being and nature. Finally, you will have dinner. </p><p>#3 </p><p>After breakfast, you will embark on a typical boat that will take you on a fishing experience in the lake. Then, you will participate in a hands-on cooking lesson and have lunch. Finally, you will check-out from the hotel.</p>

<p>#1 </p><p>Enjoy three days and two nights in the charming Titilaka hotel. The hotel is located on a private peninsula of Lake Titicaca and has a spectacular view.At arrival, you will be transferred from the airport to the hotel where you will enjoy a delicious lunch. Then you will have free time in which you can choose one of the various activities offered, which include paddling through the lake, biking, walking or navigate;  you can also choose guided tours onthe lake and land, where you can explore the ecological and cultural diversity that characterizes this region. You can visit the Acora Market, Lampa, Sillustani and the Atuncolla Community, or participate in bird watching, trekking to Kolla (private) and visit temples such as Juli and Pomata. Excursions to Tiahuanaco (Bolivia) and Isla del Sol and the Moon have an additional cost. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and spend the night. </p><p>#2 </p><p>You will have breakfast at the hotel and then have free time during themorning where you can choose one of the various activities offered, which include paddling the lake, biking, walking or surfing; and has guided toursof the lake and land, where you can explore the ecological and cultural diversity that characterizes this region. You can visit the Acora Market, Lampa, Sillustani and the Atuncolla Community, or participate in bird watching, trekking to Kolla (private) and visit temples such as Juli and Pomata. Excursions to Tiahuanaco (Bolivia) and Isla del Sol and the Moon have an additional cost. Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and overnight. After lunch you can also choose from these activities. </p><p>#3 </p><p>Finally, you will return to the hotel where you will have dinner and spend the night. After breakfast, you will be transfer to the airport.</p>

<p>#1</p><p>Leave the port of Puno to the vastness of Lake Titicaca, the highest navigable lake in the world and part of the border between Peru and Bolivia; countries sharing the Aymara population indiscriminately. The lake covers more than 8 thousand square kilometers and its islands have developed different communities that maintain their ancestral customs and clothing up today. Taquile, Uros and Amantani are the main islands. Your first stop will be at Uros; an ancient society that populates a series of artificial islands made of reeds. These are made weaving reeds in areas where it grows thicker forming a natural layer. Then visit Amantaní; a circular island with an average diameter of 3.4 kilometers whose rich highland customs have captured the attention of travellers from all over the world. In the evening dine at your host´s house and then, if desired, participate in a rural musical performance before bedtime.</p><p><br/></p><p>#2</p><p>After breakfast you will board a motorboat to head to the island of Taquile, which is Quechua speaking, unlike the other Aymara-speaking islands in the lake. In Taquile you will have lunch at a local restaurant. Then return to the boat and head back to the city of Puno.</p><div><p><br/></p></div>

<p></p><p>#1</p><p>Motor- boat excursion to Taquile Island on Lake Titicaca, inhabited by Quechua speakingnatives  who  have  developed  over  several generations both an efficient and original social system and a fine handweaving technique. Lunch at a typical restaurant.In the evening, enjoy  some traditional hospitality  having dinner  and staying at home of local islanders for the night.</p><p><br/></p><p>#2</p><p>After breakfast, start off the morning leisurely and stroll by the quaint streets of this colorful island. In the afternoon, go on a motorboat excursion over Lake Titicaca  to visit the  interesting  and unique floating islands of the  Uros. The Uros are an ancient culture that inhabits a series of artificial islands built using reeds growing in the lake itself. These are constructed by weaving reed in the areas where it grows thicker and thus forming a natural layer. Return to Puno in the afternoon.</p><p></p>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Start your journey arriving to Puerto Maldonado, where our staff will welcome you and provide the necessary information for your stay. Navigate by outboard motorized canoe along the Madre de Dios River to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion for approximately 25 minutes. Enjoy lunch with organic ingredients and native flavors. After lunch, take a walk through Concepción trails, our inside property nature trails, where you will discover and learn about primary and secondary rainforest, its components, uses, transformations by the human being, and its relevance for the world. At dusk navigate by outboard motorized canoe, at the Twilight river excursion. By sailing on the Madre de Dios River, you will learn about its ecosystem, Amazonian creeks, nocturnal animals behavior, and the southern constellations. In the evening enjoy a nice dinner in the Casa Grande dining room.</p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p>#2 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">New experiences are ahead and after taking a nutritious buffet breakfast, get ready for a long trek through terra firma into the Tambopata National Reserve towards Lake Sandoval. Here you will understand why Madre de Dios is called the biodiversity capital of Peru, while learning about the Reserves conservation efforts and the lakes formation. Navigate by dugout canoe across this mirror-like oxbow lake that is home to the endangered giant river otter (Pteronura brasiliensis), blue and yellow macaw (Ara ararauna), red howler monkey (Alouatta seniculus), black caiman (Melanosuchus niger), and one of the worlds biggest fresh water scaled fish, the paiche (Arapaima gigas). Back to the lodge for lunch, and later the Inkaterra Canopy walkway &amp; Anaconda walk await for you. Take a 20-minute ride by outboard motorized canoe to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway Interpretation Center where you will learn about its construction and the conservation projects of ITA-Inkaterra Asociación (NGO). Ascend one of the two 98-foot towers (30 m.) and cross the 7 hanging bridges that connect the treetops at 91 ft. high (28 m.). Enjoy the breathtaking vistas and get the chance to watch white-throated toucans (Ramphastos tucanus), woodpeckers, trogons, squirrel monkeys (Saimiri boliviensis) and the three-toed sloth (Choleopus hoffmani). After descending keep on feeling the incredible primary rainforest energy going along the 200-meter wooden bridge over the swamps (aguajales). You will learn about the fauna and flora of this flooded ecosystem. At night get ready for an adventure at the Rainforest by night excursion, where you will explore the Amazon rainforest that gives way to nocturnal animals who display intriguing behavior patterns, and enhanced senses as adaptation mechanisms. Listen to nocturnal living nature sounds, and penetrate this hidden world. Return to the lodge and enjoy a nice dinner. </p><p>#3 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">The sun rises once again and after an early breakfast you will take our native farm excursion where you will be able to learn local techniques of cultivation and taste some of the products during the visit. Then visit the Botanical garden, discover the immense variety of medicinal plants considered part of the natural pharmacy used by the habitants at the amazon region. Compare the medicinal uses of local medicine with traditional remedies. Getting back to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion you will have lunch. Take a rest and get ready for the cocha water excursion. Enjoy a dugout canoe ride on the Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion natural pond (cocha). Try native fishing applying the catch and release techniques, as you enjoy the scenery observing hoatzins (Opisthocomus hoazin), a diversity of fly catchers, and gray-necked woodrails (Aramides cajanea). Disembark at the remains of Fitzcarrald steam boat; learn about its history and how it inspired Inkaterra to ask Werner Herzog to film the award-winning Fitzcarraldo, best director at Cannes, 1982. You will be able to participate in the inland collpa during the evening, where you will see different species and their night routines by observing through our blinds. End up your day with a pleasant dinner. </p><p style="text-align: justify;">#4 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast buffet, take a ride back to Puerto Maldonado by outboard motorized canoe. Arrive at the Butterfly House where you may wait for your flight back home or to your next destination, while visiting this beautiful butterfly house and learning about the fascinating transformation of these colorful insects.<br/></p>

Listen to nocturnal living nature sounds, and penetrate this hidden world. Return to the lodge and enjoy a nice dinner.</div></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div>#3</div><div><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">The sun rises once again and after an early breakfast  you will take our Native farm excursion where you will be able to learn local techniques of cultivation and taste some of the products during the visit. Then visit the Botanical garden, discover the immense variety of medicinal plants considered part of the natural pharmacy used by the habitants at the amazon region. Compare the medicinal uses of local medicine with traditional remedies. Getting back to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion you will have lunch. Take a rest and get ready for the cocha water excursion. Enjoy a dugout canoe ride on the Inkaterra Hacienda Concepcion natural pond (cocha). Try native fishing applying the catch and release techniques, as you enjoy the scenery observing hoatzins (Opisthocomus hoazin), a diversity of fly catchers, and gray-necked woodrails (Aramides cajanea). Disembark at the remains of Fitzcarrald steam boat; learn about its history and how it inspired Inkaterra to ask Werner Herzog to film the award-winning Fitzcarraldo, best director at Cannes, 1982. You will be able to participate in the Inland Colpa during the evening, where you will see different species and their night routines by observing through our blinds. End up your day with a pleasant dinner.</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div>#4</div><div><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast buffet, take a ride back to Puerto Maldonado by outboard motorized canoe. Arrive at the Butterfly House where you may wait for your flight back home or to your next destination, while visiting this beautiful Butterfly house and learning about the fascinating transformation of these colorful insects.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p></div>

<p>#1 </p><p>Start your journey arriving in Puerto Maldonado, where our staff will welcome you and provide the necessary information for your stay. Navigate by outboard motorized canoe along the Madre de Dios River to Inkaterra Hacienda Concepción for approximately 25 minutes. Enjoy lunch with organic ingredients and native flavors. After lunch, take a walk through Concepcion Trails, inside property nature trails, where you will discover and learn about primary and secondary rainforest, its components, uses, human impact and its relevance for the world. At dusk navigate by outboard motorized canoe, at the Twilight River excursion. By sailing on the Madre de Dios River, you will learn about its ecosystem, Amazon creeks, nocturnal animals behaviour and the southern constellations. In the evening, enjoy dinner at Casa Grande´s dining room. </p><p><span>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). </span><span>During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</span></p><p>#2 </p><p>New experiences are ahead and after taking a nutritious buffet breakfast, get ready for a long trek through terra firma into the Tambopata National Reserve towards Lake Sandoval. Here you will understand why Madre de Dios is called the biodiversity capital of Peru, while learning about the Reserve´s conservation efforts and the lake´s formation. Navigate by dugout canoe across this mirror-like oxbow lake that is home to the endangered giant river otter (Pteronura brasiliensis), blue and yellow macaw (Ara ararauna),red howler monkey (Alouatta seniculus), black caiman (Melanosuchus niger), and one of the worlds biggest fresh water scaled fish, the paiche (Arapaima gigas). Back to the lodge for lunch, and later the INKATERRA CANOPY WALKWAY &amp; ANACONDA WALK await for you. Take a 20-minute ride by outboard motorized canoe to the Inkaterra Canopy Walkway Interpretation Center where you will learn about its construction and the conservation projects of ITA-Inkaterra Asociación (NGO). Ascend one of the two 98-foot towers (30 m.) and cross the7 hanging bridges that connect the treetops at 91 ft. high (28 m). Enjoy the breathtaking vistas and get the chance to watch white-throated toucans(Ramphastos tucanus), woodpeckers, trogons, squirrel monkeys (Saimiri boliviensis) and the three-toed sloth (Choleopus hoffmani). After descendingkeep on feeling the incredible primary rainforest energy going along the 200-meter wooden bridge over the swamps (aguajales). You will learn aboutthe fauna and flora of this flooded ecosystem. At night get ready for an adventure during the RAINFOREST BY NIGHT excursion, where you will explore the Amazon rainforest that gives way to nocturnal animals who display intriguing behavior patterns, and enhanced senses as adaptation mechanisms. Listen to nocturnal living nature sounds, and penetrate this hidden world. End up your day with a pleasant dinner. </p><p><br/></p><p>#3 </p><p>After breakfast buffet, take a ride back to Puerto Maldonado by outboard motorized canoe. Arrive at the Butterfly House where you may wait for your flight back home or to your next destination, while visiting this beautiful BUTTERFLY HOUSE and learning about the fascinating transformation of these colorful insects.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">The employees of the lodge will receive you upon your arrival at the airport and will transfer you to the fluvial port of the Infierno native community, located at 45 minutes from Puerto Maldonado. From there you will sail for two and a half hours to Refugio Amazonas. During this trip, you will enjoy lunch while you are watching a great variety of local birds, such as black skimmers, crowned egrets and flycatchers. Upon your arrival at the lodge, you will receive a brief introduction about the local wildlife and, after resting, you will take a short hike to a 35-meter high watch tower built with scaffoldings. You will watch the sunset amidst toucans, parrots and macaws, and will return to the lodge to have dinner and rest. After having breakfast, you will hike for approximately one hour to visit Lake Sachavacayoc, a flooded area that houses particular Amazonian species due to its calm waters. During a canoe trip, you will find turtles, caimans, giant otters and riparian birds, among them, the ´shansho´, which has a prehistoric appearance. Next, you will return to the lodge to have lunch, take a short rest, and hike on the Ethnobotanical Path, where you will learn about the daily use of the natural resources of the jungle as medicines, construction and food. After having breakfast, you will take a short hike to a nearby collpa. Collpas are clay deposits that parrots and macaws visit in large quantities to eat this substance, as it helps them clean their stomachs. In this same place, it is possible to watch blue-headed parrots, and Andean cocks-of-the-rocks, a beautiful endemic species. After watching hundreds of birds fluttering around you, you will walk on a path that enters the chestnut forest, as this local nut has become an important income for native communities. After learning how they are harvested and transported, you will return to the lodge to have lunch and then you will visit a parcel of native crops. In the evening, you will have dinner and go to rest. After having breakfast, a motor boat will transfer you to Puerto Maldonado and then to the airport.</p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p>

<p>The employees of the lodge will receive you upon your arrival at the airport and will transfer you to the fluvial port of the Infierno native community, located at 45 minutes from Puerto Maldonado. From there you will sail for two and a half hours to Refugio Amazonas. During this trip, you will enjoy lunch while you are watching a great variety of local birds, such as black skimmers, crowned egrets and flycatchers. You will unpack upon your arrival at the lodge, and after resting and enjoying dinner, you will take a night tour to watch the night fauna. After having breakfast, you will take a 20-minute boat trip to a farm, a small land devoted to grow local products, and an ethnobotanical garden. You will then visit a chestnut tree camp and a chestnut path, al this local nut that has become an important income for native communities. After learning the harvesting and transportation process, you will return to the lodge to have lunch. You will then visit Lake Condenado, which due to its calm waters is home to a great variety of animals, such as giant otters, caimans and ´shanshos´. After having breakfast, a motorboat will transfer you to Puerto Maldonado and then to the airport.</p><p><span>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). <p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">At arrival in the airport of Puerto Maldonado, you will be transferred to the fluvial port to reach Posada Amazónica in a two-hour boat trip. You will be able to see the great diversity of birds while touring the Madre de Dios River. The Madre de Dios River flows along 1150 kilometers, crossing most of the national Manu and Tambopata National Reserves. The Tambopata National Reserve, which has an area of one million and a half hectares, has been the subject of several TV documentaries due to its exuberant flora and fauna, which makes it a unique region in the world. After arriving at the lodge and getting installed in your bungalow, you will take a short tour to get acquainted with the area that surrounds the lodge. Have dinner and then rest to make the most out of the next day.</p><p>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After having breakfast, you will board a motor boat heading for Lake Tres Chimbadas in a 15-minute ride. Once at the lake, take a canoe trip through its shores to have a close look at the fauna that populates it. The most outstanding animals are giant otters, turtles and shanshos, a bird with a prehistoric appearance. Unlike rivers, lakes attract a different diversity of animals, as there is no need to fight the current and it represents safe, calm water for marine, terrestrial and flying animals. You will then return to the lodge to have lunch and later tour the neighboring paths. This will enable you to watch a great variety of insects and - if you are lucky - vipers, deer and other larger animals from a very short distance. Then, take a night tour after dinner to watch a variety of animals that only leave their lairs at night.</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After having breakfast, you will take a short hike to a nearby collpa. Collpas are clay deposits, rich in minerals, which parrots and macaws visit in large quantities to eat, as its nutrients are essential for these species. After watching hundreds of birds fluttering around in front of you, you will return to the lodge to have lunch. Later, go on a hike during the afternoon through a primary forest accompanied by a naturalist guide.</p><p>#4</p><p>After breakfast, a motor boat will transfer you to the airport of Puerto Maldonado to take your next flight.</p>

<p>#1</p><p>Upon your arrival at Puerto Maldonado Airport, you will visit the Butterfly House and then reach the pier just 15 minutes away. Experience the charm of the forest sailing 45 minutes along the Madre de Dios River to the hotel. After a brief presentation, enjoy a nice lunch with local ingredients. Take a nap, drink a refreshing tea and get ready for the afternoon excursion. After meeting with your guide, choose from the many excursions available. You can enter the jungle through a guided walk along the trail system in the natural area of the hostel, where you will discover the Amazon Basin. Thistour provides an excellent introduction to the rainforest environment, passing by impressive trees such as the Shihuahuaco and the strangely beautiful strangler fig, offering excellent opportunities to observe birds and exotic insects. At nightfall, do the River by Night Tour: a guided boat ride by the afternoon sailing through the Madre de Dios River. Witness the transformation from a diurnal to a nocturnal world: birds and other daytime animals give way to species specially adapted to the night. While enjoying the nature around you, watch for the appearance of nightjars, owls, capybaras and up to three different species of alligators. Then you can enjoy dinner served in the main dining room. After dinner, you can choose to go to your cabin, or join a nice talk about the experience of life in the Amazon while listening to the intriguing sounds of the jungle at night. </p><p><span>*Only medium-size luggage is allowed during your accommodation in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10kg (22lb). The maximum weight of luggage allowed in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15kg (33lb). </span><span>During your stay, larger luggage will remain stored.</span></p><p>#2</p><p>Breakfast starts very early for those who want to avoid the heat and enjoy nature to the fullest. During the morning, you can make an excursion to Lake Sandoval in the Tambopata National Reserve. Sail on a wooden canoe acrossthe lake, home to many species that are endangered such as the giant river otter, red howler monkeys, red-bellied macaws, anacondas and side-neckturtles. Then you will return to the lodge for lunch. After lunch, you can visit the Inkaterra Canopy and the Anaconda Walk. When you reach the first tower, 29 meters above the forest floor, begins your encounter with the species that inhabit the treetops. You will literally walk through the rainforest for more than a quarter mile in a suspended bridge network linking eight observation platforms, where you will see colorful toucans, woodpeckers, trogons, monkeys, sloths, among other species. If you wish, you can extend your tour to appreciate the sunset in one of the towers. Once you return to the hotel, enjoy a presentation about nature in the Eco Center and the Happy Hour. Then dinner will be served. </p><p>#3</p><p>Enjoy a walk through the Gamitana Creek, located 25 minutes by boat from the lodge. Explore the jungle for an hour and a half along the winding Gamitana Creek, home to piranhas, alligators, turtles and birds, until reaching the Sanipanga stop. Sail along the river appreciating the abundant flora and fauna of the rainforest. Return to the Gamitana model farm for a brief guided tour, where you will learn more about an Amazon model farm. Finally, return to the lodge to enjoy your lunch. After lunch, you will walk along the trails of the hostel to the Amazon Garden. This interesting garden, created by local people over 10 years ago, shows plants that inhabitants use, and typical trees and orchids of the tropical rainforest. The interpreter´s explanation will help you familiarize with the traditional uses and benefits of vegetation used for construction, including those used in native shelters and clothing. Then, enjoy the Jungle Night Tour through the trails of Inkaterra Ecological Reserve, for about 2 hours. Step into the forest and experience the mystery of the animal activity that awakens in the jungle at sunset. Listen to the night sounds and discover species that only emerge in the dark. At the end, return to the lodge. </p><p>#4</p><p>You will enjoy the day trip to the Hacienda Concepcion, built in a former cocoa and rubber plantation that once belonged to the &#34;Fundo Concepcion&#34;, a medical center of the Catholic mission in the 50s. Until 1976, this property also functioned as ecological research and training center for the local community, within 819 hectares of natural forest nestled in a natural lake. Hacienda Concepción has diverse ecosystems, tropical plants and wildlife. During his tour, you will learn the history and visit the remains of a ship called Fitzcarrald. Return to the lodge for lunch, and then you can make the trip to Aguajales, to visit an interesting palm swamps system along a 200 meters long bridge, located on the marshes of the jungle. You will recognize the transition from secondary forest to palm swamps and have the opportunity to observe different species of amphibians, snakes, birds and mammals. Learn about the different fruits and surrounding flora, its role in the ecosystem of the Aguajales, and its many uses and benefits. Finally, return to the lodge. </p><p>#5</p><p>Enjoy a full breakfast before departure. A return trip on a 45-minute boat ride along the Madre de Dios River takes you to Puerto Maldonado, where you can visit the Butterfly House. Peru has the greatest variety of butterflies in the world, with 3,700 known species. Many of these can be seen in the Butterfly House, a sample of the variety, beauty and adaptability of these fascinating insects.</p>

Take a nap, drink a refreshing tea and get ready for the afternoon excursion.After meeting with your guide, choose from the many excursions available.You can enter the jungle through a guided walk along the trail system in the natural area of the hostel, where you will discover the Amazon Basin. Thistour p<span id="selectionBoundary_1551973399387_9204648654102436" class="rangySelectionBoundary">&#65279;</span>rovides an excellent introduction to the rainforest environment, passing by impressive trees such as the Shihuahuaco and the strangelybeautiful strangler fig, offering excellent opportunities to observe birds and exotic insects. At nightfall, do the River by Night Tour: a guided boat ride by the afternoon sailing through the Madre de Dios River. Witness the transformation from a diurnal to a nocturnal world: birds and other daytime animals give way to species specially adapted to the night. While enjoying the nature around you, watch for the appearance of nightjars, owls, capybaras and up to three different species of alligators. Then you canenjoy dinner served in the main dining room. After dinner, you can choose to go to your cabin, or join a nice talk about the experience of life in the Amazon while listening to the intriguing sounds of the jungle at night. </p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Breakfast starts very early for those who want to avoid the heat and enjoy nature to the fullest. During morning, you can make an excursion to Lake Sandoval in the Tambopata National Reserve. Sail on a wooden canoe acrossthe lake, home to many species that are endangered such as the giant river otter, red howler monkeys, red-bellied macaws, anacondas and side-neckturtles. Then you will return to the lodge for lunch. After lunch, you can visit the Inkaterra Canopy and the Anaconda Walk. When you reach the first tower, 29 meters above the forest floor, begins your encounter with the species that inhabit the treetops. You will literally walk through the rainforest for more than a quarter mile in a suspended bridge networklinking eight observation platforms, where you will see colorful toucans, woodpeckers, trogons, monkeys, sloths, among other species. If you wish, you can extend your tour to appreciate the sunset in one of the towers. After descending the Canopy, go through the Anaconda Walk for about 30 minutesalong the 200 meters of wooden bridges over the Aguajales Swamps. In this lush ecosystem, recognize different species of amphibians, birds, mammals and wild flora. There is also the possibility of seeing snakes on these wetlands. The walk finishes by returning to the lodge to enjoy apresentation about nature in the Eco Center. Then dinner will be served. </p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">Enjoy a full breakfast before departure. A return trip on a 45-minute boat ride along the Madre de Dios River takes you to Puerto Maldonado, where you can visit the Butterfly House. Peru has the greatest variety of butterflies in the world, with 3,700 known species. Many of these can be seen in the Butterfly House, a sample of the variety, beauty and adaptability of thesefascinating insects.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Morning departure from Lima. We take a three-and-a-half hour drive south down the Panamerican Highway to the Paracas peninsula where youll take a boat ride to the Ballestas islands. These are inhabited by boobies sealions and Humboldt penguins. The still unexplained candelabra Lines can be seen en route. We then take a 3 hour drive to Nasca. Upon arrival, we provide accommodation at a selected hotel. You will be transferred to the airport for an overflight of the Nasca lines (subject to weather conditions). Continue south via the Panamerican Highway to Arequipa, also known as the White City due to the white volcanic stone used in its buildings.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Passengers depart from Lima early in the morning for a bus ride headed to Paracas. Upon arrival, assistance at the bus station will transfer you to the pier for a boat ride to the Ballestas Islands, inhabited by a variety of sea birds, pelicans, Humboldt penguins and sea lions. Your boat will stop in front of the peninsula to appreciate the intiguing chandelier figures. After this excursion, transfer by private transportation to the city of Ica to visit its Regional Museum, which houses important collections, mostly of archeological pre-Hispanic objects from the Ica region, of which the highlight is its ceramics and textiles. The Museum also has colonial and republican pieces of local history, as well as art and paleontology samples. After this visit, you will have free time for lunch on your own, before heading to the bus station for a trip to Nasca. Upon arrival in Nasca, assistance at the bus station and transfer to the hotel. After breakfast at the hotel, you will be transferred to the airport from where you can optionally overflight the Nasca lines (subject to weather conditions). You will have the rest of the day free to do activities on your own or rest. In the evening, transfer to the bus station to take the bus to Arequipa. At arrival, you will be taken to your selected hotel.</p>

<p>#1 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Early in the morning, you will be transferred from Lima to Pisco (250km south of the capital). Upon arrival, transfer to the airport of Pisco. Face one of the worlds greatest mysteries while overflying the Nazca Lines. The town, settled besides a small river crossing the desolate plain of San Jose, passed unnoticed until commercial airlines began crossing the desert during the late 1920s. Scientists attention was immediately drawn to Nazca but the mystery remains unsolved. What exact purpose did they meet? We dont quite know. The truth is they depict coastal, highland and jungle fauna (whale, hummingbird, monkey, spider) as well as geometric figures (lines, trapeze, triangle). Thereafter, weather and air-traffic controllers permitting, fly above the figured-scrapped hills of Palpa. The Palpa Lines are believed to predate those of Nazca, picturing animals and human figures and a prominent deity. The Nazca culture flourished between 50 B.C. and 600 A.D, studies suggesting their society suffered an environmental collapse derived from huarango deforestation.</p><p><br/></p><p>#2 </p><p style="text-align: justify;">Early morning transfer to the dock for an optional boat ride to the Ballestas Islands, inhabited by pelicans, sea lions, migratory birds and Humboldt penguins. The boat will also make a stop in front of the peninsula to view the intriguing figures of the Candelabro Lines. Return along the Pacific coast to Lima, in a trip of approximately four hours of duration.Upon arrival, transfer to the hotel.<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="color: #17365d;"></span></p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#1</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Arrival to the city of Iquitos. A representative waits for your arrival at the airport. From here, the journey starts towards Nauta, a small riverside town on the banks of the Marañón River. Once there, you are welcomed at the private port. When boarding, enjoy a fantastic panoramic view from the observation area. Participate in an orientation talk and get to know the entertainment centre that offers movies, as well as an extensive library of books about flora and fauna of the area. The ship also has a fully equipped bar where you can try any regional or international combination of liquors and aromas. Dinner is served during the afternoon.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;"></p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#2</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">Wake up early to appreciate one of the most beautiful areas of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve: Fundo Casual. The walk through this area will take you to the depths of the Terra Firme jungle (un-flooded forest) where you can appreciate the local fauna. Return to the boat for lunch on board. Then, visit a local community to meet the people who have inhabited the Amazon for generations. During the visit, the villagers share their traditions and customs with visitors.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;"><b></b></p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#3</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, take a walk through the canopy of the Amazon Park, a unique experience in the jungle! At the height of more than 25 meters and a length of 500 meters, the &#34;Canopy Walkway&#34; offers a view from the top of the trees, the best observation point to admire the jungle´s wildlife and vegetation. After lunch on the boat, cross through the Yanayacu and Pucate rivers, passing through different streams and lagoons. This is one of the best preserved and protected areas of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve. If you want to interact directly with the waters of the Amazon, go out in one of the kayaks or paddle boards; and you will even have the opportunity to swim with the curious pink dolphins. Return to the boat and dinner on board.</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">#4</p><p class="MsoNoSpacing" style="text-align: justify;">During the morning disembark in the port of Nauta and head towards the airport. On the way, visit the Floating Mammals Rescue and Rehabilitation Centre to learn about manatees and other rescued species, as well as plans for the species conservation. Finally, arrive at the airport. You are accompanied by a representative who assists you on the way to your departure from the city.</p><p><span style="color: #17365d;"></span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;"></span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12.0pt;color: #222222;"></span></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="color: #17365d;">#1</span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="color: #17365d;">Arrival to the city of Iquitos. A representative will be waiting for your arrival at the airport from where you will head to Nauta, a small riverside town on the banks of the Marañón River. Once there, enjoy a reception at the private port. Embark and enjoy a fantastic panoramic view from the observation deck. Participate in an orientation talk and enjoy the entertainment centre that offers movie projection, as well as an extensive library of books about flora and fauna of the area. Also, a fully equipped bar where you can try any regional or international combination of liquors and aromas. Dinner served during the afternoon.</span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12.0pt;color: #222222;"></span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;"># 2</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">After breakfast, explore Belluda Caño, part of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, known as the &#34;Forest of Galleries&#34;. It is an area rich in flora with a great diversity of bromeliads. You can also see the Paiche, huge fish that can weigh up to eighty kilos. After a short walk through the jungle, return to the boat to have lunch and later cross the El Dorado River, whose name comes from the myth of El Dorado, a hidden city in the Amazon rainforest built entirely with gold. During the discovery of South America, many explorers were encouraged to seek it failing at all times. At night, have dinner and rest.</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;"># 3</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">Exploration in a skiff on Lake Clavero during the morning. You can enter the lake to swim through its calm waters. After lunch, visit the private reserve of Yarapa, abundant in animal species, where you can navigate in a kayak. Afterwards, head to Puerto Miguel to get to know the village and the local community; a unique opportunity to learn about the way of life in the Amazon. Return to the boat and enjoy the farewell dinner.</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">#4</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">During the morning disembark in the port of Nauta and head towards the airport. On the way, visit the River Mammal Rescue and Rehabilitation Centre to learn about manatees and other rescued species. You will be accompanied by a representative who will assist you on the way to your departure from the city.</span></p>
