Dinner served during the afternoon.</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">#2</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">After breakfast, explore Belluda Caño, part of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, known as the &#34;Forest of Galleries&#34;. It is an area rich in flora with a great diversity of bromeliads. You can also see the Paiche, huge fish that can weigh up to 80 kilos. After a short walk through the jungle, return to the boat to have lunch and later cross the El Dorado River, whose name comes from the myth of El Dorado, a hidden city in the Amazon rainforest built entirely with gold. During the discovery of South America, many explorers were encouraged to seek it failing at all times. At night, have dinner and rest.</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">#3</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">Today, begin your journey on the Zapote River and explore it on a kayak. Return to the boat and after lunch sail through Pacaya, one of the rivers that guard the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve. The reserve is the most extensive flooded protected area in the world and at the same time one of the most biodiverse. It is possible to see howler monkeys, white alligators, sloths, pink dolphins, among others. One third of all species recorded in the world come from here, while one in five birds live in this habitat. When finished, return to the boat for dinner.</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">#4</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">Exploration in a skiff on Lake Clavero during the morning. You can enter the lake to swim in its calm waters. After lunch, visit the private reserve of Yarapa, abundant in animal species, where you can navigate in a kayak. </span><span style="color: #17365d;">Afterwards, head to Puerto Miguel to visit the village and the local community; a unique opportunity to get up close to the Amazon lifestyle. Return to the boat and enjoy the farewell dinner.</span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12.0pt;color: #222222;"></span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12.0pt;color: #17365d;">#5</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">During the morning disembark in the port of Nauta and head towards the airport. On the way, visit the River Mammal Rescue and Rehabilitation Centre to learn about manatees and other rescued species. You will be accompanied by a representative who will assist you on the way to your departure from the city.</span></p><p></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="color: #17365d;">#1</span></p><p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="color: #17365d;">Arrival to the city of Iquitos. A representative will be waiting for your arrival at the airport from where you will head to Nauta, a small riverside town on the banks of the Marañón River. Once there, enjoy a reception at the private port. Embark and enjoy a fantastic panoramic view from the observation deck. Participate in an orientation talk and enjoy the entertainment centre that offers movie projection, as well as an extensive library of books about flora and fauna of the area. Also, a fully equipped bar where you can try any regional or international combination of liquors and aromas. Dinner served during the afternoon.</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">#2</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">After breakfast, explore Belluda Caño, part of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, known as the &#34;Forest of Galleries&#34;. During the discovery of South America, many explorers were encouraged to seek it failing at all times. At night, have dinner and rest.</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">#3</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">Today, begin your journey on the Zapote River and explore it on a kayak. Return to the boat and after lunch sail through Pacaya, one of the rivers that guard the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve. You can enter the lake to swim in its calm waters. After lunch, visit the private reserve of Yarapa, abundant in animal species, where you can navigate in a kayak. </span><span style="color: #17365d;">Afterwards, head to Puerto Miguel to visit the village and the local community; a unique opportunity to get up close to the Amazon lifestyle. Return to the boat and enjoy the farewell dinner.</span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12.0pt;color: #222222;"></span></p><p><span style="font-size: 12.0pt;color: #17365d;">#5</span></p><p><span style="color: #17365d;">During the morning disembark in the port of Nauta and head towards the airport. You will be accompanied by a representative who will assist you on the way to your departure from the city.</span></p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">#1</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Land at Iquitos International Airport and after collecting your luggage; you will board an air-conditioned bus to transfer you to the main port of Iquitos where the cruise is waiting. As you settle into your spacious room, the ship will begin the journey through depths of the Amazon River. There will be a meeting on the upper deck to inform about safety on board. Then the Cruise Director and the guides will give a brief orientation talk about</div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">life on board, the places you'll go and what you will see on your Amazon journey. After that, enjoy a welcome dinner while watching the river and surrounding areas through the windows. Before retiring to rest, you can enjoy a cocktail on the upper deck under the stars.</div></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">You will wake up early to watch, for the first time, the sunrise on the Amazon River. After a delicious breakfast, with a wide variety of dishes, Peruvian specialties and fresh fruit juices, you will visit Yacapana Islands, inhabited by iguanas, to which you can take pictures. You will walk very close to the islands aboard the boat, where you can take good pictures of the flora and fauna found on our way. You will also go sport fishing and</div><div style="text-align: justify;">try to catch the famous &#34;Piranhas&#34;, for which our nature guides will take the proper tools. As the boat continues up the Amazon River, will have free time to rest or take a nap before lunch. After a light lunch in a dining room with air conditioning, the guides will give a talk on wildlife. As the sun sets, you will have the opportunity to see and photograph the gray and pink freshwater dolphin, and there is also the possibility of observing some macaws, toucans, sloths and monkeys. Then return to the cruise for dinner, but first you can take a ?pisco sour? as an appetizer on the upper deck while watching the sunset.</div></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#3</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">Very early in the morning, you will have the option of going on tour for wildlife viewing along the Ucayali River. After breakfast, visit with the nature guides, around the native community of Magdalena. Walk on a trail where you will see some bird species, insects and flora that surrounds it. You'll get to see, and in some cases buy, crafts made by this kind Amazon people. You return to the ship for lunch, followed by a nap. In the</div><div style="text-align: justify;">afternoon, you will see the two different kinds of freshwater dolphins, including the unusual pink dolphins found in the Amazon River. In addition, you will appreciate a variety of primates, among the 13 species of monkeys resident in the National Park: squirrel and howler monkeys, marmosets and pygmy monkeys. Watch for alligators, and keep your binoculars ready as they see more than 200 species of birds. Finally, return to the cruise for dinner.</div></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#4</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, the cruise will sail through the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, a flooded forest covering five million acres at the headwaters of the Amazon basin. Get up early and see one of the great spectacles of the Southern Hemisphere: the birth of the Amazon River. Here, at the junction of two major tributaries, the Ucayali and the Marañon form the greatest waterfall in the world. Your dawn excursion will be accompanied by abundant bird singing, while strolling by boat, passing along river banks populated by large-billed terns, falcons, exotic short-tailed parrots, and dozens of other birds. Then travel by motorboat to Puerto Prado native village, where you can meet and interact with local villagers in this remote part of the world and learn more about their culture, traditions and folklore. You will also have the opportunity to exchange items for local handicrafts. Return to the cruise to share a last lunch on board. That afternoon, visit the Amazon Wildlife Rescue Center, a kind of animal orphanage where manatees and dolphins receive a specialized care to save them and reintegrate them in the future into their natural habitat. Once you are off the boat, will be transferred to the airport where the guides will help you check and stay with you until you have boarded your flight back to Lima.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p></div>

<div style="text-align: justify;">#1</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">Land at Iquitos International Airport and after collecting your luggage; you will board an air-conditioned bus to transfer you to the main port of Iquitos where the cruise is waiting. Then the Cruise Director and the guides will give a brief orientation talk about</div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">life on board, the places you'll go and what you will see on your Amazon journey. After that, enjoy a welcome dinner while watching the river and surrounding areas through the windows. Before retiring to rest, you can enjoy a cocktail on the upper deck under the stars.</div></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">You will wake up early to watch, for the first time, the sunrise on the Amazon River. After breakfast, visit with the nature guides, around the native community of Magdalena. Walk on a trail where you will see some bird species, insects and flora that surrounds it. You'll get to see, and in some cases buy, crafts made by this kind Amazon people. In addition, you will appreciate a variety of primates, among the 13 species of monkeys resident in the National Park: squirrel and howler monkeys, marmosets and pygmy monkeys. Watch for alligators, and keep your binoculars ready as they see more than 200 species of birds. Finally, return to the cruise for dinner.</div></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#4</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast, the cruise will sail through the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, a flooded forest covering five million acres at the headwaters of the Amazon basin. Get up early and see one of the great spectacles of the</div><div style="text-align: justify;">Southern Hemisphere: the birth of the Amazon River. Here, at the junction of two major tributaries, the Ucayali and the Marañon form the greatest waterfall in the world. You will also have the opportunity to exchange items for local handicrafts. Return to the cruise to share a last lunch on board. After landing in Iquitos City, you will be received and transferred to the Nauta pier, from where you will navigate to the Ucamarca Confluence, where the Amazon River is formed. You will be transferred from there to the Pacaya Samiria Amazon Lodge, where you will enjoy a welcome drink and lunch, and you will hike in the restinga (muddy area) forest to watch the local flora and fauna.  Finally, you will take a nighttime hike before dinner, to watch a great variety of frogs. After having breakfast at the lodge, you will board a boat that will take you to the Reserva Pacaya Samiria, the worlds largest protected flooded area, which is also one of the most biodiverse areas. It is possible to watch howler monkeys, white caymans, sloths, and pink dolphins, among other animals. One-third of the all the species recoreded in the world come from this habitat. After visiting pink dolphins, flooded forests and practicing artisanal fishing, you will return to the lodge to have lunch and then visit the womens temporary crafts center of the San Jorge community. Next, you will take a boat tour by the Marañón River until the San Jacinto cocha, a depression that fills up and forms a lagoon during the rainy season. After visiting the community group for the protection of the paiche, the largest fresh water fish of the Amazon jungle, you will watch the exotic Victoria Regia plant, to finally return to the lodge to have dinner and rest. After breakfast, you will hike by the restinga forest, which will include a cold lunch. Upon your return, you will participate in the program called 'Afternoon Encounter with Man s Cosmovision' which will enable you to have a clearer idea of Ayahuasca and other medicinal plants. In the evening, you will have dinner and go to rest. After breakfast, you will take a boat to navigate the Marañón River to the Shirayacu cocha, where will practice artisanal fishing. You will then return to the lodge to pick up your belongings and go to Iquitos City to catch your flight.

<p style="text-align: justify;">Upon your arrival in Iquitos City, a transportation service will transfer you to the local pier, where you will board a boat to be transferred to the Ceiba Tops lodge, located 40 kilometers from Iquitos navigating the Amazon River. This exclusive accommodation has air conditioned rooms, private bathrooms with hot water and beautiful tropical gardens. You will enjoy lunch upon your arrival at the lodge and in the afternoon you will hike by the nearby jungle to watch the regional flora and fauna. We recommend that you visit the Toucan Bar in the evening, where there will be flute and guitar music and some tourist guide will decide to tell the legends that populate the  collective imagination of the Amazonian societies. You will have breakfast at the lodge in the morning, and then you will visit a community of the Yagua ethnic group, where you will see how the cerbatana (blowgun), the Amazonian traditional hunting tool, is used, and then you may barter some items manufactured by the community members. After lunch, you will take a motor boat tour to the Indiana town, founded by the Franciscan religious community. You must pay attention to the river along the route, as it is not unusual to spot the presence of curious pink dolphins. You will be transferred back to the lodge and the end of your visit. After having breakfast, you may explore some of the jungle that surrounds the lodge. You must pay attention to the sound of the jungle and inspect each corner of the area: thousands of river, flying and terrestrial animal species surround it. You will return to Iquitos after an early lunch and you will be then transferred to the airport to catch your departure flight.</p>

Upon your arrival in Iquitos City, a transportation service will transfer you to the local pier, where you will board a boat to be transferred to the Ceiba Tops lodge, located 40 kilometers from Iquitos navigating the Amazon River. This exclusive accommodation has air conditioned rooms, private bathrooms with hot water and beautiful tropical gardens. You will enjoy lunch upon your arrival at the lodge and in the afternoon you may walk by the nearby jungle to get acquainted with the regional flora and fauna. Inthe evening, you will have dinner and go to rest until the next morning. You will have breakfast at the lodge in the morning, and then you will visit a community of the Yagua ethnic group, where you will see how the cerbatana (blowgun), the Amazonian traditional hunting tool, is used, and then you may buy some items manufactured by the community members. You will then return to the lodge to have lunch and board a boat to return to Iquitos, where you will be transferred to the airport.

Today you will arrive at Iquitos, a bustling river city, and the largest city in the Peruvian Rainforest, with 600,000 people living in its four districts. During the 20th century, the city attracted many Europeans immigrants who contributed to a period of great wealth, commercial, and social development. You will be transferred by coach to the Itaya River for embarkation. After a short orientation and welcome reception, enjoy a traditional welcome dinner on board. Located downriver from Iquitos is a 250-hectare island dedicated to the preservation and protection of orphaned monkeys. Many of the monkeys on the island were found in local markets and donated to the non-profit organization that manages the island and cares for the seven different species of monkeys currently living here. Then continue sailing down the Moman River you will visit a Boras Community. The Boras inhabitants are originally from the upper Putumayo region, near the Peru-Columbia border. Attracted by the rubber boom, they migrated to this area, and still maintain their customs and cultural traditions, celebrating their myths and legends through festivals and ceremonies. You will have the opportunity to visit the Maloca ceremonial place, and see an example of their traditional dance, with their bodies painted in the pattern of a stylized snake. This morning you have the chance to search for the elusive, freshwater, pink river dolphin at the Tahuayo River. This area is a favorite feeding spot for these medium-sized dolphins with little or no dorsal. You may also have the opportunity to enjoy a refreshing swim in this pristine area. Then enter another tributary of the Amazon River that afternoon, the Yarapa River, teeming with tropical flowers, where you will explore its shores and small creeks in search of rare and unusual Amazonian wildlife, including species of monkeys, birds, and iguanas not seen elsewhere. This morning you awake at the birthplace of the Amazon River: the convergence of the Ucayali & Marañon Rivers. After exploring the area, visit the San Francisco village, where you will be greeted by happy smiles from the villagers. After taking time to stroll around the village, visiting the local people?s homes and observing their daily lives, you have an opportunity to visit the local school (when in session), and ask the teacher any questions you may have. The visit ends with a time to shop the village arts and crafts market, where all items are made by the local women an children. Then you will we enjoy that afternoon a hike through the pristine and unique terra firme ecosystem, passing through a tropical rainforest with emergent and canopy trees reaching 55 meters in height, many with buttress roots extending high up the trunks. Palms are abundant, and many of them have stilt roots. This type of forest is one of the most bio-diverse habitats on earth, supporting the largest number of plant and animal species, per unit area, on earth. Our naturalists will introduce you to the giant ceiba, or kapok tree, as well as local medicinal plants we encounter during the hike. This area of the jungle is also teeming with exotic birds, including the bluish-fronted jacamar, collared trogon, red-capped cardinals, and many other colorful species. This morning you will have the chance to explore one of the most spectacular sites of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, the black water of Yana Yacu Pucate River. Aboard skiffs explore deep into the rainforest, to admire its fantastic diversity of flora and fauna, including a myriad bird species, colorful butterflies and other diverse insects, noisy monkey troops, and active pink and grey river dolphins. This evening, you will explore Nauta Caño, its lagoons, and its dynamic, stunning panoramas. Cruising is the best way to experience the diversity of the wildlife that populates the rivers and their jungle habitats, quietly exploring marshes, streams and oxbows. Back aboard our excursion skiffs, head to a beautiful lake, with a spectacular piranha fishing spot. There are 25 different species of piranha in the Amazon basin, and contrary to popular legends, they feed on the flowers, fruit, and outer covering of the trees standing in shallow water. Once darkness begins to envelope the Amazon basin, the jungle comes alive with the cacophony of night herons, pauraques, spectacled owls, and morethan a dozen species of frogs along the river?s edge. Powerful spotlights will allow you to embark on a night safari, searching for the sources of the various sounds, and possibly catching a glimpse of bats flying over the water, scooping up fish for their dinner. In the water, search for a sighting of the spectacled and black. This is also a perfect time for stargazing, while fireflies light up the jungle around you. This morning you have a chance to interact with the local village residents. The children will perform a local dance that tells the story of their traditions, while their parents play music and share a moment of relaxation with their guests. Upon arrival in Iquitos, you will go to Al Frio y al Fuego, a floating restaurant in the middle of the river for your farewell dinner. You will savor local cuisine while watching the sun descend over the river. Upon disembarkation in Iquitos, enjoy a panoramic city tour of the farthest inland port that serves ocean vessels in the world. Pass the historic area of the city, where many of the wonderful houses of the 20th century rubber barons, including the famous ?Casa de Fierro? (the Iron House). After disembarkation, visit the Manatee Rescue Center, a fantastic project committed to saving orphaned manatees and reintroducing them into the wild. Learn about their importance to the world?s ecosystem, and why they so endangered in this part of the Amazon. Maybe you will have the opportunity to swim with these gentle giants. Following this visit, you will be transferred to the airport for your return flight.

After exploring the area, visit the San Francisco village, where you will be greeted by happy smiles from the villagers. After taking time to stroll around the village, visiting the local people?s homes and observing their daily lives, you have an opportunity to visit the local school (when in session), and ask the teacher any questions you may have. The visit ends with a time to shop the village arts and crafts market, where all items are made by the local women an children. Then you will we enjoy that afternoon a hike through the pristine and unique terra firme ecosystem, passing through a tropical rainforest with emergent and canopy trees reaching 55 meters in height, many with buttress roots extending high up the trunks. Palms are abundant, and many of them have stilt roots. This type of forest is one of the most bio-diverse habitats on earth, supporting the largest number of plant and animal species, per unit area, on earth. Our naturalists will introduce you to the giant ceiba, or kapok tree, as well as local medicinal plants we encounter during the hike. This area of the jungle is also teeming with exotic birds, including the bluish-fronted jacamar, collared trogon, red-capped cardinals, and many other colorful species. This morning you will have the chance to explore one of the most spectacular sites of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve, the black water of Yana Yacu Pucate River. Aboard skiffs explore deep into the rainforest, to admire its fantastic diversity of flora and fauna, including a myriad bird species, colorful butterflies and other diverse insects, noisy monkey troops, and active pink and grey river dolphins. This evening, you will explore Nauta Caño, its lagoons, and its dynamic, stunning panoramas. Cruising is the best way to experience the diversity of the wildlife that populates the rivers and their jungle habitats, quietly exploring marshes, streams and oxbows. Back aboard our excursion skiffs, head to a beautiful lake, with a spectacular piranha fishing spot. There are 25 different species of piranha in the Amazon basin, and contrary to popular legends, they feed on the flowers, fruit, and outer covering of the trees standing in shallow water. Once darkness begins to envelope the Amazon basin, the jungle comes alive with the cacophony of night herons, pauraques, spectacled owls, and morethan a dozen species of frogs along the river?s edge. Powerful spotlights will allow you to embark on a night safari, searching for the sources of the various sounds, and possibly catching a glimpse of bats flying over the water, scooping up fish for their dinner. In the water, search for a sighting of the spectacled and black. This is also a perfect time for stargazing, while fireflies light up the jungle around you. This morning you have a chance to interact with the local village residents. The children will perform a local dance that tells the story of their traditions, while their parents play music and share a moment of relaxation with their guests. Upon arrival in Iquitos, you will go to Al Frio y al Fuego, a floating restaurant in the middle of the river for your farewell dinner. You will savor local cuisine while watching the sun descend over the river. Upon disembarkation in Iquitos, enjoy a panoramic city tour of the farthest inland port that serves ocean vessels in the world. Pass the historic area of the city, where many of the wonderful houses of the 20th century rubber barons, including the famous Casa de Fierro (the Iron House). After disembarkation, visit the Manatee Rescue Center, a fantastic project committed to saving orphaned manatees and reintroducing them into the wild. Learn about their importance to the world?s ecosystem, and why they so endangered in this part of the Amazon. Maybe you will have the opportunity to swim with these gentle giants. Following this visit, you will be transferred to the airport for your return flight.

Today you will arrive at Iquitos, a bustling river city, and the largest city in the Peruvian Rainforest, with 600,000 people living in its four districts. During the 20th century, the city attracted many Europeans immigrants who contributed to a period of great wealth, commercial, and social development. You will be transferred by coach to the Itaya River for embarkation. After a short orientation and welcome reception, enjoy a traditional welcome dinner on board. Located downriver from Iquitos is a 250-hectare island dedicated to the preservation and protection of orphaned monkeys. Many of the monkeys on the island were found in local markets and donated to the non-profit organization that manages the island and cares for the seven different species of monkeys currently living here. Then continue sailing down the Moman River you will visit a Boras Community. The Boras inhabitants are originally from the upper Putumayo region, near the Peru-Columbia border. Attracted by the rubber boom, they migrated to this area, and still maintain their customs and cultural traditions, celebrating their myths and legends through festivals and ceremonies. You will have the opportunity to visit the Maloca ceremonial place, and see an example of their traditional dance, with their bodies painted in the pattern of a stylized snake. <p>A bus will pick you up from your hotel in Cusco in the early morning and transfer you to the town of Cachora, to start hiking from Capuliyoq. A few hours later, you may rest and enjoy a snack before you continue to hike until Playa Rosalinas, where you will have lunch. Then, you will begin the hike to Santa Rosa, where you will overnight. You will enjoy tea time upon your arrival, followed by dinner. You will begin your day with a soothing cup of tea before enjoying a nourishing breakfast. At the end, you will prepare your backpack and begin the hike to Choquequirao, the magnificent Inca citadel located in the Vilcabamba Valley. You will take a break on the way to enjoy a snack and then continue to Maranpata, where you will have lunch. Next, you will resume the hike until you reach Choquequirao about 4:30pm, to make the first visit to these impressive ruins. Finally, you will walk to the camp, where you will enjoy a cup of tea, followed by dinner, and will go to sleep. You will wake up very early in the morning with a cup of hot tea, followed by breakfast, to begin your visit to Choquequirao. After a few hours, you will take a break to enjoy a snack and continue to discover the citadel. Next, you will have lunch and will walk to the camp, from where you will take your return path to Playa Rosalinas. You will enjoy tea time upon your arrival, followed by dinner, and will finally go to sleep. You will begin your day with a soothing cup of tea before enjoying a nourishing breakfast. Today you will hike back to Capuliyoq. On the way, you may rest to enjoy a snack, before reaching your destination. A transportation unit will take you from Capuliyoq to Cachora, where you will have lunch. At the end, you will be transferred to your hotel in Cusco.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">--------------------------</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/>Level: Challenging<br/>Maximum altitude: 3137 m a.s.l<br/>Walking distance: 44.5 km - 27.65 miles<br/></p>

<p>A bus will pick you up from your hotel in Cusco in the early morning and take you to the town of Cachora. On the way, you will visit the Tarawasi archaeological complex. Upon your arrival in Cachora, you will start hiking from Capuliyoq. You may rest on the way to enjoy a snack and continue towalk to Cocamasana, where you will have lunch. Finally, you will arrive at the camp in Chiquisca, where you will enjoy tea time, followed by dinner, and will go to sleep. You will begin your day with a soothing cup of tea before enjoying a nourishing breakfast. At the end, you will prepare your backpack and start hiking to Choquequirao, the magnificent Inca citadel located in the Vilcabamba Valley. You will take a break on the way to enjoy a snack and follow to Maranpata, where you will have lunch. Then, you will resume the hike until you reach Choquequirao about 4:30pm, to make the first visit to these impressive ruins. Finally, you will descend to the camp, where you will enjoy tea time, followed by dinner, and will go to sleep. You will wake up very early with a cup of hot tea, followed by breakfast, to start your visit to Choquequirao. A few hours later, you will take a break to enjoy a snack and continue to discover the citadel. Then, you will havelunch and, at the end, you will have free time to walk around Choquequirao at leisure. Finally, you will walk to the camp, where you will enjoy tea time, dinner and will go to sleep. You will begin your day with a soothing cup of tea before enjoying a nourishing breakfast. Today you will hike back to Chiquisca. On the way, youmay rest and enjoy a snack and then you will continue to hike. A few hours later, you will have lunch on the way before reaching your destination at the camp. In the early morning, you will drink a comforting cup of tea before enjoying a nourishing breakfast. Today you will hike back to Capuliyoq. On the way, you may rest and enjoy a snack. A transportation unit will take you from Capuliyoq to Cachora, where you will have lunch. At the end, you will be transferred to your hotel in Cusco.</p><p><br/></p><p><br/><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">-------------------------</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/>Level: Challenging<br/>Maximum altitude: 3137 m a.s.l<br/>Walking distance: 44.5 km - 27.65 miles<br/></p>

<p>#1<br/></p><p>After an early breakfast, we pick you up from your hotel and leave the city of Cusco in the morning. On the route we take a short break in Mollepata  before ascending a winding mountain road to a place called Soraypampa (12,690 ft.) Here, we meet our emergency horse and begin the trek toward Salkantay Pass 4,638 m. (15,213 ft.). At the pass we stop enjoy the views of nowcapped peaks of the Vilcabamba Mountain Range in every direction, with the glaciated south face of Salkantay towering above us where Andean condors can be seen. From the pass we descend towards Wayracmachay to enjoy our lunch and then continue descending along the left bank of the Salkantay River. Observe the landscape become increasingly green, changing from highland to jungle or cloud forest. Feel the warm air rising from the jungle, accompanied by colorful butterflies and striking orchids, while arriving to the second camp at Andenes (9,481ft.), a beautiful spot surrounded with vegetation</p><p>#2<br/></p><p>Today we hike along the Santa Teresa River Valley, through more populated rural areas. We pass through banana, granadilla, avocado and coffee. A hot picnic lunch is served along the way. After lunch, we hike for about 2 hours before a private vehicle meets us for a short drive to the beginning of the Llactapata Inca Trail (30 minutes or so). From the head of the newly restored Inca trail we easily make our way to the last campsite at Lucmabamba. In the evening we will have a coffee taste demonstration with our host, a local family. Dinner will be served and then spend the night in the camp.</p><p>#3<br/></p><p>After breakfast, we will head uphill for 2-3 hours towards Llactapata Pass (8,974ft.) and descend to the archaeological complex of the same name. Besides, we will enjoy a distant but spectacular view of Machu Picchu Sanctuary from the southeast, a view few travelers get the chance to admire. Lunch is served in a scenic viewpoint, looking out to Machu Picchu. After that, begin the final descent to the Aobamba River through lush bamboo forests, orchards and coffee plantations. Finally, we will get to the Hydroelectric Power Station, to board the train to Aguas Calientes and spend the night. There you can stay in the hotel of your choice.</p><p>#4<br/></p><p>After an early breakfast, we make our way to the bus station for the ride up to Machu Picchu. The guided tour of the sanctuary lasts two hours and at the end, you shall have free time to explore the place at your leisure, or simply relax and meditate. We will return by bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch and board the train to Ollantaytambo. From this point, a private vehicle will drive us back to Cusco and you will be transferred to your hotel. You arrival to Cusco will be approximately at 7 or 8 pm.</p><p>------------------------<br/></p><p><!--StartFragment-->Level: Moderate<br/>Maximum altitude: 4650 m a.s.l.<br/>Walking distance: 40 km - 24,85 miles<br/></p><p></p>

<p>#1</p><p style="text-align: justify;">We pick you up early in the morning from your hotel in Cusco to Piscacucho (Km. 82), which is the start point of the Inca Trail. We will make a stop at Ollantaytambo Town for 15 to 20 minutes. Once we reach Piscacucho, we begin our hike until reaching a nice and quiet campsite near Wayllabamba where you will stay. Lunch will be served on the way. This day we hike 5 - 6 hours (7.7miles). At the camp, dinner will be served and spend the night.</p><p>#2</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After having breakfast, we begin the most difficult part of the trek, which consists of an abrupt and steep ascent toward the highest pass on the trail known as Warmi wañusca (4,200m / 13,776ft). Then we begin the descent to our second campsite in Pacaymayo, where we will have lunch and dinner. This day we hike 6 - 7 hours (7.2 miles).</p><p>#3</p><p style="text-align: justify;">The third day is the longest one, but also the most impressive and the most interesting, due the number of archaeological sites and the lush cloud forest area that we cross that is so rich in Andean flora and fauna. From Pacaymayo we climb to Runquracay Pass, and then descend to our lunch stop at Chaquicocha. Next we will continue up an easy climb, until we reach Phuyupatamarca Pass (3,700m / 12,073ft), walk on through the cloud forest until we reach our final campsite at Wiñayhuayna (8,792ft). This day entails 8 - 9 hours and 10 miles of hiking. At the campsite, dinner will be served and then spend the night.</p><p>#4</p><p style="text-align: justify;">After a particularly early breakfast, we depart from the campsite in order to arrive to Inti Punku (Sun Gate); entrance gate to Machu Picchu. From here, you will experience your first extraordinary view of Machu Picchu and then descend to the citadel. Walk on to this iconic ancient city, where your guide will lead a 2-hour tour, and then enjoy some time to explore on your own. After visiting Machu Picchu you will take the bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch, and then take a train ride back to Cusco in the evening.</p><p></p>

<p>#1</p><p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">Visitors will embark on one of the most incredible routes of the Peruvian Andes in the wonderful Andean Explorer. The train has 2 dining cars, a lounge car and an observation car, from where you can appreciate the majestic landscapes of the mountains. It also has elegant suites and cabins where the passenger can rest comfortably. The crossing begins leaving Cusco in the direction of Puno. You will enjoy a delicious lunch and then make the first stop in Raqchi; admirable archaeological site with remains of Temples and Inca constructions. The trip will continue and after tea time, you will be delighted with the sunset at La Raya, the border between Cusco and Puno, surrounded by imposing mountains. Dinner will be served on board and you will arrive at the Lake Titicaca station at night.<br/><br/></div><p>#2<br/></p><p><span style="text-align: justify;">Enjoy the glorious sunrise on Lake Titicaca and breakfast will be served on board. During the rest of the day you can optionally choose between visiting the Uros Islands in a typical boat, visiting the Taquile Island, having lunch in a private area on the beach or show of typical dances and demonstration of handicrafts.</span><br/></p><p></p>

Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a transfer from you hotel in Cusco to the train station of Ollantaytambo. You will continue your trip by train to Aguas Calientes, which takes about two hours. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu Pueblo' and it is the last stop before the entry to the vestiges of Peru s top archaeological complex. Aguas Calientes has a train station, a craft market, restaurants and different categories of accommodations for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and ascend in the early morning. After a short transfer, you will finally reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture which is believed to have been used as a sanctuary and leisure residence for Inca Pachacútec (1438-1471). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is a World Cultural Heritage listed by UNESCO and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. Once you are at the citadel, you may optionally visit the Intipunku, the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space) and the Inca Bridge as well. Overnight stay in Aguas Calientes. Today you may return to Machu Picchu and optionally visit any of the attractions of the citadel. You may visit the Intipunku, which means the  'Gateway of the Sun'  in Spanish, and it is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu, if the Inca Trail has been followed from Ollantaytambo. It was a sort of guardhouse and the Sun rises everyday through this gate if it is observed from the most important buildings of the citadel. You may also climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space), which means 'Young Mountain' as opposed to Machu Picchu. Its ascent takes approximately one hour and its top attraction is the 'Temple of the Moon' a complex of underground constructions probably used for the elite. You may finally go to the Inca Bridge, an amazing stone construction which served to enter or exit the citadel that has an empty space of several meters in the middle, on which they placed two large wooden boards. Thus, the Incas could isolate the city and protect themselves by removing them. In the afternoon, you will go the Aguas Calientes train station to take your train back to Ollantaytambo train staition and finally take you to your hotel in Cusco City.

Your trip to Machu Picchu will start with a transfer from you hotel in Cusco to the train station of Ollantaytambo. You will continue your trip by train to Aguas Calientes, which takes about two hours. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu Pueblo' and it is the last stop before the entry to the vestiges of Peru s top archaeological complex. Aguas Calientes has a train station, a craft market, restaurants and different categories of accommodations for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and ascend in the early morning. After a short transfer, you will finally reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture which is believed to have been used as a sanctuary and leisure residence for Inca Pachacútec (1438-1471). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is a World Cultural Heritage listed by UNESCO and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. Once you are at the citadel, you may optionally visit the Intipunku, the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space) and the Inca Bridge as well. Overnight stay in Aguas Calientes. Today you may return to Machu Picchu and optionally visit any of the attractions of the citadel. You may visit the Intipunku, which means the  'Gateway of the Sun'  in Spanish, and it is the last stop before entering Machu Picchu, if the Inca Trail has been followed from Ollantaytambo. It was a sort of guardhouse and the Sun rises everyday through this gate if it is observed from the most important buildings of the citadel. You may also climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to availability of space), which means 'Young Mountain' as opposed to Machu Picchu. Its ascent takes approximately one hour and its top attraction is the Temple of the Moon, a complex of underground constructions probably used for the elite. You may finally go to the Inca Bridge, an amazing stone construction which served to enter or exit the citadel that has an empty space of several meters in the middle, on which they placed two large wooden boards. Thus, the Incas could isolate the city and protect themselves by removing them. In the afternoon, you will go the Aguas Calientes train station to take your train back to Ollantaytambo train staition and finally take you to your hotel in Cusco City.

Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu pueblo' and is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes there is a train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnight in Aguas Calientes. Today you can return to Machu Picchu and visit any of the attractions of the citadel. Visit the Intipunku or 'Sun Gate' , the last stop before entering Machu Picchu if you follow the Inca trail from Ollantaytambo. It was a checkpoint control and the sun rises every day through this door if viewed from the most important buildings of the citadel. You may climb the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability), which means 'Young Mountain' as opposed to Machu Picchu. This ascent takes about an hour and its main attraction is the 'Temple of the Moon', a complex of underground constructions likely to have been used by the elite. Finally you will be able to know the Inca Bridge, an incredible stonebuilding that served as entrance or exit of the citadel and has a several meters gap halfway joined by two large pieces of wood. Thus the Incas could isolate the city by simply withdrawing them and remain protected. In the afternoon head to the train station of Aguas Calientes and from there take the train back to Cusco.

<p></p><div style="text-align: justify;">#1</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, a transportation service will pick you up and transfer you to the Ollanta Station to board the train to Aguas Calientes, the nearest town to Machu Picchu. Following a scenic journey through dramatic Andean landscapes, arrive at the station in Aguas Calientes. Here you board a coach for a 25-minute ride to Machu Picchu. The ‘Lost City of the Incas’ is believed to have been built in the 1450s by Inca emperor Pachacuti as a holiday retreat, yet within a century the city would be abandoned following the Spanish conquest. The Spaniards never found it, so it was spared the destruction they brought on other Inca sites. Instead, it was slowly swallowed up by the jungle and hidden from harm. A guided tour explains the origins behind this awe-inspiring citadel, presenting its most prominent landmarks such as the monumental Main Square, the Royal Quarters, the Temple of the Three Windows, the Circular Towers, the Sacred Sun Dial, and the Burial Grounds. Return to Aguas Calientes for lunch at the famous Café Inkaterra, a beautiful thatched-roof restaurant hemmed in between the railway and a mountain. </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">#2</div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">The delicious breakfast at the hotel is followed by a relaxing morning at leisure. We strongly recommend waking up early today to see the breathtaking sunrise over Machu Picchu. This is a must-have experience! The best views are from Huayna Picchu, which is about 360 metres above Machu Picchu, boasting an underground temple complex, and from Inti Punku, which offers even better views from about 390 metres above the citadel. Another option is the Inca Bridge, a secret military trail, which controlled the access to the citadel. In the afternoon, transfer to the Aguas Calientes station and take the train back to Ollanta station. From here you will be transferred to your hotel in Cusco. The second entrance to Machu Picchu is not included.</div><p></p><p></p>

Your tour begins with a train ride to Machu Picchu. In about four hours reach the town of Aguas Calientes, also known as 'Machu Picchu'. This is the last stop before entering the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. Finally you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. You may also optionally visit the Intipunku, the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnight in Aguas Calientes. Head to the train station of Aguas Calientes and from there take the train back to Poroy station.

<p>#1 </p><p>After an early breakfast, we pick you up from your hotel and leave the city of Cusco. On the route we will take a short break to visit Tarawasi Archaeological Site near the town of Limatambo (1.5 hrs. approx. from Cusco). After leaving Limatambo, we pass through the mountain village of Mollepata, where we stop for a short coffee break before ascending a winding mountain road to a place called Challacancha (12,690 ft.). Here we will meet our mule driver and emergency horse and begin our trek to Soraypampa (3,900m/ 14,730ft.). In this place we will lunch, and after a short rest continue to climb toward Humantay Lake (3,869 m). Then go back to Soraypampa, which will be the first campsite, and from where we will enjoy a magnificent view of the Salkantay Mountain (20,571ft.) the second most sacred peak in Inca mythology. </p><p>#2 </p><p>This is the main day! After an early breakfast, we hike up to the highest point on the trek which is the Salkantay Pass at 4,638m (15,213ft.). There we will stop to enjoy the amazing views of snowcapped peaks of the Vilcabamba Mountain Range in every direction, and the glaciated south face of Salkantay towering above us, where Andean condors can be seen. From the pass we descend towards Wayracmachay to lunch and then continue our descent along the left bank of the Salkantay River. Observe the landscape become increasingly green, changing from highland to jungle or cloud forest. Feel the warm air rising from the jungle, accompanied by colorful butterflies and striking orchids, while arriving to the second camp at Andenes (9,481ft.), a beautiful spot surrounded with vegetation. </p><p>#3 </p><p>Today we will hike along the Santa Teresa River Valley, through more populated rural areas. We pass through banana, granadilla, avocado and coffee plantations. A hot picnic lunch is served along the way. After lunch, we hike for about 2 hours before a private vehicle meets us for a short drive to the beginning of the ?Llactapata Inca Trail? (30 minutes approx.). From the head of the newly restored Inca Trail we easily make our way to the last campsite at Lucmabamba. In the evening we will have a coffee taste demonstration with our host, a local family. Dinner will be served and then spend the night in the camp. </p><p>#4 </p><p>After breakfast, we will head uphill for 2-3 hours towards Llactapata Pass (8,974ft.) and descend to the archaeological complex of the same name. Besides, we will enjoy a distant but spectacular view of Machu Picchu Sanctuary from the southeast, a view few travelers get the chance to admire. Lunch is served in a scenic viewpoint, looking out to Machu Picchu. After that, begin the final descent to the Aobamba River through lush bamboo forests, orchards and coffee plantations. Finally we will get to the Hydroelectric Power Station, to board the train to Aguas Calientes and spend the night. There you can stay in the hotel of your choice. </p><p>#5 </p><p>After an early breakfast, we make our way to the bus station for the ride up to Machu Picchu. The guided tour of the sanctuary lasts two hours and at the end, you shall have free time to explore the place at your leisure, or simply relax and meditate. We will return by bus to Aguas Calientes for lunch and board the train to Ollantaytambo. From this point, a private vehicle will drive us back to Cusco and you will be transferred to your hotel. You arrival to Cusco will be approximately at 7 or 8 pm.</p><p><br/></p><p><br style="font-size: 12px;"/><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">------------------------</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/>Level: Moderate<br/>Maximum altitude: 4650 m a.s.l.<br/>Walking distance: 44 km - 27,34 miles<br/></p><p></p>

<p>#1 <br/></p><p>We pick you up early in the morning from your hotel and drive you to the train station to board the train that will take us to km. 104 (the starting point of the hike). After crossing the suspension bridge over the Urubamba River we visit the archaeological site of Chachabamba (2,150m / 7,052ft). From here, we will hike upwards for about 8km (approximately 4 hours) and get to the magnificent archaeological site of Wiñayhuayna (2,700m), an impressive complex made up of an agricultural center with numerous terraces, a religious sector as well as an urban sector. On the way we will appreciate the vegetation of the forest. After having the box lunch we will continue for about one hour further onto Inti Punku (The Sun gate) at 2,730m (8,792ft), from where you will have your first dramatic and impressive view of Machu Picchu (2,430m / 7,972ft). As we are going down, we will take a side path off the trail to take a bus that transports us to Aguas Calientes where you will check into your accommodation. </p><p>#2 </p><p>After an early breakfast we will take the bus back up to Machu Picchu. A walking tour is given by a guide through the different sectors of this great Inca legacy, considered Cultural Heritage. In the afternoon, we will take a bus to Aguas Calientes. Then we will board the train back to Cusco.</p><p><br/></p><p><!--StartFragment--><span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;">------------------------</span><br style="font-size: 12px;"/>Level: Moderate<br/>Maximum altitude: Inti Punku 9005 feet | 2745 600 m a.s.l.<br/>Walking distance: 11 km - 6,83 miles<br/></p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Transfer from the hotel to the airport, visiting en route the city of Lampa, also known as the pink city or city of marvels. Lampa is a city that preserves its colonial structure, being its highlights the Temple of Santiago Apostol, where a replica of the Piedad of Miguel Angel Buonaroti is exhibited; its colonial church which houses several paintings of the Cusco school and whose altars and pulpits are sculped in wood; and its Colonial Bridge built out of sillar, which has three archs from where the picturesque extinct volcano of Coachico can be appreciated.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">This tour takes three and a half hours. A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and transfer you to Sillustani, where a guide will be waiting for you. In Sillustani, a peninsula of the Umayo Lagoon, you will</div><div style="text-align: justify;">visit one the worlds largest necropolis, in which stand out over 90 chullpas, huge and primitive stone mausoleums of over 12m high, designed with a wider top than the base. At the end of the visit you will continue to</div><div style="text-align: justify;">the Juliaca airport.</div><p style="text-align: justify;"><br/></p>
<p>A transportation service will take you from the selected hotel to Puno airport, and a representative will assist you along the way.<br/></p>

You will return to your hotel in Puno at the end of the visit.

<p style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">To begin our trip to Puno, you are transferred to the bus station. First, we stop in Andahuaylillas, a town located 40 kilometres from Cusco that has as its main attraction the Church of Andahuaylillas, nicknamed the ‘Sistine Chapel of America’, due to the impressive works of art found in it. We continue our route to Racchi, where the Wiracocha Temple is located, a surprising 30-meters wide by 20-meters high construction of rectangular shape. Have a tasty lunch in Sicuani, followed by a brief stop in La Raya, the highest point between Puno and Cusco, over 4,313 metres above sea level. Then, we arrive at the town of Pucara, where the Pucara Lithic Museum is located. Developed between the years 100 and 300 AC, this town was the first proper urban settlement of the lacustrine highlands. </span><br/></p>
A transportation service will pick you up from the hotel and will transfer you to a restaurant of your choice and bring you back at the established time.

<p style="text-align: justify;">Arrive at the beautiful Hacienda Sarapampa, where you will enjoy a day full of Peruvian traditions. Upon your arrival, the guide will receive you at the forecourt of the plantation with a welcome drink, followed by a guided tour by the agricultural processes and the corn interpretive center around the property. Then, lunch will be served at gardens of the Plantation House composed of a 3-course meal of the delicious local gourmet gastronomy. </p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Arrive to the rural community of Huilloc in the province of Urubamba, and enjoy the Yanapanakuy experience, which will give you the opportunity to contribute with the collective work of the community. You can collaborate on repainting the infrastructures of the local educational building, the medical center and the community hall, or by helping fixing furniture, as well as planting trees. The intercultural interpreter will welcome you to the community accompanied by musicians and guide you to the house of a local resident that will share with you a soothing muña infusion. Then, the authorities and community leaders will assign you a house where you can make yourself comfortable and store your belongings. Before starting to work, every participant will ask permission to the protective deities. After accomplishing the task and according to the schedule, there will be a moment to rest and enjoy refreshments. According to the tradition of the community, the men of Minka (collective work system) will give you a proper farewell with songs and dances. Finally, visit the Huilloc craft center where you will have the opportunity to take with you a traditional community garment as a souvenir of the experience.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">Arrive to the rural community of Huilloc in the province of Urubamba, to perform a traditional Inca practice called Kusi Runa, in which you will participate in a day of Chakra (agriculture). A local interpreter will welcome you and guide you to the house of the owner of the Chakra, who will invite you a muña infusion. Symbolically be an integral part of the community, and as a community member you will be dressed with typical clothes of Huilloc (ponchos for men and blankets for women) and use traditional tools. Males will do farming while the women cook for them with local organic products, ending in a spiritual ritual with local music and dance. Therefore, you will have an excellent opportunity to live and experience the everyday way of seeing and acting of the Andean Runa (male).</p>
A representative will assist you upon your arrival in Paracas and you will be transferred from the bus station to the dock (shared service)
Entrance to Julio C. Tello Museum, that exhibits a marvelous collection of objects of the Paracas Civilization.

City tour of Iquitos including the Plaza de Armas (main square),the plaza  28  de Julio and Palacio de Justicia.  On the way back, pass by the local market and the Casa de Hierro (iron house) designed by Gustave  Eiffel.  Proceed to the Quistococha lagoon, botanical park and zoo to admire the rainforest flora and fauna

You will start your tour in downtown Iquitos, visiting the Main Square, the heart of a city that has a hectic activitiy 24 hours a day. You will then visit the 28 de Julio Square, a recreational area that exhibits in its center the old locomotive that pulled Iquitos first train, and the end of the century before last, in the midst of the Rubber Boom. Next, you will visit the local Palace of Justice and then you will go to the main market that supplies the city with a great variety of Amazonian fruit, fresh water fish and local products, a feast of colors, smells and tates. Then you wll go to the Iron House, located on the Main Square, designed by Gustave Eiffel. It is an old structure built in 1887, considered the first prefabricated house in the Americas, which shows the economic splendor during the Rubber Boom. You will finally visit the Belén riparian hamlet, a community of Iquitos in which the wooden houses built on beams and columns are elevated some meters above the ground during the dry season, while in the rainy season they seem to float and its inhabitants have to cross theflooded street in canoes.

You will be picked up from your hotel and taken to the beautiful Paracas National Reserve, for a tour of 2 hours 30 min to discover natural attractions like the Cathedral, Yumaque beach, Paracas National Reserve Interpretation Museum, Flamencos Viewpoint, and the Obelisk of independence. Throughout the tour, you will be accompanied by a professional guide. In the end, you will be taken back to your hotel in Paracas.

Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride from Ollantaytambo to Aguas Calientes. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu pueblo' and is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes there is a train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Overnight in Aguas Calientes. Breakfast at the hotel. After a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge.Return to Aguas Calientes to enjoy lunch. You will return to the Aguas Calientes train station in the afternoon to board your train back to the Ollantaytambo station.

<div style="text-align: justify;">After breakfast at the hotel, a transportation service will pick you up and transfer you to the Ollanta Station to board the train to Aguas Calientes, the nearest town to Machu Picchu. Following a scenic journey through dramatic Andean landscapes, arrive at the station in Aguas Calientes. Board a coach for a 25-minute ride to Machu Picchu. The ‘Lost City of the Incas' is believed to have been built in the 1450s by Inca emperor Pachacuti as a holiday retreat, yet within a century the city would be abandoned following the Spanish conquest. The Spaniards never found it, so it was spared the destruction they brought on other Inca sites. Instead, it was slowly swallowed up by the jungle and hidden from harm. A guided tour explains the origins behind this awe-inspiring citadel and its most prominent landmarks such as the monumental Main Square, the Royal Quarters, and the Temple of the Three Windows, the Circular Towers, the Sacred Sun Dial and the Burial Grounds. Return toAguas Calientes for a tasty lunch at the famous Café Inkaterra, a beautiful thatched-roof restaurant hemmed in between the railway and a mountain. In the afternoon, transfer to the Aguas Calientes station to take the train back to the Ollanta station, from where you will be taken to your hotel in Cusco.<br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div>

Today you will ride the dunes that shape the horizon of the desert on sand buggies,with an expert driver that will show you inaccessible areas in the middle of the hottest sand dunes. Enrich your experience going up and down, discovering new routes in an experience of filled with adventure.

<p style="text-align: justify;">Today you will leave Chivay by a recommended route for mountain biking lovers, along the right bank of the Colca River, to enjoy the charm of the Valley, visiting different villages as Coporaque to discover the oldest Chapel and Temple in the Valley. Then continue to the Ocolle viewpoint, a place surrounded by Pre-Inca terraces, known as the amphitheater, which shows the impressive technology in agriculture and hydraulic system of the Collagua ethnicity. On the way you will reach the Chininia viewpoint where you can observe Colcas that were used to deposit grains by ancient people. To end the tour, enjoy a moment of relaxation in the Chaccapi hot springs (optional) and return to Chivay in transportation service. The tour lasts 03 hours (02:00 hours cycling).</p>

Today you will be driven to Yanque to meet with the guide and horses tostart the activity. The tour will take 2, 3 or 4 hours, according to theitinerary you choose (It can be Circuit  N° 1, 2 or 3).CIRCUIT N° 01: TRADITIONAL YANQUE.We start from the district of Yanque of Colca Valley, where we can observe the Immaculate Conception Colonial Temple dating from the seventeenth century, and then go to the Chacapi hot springs. Later, we cross the Cervantes Bridge spotting the countryside and watching at distance the Uyu-Uyu Archaeological Complex. Continuing the ride, we descend to the Colcas of Chininia located below the Siphon Bridge, which were places to storage agricultural products, and then go towards the district of Yanque where the circuit will end. The tour lasts 2 hours. At the end of the horse ride, you will be driven back to your hotel.CIRCUIT N° 02: YANQUE - UYU-UYU ARCHAEOLOGICAL CENTER.We start from the district of Yanque of Colca Valley, where we can observe the Immaculate Conception Colonial Temple dating from the seventeenth century, and then go to the Chacapi hot springs. Later, we cross the Cervantes Bridge heading towards the Uyu-Uyu Archaeological Complex, to find Collahua and Inca constructions. Continuing the ride, we descend to the Colcas of Chininia located below the Siphon Bridge, which were places to storage agricultural products, and then go to the district of Yanque where the circuit will end. The tour lasts 3 hours. At the end of the horse ride, you will be driven back to your hotel.CIRCUIT  N° 03: YANQUE - COPORAQUE.We start from the district of Yanque of Colca Valley, where we can observe the Immaculate Conception Colonial Temple dating from the seventeenth century, and then go to the Colcas of Chininia located below the Siphon Bridge, which were places to storage agricultural products. Continue riding up to the Ccollojani hill, from where we can see all the countryside of this area towards the archaeological site of San Antonio. Then we will go to district of Coporaque to have a break and take the opportunity to enter the colonial church called St. James of Apostle, built in 1569. We will continue heading to the great room of Collahua structure that belonged to the great Princess Mama Llachi, and then resume the ride up to Occolli viewpoint to admire the terraces. In this way, we will reach our starting point. The tour lasts 3 hours. At the end of the horse ride, you will be driven back to your hotel.

<p>A transportation service will pick you up from your hotel and will transfer you to the traditional Pueblo Libre district, to visit the Larco Museum. Founded by Rafael Larco Hoyle in 1926, the museum is located inside a viceregal mansion built on a pre-Columbian pyramid of the 7th century. The visit to this museum enables to understand over five thousand years of a high degree of development of the cultures that flourished before the Inca Empire. It shows the most complete pre-Hispanic collection of gold and silver artifacts as well pieces of erotic art. Over 45,000 archaeological artifacts bear witness of five thousand years of Peruvian history. At the end of the tour, you may do your shopping at the museum shop. You will then be transferred to Café del Museo Restaurant and back to your hotel after enjoying lunch.</p>

Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu Pueblo' and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes you will find the railway station, a crafts market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. In the afternoon return by train to Cusco.

Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes .This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu Pueblo' and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes are the railway station, a crafts market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. At the end of the tour, you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes. In the afternoon return by train to Cusco.

<p style="text-align: justify;">Early morning departure from your hotel in Urubamba to drive to the locality of Pachar, located 8 kms. from Ollantaytambo, where you will find the Via Ferrata, a climbing route  300 meters high.  Here you will have the opportunity to climb a mountain with a safety system permanently installed, for which you do not require any previous experience. While climbing,  enjoy the mystical landscapes of the Sacred Valley of the Incas. Upon completing the ascent, enjoy an energyzing snack while you admire the spectacular scenery and recover your energies. Then climb 100 more mts  until you reach the second cable of the canopy or zip-line, which will allow you to fly over the beautiful landscapes of  the Sacred Valley of the Incas, in a very safe and fun way. The canopy or zip-line, also called  flying fox, zip wire, death slide, or aerial runway, is an unforgettable experience. Through a pulley suspended by 6 cables of 100 to 500 m (with a total of 2000 m), located in an inclination, you hook the cable through a pulley fixed  to your harness and you will be impulsed by gravity, sliding from the top to the bottom, increasingly losing speed as you approach the end of the cable. At the end of your adventure, transfer back to  the hotel.</p>

<div style="text-align: justify;">During the morning, you are picked up from your hotel to visit the town of Chinchero, a traditional Cusco urban centre located at an elevated point with a beautiful view of the valley. The inhabitants of this area preserve the artisan traditions and the inherited knowledge of the Incas and display them in their clothes and customs. In Chinchero there are also archaeological remains and a beautiful seventeenth-century church, one of the first Catholic buildings in Peru. Then, you are transferred to the Living Museum of Yucay, a centre Andean traditions interpretations, where demonstrations of the elaboration of textile work, adobe, pottery and silverware are carried out in the same way in which they used to in antiquity; an original recreation of Inca traditions. Also, residents of the museum include llamas, sheep and alpacas, and they can be fed! . Upon arriving in Ollantaytambo visit its town, inhabited uninterruptedly since Inca times, and climb the iconic fortress, built next to a mountain. Later, have lunch in Ollantaytambo and live a superior Andean gastronomy experience in a restaurant, where the true spirit of the Sacred Valley is transmitted in a cosy atmosphere.! At the end of this activity, you are transferred back to your hotel. </div><div style="text-align: justify;"><br/></div><div style="text-align: justify;">*On Sundays, also visit the local market.</div>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will begin your trip visiting the archaeological vestiges of Maras and Moray. Maras is an impressive salt mine complex. These are huge salt deposits that were exchanged for other products from other parts of the empire.  Moray s archaeological vestiges are composed of mysterious concentric circular terraces which, according to some scholars, would be an agricultural research center. The higher external terraces would be at a higher temperature than the inner terraces and could simulate the different ecological strata of the empire. You will have free time to enjoy lunch and then continue the trip to the Ollantaytambo Park, stopping at various points of interest along the route. Ollantaytambo is a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas, whose residents still live in accordance with their old traditions. Pachacútec (1438-1471) conquered the town, destroyed it and built a new one, absorbing it as part of his empire, and it is currently considered one of the most remarkable archaeological complexes of the Inca Empire. Then the walk, yo can taste a delicious lunch.</p>

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your tour at the Pisac market, which has stands that exhibit and sell products manufactured by the residents of the area. Beautiful alpaca textiles, musical instruments, paintings, carvings, necklaces and replicas of old Inca pieces stand out. You will continue to the Pisac archaeological sites, which has the largest complex of terraces in the area and is a masterpiece of the agriculture of the Inca Empire. Next, you will have lunch at the previously agreed restaurant and then you will visit Ollantaytambo, making a series of tops at points of interest. The Ollantaytambo town, a typical example of the urban planning of the Incas, whose residents still live in accordance with their old traditions. Pachacútec conquered the town, destroyed it and built a new one, absorbing it as part of his empire. It exhibits one of the most remarkable complexes of the Inca Empire. You will return to your hotel in Sacred Valley.</p>

Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a train ride to Aguas Calientes. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu pueblo' and is the last stop before entering the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes there is a train station, a craft market, restaurants and hotels of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer by bus you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. Overnight in Aguas Calientes.

<p style="text-align: justify;">You will start your trip at the Chinchero town, which was one of the most important villages during the Inca Empire. Then you will have lunch and continue your trip until reaching the archaeological vestiges of Maras and Moray. Maras is a surreal view. It is near Moray and it is an impressive salt exploitation complex. Its huge salt deposits were used to trade them for other products with other parts of the empire. Moray s archaeological vestiges are composed of mysterious concentric circular terraces which, according to some scholars, would be an agricultural research center. The higher external terraces would be at a higher temperature than the inner ones and could simulate the conditions of the different ecological strata of he empire. Then you will be transferred back to your hotel in the Sacred Valley.</p>

You will be picked up from your hotel to start your journey to Ollantaytambo park, stopping at several points of interest. The town of Ollantaytambo is a typical example of inca urban planning still inhabited as in the past. Pachacutec (1438-1471) conquered the town, destroyed it and built a new one absorbing it as part of his government, becoming one of the most remarkable archaeological Inca complex. Then you will be transferred to the new and impressive Inkariy museum, which presents a new vision of the pre-Hispanic cultures of ancient Peru through dioramas that recreate iconic scenes composed of hyper realistic characters, reflecting the native man with the clothing and ornamentation specific to each era. After the visit, you will have free time for lunch. Pisac market, is the next point ahead. Daily the main square is filled with dozens of stands that display and sell products created by the residents of the area. Find beautiful alpaca textiles, musical instruments, paintings, carvings, necklaces and replicas of ancient Inca pieces. Guests can wander around and discover the delights of this part of the Sacred Valley. At the end you will return to your hotel.

<p><span style="font-size: 12px;text-align: justify;float: none;"></span></p><div style="text-align: justify;">A transportation service and a representative will pick you up at Cusco´s airport and will transfer you to your selected hotel at the Sacred Valley. On the way, taking the route to Pisaq, you will visit <span style="font-size: 12px;float: none;"> the living Museum of Yucay .</span><p>This museum is a centre of Andean handicraft traditions, where demonstrations of the elaboration of textile, adobe, ceramics and silverware are worked in the same way in which they were made in ancient times, an original recreation. At your arrival to the hotel, you will be assisted with the check-in and accomodation.</p></div><!--EndFragment--><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="font-size: 12px;"><br/></span></div><p><br/></p><p><br/></p><p></p>

Your tour to Machu Picchu begins with a trasnfer to Ollantaytambo train stsation. You will continue with a train ride to Aguas Calientes in approximately two hours. This town is also known as 'Machu Picchu pueblo' and is the last stop before the entrance to the remains of the most important archaeological site in Peru. In Aguas Calientes are the railway station, a crafts market, restaurants and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb it early. Finally, after a short transfer you will reach Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed served as a sanctuary and retreat for the Inca Pachacutec (1438 - 1572). Machu Picchu, which means 'Old Mountain', is considered a World Heritage by UNESCO and one of the new seven wonders of the world. Once in the citadel you may optionally also visit the Intipunku (Sun Gate), the Wayna Picchu (subject to space availability) and the Inca Bridge. In the afternoon return by train.

<p style="text-align: justify;">A mobility will pick you up from your hotel in Urubamba and you will be transferred to the Ollanta train station. Your trip to Machu Picchu will begin with a train trip to the town of Aguas Calientes. In Aguas Calientes there is a market for handicrafts, restaurants, and accommodation of different categories for those who prefer to spend the night at the foot of the mountain and climb up early. After a short trip, you will arrive at Machu Picchu, a masterpiece of engineering and architecture that is believed to have served as a sanctuary and resting place for the Inca Pachacutec. Machu Picchu, which means Old Mountain, is considered a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the new seven wonders of the modern world. At the end of the tour, you will return by bus to Aguas Calientes.</p>

Ollantaytambos train station is located close to the site where the old Inca hanging bridge crossed the Urubamba. Move along the tracks following the rivers course into the jungle lowlands to Aguas Calientes also known as Machu Picchu Pueblo. This is the last stop before getting to the famous archaeological complex. Find crafts, books, restaurants and array of hotels in this buzzling town. The bus zig-zags up the steep side of the mountain for nearly 20 minutes.
